.review/results/p1-adjudication-r1.codex.md:2892:I'll treat this as a design challenge, not a rubber stamp: read the preregistration, harness, and verdict rule, run the selftests, then argue whether the Mac↔Mac run can actually answer the question.Continuing the harness (ABBA schedule and measurement path) and running the selftests.Running the verdict guard tests and the harness selftest, and probing a few design-risk spots.Checking a few more concrete failure modes that could produce a wrong verdict:## 1–2. Verdict on the question and the outcomes
.review/results/p1-adjudication-r1.codex.md:2896:The only clean test of “can P1 occur with no Windows peer?” is a real same-OS pair that is not Linux↔Linux. Mac↔Mac over 10GbE is that cell. Loopback would not do (the parent plan already says a negative loopback is inconclusive). So as a *scientific* 2×2 fill, the *shape* of the question is fine.
.review/results/p1-adjudication-r1.codex.md:2904:| Linux↔Linux | No (8/8) |
.review/results/p1-adjudication-r1.codex.md:2914:| **REPRODUCES** | P1 can appear with no Windows peer **on this pair**. Not “Windows residue.” Code-level and macOS/APFS/host×role explanations stay open. Does **not** kill or confirm H1. | “H1 is confirmed” / “platform-general layout cost” / “skip Windows investigation” | **No.** Still pf-1 / fix on **rig W**, where release is blocked. |
.review/results/p1-adjudication-r1.codex.md:2922:**No outcome changes the release-critical next step:** attribute and fix (or formally re-scope) P1 on the macOS↔Windows pair. Mac↔Mac only reweights priors inside pf-1, and only if it returns a *decisive* REPRODUCES / DOES-NOT-REPRODUCE. Under the rule’s own austerity, decisive outcomes are not the mode you should bet on (below).
.review/results/p1-adjudication-r1.codex.md:2930:3. **Only if** you need the platform map for a criterion conversation: a **minimal** Mac↔Mac TCP×mixed both directions (even informal n≈4) before this six-cell cathedral. If ratios sit at ~1.00 vs ~1.30, you already know; then either formalize or stop.
.review/results/p1-adjudication-r1.codex.md:2951:| 40 ms | 15 ms | ~92% REPRODUCES, ~8% CONTROLS-NOT-CLEAN |
.review/results/p1-adjudication-r1.codex.md:2952:| 60 ms | 20 ms | ~56% REPRODUCES, ~41% CONTROLS-NOT-CLEAN |
.review/results/p1-adjudication-r1.codex.md:2953:| 80 ms | 25 ms | ~17% REPRODUCES, ~78% CONTROLS-NOT-CLEAN |
.review/results/p1-adjudication-r1.codex.md:2961:- Δ_ref = 230 ms is **W’s** absolute effect. On faster Mac↔Mac arms, T often becomes `src/10` (stricter null, easier small relative EFFECT). Not wrong, but the null gets harder on a fast pair.
.review/results/p1-adjudication-r1.codex.md:2969:| **HIGH** | EFFECT also needs **min(d) ≥ T+B** (every pair). A real ~25% effect with one soft pair → UNCLEAR, not REPRODUCES. Conservative, but weak power. |
.review/results/p1-adjudication-r1.codex.md:2993:Would I trust a null out of it? **Yes**, if I got one. Would I expect to get one on a live APFS/10GbE pair with purge/drain/fsync in the loop? **Often no** — UNCLEAR / CONTROLS-NOT-CLEAN instead. That is the rule working as a nervous instrument, not as a sharp classifier.
.review/results/p1-adjudication-r1.codex.md:3008:I did **not** find a live “false REPRODUCES / false DOES-NOT-REPRODUCE” in the graded path comparable to the historical timer/settle/control-void blockers. Remaining issues are smaller or process-level.
.review/results/p1-adjudication-r1.codex.md:3028:**DO NOT RUN IT — skip Mac↔Mac for now and spend the session on pf-1 / dial-accept diagnostics on the macOS↔Windows pair where P1 is known; no outcome of this rig changes that obligation, and the rule is likely to spend the run on CONTROLS-NOT-CLEAN.**

.review/results/p1-adjudication-r1.codex.md:3766:- Linux’s immediate-flush results provide secondary support: all eight cells pass, with the two TCP-mixed ratios at 1.092 and 1.003. One Linux direction has a larger transfer-only ratio, 1.188, but its flush medians are exactly equal at 780 ms; that is not evidence of a free-writeback artifact. Because Linux writeback differs from macOS, this control is weaker than the direct Mac flush and gRPC evidence.
.review/results/p1-adjudication-r1.codex.md:3783:- Linux’s immediate-flush results provide secondary support: all eight cells pass, with the two TCP-mixed ratios at 1.092 and 1.003. .review/results/macmac-harness-r10.codex-engine.md:2431: m=[r.randint(-600,600) for _ in range(8)]; ms=r.choice([600,1000,2000,2500,5000]); c=[r.randint(-300,300) for _ in range(8)]; s=r.choice([600,1000,2500,5000]); tp=min(s/10,230)*.5; tn=-min(s/11,230)*.5; ok=tn<min(c) and max(c)<tp; clean+=ok; counts[s]=counts.get(s,0)+ok
.review/results/macmac-harness-r10.codex-engine.md:2450:- `scripts/otp12pf_mac_verdict.py:83-99` — **BLOCKER** — Registered cell identities and roles come from mutable environment variables. Omitting a dirty control, or excluding one measurand from `VERDICT_CELLS`, can produce `REPRODUCES`/`DOES-NOT-REPRODUCE` despite the preregistration requiring all six fixed cells (`PREREGISTRATION.md:156-163`). No guard covers this.
.review/results/macmac-harness-r10.codex-engine.md:2471:- `scripts/otp12pf_mac_verdict.py:83-99` — **BLOCKER** — Registered cell identities and roles come from mutable environment variables. Omitting a dirty control, or excluding one measurand from `VERDICT_CELLS`, can produce `REPRODUCES`/`DOES-NOT-REPRODUCE` despite the preregistration requiring all six fixed cells (`PREREGISTRATION.md:156-163`). No guard covers this.
.review/results/macmac-harness-r3.grok.md:13:| `scripts/otp12pf_mac_verdict.py:289-294` + `:301-307` | **BLOCKER** | **RIG-VOID still fails open for a dirty control that the bar forgives.** Void only if `bar==FAIL` or outcome ∈ {UNSTABLE, REPRODUCES, INVERSION, BAR-FAIL-INCONSISTENT}. A control with a **real, 8/8, rig-W-sized effect** (`d_i=230` every pair) on a slow arm (`src=2500`, ratio **1.092**, bar **PASS**) is only **PARTIAL**, so it does **not** void. Session still emits **VANISHES** with a “CONTROL CAVEAT”. Same structural hole as rev‑3’s bar-tied margin: on a slow arm the bar is wider than Δ_ref, so a full Δ_ref control effect is labeled “sub-bar” and escapes. Guard/mutations never cover this path (they only kill bar-FAIL escape). |
.review/results/macmac-harness-r3.grok.md:15:| `scripts/otp12pf_mac_verdict.py:318-340` vs prereg “either direction” | **HIGH** | **Session precedence can hide a clean one-direction REPRODUCES.** Pre-reg: reproduction in *either* direction answers the question. Code ranks **BAR-FAIL-INCONSISTENT** and **UNSTABLE** above **REPRODUCES**. Driven: `nq_tcp_mixed=REPRODUCES` (8/8, +300…370) + `qn_tcp_mixed=BAR-FAIL-INCONSISTENT` → session **BAR-FAIL-INCONSISTENT**, not REPRODUCES. Not a false null, but a **false non-reproduction** against the registered “either direction” rule. |
.review/results/macmac-harness-r3.grok.md:19:| `scripts/otp12pf_mac_verdict.py:214-215` + prereg dual claim | **MEDIUM** | **CI and sign test are not duals once zeros exist.** Example: `d=[0,300…360]`, bar FAIL, `sign_p=0.0156` (7/7 pos) but `CI_lo=0` → not `pos_effect` → **BAR-FAIL-INCONSISTENT**. Conservative against false REPRODUCES; the session text still says pairs “do not agree in sign,” which is **false** here (nonzeros all agree; a zero blocked the CI). |
.review/results/macmac-harness-r3.grok.md:31:Synthetic session: measurand noise `[-4,-2,-1,0,0,1,2,3]` @ src=2000; all four controls `d_i=230` ×8 @ src=2500.
.review/results/macmac-harness-r3.grok.md:36:  nq_grpc_mixed  PARTIAL  ratio=1.092 bar=PASS  D=+230ms CI=[+230,+230] (99.2%) sign_p=0.0078 (8/8)
.review/results/macmac-harness-r3.grok.md:54:### R4 — HIGH: one-direction REPRODUCES masked
.review/results/macmac-harness-r3.grok.md:56:`nq_tcp_mixed` REPRODUCES (300–370ms) + `qn_tcp_mixed` BAR-FAIL-INCONSISTENT → session **BAR-FAIL-INCONSISTENT**, not REPRODUCES.
.review/results/macmac-harness-r3.grok.md:79:All seven mutation old-strings exist in the engine; each revert produced the forbidden verdict (`VANISHES` or `PARTIAL`/`REPRODUCES` as named). **Not vacuous for those seven fixes.** Missing coverage is the defect, not fake kills.
.review/results/macmac-harness-r3.grok.md:90:| Either-direction REPRODUCES | **cell rule yes; session precedence no** |
.review/results/macmac-harness-r3.grok.md:98:- **False REPRODUCES** from the decision rule alone without planting a real 8/8 positive material effect (good).
.review/results/macmac-harness-r3.grok.md:103:Residual physical artifact (settle/ssh ~15 ms) is documented; unlikely alone to manufacture material REPRODUCES on mixed-scale arms, but it was not re-measured on the rig here.
.review/results/macmac-harness-r3.grok.md:109:The round‑2 RIG-VOID class is only **half** closed: bar-FAIL controls void; **Δ_ref-sized PARTIAL controls do not**, and I drove a clean **VANISHES** while every control sat at **D=+230 / 8/8 / ratio 1.092**. Until control voiding uses the same absolute materiality the power gate claims (or voids any control `pos_effect` / `ci_lo ≥ DELTA_REF`), plus engine-side **n=8 & coverage** enforcement, this instrument can still print a confident null on a dirty or under-specified session.
.review/results/macmac-prereg-r2.codex.md:124:- `docs/bench/otp12-macmac-2026-07-14/PREREGISTRATION.md:94-123,140-143`; `scripts/bench_otp12pf_mac.sh:448-475,488-495` — **BLOCKER** — `S=max(d_i)-min(d_i)` is neither an MDE nor an equivalence/confidence bound at `n=8`; the “power” test ignores how close observed `D` is to 230 ms. With `srcinit=2000×8` and `d=[0,180,180,190,190,200,200,200]`, it returns PASS, `D=190`, `S=200`, `powered=yes`, hence VANISHES if repeated in both cells—despite seven positive pairs and an effect already 83% of reference. It also uses `min(src,dest)` instead of the source baseline.
.review/results/macmac-prereg-r2.codex.md:126:- `docs/bench/otp12-macmac-2026-07-14/PREREGISTRATION.md:125-161`; `scripts/bench_otp12pf_mac.sh:403-496` — **BLOCKER** — The six outcomes remain overlapping and incomplete. MIXED-SIGN is shadowed by the earlier REPRODUCES/INVERSION cases; FAIL with `|D|≤S`, sub-bar negative asymmetry, and incomplete cells are unmapped; UNDERPOWERED and UNSTABLE are extra outcomes. `compute_verdicts` implements none of the session rule, control gate, clustering, or six outcomes—it emits only per-cell PASS/FAIL/INCOMPLETE.
.review/results/macmac-prereg-r2.codex.md:142:- `docs/bench/otp12-macmac-2026-07-14/PREREGISTRATION.md:39-42`; `docs/plan/OTP12_PERF_FINDINGS.md:3-17`; `docs/DECISIONS.md:179-180` — **LOW** — The accepted sequencing conflict was changed only in `docs/STATE.md`: the active plan still says pf-1 starts now and the settled sequence says MTU→pf-1, while rev 2 inserts Mac↔Mac before pf-1.
.review/results/macmac-prereg-r2.codex.md:151:- `docs/bench/otp12-macmac-2026-07-14/PREREGISTRATION.md:94-123,140-143`; `scripts/bench_otp12pf_mac.sh:448-475,488-495` — **BLOCKER** — `S=max(d_i)-min(d_i)` is neither an MDE nor an equivalence/confidence bound at `n=8`; the “power” test ignores how close observed `D` is to 230 ms. With `srcinit=2000×8` and `d=[0,180,180,190,190,200,200,200]`, it returns PASS, `D=190`, `S=200`, `powered=yes`, hence VANISHES if repeated in both cells—despite seven positive pairs and an effect already 83% of reference. It also uses `min(src,dest)` instead of the source baseline.
.review/results/macmac-prereg-r2.codex.md:153:- `docs/bench/otp12-macmac-2026-07-14/PREREGISTRATION.md:125-161`; `scripts/bench_otp12pf_mac.sh:403-496` — **BLOCKER** — The six outcomes remain overlapping and incomplete. MIXED-SIGN is shadowed by the earlier REPRODUCES/INVERSION cases; FAIL with `|D|≤S`, sub-bar negative asymmetry, and incomplete cells are unmapped; UNDERPOWERED and UNSTABLE are extra outcomes. `compute_verdicts` implements none of the session rule, control gate, clustering, or six outcomes—it emits only per-cell PASS/FAIL/INCOMPLETE.
.review/results/macmac-prereg-r2.codex.md:169:- `docs/bench/otp12-macmac-2026-07-14/PREREGISTRATION.md:39-42`; `docs/plan/OTP12_PERF_FINDINGS.md:3-17`; `docs/DECISIONS.md:179-180` — **LOW** — The accepted sequencing conflict was changed only in `docs/STATE.md`: the active plan still says pf-1 starts now and the settled sequence says MTU→pf-1, while rev 2 inserts Mac↔Mac before pf-1.
.review/results/otp-12-worker-parity.fix-review.gpt-verdict.md:1:# otp-12-worker-parity fix review — adjudication
.review/results/otp-12-worker-parity.fix-review.gpt-verdict.md:3:**Slice**: `cfd9dd7` — first-byte-safe convergence and terminal resize refusal.
.review/results/otp-12-worker-parity.fix-review.gpt-verdict.md:5:**Raw review**: `.review/results/otp-12-worker-parity.fix-review.codex.md`
.review/results/otp-12-worker-parity.fix-review.gpt-verdict.md:29:`.review/results/otp-12-worker-parity.atomic-fix.gpt-verdict.md`.
.review/results/otp-12-worker-parity.fix-review.gpt-verdict.md:31:Full workspace fmt, strict clippy, and tests are green: 1,490 passed, 2
.review/results/otp-12-worker-parity.fix-review.gpt-verdict.md:34:Fix commit: `8e993aa`.
.review/results/macmac-harness-r6.codex.md:31:  r5: ...still escaping via the UNDERPOWERED label (one zero pair demotes the cell) -> and separately, `bar == FAIL` had NO DIRECTION, so a +1ms effect at n=16 reported REPRODUCES.
.review/results/macmac-harness-r6.codex.md:65:A concrete branch-completeness failure is emerging in the decision rule: although direction is now tied to the bar’s sign, `bar_fail_pos`/`bar_fail_neg` still bypass the CI magnitude test. I’m reproducing whether a marginal-median bar flip plus a tiny paired effect can still become `REPRODUCES`, then I’ll check whether the guard/mutations can see it.
.review/results/macmac-harness-r6.codex.md:89:The official suite is green (22/22, 15/15), but it does not cover the marginal-bar/paired-CI disagreement: a synthetic n=8 session with paired median `D=+1 ms` was labeled `REPRODUCES` solely because one outlier moved the marginal median. I’m now probing the escalation evidence and self-test classifications for similarly “present but not actually enforced” protections.
.review/results/macmac-harness-r6.codex.md:105:The control obligation is structurally complete for the null branch—an uncertified control cannot reach `VANISHES`—but it creates a separate false-positive path: `REPRODUCES` ignores uncertified controls entirely. I reproduced a headline reproduction while all four controls showed `D=+230 ms`, 7/7 nonzero positives, and CI `[0,230]`; the verdict text emitted no control caveat.
.review/results/macmac-harness-r6.codex.md:113:- [scripts/otp12pf_mac_verdict.py:299](/Users/michael/Dev/blit_v2/scripts/otp12pf_mac_verdict.py:299) — **BLOCKER** — The three-way split is incomplete. `material = bar_fail_pos OR CI_lo >= breach_hi`, so a marginal-median bar failure still bypasses the CI magnitude test. At n=16, paired `D=1 ms`, CI `[1,1]`, and sign 16/16 produces `REPRODUCES` after three outliers move the marginal median. The same defect makes a control simultaneously “certified clean” (`null_excl=yes`) and “contaminating” via [line 418](/Users/michael/Dev/blit_v2/scripts/otp12pf_mac_verdict.py:418), falsely yielding `RIG-VOID`. The preregistration itself embeds this contradiction at [PREREGISTRATION.md:227](/Users/michael/Dev/blit_v2/docs/bench/otp12-macmac-2026-07-14/PREREGISTRATION.md:227).
.review/results/macmac-harness-r6.codex.md:115:- [scripts/otp12pf_mac_verdict.py:433](/Users/michael/Dev/blit_v2/scripts/otp12pf_mac_verdict.py:433) — **BLOCKER** — Uncertified controls block only `VANISHES`; the `REPRODUCES` branch at [line 485](/Users/michael/Dev/blit_v2/scripts/otp12pf_mac_verdict.py:485) ignores them and emits no control caveat. With every control showing `D=+230 ms`, CI `[0,230]`, and 7/7 nonzero positives, the engine still confidently declares P1 reproduced. Uncertainty about a rig-wide confound is not evidence that the confound is absent.
.review/results/macmac-harness-r6.codex.md:142:src = [1000]*8 + [2000]*8
.review/results/macmac-harness-r6.codex.md:145:→ REPRODUCES
.review/results/macmac-harness-r6.codex.md:156:→ SESSION VERDICT: REPRODUCES
.review/results/macmac-harness-r6.codex.md:196:- [scripts/otp12pf_mac_verdict.py:299](/Users/michael/Dev/blit_v2/scripts/otp12pf_mac_verdict.py:299) — **BLOCKER** — The three-way split is incomplete. `material = bar_fail_pos OR CI_lo >= breach_hi`, so a marginal-median bar failure still bypasses the CI magnitude test. At n=16, paired `D=1 ms`, CI `[1,1]`, and sign 16/16 produces `REPRODUCES` after three outliers move the marginal median. The same defect makes a control simultaneously “certified clean” (`null_excl=yes`) and “contaminating” via [line 418](/Users/michael/Dev/blit_v2/scripts/otp12pf_mac_verdict.py:418), falsely yielding `RIG-VOID`. The preregistration itself embeds this contradiction at [PREREGISTRATION.md:227](/Users/michael/Dev/blit_v2/docs/bench/otp12-macmac-2026-07-14/PREREGISTRATION.md:227).

.review/results/local-bistability.codex.md:1658:.review/results/bench-script-fix.codex.md:14:Review the diff of commit b9befb8 (run: git show b9befb8). It is a two-line fix to the helper script scripts/bench_10gbe.sh: (1) module endpoint gains the required trailing slash (server:/module/ — see crates/blit-core/src/remote/endpoint.rs parse rules), (2) client flag corrected from --force-grpc-data (the daemon's flag) to --force-grpc (see blit-cli clap definitions). Context: the script predated both surfaces and its run_timed helper discards stderr under set -euo pipefail, so both errors were silent aborts; the fixed script completed a full 10GbE benchmark matrix exit 0. Check: correctness of both fixes against the actual CLI/endpoint grammar at HEAD, whether any OTHER stale CLI usage remains in this script (flags, endpoint forms, subcommands), and internal script coherence. This is a helper script — cargo gates don't apply. Output a concise markdown findings list — each finding with file:line, severity, rationale — then a final VERDICT line. Be concise; do not invoke skills.

.review/results/macmac-harness-r7.codex.md:255:+A concrete branch-completeness failure is emerging in the decision rule: although direction is now tied to the bar’s sign, `bar_fail_pos`/`bar_fail_neg` still bypass the CI magnitude test. I’m reproducing whether a marginal-median bar flip plus a tiny paired effect can still become `REPRODUCES`, then I’ll check whether the guard/mutations can see it.
.review/results/macmac-harness-r7.codex.md:279:+The official suite is green (22/22, 15/15), but it does not cover the marginal-bar/paired-CI disagreement: a synthetic n=8 session with paired median `D=+1 ms` was labeled `REPRODUCES` solely because one outlier moved the marginal median. I’m now probing the escalation evidence and self-test classifications for similarly “present but not actually enforced” protections.
.review/results/macmac-harness-r7.codex.md:295:+The control obligation is structurally complete for the null branch—an uncertified control cannot reach `VANISHES`—but it creates a separate false-positive path: `REPRODUCES` ignores uncertified controls entirely. I reproduced a headline reproduction while all four controls showed `D=+230 ms`, 7/7 nonzero positives, and CI `[0,230]`; the verdict text emitted no control caveat.
.review/results/macmac-harness-r7.codex.md:303:+- [scripts/otp12pf_mac_verdict.py:299](/Users/michael/Dev/blit_v2/scripts/otp12pf_mac_verdict.py:299) — **BLOCKER** — The three-way split is incomplete. `material = bar_fail_pos OR CI_lo >= breach_hi`, so a marginal-median bar failure still bypasses the CI magnitude test. At n=16, paired `D=1 ms`, CI `[1,1]`, and sign 16/16 produces `REPRODUCES` after three outliers move the marginal median. The same defect makes a control simultaneously “certified clean” (`null_excl=yes`) and “contaminating” via [line 418](/Users/michael/Dev/blit_v2/scripts/otp12pf_mac_verdict.py:418), falsely yielding `RIG-VOID`. The preregistration itself embeds this contradiction at [PREREGISTRATION.md:227](/Users/michael/Dev/blit_v2/docs/bench/otp12-macmac-2026-07-14/PREREGISTRATION.md:227).

With every control showing `D=+230 ms`, CI `[0,230]`, and 7/7 nonzero positives, the engine still confidently declares P1 reproduced. Uncertainty about a rig-wide confound is not evidence that the confound is absent.

.review/results/macmac-harness-r7.codex.md:1384:- **⛔ NEXT ACTION IS AN OWNER CALL, NOT A RUN. The Mac↔Mac rig (Queue 1(ii)) is BLOCKED: `codex` — the MANDATORY reviewer — is OUT OF CREDITS until 2026-07-19.** The instrument is reworked (`a9460ce`, prereg **rev 5**) but **NOT CLEARED**, and the harness **refuses a timed run** (`exit 2` without `CLEARED_BY_REVIEW=1`). **NO DATA TAKEN.** Round 4 was reviewed by **grok alone**, and D-2026-07-14-2 says grok is *"additive, never a substitute, and never runs alone"* — so **no agent may clear this**. **Owner: wait for codex (2026-07-19) / buy credits / amend the rule.** (Grok found 9 real defects including a BLOCKER, which is the argument *for* the second reviewer, not a reason to promote it.) When cleared: **⚠ THE OWNER MUST CLOSE THEIR CODEX SESSIONS** — nagatha is a bench **END** and the quiescence gate refuses while `codex`/`cargo`/`rustc` runs on **either** Mac (it fires correctly today). Time Machine OFF on both. Rig: nagatha `10.1.10.92` ↔ `q` `10.1.10.54`, 10GbE/9000, build pinned `f35702a`. **Then `pf-1`** (the HARD GATE), which two pf-0 results BIND: between-session grading is dead (a 20% recovery = 46 ms sits under the 78 ms floor), so pf-1 must **measure its own paired within-session floor** before grading; and the fast arm is **BISTABLE** — grade the distribution, not the median.
.review/results/macmac-harness-r7.codex.md:1390:- **P1 REPRODUCES ON A SECOND MAC (2026-07-13, `docs/bench/otp12-q-baseline-2026-07-13/`).** `wm_tcp_mixed` = **1.385 FAIL** on `q`↔netwatch-01 **at MTU 9000**, while all three controls PASS at **1.002–1.043** in the same session (so rig noise is ~2–4% and P1 is 10× outside it). **P1 is a property of the macOS↔Windows PAIRING, not of one machine** — the assumption **H1** rests on (corrected 2026-07-14: H5/H6/H7 are **P2** hypotheses; the earlier "H1/H5/H6/H7" was wrong), never tested until now. **And jumbo does NOT dissolve P1** — pf-0 has now measured the matched 1500 arm and killed MTU outright (above).
.review/results/macmac-harness-r7.codex.md:1393:- **P1 (the headline invariance criterion) — the one thing between blit and shipping.** Fails rig W (`wm_tcp_mixed` 1.237 and 1.300 — do NOT read that as a regression, it is **two different Mac NICs**), but **PASSES 8/8 with Linux on both ends** (`docs/bench/otp12-perf-2026-07-13/`; P1's own cell 1.092/1.003). So it is **platform-INTERACTING, not pure layout** — yet **NOT exonerated**: a code path that only bites on one platform (H1's Windows accept branch) looks identical. **P1 HAS NO ESCAPE HATCH** (codex r5 F1): D-2026-07-12-1 waives only a *cross-direction* miss for a cell that ALREADY passes invariance — P1 *is* the invariance failure. So: **fix it to ≤1.10, or the owner amends acceptance criterion 1.** Neither is assumed.

.review/results/macmac-harness-r7.codex.md:6376:- [scripts/otp12pf_mac_verdict.py:477](/Users/michael/Dev/blit_v2/scripts/otp12pf_mac_verdict.py:477) — **HIGH** — The bar still changes verdicts. It blocks control certification here, creates `BAR-FAIL-INCONSISTENT` at line 361, and participates in `UNSTABLE` at line 350. No bar path to `REPRODUCES` remains, but the stronger “reported only, never consulted” claim is false. A paired-clean control with CI `[5,5]` was blocked solely by its marginal bar.
.review/results/macmac-harness-r7.codex.md:6452:- [scripts/otp12pf_mac_verdict.py:477](/Users/michael/Dev/blit_v2/scripts/otp12pf_mac_verdict.py:477) — **HIGH** — The bar still changes verdicts. It blocks control certification here, creates `BAR-FAIL-INCONSISTENT` at line 361, and participates in `UNSTABLE` at line 350. No bar path to `REPRODUCES` remains, but the stronger “reported only, never consulted” claim is false. A paired-clean control with CI `[5,5]` was blocked solely by its marginal bar.

.review/results/macmac-harness-r2.grok.md:12:| 2 | `otp12pf_mac_verdict.py:115–140`; prereg §POWER GATE | **BLOCKER** | **VANISHES equivalence margin is `0.10 × srcinit_median`, not the reference effect.** `DELTA_REF_MS=230` is written to CSV only (line 35, 155) and **never used** in the decision. A true, tight effect of 230 ms on a 2500 ms arm (ratio 1.092, bar PASS, CI=[230,230] ⊂ ±250) yields **VANISHES** — i.e. “P1 absent / Windows may be required” while a rig-W-sized absolute Δ is present. Same class: all `d_i=190`, `src=2000` → VANISHES (breach=200). |
.review/results/macmac-harness-r2.grok.md:20:| 10 | `otp12pf_mac_verdict.py:111` | **LOW** | Integer bar: exact 1.10 ratio is **PASS** (`10*hi <= 11*lo`). A precise 10% effect never REPRODUCES. |
.review/results/macmac-harness-r2.grok.md:65:| ratio | 1.092 → bar **PASS** (`10×2730=27300 ≤ 11×2500=27500`) |
.review/results/macmac-r12.codex-harness.md:143:- [PREREGISTRATION.md:214](/Users/michael/Dev/blit_v2/docs/bench/otp12-macmac-2026-07-14/PREREGISTRATION.md:214), [otp12pf_mac_verdict.py:220](/Users/michael/Dev/blit_v2/scripts/otp12pf_mac_verdict.py:220), [otp12pf_mac_verdict_test.py:111](/Users/michael/Dev/blit_v2/scripts/otp12pf_mac_verdict_test.py:111) — **BLOCKER** — `min(10%, 230 ms)` implements “either,” not the documented “both.” The guard intentionally calls 230 ms on a 2500 ms arm `REPRODUCES` although ratio 1.092 passes the defining invariance bar. Exact 1.10 is likewise both `PASS` and `REPRODUCES`.
.review/results/macmac-r12.codex-harness.md:151:- [PREREGISTRATION.md:460](/Users/michael/Dev/blit_v2/docs/bench/otp12-macmac-2026-07-14/PREREGISTRATION.md:460), [bench_otp12_win.sh:495](/Users/michael/Dev/blit_v2/scripts/bench_otp12_win.sh:495) — **BLOCKER** — there is no known-positive run using this corrected settle method. Rig W’s defining measurements fsync immediately. A Mac↔Mac null would conflate “Windows absent” with “measurement repair removed the artifact.”
.review/results/macmac-r12.codex-harness.md:153:- [bench_otp12pf_mac.sh:499](/Users/michael/Dev/blit_v2/scripts/bench_otp12pf_mac.sh:499), [bench_otp12pf_mac.sh:557](/Users/michael/Dev/blit_v2/scripts/bench_otp12pf_mac.sh:557) — **BLOCKER** — the duplicate end-fabric gate rechecks only MSS/IP, not MTU, negotiated 10GbE media, or status. A link renegotiated to 1GbE can retain MTU 9000, MSS 8948, and the same IP and still grade.
.review/results/macmac-r12.codex-harness.md:176:- [PREREGISTRATION.md:214](/Users/michael/Dev/blit_v2/docs/bench/otp12-macmac-2026-07-14/PREREGISTRATION.md:214), [otp12pf_mac_verdict.py:220](/Users/michael/Dev/blit_v2/scripts/otp12pf_mac_verdict.py:220), [otp12pf_mac_verdict_test.py:111](/Users/michael/Dev/blit_v2/scripts/otp12pf_mac_verdict_test.py:111) — **BLOCKER** — `min(10%, 230 ms)` implements “either,” not the documented “both.” The guard intentionally calls 230 ms on a 2500 ms arm `REPRODUCES` although ratio 1.092 passes the defining invariance bar. Exact 1.10 is likewise both `PASS` and `REPRODUCES`.
.review/results/macmac-r12.codex-harness.md:184:- [PREREGISTRATION.md:460](/Users/michael/Dev/blit_v2/docs/bench/otp12-macmac-2026-07-14/PREREGISTRATION.md:460), [bench_otp12_win.sh:495](/Users/michael/Dev/blit_v2/scripts/bench_otp12_win.sh:495) — **BLOCKER** — there is no known-positive run using this corrected settle method. .review/results/macmac-harness-r9b.codex.md:5751:SESSION VERDICT: REPRODUCES
.review/results/macmac-harness-r9b.codex.md:5765:- `8830fda:scripts/otp12pf_mac_verdict.py:193` — **BLOCKER** — Completeness was `len(d) < 8`, so a 16-pair session was graded. Its CI trimmed three contradictory pairs and produced `REPRODUCES`. Current [line 200](/Users/michael/Dev/blit_v2/scripts/otp12pf_mac_verdict.py:200) correctly requires exactly eight.
.review/results/macmac-harness-r9b.codex.md:5767:- [scripts/otp12pf_mac_verdict.py:220](/Users/michael/Dev/blit_v2/scripts/otp12pf_mac_verdict.py:220) — **BLOCKER** — `B` transports raw milliseconds between controls with potentially different baselines. Identical 4.9% arm bias in every cell can certify every control yet produce `REPRODUCES` in slower measurands. Neither median nor CI-versus-range fixes this; the rule needs an explicitly justified additive-bias model or a normalized carry. Opposite-sign bias and increasing `B` are otherwise conservative.
.review/results/macmac-harness-r9b.codex.md:5787:SESSION VERDICT: REPRODUCES
.review/results/macmac-harness-r9b.codex.md:5797:SESSION VERDICT: REPRODUCES
.review/results/macmac-harness-r9b.codex.md:5828:- `8830fda:scripts/otp12pf_mac_verdict.py:193` — **BLOCKER** — Completeness was `len(d) < 8`, so a 16-pair session was graded. Its CI trimmed three contradictory pairs and produced `REPRODUCES`. Current [line 200](/Users/michael/Dev/blit_v2/scripts/otp12pf_mac_verdict.py:200) correctly requires exactly eight.
.review/results/macmac-harness-r9b.codex.md:5830:- [scripts/otp12pf_mac_verdict.py:220](/Users/michael/Dev/blit_v2/scripts/otp12pf_mac_verdict.py:220) — **BLOCKER** — `B` transports raw milliseconds between controls with potentially different baselines. Identical 4.9% arm bias in every cell can certify every control yet produce `REPRODUCES` in slower measurands. Neither median nor CI-versus-range fixes this; the rule needs an explicitly justified additive-bias model or a normalized carry. Opposite-sign bias and increasing `B` are otherwise conservative.

.review/results/otp-4a-daemon-serves-transfer.codex.md:4733:.review/results/d-2026-07-05-2-compat.codex.md:300: **2026-07-03 16:26:55Z** - **CODER (ue-r2-1c, claude)**: Landed the engine shell (REV4 `ue-r2-1c`, third slice through the code→GPT-review→fix loop). New `crates/blit-core/src/engine/` module: `TransferEngine::execute(EngineRequest)` owns strategy selection (single-file → journal probe → fast-path walk → streaming) and the streaming leg (tuning window → scan → diff/plan → sink pipeline → mirror deletions → journal checkpoints → history/predictor); `TransferOrchestrator` shrank to the local adapter (preconditions, `FsTransferSource`/`FilteredSource` + `FsTransferSink`/`NullSink` construction, option translation) with the public API preserved via re-exports — engine type thereby ratified per REV4 Design §1 (new engine + adapter, not a rename). Sequence: behavior pins first (`7730eb1`: NoWork/UpToDate + `no_work` tag, NoWork/SourceEmpty — both previously untested), the move (`dc9b0ed`: `fast_path.rs`→`engine/strategy.rs`, options/summary/history moved, tuning/mirror/journal/single-file split out; move fidelity machine-checked — 75 diff lines vs origin, all six documented transformations, zero drift), then the REV4-named accounting gap (`29e210b`: single-file strategy now records perf history tag `single_file` / `null_sink` lane + predictor, guard test proven by revert). Review: first codex run died on provider quota (121k tokens, recorded); owner restored access same day; retry PASS with 1 Low, accepted + fixed (`15e6334`: engine back-references through `crate::orchestrator` → engine-local paths; swept one more the reviewer missed). Also recorded owner-provided 10GbE test env (`admin@skippy:/mnt/generic-pool/video/test`) in STATE queue item 4. Validation: fmt/clippy clean, `cargo test --workspace` 1394 / 0 / 2 (baseline 1391, +3). All on master, unpushed. Next: `ue-r2-1d` (streaming plan foundation) — or `1e`/w4-1 per queue.
.review/results/otp-4a-daemon-serves-transfer.codex.md:4736:.review/results/d-2026-07-05-2-compat.codex.md:310: **2026-06-12 16:32:00Z** - **REVIEW (gemini-reviewer)**: Graded the two pending sentinels on `master` sequentially: **design-4-fallback-midmanifest-negotiation** (`ddfeb58`) accepted (daemon early-flush branch is now TCP-only; client-side fallback_negotiated flag added to hold need-list/manifest fallback payloads in pending_queue; 2000-file regression test passes) and **design-5-send-failure-masks-rejection** (`08d71a2`) accepted (prefer_server_error helper harvests daemon's terminal status on manifest-phase send failures; 500-file readonly_enforcement regression test passes repeatedly). Both verified.json verdicts created, sentinels deleted via git rm, and REVIEW.md status rows updated to `[x]`. Validation suite re-run: cargo fmt, clippy, and cargo test --workspace all passed successfully (1370 passed, 0 failed, 1 ignored).

.review/results/macmac-prereg.codex.md:83:- `docs/bench/otp12-macmac-2026-07-14/PREREGISTRATION.md:22-34` — **BLOCKER** — H1 is mischaracterized as Windows-exclusive. The parent defines a generic resize-time accept/dial asymmetry and says controlled ablations must kill or confirm it (`OTP12_PERF_FINDINGS.md:183-209,380-399`). Mac↔Mac reproduction could be H1 itself—or an unrelated phenocopy—while rig-W remains caused by Windows. A null supports pairing dependence, not H1 specifically.
.review/results/macmac-prereg.codex.md:99:- `docs/bench/otp12-macmac-2026-07-14/PREREGISTRATION.md:33-34` — **LOW** — The new Mac↔Mac-before-pf-1 sequence is reflected in `docs/STATE.md` but conflicts with `docs/DECISIONS.md:179-183` and the parent’s pf-1-first staging. Durable guidance remains internally inconsistent.
.review/results/macmac-prereg.codex.md:106:- `docs/bench/otp12-macmac-2026-07-14/PREREGISTRATION.md:22-34` — **BLOCKER** — H1 is mischaracterized as Windows-exclusive. .review/results/otp-12-worker-parity.atomic-fix.gpt-verdict.md:1:# otp-12-worker-parity atomic-fix review — adjudication
.review/results/otp-12-worker-parity.atomic-fix.gpt-verdict.md:3:**Slice**: `8e993aa` — atomic resize epoch/refusal arbitration.
.review/results/otp-12-worker-parity.atomic-fix.gpt-verdict.md:5:**Raw review**: `.review/results/otp-12-worker-parity.atomic-fix.codex.md`
.review/results/otp-12-worker-parity.atomic-fix.gpt-verdict.md:39:Full workspace fmt, strict clippy, and tests are green: 1,490 passed, 2
.review/results/otp-12-worker-parity.atomic-fix.gpt-verdict.md:42:Fix commit: `641916e`.
.review/results/macmac-harness-r11.codex-engine.md:89:The executable guard passes: 34/34 cases, 14/14 mutations, all five direct checks, and 300/300 fuzz inputs. I also found two synthetic counterexamples outside the current suite: one where a larger clean-control bias changes `MIXED` to `REPRODUCES`, and one where the cap lets `B > T` while the session still reports `REPRODUCES`.
.review/results/macmac-harness-r11.codex-engine.md:91:- [scripts/otp12pf_mac_verdict.py:282](/Users/michael/Dev/blit_v2/scripts/otp12pf_mac_verdict.py:282) — **HIGH** — `B` hardens each cell but can make the session verdict easier through `MIXED` precedence. With measurands `+110×8` and `−94×8` at `src=1000`, controls at zero yield `MIXED`; clean controls at `+5×8` yield `REPRODUCES`.
.review/results/macmac-harness-r11.codex-engine.md:93:- [scripts/otp12pf_mac_verdict.py:257](/Users/michael/Dev/blit_v2/scripts/otp12pf_mac_verdict.py:257) — **HIGH** — Clean controls can produce `B > T` and license an effect mostly attributable to permitted rig bias. Controls `src=1000, d=+49×8` are clean; measurands `src=10000, d=+720×8` give `T=230`, `B=490`, and `REPRODUCES`. Up to 68% is rig bias, the ratio is only 1.072, and a null is impossible. This also contradicts the spec’s claim that `min(10%, 230ms)` requires materiality by “both” standards.
.review/results/macmac-harness-r11.codex-engine.md:95:- [scripts/otp12pf_mac_verdict.py:142](/Users/michael/Dev/blit_v2/scripts/otp12pf_mac_verdict.py:142) — **MEDIUM** — The low-median convention is registered only for paired `D`, but code also applies it to `srcinit_med`, controlling `T` and `B`. With sources `[1000×4,5000×4]` and `d=100×8`, code reports `REPRODUCES`; the conventional even-sample median gives `T=230` and `NONE`.
.review/results/macmac-harness-r11.codex-engine.md:108:- [scripts/otp12pf_mac_verdict.py:282](/Users/michael/Dev/blit_v2/scripts/otp12pf_mac_verdict.py:282) — **HIGH** — `B` hardens each cell but can make the session verdict easier through `MIXED` precedence. With measurands `+110×8` and `−94×8` at `src=1000`, controls at zero yield `MIXED`; clean controls at `+5×8` yield `REPRODUCES`.
.review/results/macmac-harness-r11.codex-engine.md:110:- [scripts/otp12pf_mac_verdict.py:257](/Users/michael/Dev/blit_v2/scripts/otp12pf_mac_verdict.py:257) — **HIGH** — Clean controls can produce `B > T` and license an effect mostly attributable to permitted rig bias. Controls `src=1000, d=+49×8` are clean; measurands `src=10000, d=+720×8` give `T=230`, `B=490`, and `REPRODUCES`. Up to 68% is rig bias, the ratio is only 1.072, and a null is impossible. This also contradicts the spec’s claim that `min(10%, 230ms)` requires materiality by “both” standards.
.review/results/macmac-harness-r11.codex-engine.md:112:- [scripts/otp12pf_mac_verdict.py:142](/Users/michael/Dev/blit_v2/scripts/otp12pf_mac_verdict.py:142) — **MEDIUM** — The low-median convention is registered only for paired `D`, but code also applies it to `srcinit_med`, controlling `T` and `B`. With sources `[1000×4,5000×4]` and `d=100×8`, code reports `REPRODUCES`; the conventional even-sample median gives `T=230` and `NONE`.
.review/results/macmac-r12.codex-design.md:28:  - Linux <-> Linux: NO P1 (8/8 cells pass, the same cell reads 1.092/1.003).
.review/results/macmac-r12.codex-design.md:37:and q, 10GbE, MTU 9000), to answer ONE question: can P1 occur WITHOUT a Windows peer?
.review/results/macmac-r12.codex-design.md:68:2. WHAT DOES EACH POSSIBLE OUTCOME ACTUALLY BUY? The rig can say REPRODUCES, DOES-NOT-
.review/results/macmac-r12.codex-design.md:115:The repo’s current state already retracts one important earlier claim: Mac↔Mac is not an H1 discriminator because H1 accuses code that runs on both operating systems. I’m now checking whether the narrower “Windows necessary?” question changes the project’s next action enough to justify this instrument.
.review/results/macmac-r12.codex-design.md:137:The schedule is more careful than the causal question warrants, but I’ve found a structural issue already: the end-of-session “fabric re-check” verifies only MSS and source IP, not negotiated link speed. A 10GbE link that renegotiates to 1GbE can keep the same MTU, MSS, and IP and still be graded. I’m checking for the same live/dead protection pattern elsewhere and validating the verdict tests.
.review/results/macmac-r12.codex-design.md:175:The Mac↔Mac run is not the right next move. At best, a positive result gives you a convenient local reproduction rig. It does not adjudicate H1, identify a cause, or alter P1’s release obligation. A negative result is especially weak because the Mac↔Mac harness introduces the corrected equal-settle method that was not used for the Mac↔Windows measurements defining P1.
.review/results/macmac-r12.codex-design.md:177:First, rerun the known-failing Mac↔Windows cell with this corrected timing method while recording dial, accept, ACK, resize-epoch, first-byte, and completion timestamps; take a packet capture concurrently. If P1 survives, test H1 with a causal dial/accept counterfactual. A targeted microbenchmark comes after the trace identifies a phase, because the mixed-only interaction may disappear in isolation. A cheap two-cell Mac↔Mac screen could then establish whether local debugging is possible; the current 96-transfer apparatus is disproportionate.
.review/results/macmac-r12.codex-design.md:183:| `REPRODUCES` | P1-shaped behavior can occur on these Macs without Windows; use them as a local debugging rig. | H1 confirmed or killed; platform-general behavior; Windows exonerated as a contributor; release acceptance. |
.review/results/macmac-r12.codex-design.md:192:Only `REPRODUCES` changes anything operationally: it changes where debugging can happen. Every substantive path still leads to direct instrumentation and a causal counterfactual, so the full experiment is close to theatre even before its remaining defects.
.review/results/macmac-r12.codex-design.md:196:- **BLOCKER — no same-method positive control.** The new instrument deliberately inserts and verifies a settle before fsync at [bench_otp12pf_mac.sh:925](/Users/michael/Dev/blit_v2/scripts/bench_otp12pf_mac.sh:925), because the previous timing gap could manufacture a one-directional effect. The original rig-W harness flushes immediately at [bench_otp12_win.sh:505](/Users/michael/Dev/blit_v2/scripts/bench_otp12_win.sh:505). Before asking whether Windows is necessary, establish that P1 still exists on Mac↔Windows under the corrected method. Otherwise a Mac↔Mac null conflates platform pairing with measurement repair.
.review/results/macmac-r12.codex-design.md:210:- **BLOCKER — `min()` implements the opposite of the stated policy.** The document says an effect must matter by both the 10% bar and the 230 ms reference at [PREREGISTRATION.md:212](/Users/michael/Dev/blit_v2/docs/bench/otp12-macmac-2026-07-14/PREREGISTRATION.md:212). `min(src/10, 230)` lets either smaller standard win. The test deliberately labels `230 ms @ 2500 ms` as `REPRODUCES` at [otp12pf_mac_verdict_test.py:111](/Users/michael/Dev/blit_v2/scripts/otp12pf_mac_verdict_test.py:111), even though its ratio is 1.092 and the engine’s own acceptance bar says `PASS`. Thus the engine can confidently report “P1 reproduces” when P1’s defining invariance criterion passes.
.review/results/macmac-r12.codex-design.md:212:- **BLOCKER — the exact 10% boundary is simultaneously PASS and REPRODUCES.** The bar treats `≤1.10` as PASS at [otp12pf_mac_verdict.py:282](/Users/michael/Dev/blit_v2/scripts/otp12pf_mac_verdict.py:282), while `classify()` uses `CI_lo >= T` at [otp12pf_mac_verdict.py:251](/Users/michael/Dev/blit_v2/scripts/otp12pf_mac_verdict.py:251). The guard explicitly requires an exact 10% effect to reproduce at [otp12pf_mac_verdict_test.py:217](/Users/michael/Dev/blit_v2/scripts/otp12pf_mac_verdict_test.py:217). That is a direct policy contradiction, not statistical conservatism.
.review/results/macmac-r12.codex-design.md:224:- **BLOCKER — the end-fabric protection does not recheck the full fabric it promises.** Startup checks MTU, negotiated `10Gbase-T`, and link status at [bench_otp12pf_mac.sh:499](/Users/michael/Dev/blit_v2/scripts/bench_otp12pf_mac.sh:499). The end gate checks only MSS and local IP at [bench_otp12pf_mac.sh:557](/Users/michael/Dev/blit_v2/scripts/bench_otp12pf_mac.sh:557). A link can renegotiate from 10GbE to 1GbE while retaining MTU 9000, MSS 8948, and the same IP; the session will grade. This is exactly the duplicate-site defect class.
.review/results/macmac-r12.codex-design.md:234:**DO NOT RUN IT — first validate P1 with the corrected measurement on the known-failing Mac↔Windows pair and instrument the suspected path; the current rule can call a 1.092/PASS cell “REPRODUCES,” and the end-fabric gate is incomplete.**
.review/results/macmac-r12.codex-design.md:237:The Mac↔Mac run is not the right next move. At best, a positive result gives you a convenient local reproduction rig. It does not adjudicate H1, identify a cause, or alter P1’s release obligation. A negative result is especially weak because the Mac↔Mac harness introduces the corrected equal-settle method that was not used for the Mac↔Windows measurements defining P1.
.review/results/macmac-r12.codex-design.md:239:First, rerun the known-failing Mac↔Windows cell with this corrected timing method while recording dial, accept, ACK, resize-epoch, first-byte, and completion timestamps; take a packet capture concurrently. If P1 survives, test H1 with a causal dial/accept counterfactual. A targeted microbenchmark comes after the trace identifies a phase, because the mixed-only interaction may disappear in isolation. A cheap two-cell Mac↔Mac screen could then establish whether local debugging is possible; the current 96-transfer apparatus is disproportionate.
.review/results/macmac-r12.codex-design.md:245:| `REPRODUCES` | P1-shaped behavior can occur on these Macs without Windows; use them as a local debugging rig. | H1 confirmed or killed; platform-general behavior; Windows exonerated as a contributor; release acceptance. |
.review/results/macmac-r12.codex-design.md:254:Only `REPRODUCES` changes anything operationally: it changes where debugging can happen. Every substantive path still leads to direct instrumentation and a causal counterfactual, so the full experiment is close to theatre even before its remaining defects.
.review/results/macmac-r12.codex-design.md:258:- **BLOCKER — no same-method positive control.** The new instrument deliberately inserts and verifies a settle before fsync at [bench_otp12pf_mac.sh:925](/Users/michael/Dev/blit_v2/scripts/bench_otp12pf_mac.sh:925), because the previous timing gap could manufacture a one-directional effect. The original rig-W harness flushes immediately at [bench_otp12_win.sh:505](/Users/michael/Dev/blit_v2/scripts/bench_otp12_win.sh:505). Before asking whether Windows is necessary, establish that P1 still exists on Mac↔Windows under the corrected method. Otherwise a Mac↔Mac null conflates platform pairing with measurement repair.
.review/results/macmac-r12.codex-design.md:272:- **BLOCKER — `min()` implements the opposite of the stated policy.** The document says an effect must matter by both the 10% bar and the 230 ms reference at [PREREGISTRATION.md:212](/Users/michael/Dev/blit_v2/docs/bench/otp12-macmac-2026-07-14/PREREGISTRATION.md:212). `min(src/10, 230)` lets either smaller standard win. The test deliberately labels `230 ms @ 2500 ms` as `REPRODUCES` at [otp12pf_mac_verdict_test.py:111](/Users/michael/Dev/blit_v2/scripts/otp12pf_mac_verdict_test.py:111), even though its ratio is 1.092 and the engine’s own acceptance bar says `PASS`. Thus the engine can confidently report “P1 reproduces” when P1’s defining invariance criterion passes.
.review/results/macmac-r12.codex-design.md:274:- **BLOCKER — the exact 10% boundary is simultaneously PASS and REPRODUCES.** The bar treats `≤1.10` as PASS at [otp12pf_mac_verdict.py:282](/Users/michael/Dev/blit_v2/scripts/otp12pf_mac_verdict.py:282), while `classify()` uses `CI_lo >= T` at [otp12pf_mac_verdict.py:251](/Users/michael/Dev/blit_v2/scripts/otp12pf_mac_verdict.py:251). The guard explicitly requires an exact 10% effect to reproduce at [otp12pf_mac_verdict_test.py:217](/Users/michael/Dev/blit_v2/scripts/otp12pf_mac_verdict_test.py:217). That is a direct policy contradiction, not statistical conservatism.
.review/results/macmac-r12.codex-design.md:286:- **BLOCKER — the end-fabric protection does not recheck the full fabric it promises.** Startup checks MTU, negotiated `10Gbase-T`, and link status at [bench_otp12pf_mac.sh:499](/Users/michael/Dev/blit_v2/scripts/bench_otp12pf_mac.sh:499). The end gate checks only MSS and local IP at [bench_otp12pf_mac.sh:557](/Users/michael/Dev/blit_v2/scripts/bench_otp12pf_mac.sh:557). A link can renegotiate from 10GbE to 1GbE while retaining MTU 9000, MSS 8948, and the same IP; the session will grade. This is exactly the duplicate-site defect class.
.review/results/macmac-r12.codex-design.md:296:**DO NOT RUN IT — first validate P1 with the corrected measurement on the known-failing Mac↔Windows pair and instrument the suspected path; the current rule can call a 1.092/PASS cell “REPRODUCES,” and the end-fabric gate is incomplete.**
.review/results/macmac-harness-r8.grok.md:1:I'll attack the rewrite as an instrument that must not produce a confident wrong answer: inspect the commit and the four files, then drive the engine with synthetic cases.Files are in hand. Next I'll drive the engine with synthetic sessions and probe the harness protections that tend to fail open.Guard tests pass. Driving synthetic attacks on bimodality, the sign-test claim, bar/NONE disagreement, and control preconditions.Dead-zone and control-bleed look serious. Digging into multi-control probability, harness fail-opens, and whether `ratio` can diverge from the paired rule.Checking one harness fail-open candidate: whether `timed_run` still accepts drain via a loose glob after the strict case filter, and whether fsync errors can be swallowed.# Mac↔Mac instrument review — rev 8 / `79c1f2d`
.review/results/macmac-harness-r8.grok.md:12:| 2 | `otp12pf_mac_verdict.py:52-55,164-174`; claim in docstring | **BLOCKER** | **T/2 still certifies a half-Δ_ref confound.** Slow arms (T=230): controls with **d=+114** → `ctrl=NONE`; measurand **d=+230** → `REPRODUCES`. Driven: all four controls carry **49.6% of Δ_ref** and the session still prints a confident reproduction. Same class as “229 of 230 certified clean”, half-fixed not fixed. Specificity (TCP-only / mixed-only) is not enforced. |
.review/results/macmac-harness-r8.grok.md:14:| 4 | `otp12pf_mac_verdict.py:49-51`; n=16 path | **HIGH** | **“Sign test redundant” is false at n=16** (registered escalation). At n=8, CI=`[min,max]` ⇒ `CI_lo≥T` ⇒ all pairs ≥T. At n=16, CI=`[d_(4),d_(13)]`: data `[-500,-400,-300]+[250]×13` → **`REPRODUCES`**, sign 13/16, three pairs strongly inverted. Median claim is coherent; the written redundancy claim and “every pair clears T” intuition are not. |
.review/results/macmac-harness-r8.grok.md:27:### A. Half-Δ_ref controls + REPRODUCES (finding 2)
.review/results/macmac-harness-r8.grok.md:32:→ SESSION VERDICT: REPRODUCES
.review/results/macmac-harness-r8.grok.md:49:→ REPRODUCES, sign_p with 13/16 (three large negatives outside CI)
.review/results/macmac-harness-r8.grok.md:72:Controls at T/2, UNCLEAR, mixed, INVERTED, or EFFECT all → `CONTROLS-NOT-CLEAN` (including blocking `REPRODUCES`).
.review/results/macmac-harness-r8.grok.md:89:Two independent ways to a **confident, over-strong answer** remain: (1) **`REPRODUCES` while every control carries ~½ Δ_ref** under T/2 on slow arms; (2) **`DOES-NOT-REPRODUCE` on a clearly bimodal arm** because UNSTABLE was deleted and lockstep/bimodal differences need not widen the paired CI. Separately, the **T/2 + n=8 full-range control gate** makes a clean, bankable session unlikely at the stated noise — so the instrument may also be **unable to conclude**, which the owner already defined as broken.
.review/results/macmac-harness-r11.grok.md:20:| 216–218 | **HIGH** | **Arm-count completeness is load-bearing and unguarded.** Code requires `len(by[srcinit])==8` and `len(by[destinit])==8`. Removing only those two conjuncts still passes **all 34 cases** and is **not** what the completeness mutation isolates (that mutation kills on a short `len(d)`). Synthetic CSV: 8 pairs with `d=50` on `src=1000` (true `T=100` → null) plus extra valid low `srcinit` rows → mutant reports **`REPRODUCES`**; unmutated engine correctly **`INCOMPLETE`**. Same defect class as prior vacuous guards. |
.review/results/macmac-harness-r11.grok.md:24:| 200 / zeros→`classify(0,…)` | *(closed)* | `classify(0,0,0,0,0,0)` is **`EFFECT`**. Refused by `ms_of` non-positive check. With clean non-zero controls and zero measurand arms, dropping that check yields **`REPRODUCES`**. |
.review/results/macmac-harness-r11.grok.md:39:| 247–249, 320–322 | **LOW** | Zero-timing case sets **all** arms to 0; mutant becomes `CONTROLS-NOT-CLEAN`, not `REPRODUCES`. Mutation is non-vacuous on the `ENGINE-REFUSED` contract but does **not** exercise the dangerous zeros→`REPRODUCES` path (needs clean positive controls + zero measurand). |
.review/results/macmac-harness-r11.grok.md:42:**Fixes with no mutation (among important ones):** arm-count completeness; RANGE-vs-CI null (direct only — justified); most behavioural outcomes (REPRODUCES/MIXED/INVERTED paths) — fine as behavioural.
.review/results/macmac-harness-r11.grok.md:48:| 6–13 vs 220–250 | **MEDIUM** | **Opening “rule in one paragraph” is stale:** NONE via **CI** inside ±T; no RANGE; no B; no `−src/11`. Body (rev 8+) is what the code implements. Same class as prior doc≠code defects. |
.review/results/macmac-harness-r11.grok.md:77:Core rule at n=8 looks sound against confident false EFFECT/NONE given clean controls, but the arm-count completeness fix (prior BLOCKER class) can be deleted with a green guard suite and then grade a skewed CSV as **`REPRODUCES`**, and the registered summary text still describes the wrong null statistic.

CHECK HARD: (a) recompute the README's headline numbers from runs.csv (medians over valid rows, floor-of-mean-of-middle-two): the invariance table (11/12 PASS, wm_tcp_mixed 1.237), the two converge fails (push_tcp_small 2080/1811/1868; pull_tcp_mixed 1138/867/1284), the cross rows and the gap rows (old_push/old_pull vs new_mw_worst/new_wm_worst per fixture x carrier) against docs/bench/otp2w-baseline-2026-07-10/summary.csv; (b) is the CR post-processing legitimate and fully disclosed (raw file committed; no timing value altered - verify runs.csv and runs-raw-crlf.csv differ ONLY by \r bytes); (c) does the README avoid declaring pass/fail and avoid self-adjudicating the D-2026-07-12-1 residue (gap rows RECORDED; the platform-attribution language); (d) the escalation reasoning: no pre-registered trigger fires (verify the fails have tight spreads and are not straddles per the design doc D2's rule); (e) the cross-block consistency note (block-1 push_tcp_small 2080 vs block-2 mw_tcp_small mac_init 1922) - is it honestly recorded and is any conclusion improperly drawn; (f) any overclaim in calling wm_tcp_mixed/push_tcp_small code-shaped findings. Output a concise markdown findings list - each finding with file:line, severity, rationale - then a final VERDICT line. Be concise; do not invoke skills.

.review/results/macmac-harness-r10.grok.md:1:I'll review the instrument for confident-wrong-answer risk: inspect commit 8830fda, read the four files at HEAD, then drive the engine with synthetic sessions.HEAD is past `8830fda`; I'll compare the four instrument files and finish reading the harness, then attack the decision rule with synthetic sessions.Attacking the decision rule with synthetic sessions and hunting for unguarded paths, dead protections, and B/interleave edge cases.Probing a class-(a) hole: whether the pair-count guard still fails if `!= PAIRS` is weakened to `< PAIRS`.# Mac↔Mac instrument review — workspace HEAD (post-`8830fda`)
.review/results/macmac-harness-r10.grok.md:13:| 3 | **MEDIUM** | `otp12pf_mac_verdict.py:200`; `otp12pf_mac_verdict_test.py:284–287` | **Exact-`n` is case-guarded, not mutation-complete.** Live rule is `len(d) != PAIRS` (correct). Weakening to `len(d) < PAIRS` yields **false `REPRODUCES`** on the 16-pair trim session. The SHORT-cell mutation still passes under that weakening (`INCOMPLETE`); only the LONG **case** kills it. Preflight runs cases, so a full guard run still catches it — but the mutation that claims to prove pair-counting does not prove *exact* equality. Class (a) on the guard. |

.review/results/macmac-harness-r10.grok.md:120:Synthetic EFFECT/null probes (all on live engine): see attack tables above; no false `REPRODUCES` / false `DOES-NOT-REPRODUCE` found for harness-shaped n=8 data.
.review/results/macmac-harness-r10.grok.md:126:Under the registered design (`n≡8`, interleaved harness, pinned constants), I could **not** force a confident wrong `REPRODUCES` or `DOES-NOT-REPRODUCE`. The asymmetry is dormant but sound; EFFECT is min-based (strong); B hardens rather than softens; escalation is actually gone; settle is measured and can void.
.review/results/macmac-r12.grok-design.md:1:I'll treat this as a design challenge, not a rubber stamp: read the preregistration, harness, and verdict rule, run the selftests, then argue whether the Mac↔Mac run can actually answer the question.Continuing the harness (ABBA schedule and measurement path) and running the selftests.Running the verdict guard tests and the harness selftest, and probing a few design-risk spots.Checking a few more concrete failure modes that could produce a wrong verdict:## 1–2. Verdict on the question and the outcomes
.review/results/macmac-r12.grok-design.md:5:The only clean test of “can P1 occur with no Windows peer?” is a real same-OS pair that is not Linux↔Linux. Mac↔Mac over 10GbE is that cell. Loopback would not do (the parent plan already says a negative loopback is inconclusive). So as a *scientific* 2×2 fill, the *shape* of the question is fine.
.review/results/macmac-r12.grok-design.md:13:| Linux↔Linux | No (8/8) |
.review/results/macmac-r12.grok-design.md:23:| **REPRODUCES** | P1 can appear with no Windows peer **on this pair**. Not “Windows residue.” Code-level and macOS/APFS/host×role explanations stay open. Does **not** kill or confirm H1. | “H1 is confirmed” / “platform-general layout cost” / “skip Windows investigation” | **No.** Still pf-1 / fix on **rig W**, where release is blocked. |
.review/results/macmac-r12.grok-design.md:31:**No outcome changes the release-critical next step:** attribute and fix (or formally re-scope) P1 on the macOS↔Windows pair. Mac↔Mac only reweights priors inside pf-1, and only if it returns a *decisive* REPRODUCES / DOES-NOT-REPRODUCE. Under the rule’s own austerity, decisive outcomes are not the mode you should bet on (below).
.review/results/macmac-r12.grok-design.md:39:3. **Only if** you need the platform map for a criterion conversation: a **minimal** Mac↔Mac TCP×mixed both directions (even informal n≈4) before this six-cell cathedral. If ratios sit at ~1.00 vs ~1.30, you already know; then either formalize or stop.
.review/results/macmac-r12.grok-design.md:60:| 40 ms | 15 ms | ~92% REPRODUCES, ~8% CONTROLS-NOT-CLEAN |
.review/results/macmac-r12.grok-design.md:61:| 60 ms | 20 ms | ~56% REPRODUCES, ~41% CONTROLS-NOT-CLEAN |
.review/results/macmac-r12.grok-design.md:62:| 80 ms | 25 ms | ~17% REPRODUCES, ~78% CONTROLS-NOT-CLEAN |
.review/results/macmac-r12.grok-design.md:70:- Δ_ref = 230 ms is **W’s** absolute effect. On faster Mac↔Mac arms, T often becomes `src/10` (stricter null, easier small relative EFFECT). Not wrong, but the null gets harder on a fast pair.
.review/results/macmac-r12.grok-design.md:78:| **HIGH** | EFFECT also needs **min(d) ≥ T+B** (every pair). A real ~25% effect with one soft pair → UNCLEAR, not REPRODUCES. Conservative, but weak power. |
.review/results/macmac-r12.grok-design.md:102:Would I trust a null out of it? **Yes**, if I got one. Would I expect to get one on a live APFS/10GbE pair with purge/drain/fsync in the loop? **Often no** — UNCLEAR / CONTROLS-NOT-CLEAN instead. That is the rule working as a nervous instrument, not as a sharp classifier.
.review/results/macmac-r12.grok-design.md:117:I did **not** find a live “false REPRODUCES / false DOES-NOT-REPRODUCE” in the graded path comparable to the historical timer/settle/control-void blockers. Remaining issues are smaller or process-level.
.review/results/macmac-r12.grok-design.md:137:**DO NOT RUN IT — skip Mac↔Mac for now and spend the session on pf-1 / dial-accept diagnostics on the macOS↔Windows pair where P1 is known; no outcome of this rig changes that obligation, and the rule is likely to spend the run on CONTROLS-NOT-CLEAN.**
.review/results/macmac-harness.codex.md:39:# otp-12 Mac↔Mac rig — PRE-REGISTRATION (written before any timed run)
.review/results/macmac-harness.codex.md:60:So a Mac↔Mac reproduction is **consistent with H1**, not fatal to it — and the
.review/results/macmac-harness.codex.md:74:| **P1 REPRODUCES macOS↔macOS** | The failure needs **no Windows peer**. P1 is **not platform residue** — it is a cost of the layout/code that survives with the Windows half removed. This **closes the "accept it as platform residue" escape** (the D-2026-07-12-1 shape) and **strengthens every code-level hypothesis, H1 included**. It does **not** name the mechanism. |
.review/results/macmac-harness.codex.md:84:- **nagatha** (owner's workstation): 10GbE `en11` = **10.1.10.92**, MTU 9000.
.review/results/macmac-harness.codex.md:85:- **`q`** (M4 mini, dedicated bench Mac): 10GbE `en8` = **10.1.10.54**, MTU 9000.
.review/results/macmac-harness.codex.md:172:2. **REPRODUCES (in a named direction).** A TCP×mixed cell FAILS the 1.10 bar with
.review/results/macmac-harness.codex.md:694:- **NEXT ACTION — rev 3 of the Mac↔Mac pre-registration + harness, then run it (Queue 1(ii); the second of the two experiments that come BEFORE any pf code).** Experiment (i), the A-B-B-A MTU run, is **DONE** (pf-0 below). Rig: nagatha `10.1.10.92` ↔ `q` `10.1.10.54`, both 10GbE/9000. Pre-registered (**rev 2**, codex round 1 = 9 findings, **9/9 accepted**): `docs/bench/otp12-macmac-2026-07-14/PREREGISTRATION.md`; harness `scripts/bench_otp12pf_mac.sh`. **⚠ CORRECTED 2026-07-14 — it is NOT an H1 discriminator.** The earlier claim here ("reproduces ⇒ **H1 DIES**, H1 accuses the *Windows* accept branch") was **WRONG and is retracted**: H1 accuses **blit's own code paths** (`SourceSockets` Dial/Accept, `add_dialed_stream`, the dial-before-ACK at `transfer_session/mod.rs:3113`) — **the word "Windows" appears nowhere in H1**, and that code runs on macOS too. A Mac↔Mac reproduction is therefore *consistent with* H1, not fatal to it. What the rig **does** answer: **does P1 require the macOS↔Windows PAIRING, or is it a platform-general cost of the destination-initiated layout?** Reproduces ⇒ P1 is **not platform residue** (no Windows peer needed) → the "accept it as platform residue" escape closes and every code-level hypothesis strengthens. Vanishes ⇒ P1 is **pairing-dependent** → platform-agnostic code mechanisms weaken. Either way the hypothesis space moves, which is why it precedes pf-1. **Then `pf-1`** (the HARD GATE), which two pf-0 results now BIND: (a) **between-session grading is dead** (a 20% recovery = 46 ms sits under the 78 ms between-session floor) — pf-1 must **measure its own paired within-session floor and register a resolution check** before grading; (b) **the fast arm is BISTABLE** — grade the run distribution, not the median.
.review/results/macmac-harness.codex.md:698:- **P1 REPRODUCES ON A SECOND MAC (2026-07-13, `docs/bench/otp12-q-baseline-2026-07-13/`).** `wm_tcp_mixed` = **1.385 FAIL** on `q`↔netwatch-01 **at MTU 9000**, while all three controls PASS at **1.002–1.043** in the same session (so rig noise is ~2–4% and P1 is 10× outside it). **P1 is a property of the macOS↔Windows PAIRING, not of one machine** — the assumption **H1** rests on (corrected 2026-07-14: H5/H6/H7 are **P2** hypotheses; the earlier "H1/H5/H6/H7" was wrong), never tested until now. **And jumbo does NOT dissolve P1** — pf-0 has now measured the matched 1500 arm and killed MTU outright (above).
.review/results/macmac-harness.codex.md:701:- **P1 (the headline invariance criterion) — the one thing between blit and shipping.** Fails rig W (`wm_tcp_mixed` 1.237 and 1.300 — do NOT read that as a regression, it is **two different Mac NICs**), but **PASSES 8/8 with Linux on both ends** (`docs/bench/otp12-perf-2026-07-13/`; P1's own cell 1.092/1.003). So it is **platform-INTERACTING, not pure layout** — yet **NOT exonerated**: a code path that only bites on one platform (H1's Windows accept branch) looks identical. **P1 HAS NO ESCAPE HATCH** (codex r5 F1): D-2026-07-12-1 waives only a *cross-direction* miss for a cell that ALREADY passes invariance — P1 *is* the invariance failure. So: **fix it to ≤1.10, or the owner amends acceptance criterion 1.** Neither is assumed.
.review/results/macmac-harness.codex.md:767:   2026-07-13). Linux↔Linux = **no P1** (8/8 PASS); macOS↔Windows = **P1**

.review/results/macmac-harness.codex.md:2352:-- **NEXT ACTION — the MAC↔MAC RIG (Queue 1(ii)), the second of the two experiments that come BEFORE any pf code.** Experiment (i), the A-B-B-A MTU run, is **DONE** (pf-0 below). Rig: nagatha `10.1.10.92` ↔ `q` `10.1.10.54`, both 10GbE/9000. Pre-registered (**rev 2**, codex round 1 = 9 findings, **9/9 accepted**): `docs/bench/otp12-macmac-2026-07-14/PREREGISTRATION.md`; harness `scripts/bench_otp12pf_mac.sh`. **⚠ CORRECTED 2026-07-14 — it is NOT an H1 discriminator.** The earlier claim here ("reproduces ⇒ **H1 DIES**, H1 accuses the *Windows* accept branch") was **WRONG and is retracted**: H1 accuses **blit's own code paths** (`SourceSockets` Dial/Accept, `add_dialed_stream`, the dial-before-ACK at `transfer_session/mod.rs:3113`) — **the word "Windows" appears nowhere in H1**, and that code runs on macOS too. A Mac↔Mac reproduction is therefore *consistent with* H1, not fatal to it. What the rig **does** answer: **does P1 require the macOS↔Windows PAIRING, or is it a platform-general cost of the destination-initiated layout?** Reproduces ⇒ P1 is **not platform residue** (no Windows peer needed) → the "accept it as platform residue" escape closes and every code-level hypothesis strengthens. Vanishes ⇒ P1 is **pairing-dependent** → platform-agnostic code mechanisms weaken. Either way the hypothesis space moves, which is why it precedes pf-1. **Then `pf-1`** (the HARD GATE), which two pf-0 results now BIND: (a) **between-session grading is dead** (a 20% recovery = 46 ms sits under the 78 ms between-session floor) — pf-1 must **measure its own paired within-session floor and register a resolution check** before grading; (b) **the fast arm is BISTABLE** — grade the run distribution, not the median.

Vanishes ⇒ P1 is **pairing-dependent** → platform-agnostic code mechanisms weaken. Either way the hypothesis space moves, which is why it precedes pf-1. **Then `pf-1`** (the HARD GATE), which two pf-0 results now BIND: (a) **between-session grading is dead** (a 20% recovery = 46 ms sits under the 78 ms between-session floor) — pf-1 must **measure its own paired within-session floor and register a resolution check** before grading; (b) **the fast arm is BISTABLE** — grade the run distribution, not the median.
.review/results/macmac-harness.codex.md:2512:- `docs/bench/otp12-macmac-2026-07-14/PREREGISTRATION.md:94-123,140-143`; `scripts/bench_otp12pf_mac.sh:448-475,488-495` — **BLOCKER** — `S=max(d_i)-min(d_i)` is neither an MDE nor an equivalence/confidence bound at `n=8`; the “power” test ignores how close observed `D` is to 230 ms. With `srcinit=2000×8` and `d=[0,180,180,190,190,200,200,200]`, it returns PASS, `D=190`, `S=200`, `powered=yes`, hence VANISHES if repeated in both cells—despite seven positive pairs and an effect already 83% of reference. It also uses `min(src,dest)` instead of the source baseline.
.review/results/macmac-harness.codex.md:2514:- `docs/bench/otp12-macmac-2026-07-14/PREREGISTRATION.md:125-161`; `scripts/bench_otp12pf_mac.sh:403-496` — **BLOCKER** — The six outcomes remain overlapping and incomplete. MIXED-SIGN is shadowed by the earlier REPRODUCES/INVERSION cases; FAIL with `|D|≤S`, sub-bar negative asymmetry, and incomplete cells are unmapped; UNDERPOWERED and UNSTABLE are extra outcomes. `compute_verdicts` implements none of the session rule, control gate, clustering, or six outcomes—it emits only per-cell PASS/FAIL/INCOMPLETE.
.review/results/macmac-harness.codex.md:2530:- `docs/bench/otp12-macmac-2026-07-14/PREREGISTRATION.md:39-42`; `docs/plan/OTP12_PERF_FINDINGS.md:3-17`; `docs/DECISIONS.md:179-180` — **LOW** — The accepted sequencing conflict was changed only in `docs/STATE.md`: the active plan still says pf-1 starts now and the settled sequence says MTU→pf-1, while rev 2 inserts Mac↔Mac before pf-1.

.review/results/macmac-harness.codex.md:3156:/bin/zsh -lc "python3 -c \"src=[2000]*8; ds=[0,180,180,190,190,200,200,200]; dst=[s+x for s,x in zip(src,ds)]; med=lambda v:(sorted(v)[3]+sorted(v)[4])//2; s=med(src); d=med(dst); D=med(ds); S=max(ds)-min(ds); fast=min(s,d); print({'src_med':s,'dst_med':d,'bar':'PASS' if 10*max(s,d)<=11*min(s,d) else 'FAIL','D':D,'S':S,'powered':'yes' if S<=230 and 10*(fast+230)>11*fast else 'NO'})\"" in /Users/michael/Dev/blit_v2

.review/results/macmac-harness-r9.grok.md:1:Reviewing the Mac↔Mac benchmark instrument at `8830fda`: reading the commit and four files, then driving the engine with synthetic sessions to probe for wrong confident verdicts.Running the guard tests and probing the decision engine with synthetic sessions for false confident verdicts.Checking one more class-(a) angle: whether B should track the range, and whether the unguarded tighten is the only load-bearing step.# Mac↔Mac instrument review — `8830fda` (rev 9)
.review/results/macmac-harness-r9.grok.md:11:| 1 | **HIGH** | `otp12pf_mac_verdict.py:232` | **B is the wrong residual.** Spec intent: “bias clean controls failed to exclude.” Controls certify on the **full range** at T/2, but B = `max\|CI bound\|`. At n>8 the CI **trims** the same outliers the range still admits → B understates → **false REPRODUCES**. Class (a): fixed CI-trim for NONE, left it on B. At n=8 CI≡range so the bug is latent in the intended run. |
.review/results/macmac-harness-r9.grok.md:28:### F1 — B from CI understates residual bias → false REPRODUCES (n=16)
.review/results/macmac-harness-r9.grok.md:33:SESSION VERDICT: REPRODUCES
.review/results/macmac-harness-r9.grok.md:44:SESSION VERDICT: REPRODUCES
.review/results/macmac-harness-r9.grok.md:72:| Outlier +800 of 8 | UNCLEAR (not REPRODUCES) |
.review/results/macmac-harness-r9.grok.md:73:| B=0 / 5 / 40 on effect=100 | REPRODUCES → UNCLEAR → UNCLEAR (B only hardens) |
.review/results/macmac-harness-r9.grok.md:99:A clean harness session at n=8 is hard to push into a **confident wrong** REPRODUCES/DOES-NOT-REPRODUCE with the current rule math. The instrument is still not safe to clear: **B uses the quantity the rev-9 rewrite just forbade for nulls**, **n is not pinned to 8**, and **the instrument’s own text still points at RUNS=16**, where those holes produce a bankable false REPRODUCES (reproduced). Fix B→range (or pin n≡8 and scrub every RUNS=16 path), enforce exact pair count, fix contradictory prose, and mutation-guard the B-tighten before clearance.
.review/results/small-file-ceiling-plan.codex.md:121:+40 MiB proposes a single stream despite 10,000 files — and paid
.review/results/small-file-ceiling-plan.codex.md:283:    41	40 MiB proposes a single stream despite 10,000 files — and paid
.review/results/small-file-ceiling-plan.codex.md:1839:**2026-07-03 16:26:55Z** - **CODER (ue-r2-1c, claude)**: Landed the engine shell (REV4 `ue-r2-1c`, third slice through the code→GPT-review→fix loop). New `crates/blit-core/src/engine/` module: `TransferEngine::execute(EngineRequest)` owns strategy selection (single-file → journal probe → fast-path walk → streaming) and the streaming leg (tuning window → scan → diff/plan → sink pipeline → mirror deletions → journal checkpoints → history/predictor); `TransferOrchestrator` shrank to the local adapter (preconditions, `FsTransferSource`/`FilteredSource` + `FsTransferSink`/`NullSink` construction, option translation) with the public API preserved via re-exports — engine type thereby ratified per REV4 Design §1 (new engine + adapter, not a rename). Sequence: behavior pins first (`7730eb1`: NoWork/UpToDate + `no_work` tag, NoWork/SourceEmpty — both previously untested), the move (`dc9b0ed`: `fast_path.rs`→`engine/strategy.rs`, options/summary/history moved, tuning/mirror/journal/single-file split out; move fidelity machine-checked — 75 diff lines vs origin, all six documented transformations, zero drift), then the REV4-named accounting gap (`29e210b`: single-file strategy now records perf history tag `single_file` / `null_sink` lane + predictor, guard test proven by revert). Review: first codex run died on provider quota (121k tokens, recorded); owner restored access same day; retry PASS with 1 Low, accepted + fixed (`15e6334`: engine back-references through `crate::orchestrator` → engine-local paths; swept one more the reviewer missed). Also recorded owner-provided 10GbE test env (`admin@skippy:/mnt/generic-pool/video/test`) in STATE queue item 4. Validation: fmt/clippy clean, `cargo test --workspace` 1394 / 0 / 2 (baseline 1391, +3). All on master, unpushed. Next: `ue-r2-1d` (streaming plan foundation) — or `1e`/w4-1 per queue.
.review/results/small-file-ceiling-plan.codex.md:1849:**2026-06-12 16:32:00Z** - **REVIEW (gemini-reviewer)**: Graded the two pending sentinels on `master` sequentially: **design-4-fallback-midmanifest-negotiation** (`ddfeb58`) accepted (daemon early-flush branch is now TCP-only; client-side fallback_negotiated flag added to hold need-list/manifest fallback payloads in pending_queue; 2000-file regression test passes) and **design-5-send-failure-masks-rejection** (`08d71a2`) accepted (prefer_server_error helper harvests daemon's terminal status on manifest-phase send failures; 500-file readonly_enforcement regression test passes repeatedly). Both verified.json verdicts created, sentinels deleted via git rm, and REVIEW.md status rows updated to `[x]`. Validation suite re-run: cargo fmt, clippy, and cargo test --workspace all passed successfully (1370 passed, 0 failed, 1 ignored).
.review/results/small-file-ceiling-plan.codex.md:1857:**2026-06-12 03:55:00Z** - **CODER (autonomous overnight session)**: Owner authorized autonomous queue execution ("work on as much as you can. commit every slice as it lands. if anything gets questionable, stop." — scope: master only, no branches, local commits, never push, faster-mode WIP iff files-changed disjoint across pending sentinels; recorded in STATE.md). **7 slices completed and sentineled** (18 local commits, `1adbe0c..0af904e`): **w5-1-log-backend** (`56bda09`+`7145202`) — new `blit_core::stderr_log` backend (warn default, `BLIT_LOG` override, `<binary>: <level>: <msg>`) installed in all 4 binaries; ~22 formerly-discarded log::warn/error sites now visible; 9 bracketed stderr prefix families converged (warnings→facade, operator lines→`blit:`/`blitd:` prefixes, per-file pipeline spam→log::debug); trace-gated `[data-plane-client]` family kept (remote_parity.rs asserts the prefix); jobs-watch ticker vocabulary kept (single-command status output). **w9-5-jobs-lifecycle-e2e** (`ad773d8`) — first e2e net for detach/jobs verbs: 5 tests incl. cancel-active-delegated-job exit-0 made deterministic via a fake tonic source whose pull_sync never answers. **w2-1-delete-warmup-machinery** (`2a8a490`) — analyze_warmup_result + determine_tuning + warmup_gbps deleted; determine_remote_tuning is one honest static table (production values bit-identical); 4 tier tests added. **w9-1-ungate-windows-tests** (`9324559`) — 27 blanket #[cfg(unix)] removed from 8 remote-transfer test files (none unix-specific); 4 genuinely unix files kept gated; Windows CI will now actually compile parity-critical paths. **w9-2-revive-root-tests** (`461525d`) — dead root tests/ relocated into crates/blit-core/tests (+24 tests incl. the only MirrorPlanner coverage); connection.rs deleted; drift fixed (for_tests→load(), compare_mode, 32→300 files vs TINY_FILE_LIMIT=256, serial Mutex for config-dir globals); AGENTS.md §4 corrected. **w9-4-readonly-enforcement-tests** (`4d67210`) — TestContext::new_read_only() knob + 3 e2e tests covering all 3 read-only write gates (push/purge/delegated-pull), asserting error text + no destination mutation. **w8-1b-zero-copy-fast-eval** (`6189d82`) — analysis-only: `docs/plan/ZERO_COPY_RECEIVE_EVAL.md` (Draft) recommends DELETING zero_copy.rs (wire path is splice-friendly but the dead impl busy-waits on EAGAIN, the AsyncRead-generic pipeline resists raw-fd wiring, and the CPU win is unmeasured pending the 10GbE rig); owner verdict is the gate. **Suite: 1331 → 1368 tests, fmt/clippy/test green after every slice.** Remaining `[ ]` rows are all overlap-blocked behind the 7 pending sentinels (skip map in STATE.md), owner-gated (w2-3 plan interview), or dependent (w2-4, w6-2, w8-1). Transient note: first post-w5-1 workspace test run showed 11 "daemon failed to listen" failures under cold parallel load; isolated and clean re-runs green — same harness-contention class w9-3 addresses.
.review/results/small-file-ceiling-plan.codex.md:2061:**2026-07-03 16:26:55Z** - **CODER (ue-r2-1c, claude)**: Landed the engine shell (REV4 `ue-r2-1c`, third slice through the code→GPT-review→fix loop). Also recorded owner-provided 10GbE test env (`admin@skippy:/mnt/generic-pool/video/test`) in STATE queue item 4. .review/results/small-file-ceiling-plan.codex.md:2296:# 10GbE session evidence (2026-07-04, MTU 9000 both ends)
.review/results/small-file-ceiling-plan.codex.md:2302:Concurrent 2x push: 8.30 + 3.21 Gbps, clean completion, no resize triggered (single stream saturates 10GbE)
.review/results/macmac-harness-r9.verdict.md:18:the three pairs at −500** while keeping the thirteen at +200 → **`REPRODUCES`**.
.review/results/macmac-harness-r9.verdict.md:34:`B=5` instead of `40`, and a measurand at +105 then read `REPRODUCES`.
.review/results/otp-12-worker-parity.fix-review.codex.md:14:Review the diff of commit cfd9dd7 (run: git show cfd9dd7). It is the review-fix commit for otp-12-worker-parity under docs/plan/ONE_TRANSFER_PATH.md and docs/TRANSFER_SESSION.md. The prior review of a76b785 found: (1) serial pre-payload resize settlement delayed first byte and could trip active StallGuards; (2) resize refusal was not terminal and could be reproposed. Check that both are fully fixed without weakening exact initiator-independent worker-target parity. Check correctness regressions, first-byte/converge-up behavior, bounded backpressure, resize epoch/refusal semantics, socket/END teardown, cancellation and peer-fault propagation, byte/tree correctness, StallGuard, and that test count did not drop. Output a concise markdown findings list — each finding with file:line, severity, rationale — then a final VERDICT line. Be concise; do not invoke skills.
.review/results/pf-0-rebaseline-decision.codex.md:414: - **P1 REPRODUCES ON A SECOND MAC (2026-07-13, `docs/bench/otp12-q-baseline-2026-07-13/`).** `wm_tcp_mixed` = **1.385 FAIL** on `q`↔netwatch-01 **at MTU 9000**, while all three controls PASS at **1.002–1.043** in the same session (so rig noise is ~2–4% and P1 is 10× outside it). **P1 is a property of the macOS↔Windows PAIRING, not of one machine** — the assumption **H1** rests on (corrected 2026-07-14: H5/H6/H7 are **P2** hypotheses; the earlier "H1/H5/H6/H7" was wrong), never tested until now. **And jumbo does NOT dissolve P1** — pf-0 has now measured the matched 1500 arm and killed MTU outright (above).

.review/results/pf-0-rebaseline-decision.codex.md:2288:     9	- **P1 REPRODUCES ON A SECOND MAC (2026-07-13, `docs/bench/otp12-q-baseline-2026-07-13/`).** `wm_tcp_mixed` = **1.385 FAIL** on `q`↔netwatch-01 **at MTU 9000**, while all three controls PASS at **1.002–1.043** in the same session (so rig noise is ~2–4% and P1 is 10× outside it). **P1 is a property of the macOS↔Windows PAIRING, not of one machine** — the assumption **H1** rests on (corrected 2026-07-14: H5/H6/H7 are **P2** hypotheses; the earlier "H1/H5/H6/H7" was wrong), never tested until now. **And jumbo does NOT dissolve P1** — pf-0 has now measured the matched 1500 arm and killed MTU outright (above).
.review/results/pf-0-rebaseline-decision.codex.md:2291:    12	- **P1 (the headline invariance criterion) — the one thing between blit and shipping.** Fails rig W (`wm_tcp_mixed` 1.237 and 1.300 — do NOT read that as a regression, it is **two different Mac NICs**), but **PASSES 8/8 with Linux on both ends** (`docs/bench/otp12-perf-2026-07-13/`; P1's own cell 1.092/1.003). So it is **platform-INTERACTING, not pure layout** — yet **NOT exonerated**: a code path that only bites on one platform (H1's Windows accept branch) looks identical. **P1 HAS NO ESCAPE HATCH** (codex r5 F1): D-2026-07-12-1 waives only a *cross-direction* miss for a cell that ALREADY passes invariance — P1 *is* the invariance failure. So: **fix it to ≤1.10, or the owner amends acceptance criterion 1.** Neither is assumed.
