.review/results/macmac-harness-r5.codex.md:28:- Round 4 (grok, which reproduced it live): the equivalence margin was fixed to min(bar_breach, DELTA_REF) for the MEASURAND but left tied to the BAR for the CONTROLS -- so a control carrying a real, 8/8, rig-W-sized effect (d_i=230 at src=2500, ratio 1.092, bar PASS) landed as PARTIAL and ESCAPED RIG-VOID, and the session printed a clean VANISHES while every control was dirty.
.review/results/macmac-harness-r5.codex.md:38:4. Is the taxonomy EXHAUSTIVE, and is the session PRECEDENCE right? REPRODUCES now outranks UNSTABLE and BAR-FAIL-INCONSISTENT -- can that hide a rig problem, or leak a false positive?
.review/results/macmac-harness-r5.codex.md:106:I reproduced three headline failures against the pinned result: a false `REPRODUCES` unique to the new `n=16` path, a `VANISHES` with all four controls materially dirty but classified `UNDERPOWERED`, and the exact round-4 false-null returning when the harness-allowed `DELTA_REF_MS` override is raised. The nominal CI/sign-test arithmetic itself checks out; the failures are decision wiring and fail-closed enforcement.
.review/results/macmac-harness-r5.codex.md:112:- **BLOCKER** — [scripts/otp12pf_mac_verdict.py:228-255](/Users/michael/Dev/blit_v2/scripts/otp12pf_mac_verdict.py:228) — At `n=16`, `bar == "FAIL"` has no direction yet makes either sign material. With 13 paired `+1ms` differences and three `−110ms` differences, the CI is `[+1,+1]`, `p=.0213`, while marginal medians fail the bar in the inverse direction. The engine emits `REPRODUCES` for a `+1ms` effect with clean controls and zero voids.
.review/results/macmac-harness-r5.codex.md:114:- **BLOCKER** — [scripts/otp12pf_mac_verdict.py:340-354](/Users/michael/Dev/blit_v2/scripts/otp12pf_mac_verdict.py:340), [PREREGISTRATION.md:234-254](/Users/michael/Dev/blit_v2/docs/bench/otp12-macmac-2026-07-14/PREREGISTRATION.md:234) — The control fix covers only `PARTIAL`; `UNDERPOWERED` controls with `null_excl=false` escape. Null measurands plus all four controls at `src=2500`, `d=[0,230×7]` produced headline `VANISHES`; every control had `D=230`, `CI=[0,230]`, ratio `1.092`. This is the same materiality bug in another branch.
.review/results/macmac-harness-r5.codex.md:134:The CI arithmetic itself is correct: `99.21875%` at `n=8`, `97.8729%` at `n=16`; zero-aware sign testing, `+src/10`, `−src/11`, and the default measurand margins are correct. `REPRODUCES` precedence over a messy sibling matches the registered either-direction question; the false positive occurs earlier in per-cell materiality.
.review/results/macmac-harness-r5.codex.md:143:- **BLOCKER** — [scripts/otp12pf_mac_verdict.py:228-255](/Users/michael/Dev/blit_v2/scripts/otp12pf_mac_verdict.py:228) — At `n=16`, `bar == "FAIL"` has no direction yet makes either sign material. The engine emits `REPRODUCES` for a `+1ms` effect with clean controls and zero voids.

.review/results/macmac-harness-r5.codex.md:165:The CI arithmetic itself is correct: `99.21875%` at `n=8`, `97.8729%` at `n=16`; zero-aware sign testing, `+src/10`, `−src/11`, and the default measurand margins are correct. `REPRODUCES` precedence over a messy sibling matches the registered either-direction question; the false positive occurs earlier in per-cell materiality.
.review/results/macmac-harness-r12.codex-harness.md:35:- THE REGISTERED TOPOLOGY IS NOW ENFORCED (this was the round-11 BLOCKER: NIC/IP/MAC were env-overridable and the MTU and link speed were never checked at all, so the run could go over the 1GbE NIC or at MTU 1500). Now: the topology is a pinned literal and the harness refuses if any of those names is present in the environment; topology_gate checks the NIC's MTU, negotiated media and link status; mss_gate opens a real TCP connection to the peer's registered IP and checks the negotiated MSS (8948) AND the local address the kernel chose; the fabric is re-checked at the END of the session and voids it if it changed.
.review/results/macmac-harness-r2.codex.md:350:- **NEXT ACTION — rev 3 of the Mac↔Mac pre-registration + harness, then run it (Queue 1(ii); the second of the two experiments that come BEFORE any pf code).** Experiment (i), the A-B-B-A MTU run, is **DONE** (pf-0 below). Rig: nagatha `10.1.10.92` ↔ `q` `10.1.10.54`, both 10GbE/9000. Pre-registered (**rev 2**, codex round 1 = 9 findings, **9/9 accepted**): `docs/bench/otp12-macmac-2026-07-14/PREREGISTRATION.md`; harness `scripts/bench_otp12pf_mac.sh`. **⚠ CORRECTED 2026-07-14 — it is NOT an H1 discriminator.** The earlier claim here ("reproduces ⇒ **H1 DIES**, H1 accuses the *Windows* accept branch") was **WRONG and is retracted**: H1 accuses **blit's own code paths** (`SourceSockets` Dial/Accept, `add_dialed_stream`, the dial-before-ACK at `transfer_session/mod.rs:3113`) — **the word "Windows" appears nowhere in H1**, and that code runs on macOS too. A Mac↔Mac reproduction is therefore *consistent with* H1, not fatal to it. What the rig **does** answer: **does P1 require the macOS↔Windows PAIRING, or is it a platform-general cost of the destination-initiated layout?** Reproduces ⇒ P1 is **not platform residue** (no Windows peer needed) → the "accept it as platform residue" escape closes and every code-level hypothesis strengthens. Vanishes ⇒ P1 is **pairing-dependent** → platform-agnostic code mechanisms weaken. Either way the hypothesis space moves, which is why it precedes pf-1. **Then `pf-1`** (the HARD GATE), which two pf-0 results now BIND: (a) **between-session grading is dead** (a 20% recovery = 46 ms sits under the 78 ms between-session floor) — pf-1 must **measure its own paired within-session floor and register a resolution check** before grading; (b) **the fast arm is BISTABLE** — grade the run distribution, not the median.
.review/results/macmac-harness-r2.codex.md:354:- **P1 REPRODUCES ON A SECOND MAC (2026-07-13, `docs/bench/otp12-q-baseline-2026-07-13/`).** `wm_tcp_mixed` = **1.385 FAIL** on `q`↔netwatch-01 **at MTU 9000**, while all three controls PASS at **1.002–1.043** in the same session (so rig noise is ~2–4% and P1 is 10× outside it). **P1 is a property of the macOS↔Windows PAIRING, not of one machine** — the assumption **H1** rests on (corrected 2026-07-14: H5/H6/H7 are **P2** hypotheses; the earlier "H1/H5/H6/H7" was wrong), never tested until now. **And jumbo does NOT dissolve P1** — pf-0 has now measured the matched 1500 arm and killed MTU outright (above).
.review/results/macmac-harness-r2.codex.md:357:- **P1 (the headline invariance criterion) — the one thing between blit and shipping.** Fails rig W (`wm_tcp_mixed` 1.237 and 1.300 — do NOT read that as a regression, it is **two different Mac NICs**), but **PASSES 8/8 with Linux on both ends** (`docs/bench/otp12-perf-2026-07-13/`; P1's own cell 1.092/1.003). So it is **platform-INTERACTING, not pure layout** — yet **NOT exonerated**: a code path that only bites on one platform (H1's Windows accept branch) looks identical. **P1 HAS NO ESCAPE HATCH** (codex r5 F1): D-2026-07-12-1 waives only a *cross-direction* miss for a cell that ALREADY passes invariance — P1 *is* the invariance failure. So: **fix it to ≤1.10, or the owner amends acceptance criterion 1.** Neither is assumed.

.review/results/macmac-harness-r2.codex.md:5446:     5	- **NEXT ACTION — the MAC↔MAC RUN (Queue 1(ii)), pending its round-2 instrument review.** Design **rev 3** + the reworked harness are committed (`24660ae`): `docs/bench/otp12-macmac-2026-07-14/PREREGISTRATION.md`, `scripts/bench_otp12pf_mac.sh`, `scripts/otp12pf_mac_verdict.py` (mechanizes the rule), `scripts/otp12pf_mac_verdict_test.py` (guard test, mutation-proven). **NO DATA TAKEN YET.** Two codex rounds → **20 findings, 20/20 accepted** (design 9, instrument 11): `.review/results/macmac-{prereg,harness}.gpt-verdict.md`. **⚠ TO RUN IT THE OWNER MUST CLOSE THEIR CODEX SESSIONS** — nagatha is now a bench **END**, not just the driver, and the quiescence gate refuses to start while `codex`/`cargo`/`rustc` runs on **either** Mac (it already fired, correctly, on its first invocation). Time Machine is OFF on both Macs (required: the gate is fail-closed on autobackup merely *enabled*). Rig: nagatha `10.1.10.92` ↔ `q` `10.1.10.54`, both 10GbE/9000, build pinned `f35702a`. **Then `pf-1`** (the HARD GATE), which two pf-0 results BIND: between-session grading is dead (a 20% recovery = 46 ms sits under the 78 ms floor), so pf-1 must **measure its own paired within-session floor** before grading; and the fast arm is **BISTABLE** — grade the distribution, not the median.
.review/results/macmac-harness-r2.codex.md:5451:    10	- **P1 REPRODUCES ON A SECOND MAC (2026-07-13, `docs/bench/otp12-q-baseline-2026-07-13/`).** `wm_tcp_mixed` = **1.385 FAIL** on `q`↔netwatch-01 **at MTU 9000**, while all three controls PASS at **1.002–1.043** in the same session (so rig noise is ~2–4% and P1 is 10× outside it). **P1 is a property of the macOS↔Windows PAIRING, not of one machine** — the assumption **H1** rests on (corrected 2026-07-14: H5/H6/H7 are **P2** hypotheses; the earlier "H1/H5/H6/H7" was wrong), never tested until now. **And jumbo does NOT dissolve P1** — pf-0 has now measured the matched 1500 arm and killed MTU outright (above).
.review/results/macmac-harness-r2.codex.md:5454:    13	- **P1 (the headline invariance criterion) — the one thing between blit and shipping.** Fails rig W (`wm_tcp_mixed` 1.237 and 1.300 — do NOT read that as a regression, it is **two different Mac NICs**), but **PASSES 8/8 with Linux on both ends** (`docs/bench/otp12-perf-2026-07-13/`; P1's own cell 1.092/1.003). So it is **platform-INTERACTING, not pure layout** — yet **NOT exonerated**: a code path that only bites on one platform (H1's Windows accept branch) looks identical. **P1 HAS NO ESCAPE HATCH** (codex r5 F1): D-2026-07-12-1 waives only a *cross-direction* miss for a cell that ALREADY passes invariance — P1 *is* the invariance failure. So: **fix it to ≤1.10, or the owner amends acceptance criterion 1.** Neither is assumed.

.review/results/macmac-harness-r2.codex.md:5657:- **BLOCKER — [scripts/otp12pf_mac_verdict.py:35](/Users/michael/Dev/blit_v2/scripts/otp12pf_mac_verdict.py:35), [scripts/otp12pf_mac_verdict.py:115](/Users/michael/Dev/blit_v2/scripts/otp12pf_mac_verdict.py:115)** — BLOCKER 2 remains. `DELTA_REF_MS=230` never participates in a decision. With both measurands `srcinit=2500` and all eight `d_i=230`, the engine reports `VANISHES`: ratio 1.092, CI `[230,230]`, sign `p=.0078`, margin 250. A rig-W-sized effect is present in every pair.
.review/results/macmac-harness-r2.codex.md:5659:- **HIGH — [scripts/otp12pf_mac_verdict.py:65](/Users/michael/Dev/blit_v2/scripts/otp12pf_mac_verdict.py:65)** — The claimed 95% median CI is not trustworthy as such at `n=8`. For distinct samples it resolves to approximately `[d₂,d₇]`, whose exact population-median coverage is 92.97%, not 95%; 10k seeded resamples add no information. The lower percentile uses index 250 rather than nearest-rank index 249. The exact sign-test arithmetic is correct, but no verdict reads its result: 7/8 positives can produce `REPRODUCES` while the registered two-sided sign test says `p=.0703`.
.review/results/macmac-harness-r2.codex.md:5661:- **HIGH — [scripts/otp12pf_mac_verdict.py:115](/Users/michael/Dev/blit_v2/scripts/otp12pf_mac_verdict.py:115)** — The symmetric `±0.10·src` equivalence interval is wrong for the symmetric ratio bar. The negative boundary is `-src/11`, about −9.09%. With `src=2000` and CI `[-190,0]`, the engine says `VANISHES`, although −190 implies an inversion ratio of 1.105. `powered` also tests CI width, not exclusion of the margins, and can record `powered_for_null=no` alongside `VANISHES`.
.review/results/macmac-harness-r2.codex.md:5688:- **BLOCKER — [scripts/otp12pf_mac_verdict.py:35](/Users/michael/Dev/blit_v2/scripts/otp12pf_mac_verdict.py:35), [scripts/otp12pf_mac_verdict.py:115](/Users/michael/Dev/blit_v2/scripts/otp12pf_mac_verdict.py:115)** — BLOCKER 2 remains. `DELTA_REF_MS=230` never participates in a decision. With both measurands `srcinit=2500` and all eight `d_i=230`, the engine reports `VANISHES`: ratio 1.092, CI `[230,230]`, sign `p=.0078`, margin 250. A rig-W-sized effect is present in every pair.
.review/results/macmac-harness-r2.codex.md:5690:- **HIGH — [scripts/otp12pf_mac_verdict.py:65](/Users/michael/Dev/blit_v2/scripts/otp12pf_mac_verdict.py:65)** — The claimed 95% median CI is not trustworthy as such at `n=8`. For distinct samples it resolves to approximately `[d₂,d₇]`, whose exact population-median coverage is 92.97%, not 95%; 10k seeded resamples add no information. The lower percentile uses index 250 rather than nearest-rank index 249. The exact sign-test arithmetic is correct, but no verdict reads its result: 7/8 positives can produce `REPRODUCES` while the registered two-sided sign test says `p=.0703`.
.review/results/macmac-harness-r2.codex.md:5692:- **HIGH — [scripts/otp12pf_mac_verdict.py:115](/Users/michael/Dev/blit_v2/scripts/otp12pf_mac_verdict.py:115)** — The symmetric `±0.10·src` equivalence interval is wrong for the symmetric ratio bar. The negative boundary is `-src/11`, about −9.09%. With `src=2000` and CI `[-190,0]`, the engine says `VANISHES`, although −190 implies an inversion ratio of 1.105. `powered` also tests CI width, not exclusion of the margins, and can record `powered_for_null=no` alongside `VANISHES`.
.review/results/d-2026-07-05-3-zerocopy.codex.md:1144:DEVLOG.md:82:**2026-06-12 03:55:00Z** - **CODER (autonomous overnight session)**: Owner authorized autonomous queue execution ("work on as much as you can. commit every slice as it lands. if anything gets questionable, stop." — scope: master only, no branches, local commits, never push, faster-mode WIP iff files-changed disjoint across pending sentinels; recorded in STATE.md). **7 slices completed and sentineled** (18 local commits, `1adbe0c..0af904e`): **w5-1-log-backend** (`56bda09`+`7145202`) — new `blit_core::stderr_log` backend (warn default, `BLIT_LOG` override, `<binary>: <level>: <msg>`) installed in all 4 binaries; ~22 formerly-discarded log::warn/error sites now visible; 9 bracketed stderr prefix families converged (warnings→facade, operator lines→`blit:`/`blitd:` prefixes, per-file pipeline spam→log::debug); trace-gated `[data-plane-client]` family kept (remote_parity.rs asserts the prefix); jobs-watch ticker vocabulary kept (single-command status output). **w9-5-jobs-lifecycle-e2e** (`ad773d8`) — first e2e net for detach/jobs verbs: 5 tests incl. cancel-active-delegated-job exit-0 made deterministic via a fake tonic source whose pull_sync never answers. **w2-1-delete-warmup-machinery** (`2a8a490`) — analyze_warmup_result + determine_tuning + warmup_gbps deleted; determine_remote_tuning is one honest static table (production values bit-identical); 4 tier tests added. **w9-1-ungate-windows-tests** (`9324559`) — 27 blanket #[cfg(unix)] removed from 8 remote-transfer test files (none unix-specific); 4 genuinely unix files kept gated; Windows CI will now actually compile parity-critical paths. **w9-2-revive-root-tests** (`461525d`) — dead root tests/ relocated into crates/blit-core/tests (+24 tests incl. the only MirrorPlanner coverage); connection.rs deleted; drift fixed (for_tests→load(), compare_mode, 32→300 files vs TINY_FILE_LIMIT=256, serial Mutex for config-dir globals); AGENTS.md §4 corrected. **w9-4-readonly-enforcement-tests** (`4d67210`) — TestContext::new_read_only() knob + 3 e2e tests covering all 3 read-only write gates (push/purge/delegated-pull), asserting error text + no destination mutation. **w8-1b-zero-copy-fast-eval** (`6189d82`) — analysis-only: `docs/plan/ZERO_COPY_RECEIVE_EVAL.md` (Draft) recommends DELETING zero_copy.rs (wire path is splice-friendly but the dead impl busy-waits on EAGAIN, the AsyncRead-generic pipeline resists raw-fd wiring, and the CPU win is unmeasured pending the 10GbE rig); owner verdict is the gate. **Suite: 1331 → 1368 tests, fmt/clippy/test green after every slice.** Remaining `[ ]` rows are all overlap-blocked behind the 7 pending sentinels (skip map in STATE.md), owner-gated (w2-3 plan interview), or dependent (w2-4, w6-2, w8-1). Transient note: first post-w5-1 workspace test run showed 11 "daemon failed to listen" failures under cold parallel load; isolated and clean re-runs green — same harness-contention class w9-3 addresses.
.review/results/d-2026-07-05-3-zerocopy.codex.md:1317:docs/audit/DESIGN_MAP_2026-06-11.md:473:- `DEFAULT_BLOCK_SIZE / MAX_BLOCK_SIZE` = 1 MiB / 64 MiB (MAX at line 19) (/home/michael/dev/Blit/crates/blit-core/src/copy/file_copy/resume.rs:16) — SIMPLE-positive (client sends block_size:0 = 'use default' at blit-cli/src/transfers/remote.rs:383); daemon clamps to MAX at pull_sync.rs:84. Fixed 1 MiB granularity is never adapted to file size or link speed (mild FAST tension).

4 genuinely unix files kept gated; Windows CI will now actually compile parity-critical paths. **w9-2-revive-root-tests** (`461525d`) — dead root tests/ relocated into crates/blit-core/tests (+24 tests incl. the only MirrorPlanner coverage); connection.rs deleted; drift fixed (for_tests→load(), compare_mode, 32→300 files vs TINY_FILE_LIMIT=256, serial Mutex for config-dir globals); AGENTS.md §4 corrected. **w9-4-readonly-enforcement-tests** (`4d67210`) — TestContext::new_read_only() knob + 3 e2e tests covering all 3 read-only write gates (push/purge/delegated-pull), asserting error text + no destination mutation. **w8-1b-zero-copy-fast-eval** (`6189d82`) — analysis-only: `docs/plan/ZERO_COPY_RECEIVE_EVAL.md` (Draft) recommends DELETING zero_copy.rs (wire path is splice-friendly but the dead impl busy-waits on EAGAIN, the AsyncRead-generic pipeline resists raw-fd wiring, and the CPU win is unmeasured pending the 10GbE rig); owner verdict is the gate. **Suite: 1331 → 1368 tests, fmt/clippy/test green after every slice.** Remaining `[ ]` rows are all overlap-blocked behind the 7 pending sentinels (skip map in STATE.md), owner-gated (w2-3 plan interview), or dependent (w2-4, w6-2, w8-1). Transient note: first post-w5-1 workspace test run showed 11 "daemon failed to listen" failures under cold parallel load; isolated and clean re-runs green — same harness-contention class w9-3 addresses.

.review/results/macmac-harness-r9.codex.md:524: So a Mac↔Mac reproduction is **consistent with H1**, not fatal to it — and the
.review/results/macmac-harness-r9.codex.md:528: | **P1 REPRODUCES** | P1 **does not require a Windows peer** (on this pair), so it is **not** waivable as "Windows residue", and every code-level hypothesis strengthens. **Limits**: it does **not** establish a platform-*general* cost (two Macs are not "all platforms"); it does **not** name the mechanism; it does **not** kill H1 (the code H1 accuses runs here too); and it leaves **macOS/APFS** and **host×role** explanations fully **OPEN** — "not Windows-specific" is not "not platform-specific" (round-3 BLOCKER). |

.review/results/macmac-harness-r9.codex.md:2672:docs/STATE.md:15:- **P1 REPRODUCES ON A SECOND MAC (2026-07-13, `docs/bench/otp12-q-baseline-2026-07-13/`).** `wm_tcp_mixed` = **1.385 FAIL** on `q`↔netwatch-01 **at MTU 9000**, while all three controls PASS at **1.002–1.043** in the same session (so rig noise is ~2–4% and P1 is 10× outside it). **P1 is a property of the macOS↔Windows PAIRING, not of one machine** — the assumption **H1** rests on (corrected 2026-07-14: H5/H6/H7 are **P2** hypotheses; the earlier "H1/H5/H6/H7" was wrong), never tested until now. **And jumbo does NOT dissolve P1** — pf-0 has now measured the matched 1500 arm and killed MTU outright (above).
.review/results/macmac-harness-r9.codex.md:2673:docs/STATE.md:18:- **P1 (the headline invariance criterion) — the one thing between blit and shipping.** Fails rig W (`wm_tcp_mixed` 1.237 and 1.300 — do NOT read that as a regression, it is **two different Mac NICs**), but **PASSES 8/8 with Linux on both ends** (`docs/bench/otp12-perf-2026-07-13/`; P1's own cell 1.092/1.003). So it is **platform-INTERACTING, not pure layout** — yet **NOT exonerated**: a code path that only bites on one platform (H1's Windows accept branch) looks identical. **P1 HAS NO ESCAPE HATCH** (codex r5 F1): D-2026-07-12-1 waives only a *cross-direction* miss for a cell that ALREADY passes invariance — P1 *is* the invariance failure. So: **fix it to ≤1.10, or the owner amends acceptance criterion 1.** Neither is assumed.
.review/results/macmac-harness-r9.codex.md:2687:.review/results/macmac-harness-r2.grok.md:12:| 2 | `otp12pf_mac_verdict.py:115–140`; prereg §POWER GATE | **BLOCKER** | **VANISHES equivalence margin is `0.10 × srcinit_median`, not the reference effect.** `DELTA_REF_MS=230` is written to CSV only (line 35, 155) and **never used** in the decision. A true, tight effect of 230 ms on a 2500 ms arm (ratio 1.092, bar PASS, CI=[230,230] ⊂ ±250) yields **VANISHES** — i.e. “P1 absent / Windows may be required” while a rig-W-sized absolute Δ is present. Same class: all `d_i=190`, `src=2000` → VANISHES (breach=200). |
.review/results/macmac-harness-r9.codex.md:2695:.review/results/macmac-harness-r2.grok.md:20:| 10 | `otp12pf_mac_verdict.py:111` | **LOW** | Integer bar: exact 1.10 ratio is **PASS** (`10*hi <= 11*lo`). A precise 10% effect never REPRODUCES. |
.review/results/macmac-harness-r9.codex.md:2702:.review/results/macmac-prereg.codex.md:83:- `docs/bench/otp12-macmac-2026-07-14/PREREGISTRATION.md:22-34` — **BLOCKER** — H1 is mischaracterized as Windows-exclusive. The parent defines a generic resize-time accept/dial asymmetry and says controlled ablations must kill or confirm it (`OTP12_PERF_FINDINGS.md:183-209,380-399`). Mac↔Mac reproduction could be H1 itself—or an unrelated phenocopy—while rig-W remains caused by Windows. A null supports pairing dependence, not H1 specifically.

It is otp-12b's RECORDED-RUN half per docs/plan/OTP12_ACCEPTANCE_RUN.md (Active): (1) e21cf84 fixes the win-initiated sentinel - pwsh parses bare $rc:R as a scope-qualified variable so it never printed (found at first rig contact; a manual reproduction proved the win->mac path itself works); (2) 856af64 strips CRs from the drain outcome before runs.csv - a bare \r mid-row split every row under python universal newlines and verdicted 192 valid runs INCOMPLETE; (3) 44c2046 commits docs/bench/otp12-win-2026-07-12/ (README + sanitized runs.csv + the raw CRLF original + summary/verdicts/drain/manifest). CHECK HARD: (a) recompute the README's headline numbers from runs.csv (medians over valid rows, floor-of-mean-of-middle-two): the invariance table (11/12 PASS, wm_tcp_mixed 1.237), the two converge fails (push_tcp_small 2080/1811/1868; pull_tcp_mixed 1138/867/1284), the cross rows and the gap rows (old_push/old_pull vs new_mw_worst/new_wm_worst per fixture x carrier) against docs/bench/otp2w-baseline-2026-07-10/summary.csv; (b) is the CR post-processing legitimate and fully disclosed (raw file committed; no timing value altered - verify runs.csv and runs-raw-crlf.csv differ ONLY by \r bytes); (c) does the README avoid declaring pass/fail and avoid self-adjudicating the D-2026-07-12-1 residue (gap rows RECORDED; the platform-attribution language); (d) the escalation reasoning: no pre-registered trigger fires (verify the fails have tight spreads and are not straddles per the design doc D2's rule); (e) the cross-block consistency note (block-1 push_tcp_small 2080 vs block-2 mw_tcp_small mac_init 1922) - is it honestly recorded and is any conclusion improperly drawn; (f) any overclaim in calling wm_tcp_mixed/push_tcp_small code-shaped findings. Output a concise markdown findings list - each finding with file:line, severity, rationale - then a final VERDICT line. Be concise; do not invoke skills.

.review/results/macmac-harness-r8.codex.md:160:- [scripts/otp12pf_mac_verdict.py:191](/Users/michael/Dev/blit_v2/scripts/otp12pf_mac_verdict.py:191), [scripts/otp12pf_mac_verdict.py:235](/Users/michael/Dev/blit_v2/scripts/otp12pf_mac_verdict.py:235) — **BLOCKER** — controls inside `T/2` are accepted without incorporating their residual bias into the measurand threshold. With `T=100`, measurand `+100` and every control `+49` gives `REPRODUCES`, although a shared `+49` rig bias leaves only `+51<T`. Conversely, measurand `+99` and controls `−45` gives `DOES-NOT-REPRODUCE`, although bias-corrected effect could be `+144>T`. A dirty rig can flip either conclusion.
.review/results/macmac-harness-r8.codex.md:164:- [scripts/bench_otp12pf_mac.sh:839](/Users/michael/Dev/blit_v2/scripts/bench_otp12pf_mac.sh:839), [scripts/bench_otp12pf_mac.sh:1065](/Users/michael/Dev/blit_v2/scripts/bench_otp12pf_mac.sh:1065) — **HIGH** — controls are not contemporaneous. Both TCP-mixed measurands run first, followed by gRPC and large controls. A transient host×role contaminant covering a measurand block can produce a narrow EFFECT, disappear before every control and the end-load sample, and yield `REPRODUCES`. ABBA counterbalances arm order, not cell-order drift.
.review/results/macmac-harness-r8.codex.md:192:REPRODUCES
.review/results/macmac-harness-r8.codex.md:217:- [scripts/otp12pf_mac_verdict.py:191](/Users/michael/Dev/blit_v2/scripts/otp12pf_mac_verdict.py:191), [scripts/otp12pf_mac_verdict.py:235](/Users/michael/Dev/blit_v2/scripts/otp12pf_mac_verdict.py:235) — **BLOCKER** — controls inside `T/2` are accepted without incorporating their residual bias into the measurand threshold. With `T=100`, measurand `+100` and every control `+49` gives `REPRODUCES`, although a shared `+49` rig bias leaves only `+51<T`. Conversely, measurand `+99` and controls `−45` gives `DOES-NOT-REPRODUCE`, although bias-corrected effect could be `+144>T`. A dirty rig can flip either conclusion.
.review/results/macmac-harness-r8.codex.md:221:- [scripts/bench_otp12pf_mac.sh:839](/Users/michael/Dev/blit_v2/scripts/bench_otp12pf_mac.sh:839), [scripts/bench_otp12pf_mac.sh:1065](/Users/michael/Dev/blit_v2/scripts/bench_otp12pf_mac.sh:1065) — **HIGH** — controls are not contemporaneous. Both TCP-mixed measurands run first, followed by gRPC and large controls. A transient host×role contaminant covering a measurand block can produce a narrow EFFECT, disappear before every control and the end-load sample, and yield `REPRODUCES`. ABBA counterbalances arm order, not cell-order drift.

.review/results/otp-12-worker-parity.arbitration-fix.codex.md:14:Review the diff of commit 641916e (run: git show 641916e). It is the follow-up for otp-12-worker-parity after the review of 8e993aa found that tuner cooldown/sustain/direction were computed outside resize epoch arbitration and that the concurrency test depended on scheduler luck. Check that resize_tick now serializes eligibility, signal, live/target derivation, and claim coherently with accepted/refused settlement; that shape and tuner producers cannot reopen or skip epochs; that no lock spans await, socket I/O, or callbacks and no lock-order/deadlock, cancellation, teardown, tuning, or first-byte regression was introduced; that the deterministic test truly forces the accepted and refused interleavings in debug and release builds; that settlement side effects execute in optimized builds; that the exact role-independent worker target and byte/tree invariants remain protected; and that test count did not drop. Output a concise markdown findings list — each finding with file:line, severity, rationale — then a final VERDICT line. Be concise; do not invoke skills.

.review/results/otp-12-worker-parity.continuous-guard.codex.md:14:Review the diff of commit 42b9b38 (run: git show 42b9b38). It fixes the accepted LOW from the review of f7f12ec: the pre-call channel signal did not prove accepted settlement had reached resize arbitration and a guard-drop mutation remained scheduler-dependent. Check that the new test-only settlement hook reports an actual try_lock result, the guard-owned acquisition identity is tied to the MutexGuard and recorded at eligibility and epoch claim, and dropping/reacquiring the guard after the hook now fails deterministically regardless of waiter scheduling. Check that cfg(test) instrumentation cannot change production settlement behavior, release compilation remains correct, no lock-order/deadlock or hang was added, and refused settlement, tuning, cancellation, exact target-8 role tests, gated first-byte/target-4/tree invariants, and the full test count remain intact. Output a concise markdown findings list — each finding with file:line, severity, rationale — then a final VERDICT line. Be concise; do not invoke skills.

.review/results/d-2026-07-05-2-compat.codex.md:300: **2026-07-03 16:26:55Z** - **CODER (ue-r2-1c, claude)**: Landed the engine shell (REV4 `ue-r2-1c`, third slice through the code→GPT-review→fix loop). New `crates/blit-core/src/engine/` module: `TransferEngine::execute(EngineRequest)` owns strategy selection (single-file → journal probe → fast-path walk → streaming) and the streaming leg (tuning window → scan → diff/plan → sink pipeline → mirror deletions → journal checkpoints → history/predictor); `TransferOrchestrator` shrank to the local adapter (preconditions, `FsTransferSource`/`FilteredSource` + `FsTransferSink`/`NullSink` construction, option translation) with the public API preserved via re-exports — engine type thereby ratified per REV4 Design §1 (new engine + adapter, not a rename). Sequence: behavior pins first (`7730eb1`: NoWork/UpToDate + `no_work` tag, NoWork/SourceEmpty — both previously untested), the move (`dc9b0ed`: `fast_path.rs`→`engine/strategy.rs`, options/summary/history moved, tuning/mirror/journal/single-file split out; move fidelity machine-checked — 75 diff lines vs origin, all six documented transformations, zero drift), then the REV4-named accounting gap (`29e210b`: single-file strategy now records perf history tag `single_file` / `null_sink` lane + predictor, guard test proven by revert). Review: first codex run died on provider quota (121k tokens, recorded); owner restored access same day; retry PASS with 1 Low, accepted + fixed (`15e6334`: engine back-references through `crate::orchestrator` → engine-local paths; swept one more the reviewer missed). Also recorded owner-provided 10GbE test env (`admin@skippy:/mnt/generic-pool/video/test`) in STATE queue item 4. Validation: fmt/clippy clean, `cargo test --workspace` 1394 / 0 / 2 (baseline 1391, +3). All on master, unpushed. Next: `ue-r2-1d` (streaming plan foundation) — or `1e`/w4-1 per queue.
.review/results/d-2026-07-05-2-compat.codex.md:310: **2026-06-12 16:32:00Z** - **REVIEW (gemini-reviewer)**: Graded the two pending sentinels on `master` sequentially: **design-4-fallback-midmanifest-negotiation** (`ddfeb58`) accepted (daemon early-flush branch is now TCP-only; client-side fallback_negotiated flag added to hold need-list/manifest fallback payloads in pending_queue; 2000-file regression test passes) and **design-5-send-failure-masks-rejection** (`08d71a2`) accepted (prefer_server_error helper harvests daemon's terminal status on manifest-phase send failures; 500-file readonly_enforcement regression test passes repeatedly). Both verified.json verdicts created, sentinels deleted via git rm, and REVIEW.md status rows updated to `[x]`. Validation suite re-run: cargo fmt, clippy, and cargo test --workspace all passed successfully (1370 passed, 0 failed, 1 ignored).
.review/results/d-2026-07-05-2-compat.codex.md:318: **2026-06-12 03:55:00Z** - **CODER (autonomous overnight session)**: Owner authorized autonomous queue execution ("work on as much as you can. commit every slice as it lands. if anything gets questionable, stop." — scope: master only, no branches, local commits, never push, faster-mode WIP iff files-changed disjoint across pending sentinels; recorded in STATE.md). **7 slices completed and sentineled** (18 local commits, `1adbe0c..0af904e`): **w5-1-log-backend** (`56bda09`+`7145202`) — new `blit_core::stderr_log` backend (warn default, `BLIT_LOG` override, `<binary>: <level>: <msg>`) installed in all 4 binaries; ~22 formerly-discarded log::warn/error sites now visible; 9 bracketed stderr prefix families converged (warnings→facade, operator lines→`blit:`/`blitd:` prefixes, per-file pipeline spam→log::debug); trace-gated `[data-plane-client]` family kept (remote_parity.rs asserts the prefix); jobs-watch ticker vocabulary kept (single-command status output). **w9-5-jobs-lifecycle-e2e** (`ad773d8`) — first e2e net for detach/jobs verbs: 5 tests incl. cancel-active-delegated-job exit-0 made deterministic via a fake tonic source whose pull_sync never answers. **w2-1-delete-warmup-machinery** (`2a8a490`) — analyze_warmup_result + determine_tuning + warmup_gbps deleted; determine_remote_tuning is one honest static table (production values bit-identical); 4 tier tests added. **w9-1-ungate-windows-tests** (`9324559`) — 27 blanket #[cfg(unix)] removed from 8 remote-transfer test files (none unix-specific); 4 genuinely unix files kept gated; Windows CI will now actually compile parity-critical paths. **w9-2-revive-root-tests** (`461525d`) — dead root tests/ relocated into crates/blit-core/tests (+24 tests incl. the only MirrorPlanner coverage); connection.rs deleted; drift fixed (for_tests→load(), compare_mode, 32→300 files vs TINY_FILE_LIMIT=256, serial Mutex for config-dir globals); AGENTS.md §4 corrected. **w9-4-readonly-enforcement-tests** (`4d67210`) — TestContext::new_read_only() knob + 3 e2e tests covering all 3 read-only write gates (push/purge/delegated-pull), asserting error text + no destination mutation. **w8-1b-zero-copy-fast-eval** (`6189d82`) — analysis-only: `docs/plan/ZERO_COPY_RECEIVE_EVAL.md` (Draft) recommends DELETING zero_copy.rs (wire path is splice-friendly but the dead impl busy-waits on EAGAIN, the AsyncRead-generic pipeline resists raw-fd wiring, and the CPU win is unmeasured pending the 10GbE rig); owner verdict is the gate. **Suite: 1331 → 1368 tests, fmt/clippy/test green after every slice.** Remaining `[ ]` rows are all overlap-blocked behind the 7 pending sentinels (skip map in STATE.md), owner-gated (w2-3 plan interview), or dependent (w2-4, w6-2, w8-1). Transient note: first post-w5-1 workspace test run showed 11 "daemon failed to listen" failures under cold parallel load; isolated and clean re-runs green — same harness-contention class w9-3 addresses.

.review/results/d-2026-07-05-2-compat.codex.md:1046:docs/audit/inventory/plan-principles.md:764:> `BENCHMARK_10GBE_PLAN.md`'s NFS/SMB-mount, daemon-pair, and reverse-direction phases all need 10GbE hardware. Do them post-release when hardware is available; 0.1.0's release notes note "10+ Gbps benchmarking pending hardware access."
.review/results/d-2026-07-05-2-compat.codex.md:1097:docs/audit/inventory/plan-perf.md:816:**Description**: The bench output includes `streams` (data-plane stream count) and `tcp_fallback_used: bool`, modeling TCP↔gRPC fallback as a binary toggle. The 10GbE plan instead frames "TCP vs gRPC fallback: TCP should be 2-5× faster" as a measurement question. These are consistent in spirit but slightly mismatched: the verb plan presumes a single negotiated path per run, while the 10GbE plan implies separate runs to compare paths. Minor.

.review/results/otp-12-worker-parity.codex.md:14:Review the diff of commit a76b785 (run: git show a76b785). It implements otp-12-worker-parity under docs/plan/ONE_TRANSFER_PATH.md and .review/findings/otp-12-worker-parity.md: both initiator orientations must settle the same shape-derived stream target, and receiver max_streams=0 must mean unknown rather than a role-specific one-stream cap. Check correctness regressions, the acceptance criteria, FAST/SIMPLE/RELIABLE, byte-identical/StallGuard/cancellation/byte-accounting invariants, and that the test count did not drop. Specifically inspect deadlock or starvation risks while settling resize epochs, handling of resize refusal and concurrent SOURCE events, and performance-regression risk from settling before payload dispatch. Output a concise markdown findings list, each finding with file:line, severity, rationale, then a final VERDICT line. Be concise; do not invoke skills.

.review/results/otp-12-worker-parity.codex.md:8948:.review/findings/otp-12-worker-parity.md-4-requires one sender-owned, receiver-bounded stream policy for both role

.review/results/otp-12-worker-parity.codex.md:8950:.review/findings/otp-12-worker-parity.md-6-
.review/results/otp-12-worker-parity.codex.md:8951:.review/findings/otp-12-worker-parity.md-7-## What
.review/results/otp-12-worker-parity.codex.md:8952:.review/findings/otp-12-worker-parity.md-8-
.review/results/otp-12-worker-parity.codex.md:8953:.review/findings/otp-12-worker-parity.md-9-The unified session computed the same shape target in both orientations but
.review/results/otp-12-worker-parity.codex.md:8954:.review/findings/otp-12-worker-parity.md-10-did not guarantee reaching it. Resize advances one stream per epoch. Once
.review/results/otp-12-worker-parity.codex.md:8955:.review/findings/otp-12-worker-parity.md-11-`NeedComplete` arrived, the SOURCE resolved only the one epoch already in
.review/results/otp-12-worker-parity.codex.md:8956:.review/findings/otp-12-worker-parity.md-12-flight and stopped proposing. On the same 10,000-file fixture (shape target
.review/results/otp-12-worker-parity.codex.md:8957:.review/findings/otp-12-worker-parity.md-13-8), the source-initiator test settled at 3 streams and the
.review/results/otp-12-worker-parity.codex.md:8958:.review/findings/otp-12-worker-parity.md-14-destination-initiator test at 2.
.review/results/otp-12-worker-parity.codex.md:8959:.review/findings/otp-12-worker-parity.md-15-
.review/results/otp-12-worker-parity.codex.md:8960:.review/findings/otp-12-worker-parity.md-16-The destination-initiator admission side also interpreted an advertised
.review/results/otp-12-worker-parity.codex.md:8961:.review/findings/otp-12-worker-parity.md-17-`max_streams = 0` as a one-stream ceiling, while the SOURCE dial correctly
.review/results/otp-12-worker-parity.codex.md:8962:.review/findings/otp-12-worker-parity.md-18-interpreted the wire value as unknown/default. That was a role-specific cap.
.review/results/otp-12-worker-parity.codex.md:8963:.review/findings/otp-12-worker-parity.md-19-
.review/results/otp-12-worker-parity.codex.md:8964:.review/findings/otp-12-worker-parity.md-20-## Approach
.review/results/otp-12-worker-parity.codex.md:8965:.review/findings/otp-12-worker-parity.md-21-
.review/results/otp-12-worker-parity.codex.md:8966:.review/findings/otp-12-worker-parity.md:22:- Before each payload batch enters the shared elastic send pipeline, drive
.review/results/otp-12-worker-parity.codex.md:8967:.review/findings/otp-12-worker-parity.md-23-  the existing one-stream-per-epoch resize protocol until the currently known
.review/results/otp-12-worker-parity.codex.md:8968:.review/findings/otp-12-worker-parity.md-24-  shape target is settled. Needs and resume hashes continue to be processed
.review/results/otp-12-worker-parity.codex.md:8969:.review/findings/otp-12-worker-parity.md-25-  while acknowledgements are in flight, so the target incorporates all work
.review/results/otp-12-worker-parity.codex.md:8970:.review/findings/otp-12-worker-parity.md-26-  learned during the ramp.
.review/results/otp-12-worker-parity.codex.md:8971:.review/findings/otp-12-worker-parity.md-27-- Stop a refused ramp instead of retrying the same unattainable target under
.review/results/otp-12-worker-parity.codex.md:8972:.review/findings/otp-12-worker-parity.md-28-  fresh epochs forever.
.review/results/otp-12-worker-parity.codex.md:8973:.review/findings/otp-12-worker-parity.md-29-- Centralize receiver stream-ceiling resolution in `dial.rs` and use it on
.review/results/otp-12-worker-parity.codex.md:8974:.review/findings/otp-12-worker-parity.md-30-  both the SOURCE dial and destination-initiator admission path. Wire value
.review/results/otp-12-worker-parity.codex.md:8975:.review/findings/otp-12-worker-parity.md-31-  zero remains unknown/default, never one.
.review/results/otp-12-worker-parity.codex.md:8976:.review/findings/otp-12-worker-parity.md-32-- Strengthen both role-orientation integration pins from merely `> 1` to the
.review/results/otp-12-worker-parity.codex.md:8978:.review/findings/otp-12-worker-parity.md-49-  `transfer_session_roles` integration target passes 39/39.
.review/results/otp-12-worker-parity.codex.md:8979:.review/findings/otp-12-worker-parity.md-50-- Full workspace gate passes: `cargo fmt --all -- --check`,
.review/results/otp-12-worker-parity.codex.md:8980:.review/findings/otp-12-worker-parity.md-51-  `cargo clippy --workspace --all-targets -- -D warnings`, and
.review/results/otp-12-worker-parity.codex.md:8981:.review/findings/otp-12-worker-parity.md-52-  `cargo test --workspace` (1488 tests, 2 ignored; no failures).
.review/results/otp-12-worker-parity.codex.md:8982:.review/findings/otp-12-worker-parity.md-53-
.review/results/otp-12-worker-parity.codex.md:8983:.review/findings/otp-12-worker-parity.md-54-## Known gaps
.review/results/otp-12-worker-parity.codex.md:8984:.review/findings/otp-12-worker-parity.md-55-
.review/results/otp-12-worker-parity.codex.md:8985:.review/findings/otp-12-worker-parity.md-56-- Socket acquisition remains connection-role-specific by design: the network
.review/results/otp-12-worker-parity.codex.md:8986:.review/findings/otp-12-worker-parity.md-57-  initiator dials the responder so a pull caller does not need an inbound
.review/results/otp-12-worker-parity.codex.md:8987:.review/findings/otp-12-worker-parity.md-58-  listener through NAT/firewalls. Byte work is still one SOURCE send pipeline
.review/results/otp-12-worker-parity.codex.md:8988:.review/findings/otp-12-worker-parity.md:59:  and one DESTINATION receive pipeline. This slice removes worker-count drift;
.review/results/otp-12-worker-parity.codex.md:8989:.review/findings/otp-12-worker-parity.md-60-  it does not invert that network topology.
.review/results/otp-12-worker-parity.codex.md:8990:.review/findings/otp-12-worker-parity.md:61:- No hardware benchmark is part of this code slice. The existing otp-12
.review/results/otp-12-worker-parity.codex.md:8991:.review/findings/otp-12-worker-parity.md-62-  acceptance rigs remain the performance proof after review.
.review/results/otp-12-worker-parity.codex.md:8992:.review/findings/otp-12-worker-parity.md-63-

.review/results/otp-12-worker-parity.codex.md:9142:docs/STATE.md-3-Last updated: 2026-07-15 (52nd handoff — round 11 fixed + round-12 consensus: P1 IS REAL, the Mac↔Mac run is parked; owner to pick direction)
.review/results/otp-12-worker-parity.codex.md:9146:docs/STATE.md-7-  - **BOTH REVIEWERS: the Mac↔Mac run is NOT the next move — no outcome of it changes the release-critical action.** It answers only "can P1 occur without a Windows peer?", and every outcome still routes to fixing P1 on the pair where it lives (macOS↔Windows). Grok's power analysis: with four independent full-range controls that must ALL be clean and rig W's fast arm known-bimodal, the *most likely* successful outcome is `CONTROLS-NOT-CLEAN` — a re-run, not an answer.
.review/results/otp-12-worker-parity.codex.md:9147:docs/STATE.md-8-  - **THE DECISION FOR THE OWNER**: (1) **instrument the TCP dial/accept transfer path on rig W** — both reviewers' recommendation; P1 is now pinned to TCP + destination-initiated + mixed, so add timing spans to `SourceSockets` Dial/Accept, `add_dialed_stream`, the dial-before-ACK at `transfer_session/mod.rs:3113`; the fastest route to a fix. Or (2) **run Mac↔Mac anyway** for the 2×2 map — instrument is READY (BLOCKER closed and proved: pointing it at the 1GbE NIC trips three independent gates), but see the consensus above. **No agent may pick this; owner call.**
.review/results/otp-12-worker-parity.codex.md:9148:docs/STATE.md-9-  - **The Mac↔Mac instrument is DONE and REVIEWED** — engine 40 cases / 19 mutations, harness self-test 0-blind on both Macs, fabric gates proved by mutation. If run: nagatha↔`q`, 10GbE MTU 9000, build `f35702a` (nagatha's worktree + build were MISSING and were rebuilt this session), both Macs codex-quiet and Time Machine off. Host facts: `.agents/machines.md`.
.review/results/otp-12-worker-parity.codex.md:9149:docs/STATE.md-10-  - **⚠ ROUND-12 STILL-OPEN correctness findings (real, not yet fixed — apply before any Mac↔Mac run):** the threshold `min(src/10, 230)` can report `REPRODUCES` on a cell whose ratio (1.092) *passes* the 1.10 bar (codex BLOCKER — the `min` gives EITHER standard, the prose says BOTH); the end-fabric gate re-checks MSS/IP but **not link speed** (a 10GbE→1GbE renegotiation keeping MTU 9000 grades — my own duplicate-site bug); the `B ≥ T/2` refusal guards only the positive margin, not the smaller `src/11` negative one; two mutations "kill" for the wrong reason. Detail in `macmac-r12.codex-design.md`.
.review/results/otp-12-worker-parity.codex.md:9155:docs/STATE.md-16-- **P1 REPRODUCES ON A SECOND MAC (2026-07-13, `docs/bench/otp12-q-baseline-2026-07-13/`).** `wm_tcp_mixed` = **1.385 FAIL** on `q`↔netwatch-01 **at MTU 9000**, while all three controls PASS at **1.002–1.043** in the same session (so rig noise is ~2–4% and P1 is 10× outside it). **P1 is a property of the macOS↔Windows PAIRING, not of one machine** — the assumption **H1** rests on (corrected 2026-07-14: H5/H6/H7 are **P2** hypotheses; the earlier "H1/H5/H6/H7" was wrong), never tested until now. **And jumbo does NOT dissolve P1** — pf-0 has now measured the matched 1500 arm and killed MTU outright (above).
.review/results/otp-12-worker-parity.codex.md:9158:docs/STATE.md-19-- **P1 (the headline invariance criterion) — the one thing between blit and shipping.** Fails rig W (`wm_tcp_mixed` 1.237 and 1.300 — do NOT read that as a regression, it is **two different Mac NICs**), but **PASSES 8/8 with Linux on both ends** (`docs/bench/otp12-perf-2026-07-13/`; P1's own cell 1.092/1.003). So it is **platform-INTERACTING, not pure layout** — yet **NOT exonerated**: a code path that only bites on one platform (H1's Windows accept branch) looks identical. **P1 HAS NO ESCAPE HATCH** (codex r5 F1): D-2026-07-12-1 waives only a *cross-direction* miss for a cell that ALREADY passes invariance — P1 *is* the invariance failure. So: **fix it to ≤1.10, or the owner amends acceptance criterion 1.** Neither is assumed.

Suite **1488 as of `bb28ddd`** — the last commit to touch `crates/`+`proto/`; every commit since is docs/scripts, so the count stands unre-run. SMALL_FILE_CEILING paused (D-2026-07-05-1).
.review/results/otp-12-worker-parity.codex.md:12400:.review/results/otp-12-worker-parity.codex.md:9300:REVIEW.md:73:| otp-10c-2 | The cutover deletion — otp-10c CLOSED, one transfer path by construction: the four drivers (`remote/pull.rs` 2574 LOC, `remote/push/`, daemon `service/push/`, `service/pull_sync.rs`), `rpc Push` + `rpc PullSync` + 13 exclusive messages (incl. `DataTransferNegotiation`, the old summaries, `metadata_only`), the two wire-specific gRPC fallback sinks + `grpc_fallback.rs`, and every helper whose only callers died — out of tree AND proto, no bridge (D-2026-07-05-2). Relocated verbatim: the delegated spec builder (`DelegatedSpecOptions`/`delegated_spec_from_options` → operation_spec.rs) + `FsTransferSource`'s fs-scan helpers. A/B parity pins → absolute tree+count pins; DelegatedPull no-payload-bytes proof recorded (proto oneof + CLI byte-counter pins). Codex NEEDS FIXES → 6/6 accepted (F6 owner-gated): spec capability/capacity fields + `PeerCapabilities` deleted (orphaned since otp-9b); 5 more orphaned helpers out; the relocated builder re-pinned (7 tests) + `mirror_delete_pass` containment wiring pinned — both mutation-proven; `docs/API.md` (never swept) + 4 more doc/comment sites fixed; `w6-2b` re-scoped to the served-session dispatcher; the tracked `.claude/worktrees` snapshot deferred to the standing `725aa07` owner question. Suite 1586 → 1480 (106 retirements, all enumerated in the finding doc) → **1488** | `[x]` | `7aac28b` + review fixes `995e1cc` |
.review/results/otp-12-worker-parity.codex.md:12401:.review/results/otp-12-worker-parity.codex.md:9302:REVIEW.md:75:| otp-11a | Local transfers ride the session — the local route (`docs/plan/OTP11_LOCAL_SESSION.md` D1–D3): `run_local_session` joins both role drivers over `in_process_pair`; the LOCAL byte-carrier = process-local `LocalApply` (crate-private, NO wire shape — a peer structurally cannot select it): the destination plans (`plan_transfer_payloads`) and applies needs in-process through `FsTransferSink` — clonefile/block-clone/copy_file_range kept, `execute_sink_pipeline_streaming` stays live as the apply pipeline; `blit_app transfers/local.rs` chokepoint re-pointed (CLI+TUI call sites untouched, all verb pins green incl. the 3 move data-loss regression pins); ONE diff core both carriers (`diff_chunk_verdicts`); mirror = the in-session delete rule + apply-time unreadable guard (old R46-F2 posture, vanishing-source pin) + plan-only dry-run + split (files,dirs) counts; sink file-root File-payload ENOTDIR fix. Design-doc codex CHANGES REQUIRED → 10 findings adjudicated (3 already fixed in the slice; doc amended — D1 carrier delta stated, floor redone: 11b needs ≈+44 real pins); slice codex FAIL → 9 findings: 7 accepted+fixed, 1 doc defect (outcome parity gate kept), 1 rejected-as-regression (diff batching is session-uniform; overlap pin ports at 11b). A/B perf gate: huge/tree/small PASS (1 GiB single file 22 ms BOTH sides — clone preserved); focused noop10k surfaced the journal-skip retirement cost (~21 ms warm-journal vs ~219 ms full diff; beats the old non-journal pass at 610 ms) — OWNER question, blocks 11b per the slice doc's gate rule. Suite 1488 → 1510 → **1512**; 4 mutation guard proofs. **Addendum (owner: "neither option passes — figure out a real fix"): the old journal fast path proven UNSOUND** — `NoChanges` decays to root-dir mtime equality; deep modifications silently never synced (reproduced vs the `d2bd843` binary, transcript in the bench README); no-op cell re-baselined sound-vs-sound (session 2.8× faster) → gate PASSES, 11b unblocked (its journal deletion removes a data-loss bug); pin `deep_modification_after_warm_runs_syncs` (suite → **1513**); sound journal REPLAY filed as future session capability (slice doc D3). Addendum codex CHANGES REQUESTED → core verdict CONFIRMED (data loss real, no validation layer, Windows fallback also unsound, pin guards the shape); 4/4 record findings fixed — sound baseline re-certified by 5-run medians with the old journal cache cleared per run (old 507 ms vs session 226 ms = 2.2×, gate PASS), STATE summary line, floor redone from 1513 (≈+41), Linux ctime-arm mechanism precision. | `[x]` | design `0da65d6`+`c7b463b`; slice `dfdddd6` + review fixes `e445e8d`; bench `631255b`; addendum `d74c1ac`+`4148705` + review fixes (see verdict) |
.review/results/otp-12-worker-parity.codex.md:12880:Last updated: 2026-07-15 (52nd handoff — round 11 fixed + round-12 consensus: P1 IS REAL, the Mac↔Mac run is parked; owner to pick direction)
.review/results/otp-12-worker-parity.codex.md:12884:  - **BOTH REVIEWERS: the Mac↔Mac run is NOT the next move — no outcome of it changes the release-critical action.** It answers only "can P1 occur without a Windows peer?", and every outcome still routes to fixing P1 on the pair where it lives (macOS↔Windows). Grok's power analysis: with four independent full-range controls that must ALL be clean and rig W's fast arm known-bimodal, the *most likely* successful outcome is `CONTROLS-NOT-CLEAN` — a re-run, not an answer.
.review/results/otp-12-worker-parity.codex.md:12885:  - **THE DECISION FOR THE OWNER**: (1) **instrument the TCP dial/accept transfer path on rig W** — both reviewers' recommendation; P1 is now pinned to TCP + destination-initiated + mixed, so add timing spans to `SourceSockets` Dial/Accept, `add_dialed_stream`, the dial-before-ACK at `transfer_session/mod.rs:3113`; the fastest route to a fix. Or (2) **run Mac↔Mac anyway** for the 2×2 map — instrument is READY (BLOCKER closed and proved: pointing it at the 1GbE NIC trips three independent gates), but see the consensus above. **No agent may pick this; owner call.**
.review/results/otp-12-worker-parity.codex.md:12886:  - **The Mac↔Mac instrument is DONE and REVIEWED** — engine 40 cases / 19 mutations, harness self-test 0-blind on both Macs, fabric gates proved by mutation. If run: nagatha↔`q`, 10GbE MTU 9000, build `f35702a` (nagatha's worktree + build were MISSING and were rebuilt this session), both Macs codex-quiet and Time Machine off. Host facts: `.agents/machines.md`.
.review/results/otp-12-worker-parity.codex.md:12887:  - **⚠ ROUND-12 STILL-OPEN correctness findings (real, not yet fixed — apply before any Mac↔Mac run):** the threshold `min(src/10, 230)` can report `REPRODUCES` on a cell whose ratio (1.092) *passes* the 1.10 bar (codex BLOCKER — the `min` gives EITHER standard, the prose says BOTH); the end-fabric gate re-checks MSS/IP but **not link speed** (a 10GbE→1GbE renegotiation keeping MTU 9000 grades — my own duplicate-site bug); the `B ≥ T/2` refusal guards only the positive margin, not the smaller `src/11` negative one; two mutations "kill" for the wrong reason. Detail in `macmac-r12.codex-design.md`.
.review/results/otp-12-worker-parity.codex.md:12893:- **P1 REPRODUCES ON A SECOND MAC (2026-07-13, `docs/bench/otp12-q-baseline-2026-07-13/`).** `wm_tcp_mixed` = **1.385 FAIL** on `q`↔netwatch-01 **at MTU 9000**, while all three controls PASS at **1.002–1.043** in the same session (so rig noise is ~2–4% and P1 is 10× outside it). **P1 is a property of the macOS↔Windows PAIRING, not of one machine** — the assumption **H1** rests on (corrected 2026-07-14: H5/H6/H7 are **P2** hypotheses; the earlier "H1/H5/H6/H7" was wrong), never tested until now. **And jumbo does NOT dissolve P1** — pf-0 has now measured the matched 1500 arm and killed MTU outright (above).
.review/results/otp-12-worker-parity.codex.md:12896:- **P1 (the headline invariance criterion) — the one thing between blit and shipping.** Fails rig W (`wm_tcp_mixed` 1.237 and 1.300 — do NOT read that as a regression, it is **two different Mac NICs**), but **PASSES 8/8 with Linux on both ends** (`docs/bench/otp12-perf-2026-07-13/`; P1's own cell 1.092/1.003). So it is **platform-INTERACTING, not pure layout** — yet **NOT exonerated**: a code path that only bites on one platform (H1's Windows accept branch) looks identical. **P1 HAS NO ESCAPE HATCH** (codex r5 F1): D-2026-07-12-1 waives only a *cross-direction* miss for a cell that ALREADY passes invariance — P1 *is* the invariance failure. So: **fix it to ≤1.10, or the owner amends acceptance criterion 1.** Neither is assumed.
.review/results/otp-12-worker-parity.codex.md:12959:   2026-07-13). Linux↔Linux = **no P1** (8/8 PASS); macOS↔Windows = **P1**

.review/results/otp-5b-2-pull-data-plane-resize.codex.md:5958:DEVLOG.md:80:**2026-07-03 16:26:55Z** - **CODER (ue-r2-1c, claude)**: Landed the engine shell (REV4 `ue-r2-1c`, third slice through the code→GPT-review→fix loop). New `crates/blit-core/src/engine/` module: `TransferEngine::execute(EngineRequest)` owns strategy selection (single-file → journal probe → fast-path walk → streaming) and the streaming leg (tuning window → scan → diff/plan → sink pipeline → mirror deletions → journal checkpoints → history/predictor); `TransferOrchestrator` shrank to the local adapter (preconditions, `FsTransferSource`/`FilteredSource` + `FsTransferSink`/`NullSink` construction, option translation) with the public API preserved via re-exports — engine type thereby ratified per REV4 Design §1 (new engine + adapter, not a rename). Sequence: behavior pins first (`7730eb1`: NoWork/UpToDate + `no_work` tag, NoWork/SourceEmpty — both previously untested), the move (`dc9b0ed`: `fast_path.rs`→`engine/strategy.rs`, options/summary/history moved, tuning/mirror/journal/single-file split out; move fidelity machine-checked — 75 diff lines vs origin, all six documented transformations, zero drift), then the REV4-named accounting gap (`29e210b`: single-file strategy now records perf history tag `single_file` / `null_sink` lane + predictor, guard test proven by revert). Review: first codex run died on provider quota (121k tokens, recorded); owner restored access same day; retry PASS with 1 Low, accepted + fixed (`15e6334`: engine back-references through `crate::orchestrator` → engine-local paths; swept one more the reviewer missed). Also recorded owner-provided 10GbE test env (`admin@skippy:/mnt/generic-pool/video/test`) in STATE queue item 4. Validation: fmt/clippy clean, `cargo test --workspace` 1394 / 0 / 2 (baseline 1391, +3). All on master, unpushed. Next: `ue-r2-1d` (streaming plan foundation) — or `1e`/w4-1 per queue.
.review/results/otp-5b-2-pull-data-plane-resize.codex.md:5962:DEVLOG.md:90:**2026-06-12 16:32:00Z** - **REVIEW (gemini-reviewer)**: Graded the two pending sentinels on `master` sequentially: **design-4-fallback-midmanifest-negotiation** (`ddfeb58`) accepted (daemon early-flush branch is now TCP-only; client-side fallback_negotiated flag added to hold need-list/manifest fallback payloads in pending_queue; 2000-file regression test passes) and **design-5-send-failure-masks-rejection** (`08d71a2`) accepted (prefer_server_error helper harvests daemon's terminal status on manifest-phase send failures; 500-file readonly_enforcement regression test passes repeatedly). Both verified.json verdicts created, sentinels deleted via git rm, and REVIEW.md status rows updated to `[x]`. Validation suite re-run: cargo fmt, clippy, and cargo test --workspace all passed successfully (1370 passed, 0 failed, 1 ignored).
.review/results/otp-5b-2-pull-data-plane-resize.codex.md:5966:DEVLOG.md:98:**2026-06-12 03:55:00Z** - **CODER (autonomous overnight session)**: Owner authorized autonomous queue execution ("work on as much as you can. commit every slice as it lands. if anything gets questionable, stop." — scope: master only, no branches, local commits, never push, faster-mode WIP iff files-changed disjoint across pending sentinels; recorded in STATE.md). **7 slices completed and sentineled** (18 local commits, `1adbe0c..0af904e`): **w5-1-log-backend** (`56bda09`+`7145202`) — new `blit_core::stderr_log` backend (warn default, `BLIT_LOG` override, `<binary>: <level>: <msg>`) installed in all 4 binaries; ~22 formerly-discarded log::warn/error sites now visible; 9 bracketed stderr prefix families converged (warnings→facade, operator lines→`blit:`/`blitd:` prefixes, per-file pipeline spam→log::debug); trace-gated `[data-plane-client]` family kept (remote_parity.rs asserts the prefix); jobs-watch ticker vocabulary kept (single-command status output). **w9-5-jobs-lifecycle-e2e** (`ad773d8`) — first e2e net for detach/jobs verbs: 5 tests incl. cancel-active-delegated-job exit-0 made deterministic via a fake tonic source whose pull_sync never answers. **w2-1-delete-warmup-machinery** (`2a8a490`) — analyze_warmup_result + determine_tuning + warmup_gbps deleted; determine_remote_tuning is one honest static table (production values bit-identical); 4 tier tests added. **w9-1-ungate-windows-tests** (`9324559`) — 27 blanket #[cfg(unix)] removed from 8 remote-transfer test files (none unix-specific); 4 genuinely unix files kept gated; Windows CI will now actually compile parity-critical paths. **w9-2-revive-root-tests** (`461525d`) — dead root tests/ relocated into crates/blit-core/tests (+24 tests incl. the only MirrorPlanner coverage); connection.rs deleted; drift fixed (for_tests→load(), compare_mode, 32→300 files vs TINY_FILE_LIMIT=256, serial Mutex for config-dir globals); AGENTS.md §4 corrected. **w9-4-readonly-enforcement-tests** (`4d67210`) — TestContext::new_read_only() knob + 3 e2e tests covering all 3 read-only write gates (push/purge/delegated-pull), asserting error text + no destination mutation. **w8-1b-zero-copy-fast-eval** (`6189d82`) — analysis-only: `docs/plan/ZERO_COPY_RECEIVE_EVAL.md` (Draft) recommends DELETING zero_copy.rs (wire path is splice-friendly but the dead impl busy-waits on EAGAIN, the AsyncRead-generic pipeline resists raw-fd wiring, and the CPU win is unmeasured pending the 10GbE rig); owner verdict is the gate. **Suite: 1331 → 1368 tests, fmt/clippy/test green after every slice.** Remaining `[ ]` rows are all overlap-blocked behind the 7 pending sentinels (skip map in STATE.md), owner-gated (w2-3 plan interview), or dependent (w2-4, w6-2, w8-1). Transient note: first post-w5-1 workspace test run showed 11 "daemon failed to listen" failures under cold parallel load; isolated and clean re-runs green — same harness-contention class w9-3 addresses.
.review/results/otp-5b-2-pull-data-plane-resize.codex.md:6400:.review/results/otp-5b-source-responder-data-plane.codex.md:1490:@@ -1482,8 +1522,9 @@ async fn drive_destination(

.review/results/otp-12-worker-parity.records.codex.md:14:Review the diff of commit 316b11b (run: git show 316b11b). It records closure of otp-12 worker parity after code commit 42b9b38 and replaces stale current-state claims left by the prior session. Check internal coherence against AGENTS.md, docs/DECISIONS.md, docs/plan/ONE_TRANSFER_PATH.md, the actual transfer architecture/tests, all referenced review artifacts/commit hashes, and the live Mac-to-Mac verdict/harness source. Specifically verify it does not overclaim hardware acceptance or Mac-to-Mac data; accurately distinguishes SOURCE/DESTINATION byte roles from initiator/responder socket topology; accurately records exact 8/8 parity and 1,490/2 validation; and has evidence for the two stated measurement blockers (1.092 PASS also classified REPRODUCES, end gate omits link speed). Check STATE remains under its cap with no current contradictions, REVIEW/DEVLOG are accurate, and docs checks pass. Output a concise markdown findings list — each finding with file:line, severity, rationale — then a final VERDICT line. Be concise; do not invoke skills.

.review/results/otp-12-worker-parity.records.codex.md:241:+# otp-12-worker-parity continuous-guard review — adjudication
.review/results/otp-12-worker-parity.records.codex.md:243:+**Slice**: `42b9b38` — scheduler-independent resize guard proof.
.review/results/otp-12-worker-parity.records.codex.md:245:+**Raw review**: `.review/results/otp-12-worker-parity.continuous-guard.codex.md`
