I’m now checking whether its proposed local Mac↔Mac experiment can actually distinguish layout from host/network effects, and whether every acceptance arm is tied to a final-build measurement.
.review/results/otp-12-worker-parity.records.codex.md:608:.review/results/otp12-perf-findings-r4.codex.md:121:- **HIGH — [OTP12_PERF_FINDINGS.md:86](/Users/michael/Dev/blit_v2/docs/plan/OTP12_PERF_FINDINGS.md:86): the confound repair is partly cosmetic.** Mac↔Mac loopback removes the real NIC/RTT and separate-host/disk interaction. A positive result is suggestive; a negative proves nothing. Falling back to rig W preserves the Windows-source-accept/Mac-destination-dial alias. Although H1 describes both halves, it attributes the suspect to `Accept` without a causal counterfactual separating the synchronous dial-before-ACK path ([mod.rs:3113](/Users/michael/Dev/blit_v2/crates/blit-core/src/transfer_session/mod.rs:3113)). All three fixtures also target eight streams before clamping ([dial.rs:474](/Users/michael/Dev/blit_v2/crates/blit-core/src/dial.rs:474)), so resize count alone cannot explain mixed-only behavior. Lines 411–413 retain the stale destination-initiator overclaim.
.review/results/otp-12-worker-parity.records.codex.md:609:.review/results/otp12-perf-findings-r4.codex.md:138:- **HIGH — [OTP12_PERF_FINDINGS.md:86](/Users/michael/Dev/blit_v2/docs/plan/OTP12_PERF_FINDINGS.md:86): the confound repair is partly cosmetic.** Mac↔Mac loopback removes the real NIC/RTT and separate-host/disk interaction. A positive result is suggestive; a negative proves nothing. Falling back to rig W preserves the Windows-source-accept/Mac-destination-dial alias. Although H1 describes both halves, it attributes the suspect to `Accept` without a causal counterfactual separating the synchronous dial-before-ACK path ([mod.rs:3113](/Users/michael/Dev/blit_v2/crates/blit-core/src/transfer_session/mod.rs:3113)). All three fixtures also target eight streams before clamping ([dial.rs:474](/Users/michael/Dev/blit_v2/crates/blit-core/src/dial.rs:474)), so resize count alone cannot explain mixed-only behavior. Lines 411–413 retain the stale destination-initiator overclaim.
.review/results/otp-12-worker-parity.records.codex.md:610:.review/results/otp12-perf-findings-r5.codex.md:18:2. THE CORRECTED RESULT (docs/bench/otp12-perf-2026-07-13/, harness scripts/bench_otp12pf_linux.sh, full otp-12 methodology — cold caches BOTH ends, destination drained, ABBA, pair-void, RUNS=4, 64 runs, 8/8 cells, zero voided): 8/8 invariance cells PASS. P1 own cell = 1.092 (sm_tcp_mixed) and 1.003 (ms_tcp_mixed). ms_grpc_mixed failed 1.392 at RUNS=4 on 25-37% spreads, tripped the D2 escalation trigger, and passed at 1.063 on RUNS=8. So P1 does NOT reproduce with Linux on both ends, and the plan now says P1 is platform-INTERACTING, not pure layout.

.review/results/otp-12-worker-parity.records.codex.md:656:.review/results/p1-adjudication-r1.codex.md:2805:- **BLOCKER — `min()` implements the opposite of the stated policy.** The document says an effect must matter by both the 10% bar and the 230 ms reference at [PREREGISTRATION.md:212](/Users/michael/Dev/blit_v2/docs/bench/otp12-macmac-2026-07-14/PREREGISTRATION.md:212). `min(src/10, 230)` lets either smaller standard win. The test deliberately labels `230 ms @ 2500 ms` as `REPRODUCES` at [otp12pf_mac_verdict_test.py:111](/Users/michael/Dev/blit_v2/scripts/otp12pf_mac_verdict_test.py:111), even though its ratio is 1.092 and the engine’s own acceptance bar says `PASS`. Thus the engine can confidently report “P1 reproduces” when P1’s defining invariance criterion passes.
.review/results/otp-12-worker-parity.records.codex.md:657:.review/results/p1-adjudication-r1.codex.md:2807:- **BLOCKER — the exact 10% boundary is simultaneously PASS and REPRODUCES.** The bar treats `≤1.10` as PASS at [otp12pf_mac_verdict.py:282](/Users/michael/Dev/blit_v2/scripts/otp12pf_mac_verdict.py:282), while `classify()` uses `CI_lo >= T` at [otp12pf_mac_verdict.py:251](/Users/michael/Dev/blit_v2/scripts/otp12pf_mac_verdict.py:251). The guard explicitly requires an exact 10% effect to reproduce at [otp12pf_mac_verdict_test.py:217](/Users/michael/Dev/blit_v2/scripts/otp12pf_mac_verdict_test.py:217). That is a direct policy contradiction, not statistical conservatism.

.review/results/otp-12-worker-parity.records.codex.md:796:.review/results/macmac-harness-r10.codex-engine.md:2431: m=[r.randint(-600,600) for _ in range(8)]; ms=r.choice([600,1000,2000,2500,5000]); c=[r.randint(-300,300) for _ in range(8)]; s=r.choice([600,1000,2500,5000]); tp=min(s/10,230)*.5; tn=-min(s/11,230)*.5; ok=tn<min(c) and max(c)<tp; clean+=ok; counts[s]=counts.get(s,0)+ok
.review/results/otp-12-worker-parity.records.codex.md:799:.review/results/macmac-harness-r3.grok.md:13:| `scripts/otp12pf_mac_verdict.py:289-294` + `:301-307` | **BLOCKER** | **RIG-VOID still fails open for a dirty control that the bar forgives.** Void only if `bar==FAIL` or outcome ∈ {UNSTABLE, REPRODUCES, INVERSION, BAR-FAIL-INCONSISTENT}. A control with a **real, 8/8, rig-W-sized effect** (`d_i=230` every pair) on a slow arm (`src=2500`, ratio **1.092**, bar **PASS**) is only **PARTIAL**, so it does **not** void. Session still emits **VANISHES** with a “CONTROL CAVEAT”. Same structural hole as rev‑3’s bar-tied margin: on a slow arm the bar is wider than Δ_ref, so a full Δ_ref control effect is labeled “sub-bar” and escapes. Guard/mutations never cover this path (they only kill bar-FAIL escape). |
.review/results/otp-12-worker-parity.records.codex.md:800:.review/results/macmac-harness-r3.grok.md:15:| `scripts/otp12pf_mac_verdict.py:318-340` vs prereg “either direction” | **HIGH** | **Session precedence can hide a clean one-direction REPRODUCES.** Pre-reg: reproduction in *either* direction answers the question. Code ranks **BAR-FAIL-INCONSISTENT** and **UNSTABLE** above **REPRODUCES**. Driven: `nq_tcp_mixed=REPRODUCES` (8/8, +300…370) + `qn_tcp_mixed=BAR-FAIL-INCONSISTENT` → session **BAR-FAIL-INCONSISTENT**, not REPRODUCES. Not a false null, but a **false non-reproduction** against the registered “either direction” rule. |
.review/results/otp-12-worker-parity.records.codex.md:801:.review/results/macmac-harness-r3.grok.md:19:| `scripts/otp12pf_mac_verdict.py:214-215` + prereg dual claim | **MEDIUM** | **CI and sign test are not duals once zeros exist.** Example: `d=[0,300…360]`, bar FAIL, `sign_p=0.0156` (7/7 pos) but `CI_lo=0` → not `pos_effect` → **BAR-FAIL-INCONSISTENT**. Conservative against false REPRODUCES; the session text still says pairs “do not agree in sign,” which is **false** here (nonzeros all agree; a zero blocked the CI). |
.review/results/otp-12-worker-parity.records.codex.md:802:.review/results/macmac-harness-r3.grok.md:31:Synthetic session: measurand noise `[-4,-2,-1,0,0,1,2,3]` @ src=2000; all four controls `d_i=230` ×8 @ src=2500.
.review/results/otp-12-worker-parity.records.codex.md:803:.review/results/macmac-harness-r3.grok.md:36:  nq_grpc_mixed  PARTIAL  ratio=1.092 bar=PASS  D=+230ms CI=[+230,+230] (99.2%) sign_p=0.0078 (8/8)
.review/results/otp-12-worker-parity.records.codex.md:805:.review/results/macmac-harness-r3.grok.md:56:`nq_tcp_mixed` REPRODUCES (300–370ms) + `qn_tcp_mixed` BAR-FAIL-INCONSISTENT → session **BAR-FAIL-INCONSISTENT**, not REPRODUCES.
.review/results/otp-12-worker-parity.records.codex.md:810:.review/results/macmac-harness-r3.grok.md:109:The round‑2 RIG-VOID class is only **half** closed: bar-FAIL controls void; **Δ_ref-sized PARTIAL controls do not**, and I drove a clean **VANISHES** while every control sat at **D=+230 / 8/8 / ratio 1.092**. Until control voiding uses the same absolute materiality the power gate claims (or voids any control `pos_effect` / `ci_lo ≥ DELTA_REF`), plus engine-side **n=8 & coverage** enforcement, this instrument can still print a confident null on a dirty or under-specified session.
.review/results/otp-12-worker-parity.records.codex.md:811:.review/results/macmac-prereg-r2.codex.md:124:- `docs/bench/otp12-macmac-2026-07-14/PREREGISTRATION.md:94-123,140-143`; `scripts/bench_otp12pf_mac.sh:448-475,488-495` — **BLOCKER** — `S=max(d_i)-min(d_i)` is neither an MDE nor an equivalence/confidence bound at `n=8`; the “power” test ignores how close observed `D` is to 230 ms. With `srcinit=2000×8` and `d=[0,180,180,190,190,200,200,200]`, it returns PASS, `D=190`, `S=200`, `powered=yes`, hence VANISHES if repeated in both cells—despite seven positive pairs and an effect already 83% of reference. It also uses `min(src,dest)` instead of the source baseline.
.review/results/otp-12-worker-parity.records.codex.md:812:.review/results/macmac-prereg-r2.codex.md:126:- `docs/bench/otp12-macmac-2026-07-14/PREREGISTRATION.md:125-161`; `scripts/bench_otp12pf_mac.sh:403-496` — **BLOCKER** — The six outcomes remain overlapping and incomplete. MIXED-SIGN is shadowed by the earlier REPRODUCES/INVERSION cases; FAIL with `|D|≤S`, sub-bar negative asymmetry, and incomplete cells are unmapped; UNDERPOWERED and UNSTABLE are extra outcomes. `compute_verdicts` implements none of the session rule, control gate, clustering, or six outcomes—it emits only per-cell PASS/FAIL/INCOMPLETE.
.review/results/otp-12-worker-parity.records.codex.md:813:.review/results/macmac-prereg-r2.codex.md:142:- `docs/bench/otp12-macmac-2026-07-14/PREREGISTRATION.md:39-42`; `docs/plan/OTP12_PERF_FINDINGS.md:3-17`; `docs/DECISIONS.md:179-180` — **LOW** — The accepted sequencing conflict was changed only in `docs/STATE.md`: the active plan still says pf-1 starts now and the settled sequence says MTU→pf-1, while rev 2 inserts Mac↔Mac before pf-1.
.review/results/otp-12-worker-parity.records.codex.md:814:.review/results/macmac-prereg-r2.codex.md:151:- `docs/bench/otp12-macmac-2026-07-14/PREREGISTRATION.md:94-123,140-143`; `scripts/bench_otp12pf_mac.sh:448-475,488-495` — **BLOCKER** — `S=max(d_i)-min(d_i)` is neither an MDE nor an equivalence/confidence bound at `n=8`; the “power” test ignores how close observed `D` is to 230 ms. With `srcinit=2000×8` and `d=[0,180,180,190,190,200,200,200]`, it returns PASS, `D=190`, `S=200`, `powered=yes`, hence VANISHES if repeated in both cells—despite seven positive pairs and an effect already 83% of reference. It also uses `min(src,dest)` instead of the source baseline.
.review/results/otp-12-worker-parity.records.codex.md:815:.review/results/macmac-prereg-r2.codex.md:153:- `docs/bench/otp12-macmac-2026-07-14/PREREGISTRATION.md:125-161`; `scripts/bench_otp12pf_mac.sh:403-496` — **BLOCKER** — The six outcomes remain overlapping and incomplete. .review/results/otp-12-worker-parity.records.codex.md:823:.review/results/macmac-harness-r6.codex.md:31:  r5: ...still escaping via the UNDERPOWERED label (one zero pair demotes the cell) -> and separately, `bar == FAIL` had NO DIRECTION, so a +1ms effect at n=16 reported REPRODUCES.
.review/results/otp-12-worker-parity.records.codex.md:827:.review/results/macmac-harness-r6.codex.md:113:- [scripts/otp12pf_mac_verdict.py:299](/Users/michael/Dev/blit_v2/scripts/otp12pf_mac_verdict.py:299) — **BLOCKER** — The three-way split is incomplete. `material = bar_fail_pos OR CI_lo >= breach_hi`, so a marginal-median bar failure still bypasses the CI magnitude test. At n=16, paired `D=1 ms`, CI `[1,1]`, and sign 16/16 produces `REPRODUCES` after three outliers move the marginal median. The same defect makes a control simultaneously “certified clean” (`null_excl=yes`) and “contaminating” via [line 418](/Users/michael/Dev/blit_v2/scripts/otp12pf_mac_verdict.py:418), falsely yielding `RIG-VOID`. The preregistration itself embeds this contradiction at [PREREGISTRATION.md:227](/Users/michael/Dev/blit_v2/docs/bench/otp12-macmac-2026-07-14/PREREGISTRATION.md:227).

At n=16, paired `D=1 ms`, CI `[1,1]`, and sign 16/16 produces `REPRODUCES` after three outliers move the marginal median. The same defect makes a control simultaneously “certified clean” (`null_excl=yes`) and “contaminating” via [line 418](/Users/michael/Dev/blit_v2/scripts/otp12pf_mac_verdict.py:418), falsely yielding `RIG-VOID`. The preregistration itself embeds this contradiction at [PREREGISTRATION.md:227](/Users/michael/Dev/blit_v2/docs/bench/otp12-macmac-2026-07-14/PREREGISTRATION.md:227).

.review/results/otp-12-worker-parity.records.codex.md:841:.review/results/local-bistability.codex.md:366:- **P1 (the headline invariance criterion) — the one thing between blit and shipping.** Fails rig W (`wm_tcp_mixed` 1.237 and 1.300 — do NOT read that as a regression, it is **two different Mac NICs**), but **PASSES 8/8 with Linux on both ends** (`docs/bench/otp12-perf-2026-07-13/`; P1's own cell 1.092/1.003). So it is **platform-INTERACTING, not pure layout** — yet **NOT exonerated**: a code path that only bites on one platform (H1's Windows accept branch) looks identical. **P1 HAS NO ESCAPE HATCH** (codex r5 F1): D-2026-07-12-1 waives only a *cross-direction* miss for a cell that ALREADY passes invariance — P1 *is* the invariance failure. So: **fix it to ≤1.10, or the owner amends acceptance criterion 1.** Neither is assumed.

.review/results/otp-12-worker-parity.records.codex.md:852:.review/results/local-bistability.codex.md:1658:.review/results/bench-script-fix.codex.md:14:Review the diff of commit b9befb8 (run: git show b9befb8). It is a two-line fix to the helper script scripts/bench_10gbe.sh: (1) module endpoint gains the required trailing slash (server:/module/ — see crates/blit-core/src/remote/endpoint.rs parse rules), (2) client flag corrected from --force-grpc-data (the daemon's flag) to --force-grpc (see blit-cli clap definitions). Context: the script predated both surfaces and its run_timed helper discards stderr under set -euo pipefail, so both errors were silent aborts; the fixed script completed a full 10GbE benchmark matrix exit 0. Check: correctness of both fixes against the actual CLI/endpoint grammar at HEAD, whether any OTHER stale CLI usage remains in this script (flags, endpoint forms, subcommands), and internal script coherence. This is a helper script — cargo gates don't apply. Output a concise markdown findings list — each finding with file:line, severity, rationale — then a final VERDICT line. Be concise; do not invoke skills.

.review/results/otp-12-worker-parity.records.codex.md:866:.review/results/macmac-harness-r7.codex.md:303:+- [scripts/otp12pf_mac_verdict.py:299](/Users/michael/Dev/blit_v2/scripts/otp12pf_mac_verdict.py:299) — **BLOCKER** — The three-way split is incomplete. `material = bar_fail_pos OR CI_lo >= breach_hi`, so a marginal-median bar failure still bypasses the CI magnitude test. At n=16, paired `D=1 ms`, CI `[1,1]`, and sign 16/16 produces `REPRODUCES` after three outliers move the marginal median. The same defect makes a control simultaneously “certified clean” (`null_excl=yes`) and “contaminating” via [line 418](/Users/michael/Dev/blit_v2/scripts/otp12pf_mac_verdict.py:418), falsely yielding `RIG-VOID`. The preregistration itself embeds this contradiction at [PREREGISTRATION.md:227](/Users/michael/Dev/blit_v2/docs/bench/otp12-macmac-2026-07-14/PREREGISTRATION.md:227).

.review/results/otp-12-worker-parity.records.codex.md:907:.review/results/macmac-harness-r7.codex.md:1384:- **⛔ NEXT ACTION IS AN OWNER CALL, NOT A RUN. The Mac↔Mac rig (Queue 1(ii)) is BLOCKED: `codex` — the MANDATORY reviewer — is OUT OF CREDITS until 2026-07-19.** The instrument is reworked (`a9460ce`, prereg **rev 5**) but **NOT CLEARED**, and the harness **refuses a timed run** (`exit 2` without `CLEARED_BY_REVIEW=1`). **NO DATA TAKEN.** Round 4 was reviewed by **grok alone**, and D-2026-07-14-2 says grok is *"additive, never a substitute, and never runs alone"* — so **no agent may clear this**. **Owner: wait for codex (2026-07-19) / buy credits / amend the rule.** (Grok found 9 real defects including a BLOCKER, which is the argument *for* the second reviewer, not a reason to promote it.) When cleared: **⚠ THE OWNER MUST CLOSE THEIR CODEX SESSIONS** — nagatha is a bench **END** and the quiescence gate refuses while `codex`/`cargo`/`rustc` runs on **either** Mac (it fires correctly today). Time Machine OFF on both. Rig: nagatha `10.1.10.92` ↔ `q` `10.1.10.54`, 10GbE/9000, build pinned `f35702a`. **Then `pf-1`** (the HARD GATE), which two pf-0 results BIND: between-session grading is dead (a 20% recovery = 46 ms sits under the 78 ms floor), so pf-1 must **measure its own paired within-session floor** before grading; and the fast arm is **BISTABLE** — grade the distribution, not the median.
.review/results/otp-12-worker-parity.records.codex.md:908:.review/results/macmac-harness-r7.codex.md:1390:- **P1 REPRODUCES ON A SECOND MAC (2026-07-13, `docs/bench/otp12-q-baseline-2026-07-13/`).** `wm_tcp_mixed` = **1.385 FAIL** on `q`↔netwatch-01 **at MTU 9000**, while all three controls PASS at **1.002–1.043** in the same session (so rig noise is ~2–4% and P1 is 10× outside it). **P1 is a property of the macOS↔Windows PAIRING, not of one machine** — the assumption **H1** rests on (corrected 2026-07-14: H5/H6/H7 are **P2** hypotheses; the earlier "H1/H5/H6/H7" was wrong), never tested until now. **And jumbo does NOT dissolve P1** — pf-0 has now measured the matched 1500 arm and killed MTU outright (above).
.review/results/otp-12-worker-parity.records.codex.md:909:.review/results/macmac-harness-r7.codex.md:1393:- **P1 (the headline invariance criterion) — the one thing between blit and shipping.** Fails rig W (`wm_tcp_mixed` 1.237 and 1.300 — do NOT read that as a regression, it is **two different Mac NICs**), but **PASSES 8/8 with Linux on both ends** (`docs/bench/otp12-perf-2026-07-13/`; P1's own cell 1.092/1.003). So it is **platform-INTERACTING, not pure layout** — yet **NOT exonerated**: a code path that only bites on one platform (H1's Windows accept branch) looks identical. **P1 HAS NO ESCAPE HATCH** (codex r5 F1): D-2026-07-12-1 waives only a *cross-direction* miss for a cell that ALREADY passes invariance — P1 *is* the invariance failure. So: **fix it to ≤1.10, or the owner amends acceptance criterion 1.** Neither is assumed.

.review/results/otp-12-worker-parity.records.codex.md:1020:.review/results/macmac-harness-r7.codex.md:6376:- [scripts/otp12pf_mac_verdict.py:477](/Users/michael/Dev/blit_v2/scripts/otp12pf_mac_verdict.py:477) — **HIGH** — The bar still changes verdicts. It blocks control certification here, creates `BAR-FAIL-INCONSISTENT` at line 361, and participates in `UNSTABLE` at line 350. No bar path to `REPRODUCES` remains, but the stronger “reported only, never consulted” claim is false. A paired-clean control with CI `[5,5]` was blocked solely by its marginal bar.
.review/results/otp-12-worker-parity.records.codex.md:1021:.review/results/macmac-harness-r7.codex.md:6452:- [scripts/otp12pf_mac_verdict.py:477](/Users/michael/Dev/blit_v2/scripts/otp12pf_mac_verdict.py:477) — **HIGH** — The bar still changes verdicts. It blocks control certification here, creates `BAR-FAIL-INCONSISTENT` at line 361, and participates in `UNSTABLE` at line 350. No bar path to `REPRODUCES` remains, but the stronger “reported only, never consulted” claim is false. A paired-clean control with CI `[5,5]` was blocked solely by its marginal bar.

.review/results/otp-12-worker-parity.records.codex.md:1154:.review/results/macmac-harness-r9b.codex.md:5765:- `8830fda:scripts/otp12pf_mac_verdict.py:193` — **BLOCKER** — Completeness was `len(d) < 8`, so a 16-pair session was graded. Its CI trimmed three contradictory pairs and produced `REPRODUCES`. Current [line 200](/Users/michael/Dev/blit_v2/scripts/otp12pf_mac_verdict.py:200) correctly requires exactly eight.
.review/results/otp-12-worker-parity.records.codex.md:1155:.review/results/macmac-harness-r9b.codex.md:5767:- [scripts/otp12pf_mac_verdict.py:220](/Users/michael/Dev/blit_v2/scripts/otp12pf_mac_verdict.py:220) — **BLOCKER** — `B` transports raw milliseconds between controls with potentially different baselines. Identical 4.9% arm bias in every cell can certify every control yet produce `REPRODUCES` in slower measurands. Neither median nor CI-versus-range fixes this; the rule needs an explicitly justified additive-bias model or a normalized carry. Opposite-sign bias and increasing `B` are otherwise conservative.
.review/results/otp-12-worker-parity.records.codex.md:1158:.review/results/macmac-harness-r9b.codex.md:5828:- `8830fda:scripts/otp12pf_mac_verdict.py:193` — **BLOCKER** — Completeness was `len(d) < 8`, so a 16-pair session was graded. Its CI trimmed three contradictory pairs and produced `REPRODUCES`. Current [line 200](/Users/michael/Dev/blit_v2/scripts/otp12pf_mac_verdict.py:200) correctly requires exactly eight.
.review/results/otp-12-worker-parity.records.codex.md:1159:.review/results/macmac-harness-r9b.codex.md:5830:- [scripts/otp12pf_mac_verdict.py:220](/Users/michael/Dev/blit_v2/scripts/otp12pf_mac_verdict.py:220) — **BLOCKER** — `B` transports raw milliseconds between controls with potentially different baselines. Identical 4.9% arm bias in every cell can certify every control yet produce `REPRODUCES` in slower measurands. Neither median nor CI-versus-range fixes this; the rule needs an explicitly justified additive-bias model or a normalized carry. Opposite-sign bias and increasing `B` are otherwise conservative.

.review/results/otp-12-worker-parity.records.codex.md:1169:.review/results/otp-4a-daemon-serves-transfer.codex.md:4733:.review/results/d-2026-07-05-2-compat.codex.md:300: **2026-07-03 16:26:55Z** - **CODER (ue-r2-1c, claude)**: Landed the engine shell (REV4 `ue-r2-1c`, third slice through the code→GPT-review→fix loop). New `crates/blit-core/src/engine/` module: `TransferEngine::execute(EngineRequest)` owns strategy selection (single-file → journal probe → fast-path walk → streaming) and the streaming leg (tuning window → scan → diff/plan → sink pipeline → mirror deletions → journal checkpoints → history/predictor); `TransferOrchestrator` shrank to the local adapter (preconditions, `FsTransferSource`/`FilteredSource` + `FsTransferSink`/`NullSink` construction, option translation) with the public API preserved via re-exports — engine type thereby ratified per REV4 Design §1 (new engine + adapter, not a rename). Sequence: behavior pins first (`7730eb1`: NoWork/UpToDate + `no_work` tag, NoWork/SourceEmpty — both previously untested), the move (`dc9b0ed`: `fast_path.rs`→`engine/strategy.rs`, options/summary/history moved, tuning/mirror/journal/single-file split out; move fidelity machine-checked — 75 diff lines vs origin, all six documented transformations, zero drift), then the REV4-named accounting gap (`29e210b`: single-file strategy now records perf history tag `single_file` / `null_sink` lane + predictor, guard test proven by revert). Review: first codex run died on provider quota (121k tokens, recorded); owner restored access same day; retry PASS with 1 Low, accepted + fixed (`15e6334`: engine back-references through `crate::orchestrator` → engine-local paths; swept one more the reviewer missed). Also recorded owner-provided 10GbE test env (`admin@skippy:/mnt/generic-pool/video/test`) in STATE queue item 4. Validation: fmt/clippy clean, `cargo test --workspace` 1394 / 0 / 2 (baseline 1391, +3). All on master, unpushed. Next: `ue-r2-1d` (streaming plan foundation) — or `1e`/w4-1 per queue.
.review/results/otp-12-worker-parity.records.codex.md:1170:.review/results/otp-4a-daemon-serves-transfer.codex.md:4736:.review/results/d-2026-07-05-2-compat.codex.md:310: **2026-06-12 16:32:00Z** - **REVIEW (gemini-reviewer)**: Graded the two pending sentinels on `master` sequentially: **design-4-fallback-midmanifest-negotiation** (`ddfeb58`) accepted (daemon early-flush branch is now TCP-only; client-side fallback_negotiated flag added to hold need-list/manifest fallback payloads in pending_queue; 2000-file regression test passes) and **design-5-send-failure-masks-rejection** (`08d71a2`) accepted (prefer_server_error helper harvests daemon's terminal status on manifest-phase send failures; 500-file readonly_enforcement regression test passes repeatedly). .review/results/otp-12-worker-parity.records.codex.md:1173:.review/results/otp-4a-daemon-serves-transfer.codex.md:5103:.review/results/small-file-ceiling-plan.codex.md:1839:**2026-07-03 16:26:55Z** - **CODER (ue-r2-1c, claude)**: Landed the engine shell (REV4 `ue-r2-1c`, third slice through the code→GPT-review→fix loop). New `crates/blit-core/src/engine/` module: `TransferEngine::execute(EngineRequest)` owns strategy selection (single-file → journal probe → fast-path walk → streaming) and the streaming leg (tuning window → scan → diff/plan → sink pipeline → mirror deletions → journal checkpoints → history/predictor); `TransferOrchestrator` shrank to the local adapter (preconditions, `FsTransferSource`/`FilteredSource` + `FsTransferSink`/`NullSink` construction, option translation) with the public API preserved via re-exports — engine type thereby ratified per REV4 Design §1 (new engine + adapter, not a rename). Sequence: behavior pins first (`7730eb1`: NoWork/UpToDate + `no_work` tag, NoWork/SourceEmpty — both previously untested), the move (`dc9b0ed`: `fast_path.rs`→`engine/strategy.rs`, options/summary/history moved, tuning/mirror/journal/single-file split out; move fidelity machine-checked — 75 diff lines vs origin, all six documented transformations, zero drift), then the REV4-named accounting gap (`29e210b`: single-file strategy now records perf history tag `single_file` / `null_sink` lane + predictor, guard test proven by revert). Review: first codex run died on provider quota (121k tokens, recorded); owner restored access same day; retry PASS with 1 Low, accepted + fixed (`15e6334`: engine back-references through `crate::orchestrator` → engine-local paths; swept one more the reviewer missed). Also recorded owner-provided 10GbE test env (`admin@skippy:/mnt/generic-pool/video/test`) in STATE queue item 4. Validation: fmt/clippy clean, `cargo test --workspace` 1394 / 0 / 2 (baseline 1391, +3). All on master, unpushed. Next: `ue-r2-1d` (streaming plan foundation) — or `1e`/w4-1 per queue.

.review/results/otp-12-worker-parity.records.codex.md:1183:.review/results/macmac-prereg.codex.md:83:- `docs/bench/otp12-macmac-2026-07-14/PREREGISTRATION.md:22-34` — **BLOCKER** — H1 is mischaracterized as Windows-exclusive. The parent defines a generic resize-time accept/dial asymmetry and says controlled ablations must kill or confirm it (`OTP12_PERF_FINDINGS.md:183-209,380-399`). Mac↔Mac reproduction could be H1 itself—or an unrelated phenocopy—while rig-W remains caused by Windows. A null supports pairing dependence, not H1 specifically.
.review/results/otp-12-worker-parity.records.codex.md:1184:.review/results/macmac-prereg.codex.md:99:- `docs/bench/otp12-macmac-2026-07-14/PREREGISTRATION.md:33-34` — **LOW** — The new Mac↔Mac-before-pf-1 sequence is reflected in `docs/STATE.md` but conflicts with `docs/DECISIONS.md:179-183` and the parent’s pf-1-first staging. Durable guidance remains internally inconsistent.
.review/results/otp-12-worker-parity.records.codex.md:1185:.review/results/macmac-prereg.codex.md:106:- `docs/bench/otp12-macmac-2026-07-14/PREREGISTRATION.md:22-34` — **BLOCKER** — H1 is mischaracterized as Windows-exclusive. .review/results/otp-12-worker-parity.records.codex.md:1194:.review/results/macmac-harness-r11.codex-engine.md:93:- [scripts/otp12pf_mac_verdict.py:257](/Users/michael/Dev/blit_v2/scripts/otp12pf_mac_verdict.py:257) — **HIGH** — Clean controls can produce `B > T` and license an effect mostly attributable to permitted rig bias. Controls `src=1000, d=+49×8` are clean; measurands `src=10000, d=+720×8` give `T=230`, `B=490`, and `REPRODUCES`. Up to 68% is rig bias, the ratio is only 1.072, and a null is impossible. This also contradicts the spec’s claim that `min(10%, 230ms)` requires materiality by “both” standards.
.review/results/otp-12-worker-parity.records.codex.md:1197:.review/results/macmac-harness-r11.codex-engine.md:110:- [scripts/otp12pf_mac_verdict.py:257](/Users/michael/Dev/blit_v2/scripts/otp12pf_mac_verdict.py:257) — **HIGH** — Clean controls can produce `B > T` and license an effect mostly attributable to permitted rig bias. Controls `src=1000, d=+49×8` are clean; measurands `src=10000, d=+720×8` give `T=230`, `B=490`, and `REPRODUCES`. Up to 68% is rig bias, the ratio is only 1.072, and a null is impossible. This also contradicts the spec’s claim that `min(10%, 230ms)` requires materiality by “both” standards.
.review/results/otp-12-worker-parity.records.codex.md:1209:.review/results/macmac-r12.codex-design.md:210:- **BLOCKER — `min()` implements the opposite of the stated policy.** The document says an effect must matter by both the 10% bar and the 230 ms reference at [PREREGISTRATION.md:212](/Users/michael/Dev/blit_v2/docs/bench/otp12-macmac-2026-07-14/PREREGISTRATION.md:212). `min(src/10, 230)` lets either smaller standard win. The test deliberately labels `230 ms @ 2500 ms` as `REPRODUCES` at [otp12pf_mac_verdict_test.py:111](/Users/michael/Dev/blit_v2/scripts/otp12pf_mac_verdict_test.py:111), even though its ratio is 1.092 and the engine’s own acceptance bar says `PASS`. Thus the engine can confidently report “P1 reproduces” when P1’s defining invariance criterion passes.
.review/results/otp-12-worker-parity.records.codex.md:1210:.review/results/macmac-r12.codex-design.md:212:- **BLOCKER — the exact 10% boundary is simultaneously PASS and REPRODUCES.** The bar treats `≤1.10` as PASS at [otp12pf_mac_verdict.py:282](/Users/michael/Dev/blit_v2/scripts/otp12pf_mac_verdict.py:282), while `classify()` uses `CI_lo >= T` at [otp12pf_mac_verdict.py:251](/Users/michael/Dev/blit_v2/scripts/otp12pf_mac_verdict.py:251). The guard explicitly requires an exact 10% effect to reproduce at [otp12pf_mac_verdict_test.py:217](/Users/michael/Dev/blit_v2/scripts/otp12pf_mac_verdict_test.py:217). That is a direct policy contradiction, not statistical conservatism.
.review/results/otp-12-worker-parity.records.codex.md:1212:.review/results/macmac-r12.codex-design.md:234:**DO NOT RUN IT — first validate P1 with the corrected measurement on the known-failing Mac↔Windows pair and instrument the suspected path; the current rule can call a 1.092/PASS cell “REPRODUCES,” and the end-fabric gate is incomplete.**
.review/results/otp-12-worker-parity.records.codex.md:1218:.review/results/macmac-r12.codex-design.md:272:- **BLOCKER — `min()` implements the opposite of the stated policy.** The document says an effect must matter by both the 10% bar and the 230 ms reference at [PREREGISTRATION.md:212](/Users/michael/Dev/blit_v2/docs/bench/otp12-macmac-2026-07-14/PREREGISTRATION.md:212). `min(src/10, 230)` lets either smaller standard win. .review/results/otp-12-worker-parity.records.codex.md:1230:.review/results/macmac-harness-r11.grok.md:20:| 216–218 | **HIGH** | **Arm-count completeness is load-bearing and unguarded.** Code requires `len(by[srcinit])==8` and `len(by[destinit])==8`. Removing only those two conjuncts still passes **all 34 cases** and is **not** what the completeness mutation isolates (that mutation kills on a short `len(d)`). Synthetic CSV: 8 pairs with `d=50` on `src=1000` (true `T=100` → null) plus extra valid low `srcinit` rows → mutant reports **`REPRODUCES`**; unmutated engine correctly **`INCOMPLETE`**. Same defect class as prior vacuous guards. |
.review/results/otp-12-worker-parity.records.codex.md:1231:.review/results/macmac-harness-r11.grok.md:24:| 200 / zeros→`classify(0,…)` | *(closed)* | `classify(0,0,0,0,0,0)` is **`EFFECT`**. Refused by `ms_of` non-positive check. With clean non-zero controls and zero measurand arms, dropping that check yields **`REPRODUCES`**. |
.review/results/otp-12-worker-parity.records.codex.md:1232:.review/results/macmac-harness-r11.grok.md:39:| 247–249, 320–322 | **LOW** | Zero-timing case sets **all** arms to 0; mutant becomes `CONTROLS-NOT-CLEAN`, not `REPRODUCES`. Mutation is non-vacuous on the `ENGINE-REFUSED` contract but does **not** exercise the dangerous zeros→`REPRODUCES` path (needs clean positive controls + zero measurand). |
.review/results/otp-12-worker-parity.records.codex.md:1234:.review/results/macmac-harness-r11.grok.md:48:| 6–13 vs 220–250 | **MEDIUM** | **Opening “rule in one paragraph” is stale:** NONE via **CI** inside ±T; no RANGE; no B; no `−src/11`. Body (rev 8+) is what the code implements. Same class as prior doc≠code defects. |

It is otp-12b's RECORDED-RUN half per docs/plan/OTP12_ACCEPTANCE_RUN.md (Active): (1) e21cf84 fixes the win-initiated sentinel - pwsh parses bare $rc:R as a scope-qualified variable so it never printed (found at first rig contact; a manual reproduction proved the win->mac path itself works); (2) 856af64 strips CRs from the drain outcome before runs.csv - a bare \r mid-row split every row under python universal newlines and verdicted 192 valid runs INCOMPLETE; (3) 44c2046 commits docs/bench/otp12-win-2026-07-12/ (README + sanitized runs.csv + the raw CRLF original + summary/verdicts/drain/manifest). CHECK HARD: (a) recompute the README's headline numbers from runs.csv (medians over valid rows, floor-of-mean-of-middle-two): the invariance table (11/12 PASS, wm_tcp_mixed 1.237), the two converge fails (push_tcp_small 2080/1811/1868; pull_tcp_mixed 1138/867/1284), the cross rows and the gap rows (old_push/old_pull vs new_mw_worst/new_wm_worst per fixture x carrier) against docs/bench/otp2w-baseline-2026-07-10/summary.csv; (b) is the CR post-processing legitimate and fully disclosed (raw file committed; no timing value altered - verify runs.csv and runs-raw-crlf.csv differ ONLY by \r bytes); (c) does the README avoid declaring pass/fail and avoid self-adjudicating the D-2026-07-12-1 residue (gap rows RECORDED; the platform-attribution language); (d) the escalation reasoning: no pre-registered trigger fires (verify the fails have tight spreads and are not straddles per the design doc D2's rule); (e) the cross-block consistency note (block-1 push_tcp_small 2080 vs block-2 mw_tcp_small mac_init 1922) - is it honestly recorded and is any conclusion improperly drawn; (f) any overclaim in calling wm_tcp_mixed/push_tcp_small code-shaped findings. Output a concise markdown findings list - each finding with file:line, severity, rationale - then a final VERDICT line. Be concise; do not invoke skills.

Check that epoch/pending/refusal arbitration is now race-safe for both shape and tuner producers, including accepted-settlement ABA, that target/live/epoch come from coherent state, and that locking cannot deadlock or regress tuning, exact role-independent worker targets, first-byte progress, cancellation/teardown, or byte/tree correctness. Be concise; do not invoke skills.

.review/results/otp-12-worker-parity.records.codex.md:1257:.review/results/macmac-harness-r10.grok.md:13:| 3 | **MEDIUM** | `otp12pf_mac_verdict.py:200`; `otp12pf_mac_verdict_test.py:284–287` | **Exact-`n` is case-guarded, not mutation-complete.** Live rule is `len(d) != PAIRS` (correct). Weakening to `len(d) < PAIRS` yields **false `REPRODUCES`** on the 16-pair trim session. The SHORT-cell mutation still passes under that weakening (`INCOMPLETE`); only the LONG **case** kills it. Preflight runs cases, so a full guard run still catches it — but the mutation that claims to prove pair-counting does not prove *exact* equality. Class (a) on the guard. |

.review/results/otp-12-worker-parity.records.codex.md:1286:.review/results/macmac-harness.codex.md:172:2. **REPRODUCES (in a named direction).** A TCP×mixed cell FAILS the 1.10 bar with
.review/results/otp-12-worker-parity.records.codex.md:1287:.review/results/macmac-harness.codex.md:694:- **NEXT ACTION — rev 3 of the Mac↔Mac pre-registration + harness, then run it (Queue 1(ii); the second of the two experiments that come BEFORE any pf code).** Experiment (i), the A-B-B-A MTU run, is **DONE** (pf-0 below). Rig: nagatha `10.1.10.92` ↔ `q` `10.1.10.54`, both 10GbE/9000. Pre-registered (**rev 2**, codex round 1 = 9 findings, **9/9 accepted**): `docs/bench/otp12-macmac-2026-07-14/PREREGISTRATION.md`; harness `scripts/bench_otp12pf_mac.sh`. **⚠ CORRECTED 2026-07-14 — it is NOT an H1 discriminator.** The earlier claim here ("reproduces ⇒ **H1 DIES**, H1 accuses the *Windows* accept branch") was **WRONG and is retracted**: H1 accuses **blit's own code paths** (`SourceSockets` Dial/Accept, `add_dialed_stream`, the dial-before-ACK at `transfer_session/mod.rs:3113`) — **the word "Windows" appears nowhere in H1**, and that code runs on macOS too. A Mac↔Mac reproduction is therefore *consistent with* H1, not fatal to it. What the rig **does** answer: **does P1 require the macOS↔Windows PAIRING, or is it a platform-general cost of the destination-initiated layout?** Reproduces ⇒ P1 is **not platform residue** (no Windows peer needed) → the "accept it as platform residue" escape closes and every code-level hypothesis strengthens. Vanishes ⇒ P1 is **pairing-dependent** → platform-agnostic code mechanisms weaken. Either way the hypothesis space moves, which is why it precedes pf-1. **Then `pf-1`** (the HARD GATE), which two pf-0 results now BIND: (a) **between-session grading is dead** (a 20% recovery = 46 ms sits under the 78 ms between-session floor) — pf-1 must **measure its own paired within-session floor and register a resolution check** before grading; (b) **the fast arm is BISTABLE** — grade the run distribution, not the median.
.review/results/otp-12-worker-parity.records.codex.md:1288:.review/results/macmac-harness.codex.md:698:- **P1 REPRODUCES ON A SECOND MAC (2026-07-13, `docs/bench/otp12-q-baseline-2026-07-13/`).** `wm_tcp_mixed` = **1.385 FAIL** on `q`↔netwatch-01 **at MTU 9000**, while all three controls PASS at **1.002–1.043** in the same session (so rig noise is ~2–4% and P1 is 10× outside it). **P1 is a property of the macOS↔Windows PAIRING, not of one machine** — the assumption **H1** rests on (corrected 2026-07-14: H5/H6/H7 are **P2** hypotheses; the earlier "H1/H5/H6/H7" was wrong), never tested until now. **And jumbo does NOT dissolve P1** — pf-0 has now measured the matched 1500 arm and killed MTU outright (above).
.review/results/otp-12-worker-parity.records.codex.md:1289:.review/results/macmac-harness.codex.md:701:- **P1 (the headline invariance criterion) — the one thing between blit and shipping.** Fails rig W (`wm_tcp_mixed` 1.237 and 1.300 — do NOT read that as a regression, it is **two different Mac NICs**), but **PASSES 8/8 with Linux on both ends** (`docs/bench/otp12-perf-2026-07-13/`; P1's own cell 1.092/1.003). So it is **platform-INTERACTING, not pure layout** — yet **NOT exonerated**: a code path that only bites on one platform (H1's Windows accept branch) looks identical. **P1 HAS NO ESCAPE HATCH** (codex r5 F1): D-2026-07-12-1 waives only a *cross-direction* miss for a cell that ALREADY passes invariance — P1 *is* the invariance failure. So: **fix it to ≤1.10, or the owner amends acceptance criterion 1.** Neither is assumed.

.review/results/otp-12-worker-parity.records.codex.md:1322:.review/results/macmac-harness.codex.md:2512:- `docs/bench/otp12-macmac-2026-07-14/PREREGISTRATION.md:94-123,140-143`; `scripts/bench_otp12pf_mac.sh:448-475,488-495` — **BLOCKER** — `S=max(d_i)-min(d_i)` is neither an MDE nor an equivalence/confidence bound at `n=8`; the “power” test ignores how close observed `D` is to 230 ms. With `srcinit=2000×8` and `d=[0,180,180,190,190,200,200,200]`, it returns PASS, `D=190`, `S=200`, `powered=yes`, hence VANISHES if repeated in both cells—despite seven positive pairs and an effect already 83% of reference. It also uses `min(src,dest)` instead of the source baseline.
.review/results/otp-12-worker-parity.records.codex.md:1323:.review/results/macmac-harness.codex.md:2514:- `docs/bench/otp12-macmac-2026-07-14/PREREGISTRATION.md:125-161`; `scripts/bench_otp12pf_mac.sh:403-496` — **BLOCKER** — The six outcomes remain overlapping and incomplete. MIXED-SIGN is shadowed by the earlier REPRODUCES/INVERSION cases; FAIL with `|D|≤S`, sub-bar negative asymmetry, and incomplete cells are unmapped; UNDERPOWERED and UNSTABLE are extra outcomes. `compute_verdicts` implements none of the session rule, control gate, clustering, or six outcomes—it emits only per-cell PASS/FAIL/INCOMPLETE.
.review/results/otp-12-worker-parity.records.codex.md:1324:.review/results/macmac-harness.codex.md:2530:- `docs/bench/otp12-macmac-2026-07-14/PREREGISTRATION.md:39-42`; `docs/plan/OTP12_PERF_FINDINGS.md:3-17`; `docs/DECISIONS.md:179-180` — **LOW** — The accepted sequencing conflict was changed only in `docs/STATE.md`: the active plan still says pf-1 starts now and the settled sequence says MTU→pf-1, while rev 2 inserts Mac↔Mac before pf-1.
.review/results/otp-12-worker-parity.records.codex.md:1325:.review/results/macmac-harness.codex.md:2539:- `docs/bench/otp12-macmac-2026-07-14/PREREGISTRATION.md:94-123,140-143`; `scripts/bench_otp12pf_mac.sh:448-475,488-495` — **BLOCKER** — `S=max(d_i)-min(d_i)` is neither an MDE nor an equivalence/confidence bound at `n=8`; the “power” test ignores how close observed `D` is to 230 ms. With `srcinit=2000×8` and `d=[0,180,180,190,190,200,200,200]`, it returns PASS, `D=190`, `S=200`, `powered=yes`, hence VANISHES if repeated in both cells—despite seven positive pairs and an effect already 83% of reference. It also uses `min(src,dest)` instead of the source baseline.
.review/results/otp-12-worker-parity.records.codex.md:1326:.review/results/macmac-harness.codex.md:2541:- `docs/bench/otp12-macmac-2026-07-14/PREREGISTRATION.md:125-161`; `scripts/bench_otp12pf_mac.sh:403-496` — **BLOCKER** — The six outcomes remain overlapping and incomplete. MIXED-SIGN is shadowed by the earlier REPRODUCES/INVERSION cases; FAIL with `|D|≤S`, sub-bar negative asymmetry, and incomplete cells are unmapped; UNDERPOWERED and UNSTABLE are extra outcomes. `compute_verdicts` implements none of the session rule, control gate, clustering, or six outcomes—it emits only per-cell PASS/FAIL/INCOMPLETE.
.review/results/otp-12-worker-parity.records.codex.md:1327:.review/results/macmac-harness.codex.md:2557:- `docs/bench/otp12-macmac-2026-07-14/PREREGISTRATION.md:39-42`; `docs/plan/OTP12_PERF_FINDINGS.md:3-17`; `docs/DECISIONS.md:179-180` — **LOW** — The accepted sequencing conflict was changed only in `docs/STATE.md`: the active plan still says pf-1 starts now and the settled sequence says MTU→pf-1, while rev 2 inserts Mac↔Mac before pf-1.

.review/results/otp-12-worker-parity.records.codex.md:1336:.review/results/macmac-harness.codex.md:2745:   119	**8/8 invariance cells PASS** (`ms_grpc_mixed` via its pre-registered
.review/results/otp-12-worker-parity.records.codex.md:1338:.review/results/macmac-harness.codex.md:2894:docs/STATE.md:6:- **NEXT ACTION — rev 3 of the Mac↔Mac pre-registration + harness, then run it (Queue 1(ii); the second of the two experiments that come BEFORE any pf code).** Experiment (i), the A-B-B-A MTU run, is **DONE** (pf-0 below). Rig: nagatha `10.1.10.92` ↔ `q` `10.1.10.54`, both 10GbE/9000. Pre-registered (**rev 2**, codex round 1 = 9 findings, **9/9 accepted**): `docs/bench/otp12-macmac-2026-07-14/PREREGISTRATION.md`; harness `scripts/bench_otp12pf_mac.sh`. **⚠ CORRECTED 2026-07-14 — it is NOT an H1 discriminator.** The earlier claim here ("reproduces ⇒ **H1 DIES**, H1 accuses the *Windows* accept branch") was **WRONG and is retracted**: H1 accuses **blit's own code paths** (`SourceSockets` Dial/Accept, `add_dialed_stream`, the dial-before-ACK at `transfer_session/mod.rs:3113`) — **the word "Windows" appears nowhere in H1**, and that code runs on macOS too. A Mac↔Mac reproduction is therefore *consistent with* H1, not fatal to it. What the rig **does** answer: **does P1 require the macOS↔Windows PAIRING, or is it a platform-general cost of the destination-initiated layout?** Reproduces ⇒ P1 is **not platform residue** (no Windows peer needed) → the "accept it as platform residue" escape closes and every code-level hypothesis strengthens. Vanishes ⇒ P1 is **pairing-dependent** → platform-agnostic code mechanisms weaken. Either way the hypothesis space moves, which is why it precedes pf-1. **Then `pf-1`** (the HARD GATE), which two pf-0 results now BIND: (a) **between-session grading is dead** (a 20% recovery = 46 ms sits under the 78 ms between-session floor) — pf-1 must **measure its own paired within-session floor and register a resolution check** before grading; (b) **the fast arm is BISTABLE** — grade the run distribution, not the median.
.review/results/otp-12-worker-parity.records.codex.md:1339:.review/results/macmac-harness.codex.md:3131:src = [2000] * 8
.review/results/otp-12-worker-parity.records.codex.md:1340:.review/results/macmac-harness.codex.md:3156:/bin/zsh -lc "python3 -c \"src=[2000]*8; ds=[0,180,180,190,190,200,200,200]; dst=[s+x for s,x in zip(src,ds)]; med=lambda v:(sorted(v)[3]+sorted(v)[4])//2; s=med(src); d=med(dst); D=med(ds); S=max(ds)-min(ds); fast=min(s,d); print({'src_med':s,'dst_med':d,'bar':'PASS' if 10*max(s,d)<=11*min(s,d) else 'FAIL','D':D,'S':S,'powered':'yes' if S<=230 and 10*(fast+230)>11*fast else 'NO'})\"" in /Users/michael/Dev/blit_v2
.review/results/otp-12-worker-parity.records.codex.md:1341:.review/results/macmac-harness.codex.md:3162:{'src_med': 2000, 'dst_med': 2190, 'bar': 'PASS', 'D': 190, 'S': 200, 'powered': 'yes'}

For `srcinit=2000×8`, `d=[0,180,180,190,190,200,200,200]`, the code reports bar `PASS`, `D=190`, `S=200`, `powered=yes`; repeated in both directions, the rule declares `VANISHES` despite 7/8 positive pairs and 83% of the reference effect.

.review/results/otp-12-worker-parity.records.codex.md:1361:.review/results/small-file-ceiling-plan.codex.md:1839:**2026-07-03 16:26:55Z** - **CODER (ue-r2-1c, claude)**: Landed the engine shell (REV4 `ue-r2-1c`, third slice through the code→GPT-review→fix loop). New `crates/blit-core/src/engine/` module: `TransferEngine::execute(EngineRequest)` owns strategy selection (single-file → journal probe → fast-path walk → streaming) and the streaming leg (tuning window → scan → diff/plan → sink pipeline → mirror deletions → journal checkpoints → history/predictor); `TransferOrchestrator` shrank to the local adapter (preconditions, `FsTransferSource`/`FilteredSource` + `FsTransferSink`/`NullSink` construction, option translation) with the public API preserved via re-exports — engine type thereby ratified per REV4 Design §1 (new engine + adapter, not a rename). Sequence: behavior pins first (`7730eb1`: NoWork/UpToDate + `no_work` tag, NoWork/SourceEmpty — both previously untested), the move (`dc9b0ed`: `fast_path.rs`→`engine/strategy.rs`, options/summary/history moved, tuning/mirror/journal/single-file split out; move fidelity machine-checked — 75 diff lines vs origin, all six documented transformations, zero drift), then the REV4-named accounting gap (`29e210b`: single-file strategy now records perf history tag `single_file` / `null_sink` lane + predictor, guard test proven by revert). Review: first codex run died on provider quota (121k tokens, recorded); owner restored access same day; retry PASS with 1 Low, accepted + fixed (`15e6334`: engine back-references through `crate::orchestrator` → engine-local paths; swept one more the reviewer missed). Also recorded owner-provided 10GbE test env (`admin@skippy:/mnt/generic-pool/video/test`) in STATE queue item 4. Validation: fmt/clippy clean, `cargo test --workspace` 1394 / 0 / 2 (baseline 1391, +3). All on master, unpushed. Next: `ue-r2-1d` (streaming plan foundation) — or `1e`/w4-1 per queue.
.review/results/otp-12-worker-parity.records.codex.md:1362:.review/results/small-file-ceiling-plan.codex.md:1849:**2026-06-12 16:32:00Z** - **REVIEW (gemini-reviewer)**: Graded the two pending sentinels on `master` sequentially: **design-4-fallback-midmanifest-negotiation** (`ddfeb58`) accepted (daemon early-flush branch is now TCP-only; client-side fallback_negotiated flag added to hold need-list/manifest fallback payloads in pending_queue; 2000-file regression test passes) and **design-5-send-failure-masks-rejection** (`08d71a2`) accepted (prefer_server_error helper harvests daemon's terminal status on manifest-phase send failures; 500-file readonly_enforcement regression test passes repeatedly). Both verified.json verdicts created, sentinels deleted via git rm, and REVIEW.md status rows updated to `[x]`. Validation suite re-run: cargo fmt, clippy, and cargo test --workspace all passed successfully (1370 passed, 0 failed, 1 ignored).
.review/results/otp-12-worker-parity.records.codex.md:1363:.review/results/small-file-ceiling-plan.codex.md:1857:**2026-06-12 03:55:00Z** - **CODER (autonomous overnight session)**: Owner authorized autonomous queue execution ("work on as much as you can. commit every slice as it lands. if anything gets questionable, stop." — scope: master only, no branches, local commits, never push, faster-mode WIP iff files-changed disjoint across pending sentinels; recorded in STATE.md). **7 slices completed and sentineled** (18 local commits, `1adbe0c..0af904e`): **w5-1-log-backend** (`56bda09`+`7145202`) — new `blit_core::stderr_log` backend (warn default, `BLIT_LOG` override, `<binary>: <level>: <msg>`) installed in all 4 binaries; ~22 formerly-discarded log::warn/error sites now visible; 9 bracketed stderr prefix families converged (warnings→facade, operator lines→`blit:`/`blitd:` prefixes, per-file pipeline spam→log::debug); trace-gated `[data-plane-client]` family kept (remote_parity.rs asserts the prefix); jobs-watch ticker vocabulary kept (single-command status output). **w9-5-jobs-lifecycle-e2e** (`ad773d8`) — first e2e net for detach/jobs verbs: 5 tests incl. cancel-active-delegated-job exit-0 made deterministic via a fake tonic source whose pull_sync never answers. **w2-1-delete-warmup-machinery** (`2a8a490`) — analyze_warmup_result + determine_tuning + warmup_gbps deleted; determine_remote_tuning is one honest static table (production values bit-identical); 4 tier tests added. **w9-1-ungate-windows-tests** (`9324559`) — 27 blanket #[cfg(unix)] removed from 8 remote-transfer test files (none unix-specific); 4 genuinely unix files kept gated; Windows CI will now actually compile parity-critical paths. **w9-2-revive-root-tests** (`461525d`) — dead root tests/ relocated into crates/blit-core/tests (+24 tests incl. the only MirrorPlanner coverage); connection.rs deleted; drift fixed (for_tests→load(), compare_mode, 32→300 files vs TINY_FILE_LIMIT=256, serial Mutex for config-dir globals); AGENTS.md §4 corrected. **w9-4-readonly-enforcement-tests** (`4d67210`) — TestContext::new_read_only() knob + 3 e2e tests covering all 3 read-only write gates (push/purge/delegated-pull), asserting error text + no destination mutation. **w8-1b-zero-copy-fast-eval** (`6189d82`) — analysis-only: `docs/plan/ZERO_COPY_RECEIVE_EVAL.md` (Draft) recommends DELETING zero_copy.rs (wire path is splice-friendly but the dead impl busy-waits on EAGAIN, the AsyncRead-generic pipeline resists raw-fd wiring, and the CPU win is unmeasured pending the 10GbE rig); owner verdict is the gate. **Suite: 1331 → 1368 tests, fmt/clippy/test green after every slice.** Remaining `[ ]` rows are all overlap-blocked behind the 7 pending sentinels (skip map in STATE.md), owner-gated (w2-3 plan interview), or dependent (w2-4, w6-2, w8-1). Transient note: first post-w5-1 workspace test run showed 11 "daemon failed to listen" failures under cold parallel load; isolated and clean re-runs green — same harness-contention class w9-3 addresses.

.review/results/otp-12-worker-parity.records.codex.md:1367:.review/results/small-file-ceiling-plan.codex.md:2296:# 10GbE session evidence (2026-07-04, MTU 9000 both ends)
.review/results/otp-12-worker-parity.records.codex.md:1369:.review/results/macmac-harness-r9.verdict.md:18:the three pairs at −500** while keeping the thirteen at +200 → **`REPRODUCES`**.
.review/results/otp-12-worker-parity.records.codex.md:1371:.review/results/otp-12-worker-parity.fix-review.codex.md:14:Review the diff of commit cfd9dd7 (run: git show cfd9dd7). It is the review-fix commit for otp-12-worker-parity under docs/plan/ONE_TRANSFER_PATH.md and docs/TRANSFER_SESSION.md. The prior review of a76b785 found: (1) serial pre-payload resize settlement delayed first byte and could trip active StallGuards; (2) resize refusal was not terminal and could be reproposed. Check that both are fully fixed without weakening exact initiator-independent worker-target parity. Check correctness regressions, first-byte/converge-up behavior, bounded backpressure, resize epoch/refusal semantics, socket/END teardown, cancellation and peer-fault propagation, byte/tree correctness, StallGuard, and that test count did not drop. Output a concise markdown findings list — each finding with file:line, severity, rationale — then a final VERDICT line. Be concise; do not invoke skills.
.review/results/otp-12-worker-parity.records.codex.md:1372:.review/results/pf-0-rebaseline-decision.codex.md:414: - **P1 REPRODUCES ON A SECOND MAC (2026-07-13, `docs/bench/otp12-q-baseline-2026-07-13/`).** `wm_tcp_mixed` = **1.385 FAIL** on `q`↔netwatch-01 **at MTU 9000**, while all three controls PASS at **1.002–1.043** in the same session (so rig noise is ~2–4% and P1 is 10× outside it). **P1 is a property of the macOS↔Windows PAIRING, not of one machine** — the assumption **H1** rests on (corrected 2026-07-14: H5/H6/H7 are **P2** hypotheses; the earlier "H1/H5/H6/H7" was wrong), never tested until now. **And jumbo does NOT dissolve P1** — pf-0 has now measured the matched 1500 arm and killed MTU outright (above).

.review/results/otp-12-worker-parity.records.codex.md:1394:.review/results/pf-0-rebaseline-decision.codex.md:2288:     9	- **P1 REPRODUCES ON A SECOND MAC (2026-07-13, `docs/bench/otp12-q-baseline-2026-07-13/`).** `wm_tcp_mixed` = **1.385 FAIL** on `q`↔netwatch-01 **at MTU 9000**, while all three controls PASS at **1.002–1.043** in the same session (so rig noise is ~2–4% and P1 is 10× outside it). **P1 is a property of the macOS↔Windows PAIRING, not of one machine** — the assumption **H1** rests on (corrected 2026-07-14: H5/H6/H7 are **P2** hypotheses; the earlier "H1/H5/H6/H7" was wrong), never tested until now. **And jumbo does NOT dissolve P1** — pf-0 has now measured the matched 1500 arm and killed MTU outright (above).
.review/results/otp-12-worker-parity.records.codex.md:1395:.review/results/pf-0-rebaseline-decision.codex.md:2291:    12	- **P1 (the headline invariance criterion) — the one thing between blit and shipping.** Fails rig W (`wm_tcp_mixed` 1.237 and 1.300 — do NOT read that as a regression, it is **two different Mac NICs**), but **PASSES 8/8 with Linux on both ends** (`docs/bench/otp12-perf-2026-07-13/`; P1's own cell 1.092/1.003). So it is **platform-INTERACTING, not pure layout** — yet **NOT exonerated**: a code path that only bites on one platform (H1's Windows accept branch) looks identical. **P1 HAS NO ESCAPE HATCH** (codex r5 F1): D-2026-07-12-1 waives only a *cross-direction* miss for a cell that ALREADY passes invariance — P1 *is* the invariance failure. So: **fix it to ≤1.10, or the owner amends acceptance criterion 1.** Neither is assumed.

.review/results/otp-12-worker-parity.records.codex.md:1422:.review/results/pf-0-rebaseline-decision.codex.md:5217:    11	- **P1 (the headline invariance criterion) — the one thing between blit and shipping.** Fails rig W (`wm_tcp_mixed` 1.237 and 1.300 — do NOT read that as a regression, it is **two different Mac NICs**), but **PASSES 8/8 with Linux on both ends** (`docs/bench/otp12-perf-2026-07-13/`; P1's own cell 1.092/1.003). So it is **platform-INTERACTING, not pure layout** — yet **NOT exonerated**: a code path that only bites on one platform (H1's Windows accept branch) looks identical. **P1 HAS NO ESCAPE HATCH** (codex r5 F1): D-2026-07-12-1 waives only a *cross-direction* miss for a cell that ALREADY passes invariance — P1 *is* the invariance failure. So: **fix it to ≤1.10, or the owner amends acceptance criterion 1.** Neither is assumed.

.review/results/otp-12-worker-parity.records.codex.md:1439:.review/results/macmac-harness-r5.codex.md:28:- Round 4 (grok, which reproduced it live): the equivalence margin was fixed to min(bar_breach, DELTA_REF) for the MEASURAND but left tied to the BAR for the CONTROLS -- so a control carrying a real, 8/8, rig-W-sized effect (d_i=230 at src=2500, ratio 1.092, bar PASS) landed as PARTIAL and ESCAPED RIG-VOID, and the session printed a clean VANISHES while every control was dirty.
.review/results/otp-12-worker-parity.records.codex.md:1440:.review/results/macmac-harness-r5.codex.md:38:4. Is the taxonomy EXHAUSTIVE, and is the session PRECEDENCE right? REPRODUCES now outranks UNSTABLE and BAR-FAIL-INCONSISTENT -- can that hide a rig problem, or leak a false positive?
.review/results/otp-12-worker-parity.records.codex.md:1442:.review/results/macmac-harness-r5.codex.md:112:- **BLOCKER** — [scripts/otp12pf_mac_verdict.py:228-255](/Users/michael/Dev/blit_v2/scripts/otp12pf_mac_verdict.py:228) — At `n=16`, `bar == "FAIL"` has no direction yet makes either sign material. With 13 paired `+1ms` differences and three `−110ms` differences, the CI is `[+1,+1]`, `p=.0213`, while marginal medians fail the bar in the inverse direction. The engine emits `REPRODUCES` for a `+1ms` effect with clean controls and zero voids.
.review/results/otp-12-worker-parity.records.codex.md:1443:.review/results/macmac-harness-r5.codex.md:114:- **BLOCKER** — [scripts/otp12pf_mac_verdict.py:340-354](/Users/michael/Dev/blit_v2/scripts/otp12pf_mac_verdict.py:340), [PREREGISTRATION.md:234-254](/Users/michael/Dev/blit_v2/docs/bench/otp12-macmac-2026-07-14/PREREGISTRATION.md:234) — The control fix covers only `PARTIAL`; `UNDERPOWERED` controls with `null_excl=false` escape. Null measurands plus all four controls at `src=2500`, `d=[0,230×7]` produced headline `VANISHES`; every control had `D=230`, `CI=[0,230]`, ratio `1.092`. This is the same materiality bug in another branch.

.review/results/otp-12-worker-parity.records.codex.md:1449:.review/results/macmac-harness-r2.codex.md:350:- **NEXT ACTION — rev 3 of the Mac↔Mac pre-registration + harness, then run it (Queue 1(ii); the second of the two experiments that come BEFORE any pf code).** Experiment (i), the A-B-B-A MTU run, is **DONE** (pf-0 below). Rig: nagatha `10.1.10.92` ↔ `q` `10.1.10.54`, both 10GbE/9000. Pre-registered (**rev 2**, codex round 1 = 9 findings, **9/9 accepted**): `docs/bench/otp12-macmac-2026-07-14/PREREGISTRATION.md`; harness `scripts/bench_otp12pf_mac.sh`. **⚠ CORRECTED 2026-07-14 — it is NOT an H1 discriminator.** The earlier claim here ("reproduces ⇒ **H1 DIES**, H1 accuses the *Windows* accept branch") was **WRONG and is retracted**: H1 accuses **blit's own code paths** (`SourceSockets` Dial/Accept, `add_dialed_stream`, the dial-before-ACK at `transfer_session/mod.rs:3113`) — **the word "Windows" appears nowhere in H1**, and that code runs on macOS too. A Mac↔Mac reproduction is therefore *consistent with* H1, not fatal to it. What the rig **does** answer: **does P1 require the macOS↔Windows PAIRING, or is it a platform-general cost of the destination-initiated layout?** Reproduces ⇒ P1 is **not platform residue** (no Windows peer needed) → the "accept it as platform residue" escape closes and every code-level hypothesis strengthens. Vanishes ⇒ P1 is **pairing-dependent** → platform-agnostic code mechanisms weaken. Either way the hypothesis space moves, which is why it precedes pf-1. **Then `pf-1`** (the HARD GATE), which two pf-0 results now BIND: (a) **between-session grading is dead** (a 20% recovery = 46 ms sits under the 78 ms between-session floor) — pf-1 must **measure its own paired within-session floor and register a resolution check** before grading; (b) **the fast arm is BISTABLE** — grade the run distribution, not the median.
.review/results/otp-12-worker-parity.records.codex.md:1450:.review/results/macmac-harness-r2.codex.md:354:- **P1 REPRODUCES ON A SECOND MAC (2026-07-13, `docs/bench/otp12-q-baseline-2026-07-13/`).** `wm_tcp_mixed` = **1.385 FAIL** on `q`↔netwatch-01 **at MTU 9000**, while all three controls PASS at **1.002–1.043** in the same session (so rig noise is ~2–4% and P1 is 10× outside it). **P1 is a property of the macOS↔Windows PAIRING, not of one machine** — the assumption **H1** rests on (corrected 2026-07-14: H5/H6/H7 are **P2** hypotheses; the earlier "H1/H5/H6/H7" was wrong), never tested until now. **And jumbo does NOT dissolve P1** — pf-0 has now measured the matched 1500 arm and killed MTU outright (above).

So it is **platform-INTERACTING, not pure layout** — yet **NOT exonerated**: a code path that only bites on one platform (H1's Windows accept branch) looks identical. **P1 HAS NO ESCAPE HATCH** (codex r5 F1): D-2026-07-12-1 waives only a *cross-direction* miss for a cell that ALREADY passes invariance — P1 *is* the invariance failure. So: **fix it to ≤1.10, or the owner amends acceptance criterion 1.** Neither is assumed.

.review/results/otp-12-worker-parity.records.codex.md:1536:.review/results/macmac-harness-r2.codex.md:5446:     5	- **NEXT ACTION — the MAC↔MAC RUN (Queue 1(ii)), pending its round-2 instrument review.** Design **rev 3** + the reworked harness are committed (`24660ae`): `docs/bench/otp12-macmac-2026-07-14/PREREGISTRATION.md`, `scripts/bench_otp12pf_mac.sh`, `scripts/otp12pf_mac_verdict.py` (mechanizes the rule), `scripts/otp12pf_mac_verdict_test.py` (guard test, mutation-proven). **NO DATA TAKEN YET.** Two codex rounds → **20 findings, 20/20 accepted** (design 9, instrument 11): `.review/results/macmac-{prereg,harness}.gpt-verdict.md`. **⚠ TO RUN IT THE OWNER MUST CLOSE THEIR CODEX SESSIONS** — nagatha is now a bench **END**, not just the driver, and the quiescence gate refuses to start while `codex`/`cargo`/`rustc` runs on **either** Mac (it already fired, correctly, on its first invocation). Time Machine is OFF on both Macs (required: the gate is fail-closed on autobackup merely *enabled*). Rig: nagatha `10.1.10.92` ↔ `q` `10.1.10.54`, both 10GbE/9000, build pinned `f35702a`. **Then `pf-1`** (the HARD GATE), which two pf-0 results BIND: between-session grading is dead (a 20% recovery = 46 ms sits under the 78 ms floor), so pf-1 must **measure its own paired within-session floor** before grading; and the fast arm is **BISTABLE** — grade the distribution, not the median.
.review/results/otp-12-worker-parity.records.codex.md:1537:.review/results/macmac-harness-r2.codex.md:5451:    10	- **P1 REPRODUCES ON A SECOND MAC (2026-07-13, `docs/bench/otp12-q-baseline-2026-07-13/`).** `wm_tcp_mixed` = **1.385 FAIL** on `q`↔netwatch-01 **at MTU 9000**, while all three controls PASS at **1.002–1.043** in the same session (so rig noise is ~2–4% and P1 is 10× outside it). **P1 is a property of the macOS↔Windows PAIRING, not of one machine** — the assumption **H1** rests on (corrected 2026-07-14: H5/H6/H7 are **P2** hypotheses; the earlier "H1/H5/H6/H7" was wrong), never tested until now. **And jumbo does NOT dissolve P1** — pf-0 has now measured the matched 1500 arm and killed MTU outright (above).
.review/results/otp-12-worker-parity.records.codex.md:1538:.review/results/macmac-harness-r2.codex.md:5454:    13	- **P1 (the headline invariance criterion) — the one thing between blit and shipping.** Fails rig W (`wm_tcp_mixed` 1.237 and 1.300 — do NOT read that as a regression, it is **two different Mac NICs**), but **PASSES 8/8 with Linux on both ends** (`docs/bench/otp12-perf-2026-07-13/`; P1's own cell 1.092/1.003). So it is **platform-INTERACTING, not pure layout** — yet **NOT exonerated**: a code path that only bites on one platform (H1's Windows accept branch) looks identical. **P1 HAS NO ESCAPE HATCH** (codex r5 F1): D-2026-07-12-1 waives only a *cross-direction* miss for a cell that ALREADY passes invariance — P1 *is* the invariance failure. So: **fix it to ≤1.10, or the owner amends acceptance criterion 1.** Neither is assumed.
.review/results/otp-12-worker-parity.records.codex.md:1541:.review/results/macmac-harness-r2.codex.md:5610:   207	        verdict = "REPRODUCES"
.review/results/otp-12-worker-parity.records.codex.md:1544:.review/results/macmac-harness-r2.codex.md:5661:- **HIGH — [scripts/otp12pf_mac_verdict.py:115](/Users/michael/Dev/blit_v2/scripts/otp12pf_mac_verdict.py:115)** — The symmetric `±0.10·src` equivalence interval is wrong for the symmetric ratio bar. The negative boundary is `-src/11`, about −9.09%. With `src=2000` and CI `[-190,0]`, the engine says `VANISHES`, although −190 implies an inversion ratio of 1.105. `powered` also tests CI width, not exclusion of the margins, and can record `powered_for_null=no` alongside `VANISHES`.
.review/results/otp-12-worker-parity.records.codex.md:1547:.review/results/macmac-harness-r2.codex.md:5692:- **HIGH — [scripts/otp12pf_mac_verdict.py:115](/Users/michael/Dev/blit_v2/scripts/otp12pf_mac_verdict.py:115)** — The symmetric `±0.10·src` equivalence interval is wrong for the symmetric ratio bar. The negative boundary is `-src/11`, about −9.09%. With `src=2000` and CI `[-190,0]`, the engine says `VANISHES`, although −190 implies an inversion ratio of 1.105. `powered` also tests CI width, not exclusion of the margins, and can record `powered_for_null=no` alongside `VANISHES`.
.review/results/otp-12-worker-parity.records.codex.md:1548:.review/results/d-2026-07-05-3-zerocopy.codex.md:1144:DEVLOG.md:82:**2026-06-12 03:55:00Z** - **CODER (autonomous overnight session)**: Owner authorized autonomous queue execution ("work on as much as you can. commit every slice as it lands. if anything gets questionable, stop." — scope: master only, no branches, local commits, never push, faster-mode WIP iff files-changed disjoint across pending sentinels; recorded in STATE.md). **7 slices completed and sentineled** (18 local commits, `1adbe0c..0af904e`): **w5-1-log-backend** (`56bda09`+`7145202`) — new `blit_core::stderr_log` backend (warn default, `BLIT_LOG` override, `<binary>: <level>: <msg>`) installed in all 4 binaries; ~22 formerly-discarded log::warn/error sites now visible; 9 bracketed stderr prefix families converged (warnings→facade, operator lines→`blit:`/`blitd:` prefixes, per-file pipeline spam→log::debug); trace-gated `[data-plane-client]` family kept (remote_parity.rs asserts the prefix); jobs-watch ticker vocabulary kept (single-command status output). **w9-5-jobs-lifecycle-e2e** (`ad773d8`) — first e2e net for detach/jobs verbs: 5 tests incl. cancel-active-delegated-job exit-0 made deterministic via a fake tonic source whose pull_sync never answers. **w2-1-delete-warmup-machinery** (`2a8a490`) — analyze_warmup_result + determine_tuning + warmup_gbps deleted; determine_remote_tuning is one honest static table (production values bit-identical); 4 tier tests added. **w9-1-ungate-windows-tests** (`9324559`) — 27 blanket #[cfg(unix)] removed from 8 remote-transfer test files (none unix-specific); 4 genuinely unix files kept gated; Windows CI will now actually compile parity-critical paths. **w9-2-revive-root-tests** (`461525d`) — dead root tests/ relocated into crates/blit-core/tests (+24 tests incl. the only MirrorPlanner coverage); connection.rs deleted; drift fixed (for_tests→load(), compare_mode, 32→300 files vs TINY_FILE_LIMIT=256, serial Mutex for config-dir globals); AGENTS.md §4 corrected. **w9-4-readonly-enforcement-tests** (`4d67210`) — TestContext::new_read_only() knob + 3 e2e tests covering all 3 read-only write gates (push/purge/delegated-pull), asserting error text + no destination mutation. **w8-1b-zero-copy-fast-eval** (`6189d82`) — analysis-only: `docs/plan/ZERO_COPY_RECEIVE_EVAL.md` (Draft) recommends DELETING zero_copy.rs (wire path is splice-friendly but the dead impl busy-waits on EAGAIN, the AsyncRead-generic pipeline resists raw-fd wiring, and the CPU win is unmeasured pending the 10GbE rig); owner verdict is the gate. **Suite: 1331 → 1368 tests, fmt/clippy/test green after every slice.** Remaining `[ ]` rows are all overlap-blocked behind the 7 pending sentinels (skip map in STATE.md), owner-gated (w2-3 plan interview), or dependent (w2-4, w6-2, w8-1). Transient note: first post-w5-1 workspace test run showed 11 "daemon failed to listen" failures under cold parallel load; isolated and clean re-runs green — same harness-contention class w9-3 addresses.
.review/results/otp-12-worker-parity.records.codex.md:1549:.review/results/d-2026-07-05-3-zerocopy.codex.md:1317:docs/audit/DESIGN_MAP_2026-06-11.md:473:- `DEFAULT_BLOCK_SIZE / MAX_BLOCK_SIZE` = 1 MiB / 64 MiB (MAX at line 19) (/home/michael/dev/Blit/crates/blit-core/src/copy/file_copy/resume.rs:16) — SIMPLE-positive (client sends block_size:0 = 'use default' at blit-cli/src/transfers/remote.rs:383); daemon clamps to MAX at pull_sync.rs:84. Fixed 1 MiB granularity is never adapted to file size or link speed (mild FAST tension).

.review/results/otp-12-worker-parity.records.codex.md:1612:.review/results/macmac-harness-r9.codex.md:2672:docs/STATE.md:15:- **P1 REPRODUCES ON A SECOND MAC (2026-07-13, `docs/bench/otp12-q-baseline-2026-07-13/`).** `wm_tcp_mixed` = **1.385 FAIL** on `q`↔netwatch-01 **at MTU 9000**, while all three controls PASS at **1.002–1.043** in the same session (so rig noise is ~2–4% and P1 is 10× outside it). **P1 is a property of the macOS↔Windows PAIRING, not of one machine** — the assumption **H1** rests on (corrected 2026-07-14: H5/H6/H7 are **P2** hypotheses; the earlier "H1/H5/H6/H7" was wrong), never tested until now. **And jumbo does NOT dissolve P1** — pf-0 has now measured the matched 1500 arm and killed MTU outright (above).
.review/results/otp-12-worker-parity.records.codex.md:1613:.review/results/macmac-harness-r9.codex.md:2673:docs/STATE.md:18:- **P1 (the headline invariance criterion) — the one thing between blit and shipping.** Fails rig W (`wm_tcp_mixed` 1.237 and 1.300 — do NOT read that as a regression, it is **two different Mac NICs**), but **PASSES 8/8 with Linux on both ends** (`docs/bench/otp12-perf-2026-07-13/`; P1's own cell 1.092/1.003). So it is **platform-INTERACTING, not pure layout** — yet **NOT exonerated**: a code path that only bites on one platform (H1's Windows accept branch) looks identical. **P1 HAS NO ESCAPE HATCH** (codex r5 F1): D-2026-07-12-1 waives only a *cross-direction* miss for a cell that ALREADY passes invariance — P1 *is* the invariance failure. So: **fix it to ≤1.10, or the owner amends acceptance criterion 1.** Neither is assumed.
.review/results/otp-12-worker-parity.records.codex.md:1614:.review/results/macmac-harness-r9.codex.md:2687:.review/results/macmac-harness-r2.grok.md:12:| 2 | `otp12pf_mac_verdict.py:115–140`; prereg §POWER GATE | **BLOCKER** | **VANISHES equivalence margin is `0.10 × srcinit_median`, not the reference effect.** `DELTA_REF_MS=230` is written to CSV only (line 35, 155) and **never used** in the decision. A true, tight effect of 230 ms on a 2500 ms arm (ratio 1.092, bar PASS, CI=[230,230] ⊂ ±250) yields **VANISHES** — i.e. “P1 absent / Windows may be required” while a rig-W-sized absolute Δ is present. Same class: all `d_i=190`, `src=2000` → VANISHES (breach=200). |
.review/results/otp-12-worker-parity.records.codex.md:1615:.review/results/macmac-harness-r9.codex.md:2695:.review/results/macmac-harness-r2.grok.md:20:| 10 | `otp12pf_mac_verdict.py:111` | **LOW** | Integer bar: exact 1.10 ratio is **PASS** (`10*hi <= 11*lo`). A precise 10% effect never REPRODUCES. |
.review/results/otp-12-worker-parity.records.codex.md:1616:.review/results/macmac-harness-r9.codex.md:2702:.review/results/macmac-prereg.codex.md:83:- `docs/bench/otp12-macmac-2026-07-14/PREREGISTRATION.md:22-34` — **BLOCKER** — H1 is mischaracterized as Windows-exclusive. The parent defines a generic resize-time accept/dial asymmetry and says controlled ablations must kill or confirm it (`OTP12_PERF_FINDINGS.md:183-209,380-399`). Mac↔Mac reproduction could be H1 itself—or an unrelated phenocopy—while rig-W remains caused by Windows. A null supports pairing dependence, not H1 specifically.

.review/results/otp-12-worker-parity.records.codex.md:1708:.review/results/otp-12-worker-parity.arbitration-fix.codex.md:14:Review the diff of commit 641916e (run: git show 641916e). It is the follow-up for otp-12-worker-parity after the review of 8e993aa found that tuner cooldown/sustain/direction were computed outside resize epoch arbitration and that the concurrency test depended on scheduler luck. Check that resize_tick now serializes eligibility, signal, live/target derivation, and claim coherently with accepted/refused settlement; that shape and tuner producers cannot reopen or skip epochs; that no lock spans await, socket I/O, or callbacks and no lock-order/deadlock, cancellation, teardown, tuning, or first-byte regression was introduced; that the deterministic test truly forces the accepted and refused interleavings in debug and release builds; that settlement side effects execute in optimized builds; that the exact role-independent worker target and byte/tree invariants remain protected; and that test count did not drop. Output a concise markdown findings list — each finding with file:line, severity, rationale — then a final VERDICT line. Be concise; do not invoke skills.

Check that the new test-only settlement hook reports an actual try_lock result, the guard-owned acquisition identity is tied to the MutexGuard and recorded at eligibility and epoch claim, and dropping/reacquiring the guard after the hook now fails deterministically regardless of waiter scheduling. Check that cfg(test) instrumentation cannot change production settlement behavior, release compilation remains correct, no lock-order/deadlock or hang was added, and refused settlement, tuning, cancellation, exact target-8 role tests, gated first-byte/target-4/tree invariants, and the full test count remain intact. Output a concise markdown findings list — each finding with file:line, severity, rationale — then a final VERDICT line. Be concise; do not invoke skills.

.review/results/otp-12-worker-parity.records.codex.md:1724:.review/results/d-2026-07-05-2-compat.codex.md:300: **2026-07-03 16:26:55Z** - **CODER (ue-r2-1c, claude)**: Landed the engine shell (REV4 `ue-r2-1c`, third slice through the code→GPT-review→fix loop). New `crates/blit-core/src/engine/` module: `TransferEngine::execute(EngineRequest)` owns strategy selection (single-file → journal probe → fast-path walk → streaming) and the streaming leg (tuning window → scan → diff/plan → sink pipeline → mirror deletions → journal checkpoints → history/predictor); `TransferOrchestrator` shrank to the local adapter (preconditions, `FsTransferSource`/`FilteredSource` + `FsTransferSink`/`NullSink` construction, option translation) with the public API preserved via re-exports — engine type thereby ratified per REV4 Design §1 (new engine + adapter, not a rename). Sequence: behavior pins first (`7730eb1`: NoWork/UpToDate + `no_work` tag, NoWork/SourceEmpty — both previously untested), the move (`dc9b0ed`: `fast_path.rs`→`engine/strategy.rs`, options/summary/history moved, tuning/mirror/journal/single-file split out; move fidelity machine-checked — 75 diff lines vs origin, all six documented transformations, zero drift), then the REV4-named accounting gap (`29e210b`: single-file strategy now records perf history tag `single_file` / `null_sink` lane + predictor, guard test proven by revert). Review: first codex run died on provider quota (121k tokens, recorded); owner restored access same day; retry PASS with 1 Low, accepted + fixed (`15e6334`: engine back-references through `crate::orchestrator` → engine-local paths; swept one more the reviewer missed). Also recorded owner-provided 10GbE test env (`admin@skippy:/mnt/generic-pool/video/test`) in STATE queue item 4. Validation: fmt/clippy clean, `cargo test --workspace` 1394 / 0 / 2 (baseline 1391, +3). All on master, unpushed. Next: `ue-r2-1d` (streaming plan foundation) — or `1e`/w4-1 per queue.

Both verified.json verdicts created, sentinels deleted via git rm, and REVIEW.md status rows updated to `[x]`. Validation suite re-run: cargo fmt, clippy, and cargo test --workspace all passed successfully (1370 passed, 0 failed, 1 ignored).
.review/results/otp-12-worker-parity.records.codex.md:1726:.review/results/d-2026-07-05-2-compat.codex.md:318: **2026-06-12 03:55:00Z** - **CODER (autonomous overnight session)**: Owner authorized autonomous queue execution ("work on as much as you can. commit every slice as it lands. if anything gets questionable, stop." — scope: master only, no branches, local commits, never push, faster-mode WIP iff files-changed disjoint across pending sentinels; recorded in STATE.md). **7 slices completed and sentineled** (18 local commits, `1adbe0c..0af904e`): **w5-1-log-backend** (`56bda09`+`7145202`) — new `blit_core::stderr_log` backend (warn default, `BLIT_LOG` override, `<binary>: <level>: <msg>`) installed in all 4 binaries; ~22 formerly-discarded log::warn/error sites now visible; 9 bracketed stderr prefix families converged (warnings→facade, operator lines→`blit:`/`blitd:` prefixes, per-file pipeline spam→log::debug); trace-gated `[data-plane-client]` family kept (remote_parity.rs asserts the prefix); jobs-watch ticker vocabulary kept (single-command status output). **w9-5-jobs-lifecycle-e2e** (`ad773d8`) — first e2e net for detach/jobs verbs: 5 tests incl. cancel-active-delegated-job exit-0 made deterministic via a fake tonic source whose pull_sync never answers. **w2-1-delete-warmup-machinery** (`2a8a490`) — analyze_warmup_result + determine_tuning + warmup_gbps deleted; determine_remote_tuning is one honest static table (production values bit-identical); 4 tier tests added. **w9-1-ungate-windows-tests** (`9324559`) — 27 blanket #[cfg(unix)] removed from 8 remote-transfer test files (none unix-specific); 4 genuinely unix files kept gated; Windows CI will now actually compile parity-critical paths. **w9-2-revive-root-tests** (`461525d`) — dead root tests/ relocated into crates/blit-core/tests (+24 tests incl. the only MirrorPlanner coverage); connection.rs deleted; drift fixed (for_tests→load(), compare_mode, 32→300 files vs TINY_FILE_LIMIT=256, serial Mutex for config-dir globals); AGENTS.md §4 corrected. **w9-4-readonly-enforcement-tests** (`4d67210`) — TestContext::new_read_only() knob + 3 e2e tests covering all 3 read-only write gates (push/purge/delegated-pull), asserting error text + no destination mutation. **w8-1b-zero-copy-fast-eval** (`6189d82`) — analysis-only: `docs/plan/ZERO_COPY_RECEIVE_EVAL.md` (Draft) recommends DELETING zero_copy.rs (wire path is splice-friendly but the dead impl busy-waits on EAGAIN, the AsyncRead-generic pipeline resists raw-fd wiring, and the CPU win is unmeasured pending the 10GbE rig); owner verdict is the gate. **Suite: 1331 → 1368 tests, fmt/clippy/test green after every slice.** Remaining `[ ]` rows are all overlap-blocked behind the 7 pending sentinels (skip map in STATE.md), owner-gated (w2-3 plan interview), or dependent (w2-4, w6-2, w8-1). Transient note: first post-w5-1 workspace test run showed 11 "daemon failed to listen" failures under cold parallel load; isolated and clean re-runs green — same harness-contention class w9-3 addresses.

.review/results/otp-12-worker-parity.records.codex.md:1729:.review/results/d-2026-07-05-2-compat.codex.md:1097:docs/audit/inventory/plan-perf.md:816:**Description**: The bench output includes `streams` (data-plane stream count) and `tcp_fallback_used: bool`, modeling TCP↔gRPC fallback as a binary toggle. The 10GbE plan instead frames "TCP vs gRPC fallback: TCP should be 2-5× faster" as a measurement question. These are consistent in spirit but slightly mismatched: the verb plan presumes a single negotiated path per run, while the 10GbE plan implies separate runs to compare paths. Minor.
