     1	syntax = "proto3";
     2	package blit.v2;
     3
     4	// The main service for all data transfer and remote management operations.
     5	service Blit {
     6	  // ONE_TRANSFER_PATH (otp-1): the single role-tagged transfer session.
     7	  // Contract: docs/TRANSFER_SESSION.md. This is the ONLY byte-moving
     8	  // RPC. Deleted at cutover (otp-10c-2, D-2026-07-05-1): rpc Push and
     9	  // rpc PullSync, with every message only they referenced (their
    10	  // exclusive frame sets, DataTransferNegotiation, the old summaries,
    11	  // and TransferOperationSpec.metadata_only — the relay scan flag,
    12	  // whose caller was removed at otp-10c-1). Earlier deletion, same
    13	  // rule: rpc Pull (2026-07-03, ue-r2-1h). No bridge, no
    14	  // version-tolerance — same-build peers only (D-2026-07-05-2).
    15	  rpc Transfer(stream TransferFrame) returns (stream TransferFrame);
    16
    17	  // Lists contents of a remote directory.
    18	  rpc List(ListRequest) returns (ListResponse);
    19
    20	  // Deletes files/directories on the server for mirror operations.
    21	  rpc Purge(PurgeRequest) returns (PurgeResponse);
    22
    23	  // Provides path completion suggestions for a given remote path prefix.
    24	  rpc CompletePath(CompletionRequest) returns (CompletionResponse);
    25
    26	  // Lists the available modules on the server.
    27	  rpc ListModules(ListModulesRequest) returns (ListModulesResponse);
    28
    29	  // Recursively finds files/directories starting at a module path.
    30	  rpc Find(FindRequest) returns (stream FindEntry);
    31
    32	  // Summarises disk usage for a subtree (du-style).
     1	//! Client-side entry for initiating a unified transfer session.
     2	//!
     3	//! [`run_push_session`] declares the SOURCE role (push-equivalent,
     4	//! otp-4): open the `Transfer` RPC, stream the manifest + payloads; the
     5	//! daemon answers as the DESTINATION Responder. [`run_pull_session`]
     6	//! declares the DESTINATION role (pull-equivalent, otp-5a): the daemon
     7	//! answers as the SOURCE Responder and streams its module tree, which
     8	//! this end diffs and writes. Both build a gRPC-backed [`FrameTransport`]
     9	//! over `BlitClient::transfer` and run the matching role driver; role is
    10	//! carried in `SessionOpen.initiator_role`, never a second code path.
    11	//!
    12	//! Verb wiring: the push-shaped verb (CLI `copy`/`mirror`/`move` to a
    13	//! remote destination, TUI F1 push) rides [`run_push_session`] since
    14	//! otp-10a via `blit_app::transfers::remote::run_remote_push`; the
    15	//! pull-shaped verb (remote source → local destination, TUI F3 pull)
    16	//! rides [`run_pull_session`] since otp-10b-2 via
    17	//! `blit_app::transfers::remote::run_remote_pull`. Both push (otp-4b)
    18	//! and pull (otp-5b) default to the TCP data plane; the in-stream
    19	//! carrier is the requested fallback either direction.
    20
    21	use std::path::PathBuf;
    22	use std::sync::Arc;
    23	use std::time::Duration;
    24
    25	use eyre::{eyre, Result};
    26	use tokio::sync::mpsc;
    27	use tokio_stream::wrappers::ReceiverStream;
    28	use tonic::transport::{Channel, Endpoint};
    29
    30	use crate::generated::blit_client::BlitClient;
    31	use crate::generated::{
    32	    ComparisonMode, FilterSpec, MirrorMode, ResumeSettings, SessionOpen, TransferRole,
    33	    TransferSummary,
    34	};
    35	use crate::remote::endpoint::{RemoteEndpoint, RemotePath};
    36	use crate::remote::transfer::source::TransferSource;
    37	use crate::remote::transfer::{ByteProgressSink, RemoteTransferProgress};
    38	use crate::transfer_plan::PlanOptions;
    39	use crate::transfer_session::transport::{grpc_client_transport, GRPC_CHANNEL_FRAMES};
    40	use crate::transfer_session::{
    41	    run_destination, run_source, DestinationInstruments, DestinationOutcome,
    42	    DestinationSessionConfig, DestinationTarget, HelloConfig, SessionEndpoint, SourceInstruments,
    43	    SourceSessionConfig,
    44	};
    45
   105	            trace_data_plane: false,
   106	        }
   107	    }
   108	}
   109
   110	/// Connect to `endpoint`'s daemon and run one SOURCE-role transfer
   111	/// session pushing `source`'s tree into the endpoint's module/path.
   112	/// Returns the destination-computed [`TransferSummary`] (contract:
   113	/// DESTINATION is the scorer).
   114	pub async fn run_push_session(
   115	    endpoint: &RemoteEndpoint,
   116	    source: Arc<dyn TransferSource>,
   117	    options: PushSessionOptions,
   118	) -> Result<TransferSummary> {
   119	    // The responder resolves module→root; the initiator's own local
   120	    // path never crosses the wire (contract §SessionOpen).
   121	    let (module, path) = endpoint_module_path(endpoint)?;
   122
   123	    let mut client = connect_transfer_client(endpoint).await?;
   124
   125	    let open = SessionOpen {
   126	        initiator_role: TransferRole::Source as i32,
   127	        module,
   128	        path,
   129	        compare_mode: options.compare_mode as i32,
   130	        ignore_existing: options.ignore_existing,
   131	        require_complete_scan: options.require_complete_scan,
   132	        // otp-4b: default to the TCP data plane; the responder grants it
   133	        // in SessionAccept unless this asks for the in-stream fallback.
   134	        in_stream_bytes: options.in_stream_bytes,
   135	        // otp-7b: resume rides the open (plan D6 — the flag is in the
   136	        // open, so resume runs identically whichever end initiated).
   137	        resume: options.resume.then_some(ResumeSettings {
   138	            enabled: true,
   139	            block_size: options.resume_block_size,
   140	        }),
   141	        // otp-10a: filter + mirror ride the open (otp-6a/6b session
   142	        // support; this is the client wiring, symmetric with pull's
   143	        // otp-9a).
   144	        filter: options.filter,
   145	        mirror_enabled: options.mirror_enabled,
   146	        mirror_kind: options.mirror_kind as i32,
   147	        ..Default::default()
   148	    };
   149
   150	    // Open the bidi RPC: the request stream is fed by `out_tx`, the
   151	    // response stream is the inbound half. The handler returns its
   152	    // response stream immediately (it spawns the session), so this
   153	    // await resolves before any frame flows — no deadlock.
   154	    let (out_tx, out_rx) = mpsc::channel(GRPC_CHANNEL_FRAMES);
   155	    let inbound = client
   156	        .transfer(ReceiverStream::new(out_rx))
   157	        .await
   158	        .map_err(|status| eyre::Report::new(transfer_open_refusal(status)))?
   159	        .into_inner();
   160	    let transport = grpc_client_transport(out_tx, inbound);
   161
   162	    // otp-10a: own the unreadable-scan accumulator so a partial source
   163	    // scan fails the push after the session completes — the old push
   164	    // driver's exact posture (send what's readable, then error), which
   165	    // `blit move`'s source-delete gate relies on: an error here means
   166	    // move never deletes a source whose files were silently skipped.
   167	    let unreadable: Arc<std::sync::Mutex<Vec<String>>> = Arc::default();
   168
   169	    let cfg = SourceSessionConfig {
   170	        hello: HelloConfig::default(),
   171	        endpoint: SessionEndpoint::initiator(open),
   172	        plan_options: options.plan_options,
   173	        // The initiator dials the data plane on the same host it reached
   174	        // the control plane on (contract §Transport: initiator dials).
   175	        data_plane_host: Some(endpoint.host.clone()),
   176	        instruments: SourceInstruments {
   177	            progress: options.progress,
   178	            unreadable: Some(Arc::clone(&unreadable)),
   179	            trace_data_plane: options.trace_data_plane,
   180	        },
   270	/// DESTINATION, so the daemon becomes the SOURCE Responder (streaming
   271	/// its module tree). Returns the [`DestinationOutcome`] this end
   272	/// computed (contract: the DESTINATION is the scorer).
   273	///
   274	/// otp-5b: the default carrier is the TCP data plane — the SOURCE
   275	/// responder binds+grants+accepts sockets while sending, and this
   276	/// DESTINATION initiator dials + receives over them (the transport/role
   277	/// decoupling). `PullSessionOptions::in_stream_bytes` forces the in-stream
   278	/// fallback (diagnostics / unreachable data plane).
   279	pub async fn run_pull_session(
   280	    endpoint: &RemoteEndpoint,
   281	    dest_root: PathBuf,
   282	    options: PullSessionOptions,
   283	) -> Result<DestinationOutcome> {
   284	    let client = connect_transfer_client(endpoint).await?;
   285	    run_pull_session_with_client(client, endpoint, dest_root, options).await
   286	}
   287
   288	/// [`run_pull_session`] over an already-connected client (otp-9b). The
   289	/// delegated dst daemon connects separately so a connect failure keeps
   290	/// its own error phase (`ConnectSource`) structurally, without string
   291	/// matching on the session error.
   292	pub async fn run_pull_session_with_client(
   293	    mut client: BlitClient<Channel>,
   294	    endpoint: &RemoteEndpoint,
   295	    dest_root: PathBuf,
   296	    options: PullSessionOptions,
   297	) -> Result<DestinationOutcome> {
   298	    let (module, path) = endpoint_module_path(endpoint)?;
   299
   300	    let open = SessionOpen {
   301	        initiator_role: TransferRole::Destination as i32,
   302	        module,
   303	        path,
   304	        compare_mode: options.compare_mode as i32,
   305	        ignore_existing: options.ignore_existing,
   306	        require_complete_scan: options.require_complete_scan,
   307	        // otp-5b: default to the TCP data plane; the SOURCE responder
   308	        // grants it in SessionAccept unless this asks for the in-stream
   309	        // fallback.
   310	        in_stream_bytes: options.in_stream_bytes,
   311	        // otp-7b: resume rides the open, role-agnostic (plan D6).
   312	        resume: options.resume.then_some(ResumeSettings {
   313	            enabled: true,
   314	            block_size: options.resume_block_size,
   315	        }),
   316	        // otp-9a: filter + mirror ride the open (otp-6a/6b session
   317	        // support; this is the client wiring).
   318	        filter: options.filter,
   319	        mirror_enabled: options.mirror_enabled,
   320	        mirror_kind: options.mirror_kind as i32,
   321	        ..Default::default()
   322	    };
   323
   324	    let (out_tx, out_rx) = mpsc::channel(GRPC_CHANNEL_FRAMES);
   325	    let inbound = client
   326	        .transfer(ReceiverStream::new(out_rx))
   327	        .await
   328	        .map_err(|status| eyre::Report::new(transfer_open_refusal(status)))?
   329	        .into_inner();
   330	    let transport = grpc_client_transport(out_tx, inbound);
   331
   332	    let cfg = DestinationSessionConfig {
   333	        hello: HelloConfig::default(),
   334	        endpoint: SessionEndpoint::initiator(open),
   335	        // The initiator dials the data plane on the same host it reached
   336	        // the control plane on (contract §Transport: initiator dials).
   337	        data_plane_host: Some(endpoint.host.clone()),
   338	        instruments: DestinationInstruments {
   339	            progress: options.progress,
   340	            byte_progress: options.byte_progress,
   341	            trace_data_plane: options.trace_data_plane,
   342	        },
   343	        local_apply: None,
   344	    };
   345	    run_destination(cfg, transport, DestinationTarget::Fixed(dest_root)).await
    20	//!   [`DelegatedPullOutcome`] — delegated remote→remote
    21	//!   orchestration. Builds the `DelegatedPullRequest`, connects
    22	//!   to the destination's `BlitClient`, consumes the streamed
    23	//!   payload (ManifestBatch / BytesProgress / Summary / Error),
    24	//!   maps errors via [`map_delegated_error`], and returns the
    25	//!   summary. The `on_started` callback fires once when the
    26	//!   destination emits its `Started` event, giving the caller a
    27	//!   live hook for verbose-mode diagnostics without baking
    28	//!   presentation into the library.
    29	//! - [`run_remote_push`] + [`PushExecution`] +
    30	//!   [`PushExecutionOutcome`] — push entry-point orchestration.
    31	//!   The library wraps the local source root in an
    32	//!   `FsTransferSource` and runs the unified transfer session
    33	//!   as its SOURCE (`run_push_session`, otp-10a) — the old
    34	//!   per-direction `RemotePushClient::push` driver is no longer
    35	//!   reachable from any verb and is deleted at otp-10c. A push
    36	//!   source is local by construction: `--relay-via-cli` (the
    37	//!   one remote-source push shape) was removed at otp-10c-1
    38	//!   (D-2026-07-11-1). The CLI-side progress monitor stays in
    39	//!   `blit-cli` (M-C `AppProgressEvent` reshape is its own
    40	//!   pause point).
    41	//!
    42	//! No further `transfers/remote.rs` orchestration lives in
    43	//! `blit-cli` after this slice — the CLI's `transfers/remote.rs`
    44	//! retains only the clap-arg wrappers and presentation
    45	//! (progress monitor + JSON / human printers).
    46
    47	use blit_core::generated::delegated_pull_error::Phase as DelegatedPullPhase;
    48	use blit_core::generated::delegated_pull_progress::Payload as DelegatedPayload;
    49	use blit_core::generated::{
    50	    BytesProgress, ComparisonMode, DelegatedPullRequest, DelegatedPullStarted,
    51	    DelegatedPullSummary, FilterSpec, MirrorMode, RemoteSourceLocator, TransferSummary,
    52	};
    53	use blit_core::remote::transfer::operation_spec::{
    54	    delegated_spec_from_options, DelegatedSpecOptions,
    55	};
    56	use blit_core::remote::transfer::session_client::{
    57	    run_pull_session, run_push_session, PullSessionOptions, PushSessionOptions,
    58	};
    59	use blit_core::remote::transfer::source::{FsTransferSource, TransferSource};
    60	use blit_core::remote::transfer::RemoteTransferProgress;
    61	use blit_core::remote::{RemoteEndpoint, RemotePath};
    62	use eyre::{bail, eyre, Context, Result};
    63	use std::path::{Path, PathBuf};
    64	use std::sync::Arc;
    65	use tonic::Code;
    66
    67	/// Inputs for [`run_remote_push`]. Primitive fields only — no
    68	/// clap, no presentation. CLI builds this from `&TransferArgs`;
    69	/// the TUI builds it directly.
    70	///
   125	/// into its own `DeferredPushState`.
   126	pub struct PushExecutionOutcome {
   127	    pub summary: TransferSummary,
   128	    pub destination: String,
   129	}
   130
   131	/// Run a remote push end-to-end (otp-10a: the push-shaped verb on the
   132	/// unified transfer session): wrap the local source root in an
   133	/// `FsTransferSource`, then initiate one SOURCE-role `Transfer`
   134	/// session against the destination daemon via `run_push_session`. No
   135	/// mirror-purge step exists on the push side — mirror deletes happen
   136	/// on the DESTINATION (the one delete rule, otp-6b) and surface
   137	/// through the returned summary's `entries_deleted`.
   138	///
   139	/// A source scan that skips unreadable files fails the call after the
   140	/// transfer (the readable subset still lands) — same posture as the
   141	/// old driver; `blit move`'s source-delete gate relies on that error.
   142	///
   143	/// `progress` is borrowed for the duration of the session. The caller
   144	/// owns the channel + monitor task; this function never spawns or
   145	/// awaits the monitor. Standard lifecycle:
   146	///
   147	/// ```text
   148	/// let (handle, task) = spawn_progress_monitor(...);
   149	/// let outcome = run_remote_push(execution, handle.as_ref()).await?;
   150	/// drop(handle);
   151	/// if let Some(t) = task { let _ = t.await; }
   152	/// ```
   153	///
   154	/// Unlike the pull side, there is no need to split this into
   155	/// pre-/post-purge halves — push has no post-RPC destructive
   156	/// step on the caller's filesystem, so the monitor's lifetime
   157	/// already lines up cleanly with the RPC.
   158	pub async fn run_remote_push(
   159	    execution: PushExecution,
   160	    progress: Option<&RemoteTransferProgress>,
   161	) -> Result<PushExecutionOutcome> {
   162	    let source: Arc<dyn TransferSource> = Arc::new(FsTransferSource::new(execution.source));
   163
   164	    let options = PushSessionOptions {
   165	        compare_mode: execution.compare_mode,
   166	        ignore_existing: execution.ignore_existing,
   167	        require_complete_scan: execution.require_complete_scan,
   168	        // `--force-grpc`: the session's in-stream byte carrier is the
   169	        // gRPC-fallback lane (otp-8).
   170	        in_stream_bytes: execution.force_grpc,
   171	        resume: execution.resume,
   172	        resume_block_size: execution.resume_block_size,
   173	        filter: execution.filter,
   174	        mirror_enabled: execution.mirror_mode,
   175	        mirror_kind: if execution.mirror_mode {
   176	            execution.mirror_kind
   177	        } else {
   178	            MirrorMode::Off
   179	        },
   180	        progress: progress.cloned(),
   181	        trace_data_plane: execution.trace_data_plane,
   182	        ..PushSessionOptions::default()
   183	    };
   184
   185	    let summary = run_push_session(&execution.remote, source, options)
   186	        .await
   187	        .with_context(|| format!("pushing to {}", execution.remote_label))?;
   188
   189	    Ok(PushExecutionOutcome {
   190	        summary,
   191	        destination: execution.remote_label,
   192	    })
   193	}
   194
   195	/// Inputs for [`run_remote_pull`] (otp-10b-2: the pull-shaped verb on
   196	/// the unified transfer session). Primitive fields only — no clap, no
   197	/// presentation. CLI builds this from `&TransferArgs`; the TUI builds
   198	/// it directly. Field semantics match [`PushExecution`] exactly (the
   199	/// one option surface, roles flipped): `filter` rides
   200	/// `SessionOpen.filter` and is applied by the daemon SOURCE through
   201	/// the universal `FilteredSource` chokepoint; `mirror_kind` carries
   202	/// `--delete-scope`; `compare_mode` comes from the one
   203	/// `transfers::compare` mapping.
   204	pub struct PullExecution {
   205	    pub remote: RemoteEndpoint,
   230	/// DESTINATION computed (contract: the end that wrote the bytes is the
   231	/// scorer — here that's us) plus the destination root echoed back for
   232	/// the printer. Mirror deletions ran in-session (the one delete rule,
   233	/// otp-6b) and are scored in `summary.entries_deleted` — there is no
   234	/// post-transfer purge step and no separate purge stats.
   235	pub struct PullVerbOutcome {
   236	    pub summary: TransferSummary,
   237	    pub dest_root: PathBuf,
   238	}
   239
   240	/// Run a remote pull end-to-end (otp-10b-2): initiate one
   241	/// DESTINATION-role `Transfer` session against the source daemon via
   242	/// `run_pull_session`. The daemon becomes the SOURCE responder and
   243	/// streams its module tree; this end diffs, receives, and (in mirror
   244	/// mode) deletes extraneous local entries at SourceDone.
   245	///
   246	/// `progress` is borrowed for the duration of the session — the caller
   247	/// owns the channel + monitor task, exactly as with
   248	/// [`run_remote_push`]. Unlike the old pull there is no post-RPC
   249	/// destructive step (deletes are in-session), so the monitor's
   250	/// lifetime lines up with the one call and the run_pull_sync /
   251	/// apply_pull_mirror_purge split does not apply here. Move's
   252	/// remote-source delete remains a caller-side follow-up, gated by
   253	/// `require_complete_scan`.
   254	pub async fn run_remote_pull(
   255	    execution: PullExecution,
   256	    progress: Option<&RemoteTransferProgress>,
   257	) -> Result<PullVerbOutcome> {
   258	    // No pre-created destination directories: the session sink creates
   259	    // each write target's parent chain itself (including the
   260	    // single-file case, where `dest_root` IS the target file path and
   261	    // must never be mkdir'd) — pinned by
   262	    // `single_file_pull_lands_at_the_target_file_path` against a
   263	    // missing parent. The old pull's explicit parent-creation step is
   264	    // redundant on this path.
   265	    let options = PullSessionOptions {
   266	        compare_mode: execution.compare_mode,
   267	        ignore_existing: execution.ignore_existing,
   268	        require_complete_scan: execution.require_complete_scan,
   269	        // `--force-grpc`: the session's in-stream byte carrier is the
   270	        // gRPC-fallback lane (otp-8).
   271	        in_stream_bytes: execution.force_grpc,
   272	        resume: execution.resume,
   273	        resume_block_size: execution.resume_block_size,
   274	        filter: execution.filter,
   275	        mirror_enabled: execution.mirror_mode,
   276	        mirror_kind: if execution.mirror_mode {
   277	            execution.mirror_kind
   278	        } else {
   279	            MirrorMode::Off
   280	        },
   281	        byte_progress: None,
   282	        progress: progress.cloned(),
   283	        trace_data_plane: execution.trace_data_plane,
   284	    };
   285	    let outcome = run_pull_session(&execution.remote, execution.dest_root.clone(), options)
   286	        .await
   287	        .with_context(|| {
   288	            format!(
   289	                "pulling from {} into {}",
   290	                execution.remote_label,
   291	                execution.dest_root.display()
   292	            )
   293	        })?;
   294
   295	    Ok(PullVerbOutcome {
   296	        summary: outcome.summary,
   297	        dest_root: execution.dest_root,
   298	    })
   299	}
   300
     1	//! Unified transfer session — the ONE block of transfer code
     2	//! (docs/plan/ONE_TRANSFER_PATH.md, D-2026-07-05-1).
     3	//!
     4	//! A transfer has a SOURCE role and a DESTINATION role; which end
     5	//! initiated and which CLI verb was used select roles, never code.
     6	//! Both roles run the drivers below over a [`transport::FrameTransport`];
     7	//! the wire contract they implement — phases, frame table, record
     8	//! grammar, error semantics — is `docs/TRANSFER_SESSION.md` (otp-1).
     9	//!
    10	//! otp-3 scope: the role-parameterized state machine over the existing
    11	//! engine with the in-process transport and the in-stream byte
    12	//! carrier. The TCP data plane, daemon serving, ActiveJobs/cancel and
    13	//! progress wiring land at otp-4; mirror otp-6; resume otp-7;
    14	//! delegated otp-9 (see the slice list in the plan).
    15
    16	mod data_plane;
    17	pub mod local;
    18	pub mod transport;
    19
    20	pub use local::{
    21	    run_local_session, LocalCompareMode, LocalMirrorDeleteScope, LocalMirrorOptions,
    22	    LocalMirrorSummary, TransferOutcome,
   145	}
   146
   147	impl Default for HelloConfig {
   148	    fn default() -> Self {
   149	        Self {
   150	            build_id: session_build_id().to_string(),
   151	            contract_version: CONTRACT_VERSION,
   152	        }
   153	    }
   154	}
   155
   156	/// Which handshake part this end plays. Orthogonal to role: all four
   157	/// initiator/role combinations run the same state machine (contract
   158	/// §Invariants 3).
   159	pub enum SessionEndpoint {
   160	    /// This end opened the transport; it sends `SessionOpen`.
   161	    /// (Boxed: `SessionOpen` dwarfs the bare `Responder` variant.)
   162	    Initiator { open: Box<SessionOpen> },
   163	    /// This end answers `SessionOpen` with `SessionAccept`. Daemon
   164	    /// module/path/read-only validation attaches here at otp-4.
   165	    Responder,
   166	}
   167
   168	impl SessionEndpoint {
   169	    /// Convenience constructor so callers don't spell the `Box`.
   170	    pub fn initiator(open: SessionOpen) -> Self {
   171	        SessionEndpoint::Initiator {
   172	            open: Box::new(open),
   173	        }
   174	    }
   175	}
   210	    /// treat a partial transfer as success (the push verb, `blit move`)
   211	    /// inspect this after the session returns. `None` = the session
   212	    /// keeps its own private accumulator (unchanged behavior).
   213	    pub unreadable: Option<Arc<StdMutex<Vec<String>>>>,
   214	    /// Emit `[data-plane-client]` connect traces on the data-plane
   215	    /// sockets this SOURCE acquires (`--trace-data-plane`).
   216	    pub trace_data_plane: bool,
   217	}
   218
   219	pub struct DestinationSessionConfig {
   220	    pub hello: HelloConfig,
   221	    pub endpoint: SessionEndpoint,
   222	    /// Host to dial the granted TCP data plane on when this end is the
   223	    /// **initiator** (pull-equivalent, otp-5b): the DESTINATION initiator
   224	    /// dials the SOURCE responder's granted sockets on the same host it
   225	    /// reached the control plane on (contract §Transport: the initiator
   226	    /// always dials). `None` — or a DESTINATION responder, which binds
   227	    /// rather than dials — falls back to the in-stream carrier. Symmetric
   228	    /// with [`SourceSessionConfig::data_plane_host`].
   229	    pub data_plane_host: Option<String>,
   230	    /// Caller-side observability hooks (otp-10b-2). All default-off; the
   231	    /// daemon DESTINATION responder runs with the defaults. Symmetric
   232	    /// with [`SourceSessionConfig::instruments`].
   233	    pub instruments: DestinationInstruments,
   234	    /// otp-11: the LOCAL byte-carrier. When set, this destination
   235	    /// applies needed files in-process through the local sink instead
   236	    /// of requesting them from the source — no payload byte rides any
   237	    /// transport. Process-local config with no wire representation:
   238	    /// only [`local::run_local_session`] (which holds BOTH roots in
   239	    /// this process) can construct a [`local::LocalApply`], so no wire
   240	    /// peer can ever select it. `None` (every remote caller and the
   241	    /// daemon responder) keeps the wire carriers exactly as before.
   242	    pub local_apply: Option<local::LocalApply>,
   243	}
   244
   245	/// Observability hooks a DESTINATION-side caller can attach to its
   246	/// session (otp-10b-2 — the pull-shaped verb's progress line rides
   247	/// these; `byte_progress` predates them, otp-9a). Everything defaults
   248	/// to off; the session's behavior on the wire is identical either way.
   249	#[derive(Clone, Default)]
   250	pub struct DestinationInstruments {
   251	    /// w6-1 progress events from this end's receive side:
   252	    /// `ManifestBatch` per NeedBatch emitted (the pull-direction
   253	    /// denominator — files this DESTINATION requested, the same
   254	    /// files-to-transfer semantic the push verb reports),
   255	    /// `Payload`/`FileComplete` per record received on either carrier.
   256	    pub progress: Option<RemoteTransferProgress>,
   257	    /// Live byte counter for this DESTINATION's writes (otp-9a). The
   258	    /// session sink reports applied payload bytes against it — the same
   259	    /// `ByteProgressSink` contract the old drivers used, so a caller
   260	    /// that owns a jobs row (the delegated dst daemon, otp-9) can watch
   261	    /// bytes land while the session runs. `None` = no reporting.
   262	    pub byte_progress: Option<crate::remote::transfer::ByteProgressSink>,
   263	    /// Emit `[data-plane-client]` connect traces on the data-plane
   264	    /// sockets this DESTINATION initiator dials (`--trace-data-plane`).
   265	    /// A DESTINATION responder accepts rather than dials; the flag is
   266	    /// inert there.
   267	    pub trace_data_plane: bool,
   268	}
   269
   270	/// A session-terminating fault: either end refusing, aborting, or
   271	/// catching the peer in a protocol violation. Carried as the error
   272	/// payload of the drivers' `eyre::Report`s — downcast to inspect the
   273	/// wire code.
   274	#[derive(Debug, Clone)]
   275	pub struct SessionFault {
   770	                Some(resolved.root)
   771	            }
   772	            Err(fault) => return Err(notify_and_wrap(transport, fault).await),
   773	        },
   774	        None => None,
   775	    };
   776	    // Data plane (otp-4b/5b): a responder binds a TCP listener and grants
   777	    // it, unless the initiator requested the in-stream carrier or the bind
   778	    // fails (grant-less accept ⇒ in-stream fallback). This is role-agnostic
   779	    // (otp-5b): the RESPONDER binds+accepts and the INITIATOR dials, while
   780	    // byte direction is set by role — a DESTINATION responder accepts+
   781	    // receives (push, otp-4b), a SOURCE responder accepts+sends (pull,
   782	    // otp-5b). The bound listener travels in `Negotiated.responder_data_plane`
   783	    // and is consumed by whichever role's driver runs.
   784	    //
   785	    //
   786	    // otp-7b: resume sessions ride the data plane too — block records
   787	    // travel as binary BLOCK/BLOCK_COMPLETE records on the sockets (the
   788	    // otp-7a in-stream frames remain the fallback carrier), so the grant
   789	    // is no longer suppressed for a resume session.
   790	    let responder_data_plane = if open.in_stream_bytes || policy.force_in_stream {
   791	        None
   792	    } else {
   793	        data_plane::prepare_responder_data_plane().await
   794	    };
   795	    let accept = SessionAccept {
   796	        // The byte RECEIVER advertises capacity at session
   797	        // open (D-2026-06-20-1/-2); consumed by the dial when
   798	        // the data plane lands (otp-4b).
   799	        receiver_capacity: if local_role == TransferRole::Destination {
   800	            Some(crate::dial::local_receiver_capacity())
   801	        } else {
   802	            None
   803	        },
   804	        // Grant present ⇒ TCP data plane; absent ⇒ in-stream.
   805	        data_plane: responder_data_plane.as_ref().map(|dp| dp.grant()),
   806	    };
   807	    transport.send(frame(Frame::Accept(accept.clone()))).await?;
   808	    Ok(Negotiated {
   809	        open,
   810	        accept,
  1020	/// The SOURCE session body after establish: spawn the receive half,
  1021	/// run the send half, and map a fault to a peer-notified report. Shared
  1022	/// by [`run_source`] (initiator or direct-responder) and
  1023	/// [`run_responder`] (the daemon SOURCE responder), so the send/receive
  1024	/// choreography is single-sourced.
  1025	async fn drive_source(
  1026	    plan_options: PlanOptions,
  1027	    data_plane_host: Option<String>,
  1028	    instruments: SourceInstruments,
  1029	    mut negotiated: Negotiated,
  1030	    transport: FrameTransport,
  1031	    source: Arc<dyn TransferSource>,
  1032	) -> Result<TransferSummary> {
  1033	    // A SOURCE responder (pull, otp-5b) carries a bound listener to accept
  1034	    // its send sockets on; a SOURCE initiator (push) has none and dials the
  1035	    // grant it received instead. Take it here so the send half owns it.
  1036	    let responder_data_plane = negotiated.responder_data_plane.take();
  1037	    let (mut tx, rx) = transport.split();
  1038	    let sent: Arc<StdMutex<HashMap<String, FileHeader>>> = Arc::default();
  1039	    // Set by the send half the moment ManifestComplete goes out. On
  1040	    // an ordered transport, a NeedComplete arriving while this is
  1041	    // still false is provably premature — the peer cannot have
  1042	    // received what we have not sent (contract: NeedComplete only
  1043	    // after ManifestComplete received + all entries diffed).
  1044	    let manifest_sent = Arc::new(AtomicBool::new(false));
  1045	    let (event_tx, event_rx) = mpsc::unbounded_channel();
  1046	    // Fault side-channel (codex otp-8 F1): the in-stream send path
  1047	    // races this signal against blocked record sends; see
  1048	    // `SourceEventSender`.
  1049	    let (fault_tx, fault_rx) = watch::channel(None::<SessionFault>);
  1050	    // AbortOnDrop: an early error return below must abort the receive
  1051	    // half instead of leaking it (same rationale as design-2 / w4-1).
  1052	    let _recv_guard = AbortOnDrop::new(tokio::spawn(source_recv_half(
  1053	        rx,
  1054	        Arc::clone(&sent),
  1055	        Arc::clone(&manifest_sent),
  1056	        resume_negotiated(&negotiated.open),
  1057	        // otp-10a: the recv half owns need-batch arrival, which is the
  1058	        // push-direction progress denominator (contract on
  1059	        // `ProgressEvent::ManifestBatch`: "push: need-list batches").
  1060	        instruments.progress.clone(),
  1240	    mut events: mpsc::UnboundedReceiver<SourceEvent>,
  1241	    mut fault_signal: watch::Receiver<Option<SessionFault>>,
  1242	) -> Result<TransferSummary> {
  1243	    let mut pending: Vec<FileHeader> = Vec::new();
  1244	    let mut resume: ResumeSendState = ResumeSendState::default();
  1245	    let mut need_complete = false;
  1246
  1247	    // Data plane (otp-4b/5b): set up the send sockets up front — BEFORE
  1248	    // streaming the manifest — so the peer sees the connections promptly
  1249	    // rather than waiting out a bounded-accept/connect timeout while a long
  1250	    // manifest streams. Which end connects depends on connection role
  1251	    // (otp-5b): a SOURCE **responder** (pull) accepts sockets off its bound
  1252	    // listener; a SOURCE **initiator** (push) dials the grant it received.
  1253	    // Byte direction is the same either way (SOURCE sends), so both yield a
  1254	    // `SourceDataPlane` driven identically below. `None` on both ⇒ the
  1255	    // in-stream carrier (fallback), which needs no early setup.
  1256	    let mut data_plane = match responder_data_plane {
  1257	        // SOURCE responder (pull, otp-5b): accept + send. The DESTINATION
  1258	        // initiator advertised its capacity in the open (byte RECEIVER
  1259	        // advertises, wherever it initiates); the accept plane is single-
  1260	        // stream (otp-5b-1).
  1261	        Some(bound) => Some(
  1262	            data_plane::accept_source_data_plane(
  1263	                bound,
  1264	                negotiated.open.receiver_capacity.as_ref(),
  1265	                Arc::clone(&source),
  1266	                &instruments,
  1267	            )
  1268	            .await?,
  1269	        ),
  1270	        // SOURCE initiator (push, otp-4b): dial the grant if the responder
  1271	        // granted a data plane; else in-stream.
  1272	        None => match &negotiated.accept.data_plane {
  1273	            Some(grant) => {
  1274	                let host = data_plane_host.ok_or_else(|| {
  1275	                    eyre::Report::new(SessionFault::internal(
  1276	                        "responder granted a TCP data plane but this initiator has no host to dial",
  1277	                    ))
  1278	                })?;
  1279	                Some(
  1280	                    data_plane::dial_source_data_plane(
  1281	                        host,
  1282	                        grant,
  1283	                        negotiated.accept.receiver_capacity.as_ref(),
  1284	                        Arc::clone(&source),
  1285	                        &instruments,
  1286	                    )
  1287	                    .await?,
  1288	                )
  1289	            }
  1290	            None => None,
  2238	// DESTINATION driver
  2239	// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
  2240
  2241	/// What the destination end can report after a completed session.
  2242	#[derive(Debug, Clone)]
  2243	pub struct DestinationOutcome {
  2244	    /// The summary this end computed and sent (contract: DESTINATION
  2245	    /// is the scorer).
  2246	    pub summary: TransferSummary,
  2247	    /// Paths this end put on the need list, in emission order. The
  2248	    /// role suite pins these identical across role assignments — the
  2249	    /// executable form of the owner's invariance requirement.
  2250	    pub needed_paths: Vec<String>,
  2251	    /// The settled data-plane stream count this end observed (epoch-0 +
  2252	    /// accepted resizes), or `None` for the in-stream carrier. The sf-2
  2253	    /// pin (otp-4b-2) reads it to assert shape correction grew the
  2254	    /// stream set past the zero-knowledge single-stream grant.
  2255	    pub data_plane_streams: Option<usize>,
  2256	}
  2257
  2258	/// Run the DESTINATION role of one transfer session over `transport`,
  2259	/// writing under the root named by `target`. Diffs the streamed
  2260	/// manifest against its own filesystem (the destination is the one
  2261	/// diff owner — plan §Design 3), returns the summary it computed and
  2262	/// sent.
  2263	///
  2264	/// `target` is [`DestinationTarget::Fixed`] when the root is known up
  2265	/// front (an Initiator's own local root, or a test), or
  2266	/// [`DestinationTarget::Resolve`] when the root must be resolved from
  2267	/// the received `SessionOpen` mid-handshake (the daemon Responder,
  2268	/// where the wire module name selects the root).
  2269	pub async fn run_destination(
  2270	    cfg: DestinationSessionConfig,
  2271	    transport: FrameTransport,
  2272	    target: DestinationTarget,
  2273	) -> Result<DestinationOutcome> {
  2274	    let mut transport = transport;
  2275	    let endpoint = match cfg.endpoint {
  2276	        SessionEndpoint::Initiator { mut open } => {
  2277	            let declared = TransferRole::try_from(open.initiator_role);
  2278	            if declared != Ok(TransferRole::Destination) {
  2279	                eyre::bail!(
  2280	                    "run_destination initiator must declare TRANSFER_ROLE_DESTINATION in SessionOpen"
  2281	                );
  2282	            }
  2283	            if let Err(fault) = destination_open_validator(&open) {
  2284	                eyre::bail!("run_destination initiator config unsupported: {fault}");
  2285	            }
  2286	            // Dial contract: the byte receiver advertises capacity in
  2287	            // its open when it is the initiator (contract §Invariants 5).
  2288	            if open.receiver_capacity.is_none() {
  2289	                open.receiver_capacity = Some(crate::dial::local_receiver_capacity());
  2290	            }
  2291	            SessionEndpoint::Initiator { open }
  2292	        }
  2293	        SessionEndpoint::Responder => SessionEndpoint::Responder,
  2294	    };
  2295
  2690	    // when mirroring) so the pass can diff it against the dest at SourceDone.
  2691	    let mirror_enabled = negotiated.open.mirror_enabled;
  2692	    let mirror_kind = MirrorMode::try_from(negotiated.open.mirror_kind).unwrap_or(MirrorMode::Off);
  2693	    let mirror_filter: crate::fs_enum::FileFilter =
  2694	        if mirror_enabled && mirror_kind == MirrorMode::FilteredSubset {
  2695	            // otp-11: the local carrier threads the user's FileFilter
  2696	            // directly (process-local; no wire FilterSpec round-trip) —
  2697	            // same type, same delete pass, same scope semantics.
  2698	            if let Some(la) = &local_apply {
  2699	                la.mirror_scope_filter.clone_without_cache()
  2700	            } else {
  2701	                match negotiated.open.filter.as_ref() {
  2702	                    Some(spec) if *spec != FilterSpec::default() => {
  2703	                        crate::remote::transfer::operation_spec::filter_from_spec(spec.clone())
  2704	                            .map_err(|e| {
  2705	                                eyre::Report::new(SessionFault::internal(format!(
  2706	                                    "invalid filter: {e:#}"
  2707	                                )))
  2708	                            })?
  2709	                    }
  2710	                    _ => crate::fs_enum::FileFilter::default(),
  2711	                }
  2712	            }
  2713	        } else {
  2714	            crate::fs_enum::FileFilter::default()
  2715	        };
  2716	    let mut source_files: HashSet<String> = HashSet::new();
  2717
  2718	    // otp-7a: resume. Headers of resume-granted needs are retained so a
  2719	    // record's completion can finalize with the manifest's
  2720	    // size/mtime/permissions and be validated against the grant. Both
  2721	    // the header map and the resumed counter are SHARED with the
  2722	    // data-plane receive (otp-7b) exactly as `outstanding` is: on the
  2723	    // data plane the control loop never sees block records, so the
  2724	    // NeedListSink claims resume grants and counts completions as they
  2725	    // land on the sockets. The block size is chosen below, once the
  2726	    // carrier is known (the ceiling is per carrier).
  2727	    let resume_enabled = resume_negotiated(&negotiated.open);
  2728	    let resume_headers: data_plane::ResumeHeaders = Arc::default();
  2729	    let files_resumed = Arc::new(std::sync::atomic::AtomicU64::new(0));
  2730
  2731	    // Two sets, deliberately separate (codex otp-4b-1 fix-review F1):
  2732	    // `granted` is the ever-granted DEDUP set — control-loop-local,
  2733	    // insert-only, never removed, so a concurrent data-plane claim can
  2734	    // never re-open a grant (a duplicate manifest path is granted at
  2735	    // most once regardless of delivery timing). `outstanding` is the
  2736	    // not-yet-delivered COMPLETION set — inserted for each freshly
  2737	    // granted path before its NeedBatch, claimed by both carriers (the
  2738	    // in-stream arms inline, the data-plane NeedListSink as payloads
  2739	    // land), and empty at SourceDone. A count proxy was insufficient
  2740	    // (F1); merging the two into one set raced the data-plane claim
  2741	    // against the diff (fix-review F1).
  2742	    let mut granted: HashSet<String> = HashSet::new();
  2743	    let outstanding: data_plane::OutstandingNeeds = Arc::new(StdMutex::new(HashSet::new()));
  2744
  2745	    // Data plane (otp-4b/5b): when a TCP data plane is in play, payload
  2746	    // bytes arrive on sockets (not the control lane). Set it up NOW —
  2747	    // concurrent with the diff loop below, and before the peer sends — so
  2748	    // the connections are established promptly. Which end connects depends
  2749	    // on connection role (otp-5b): a DESTINATION **responder** (push)
  2750	    // accepts sockets off its bound listener; a DESTINATION **initiator**
  2751	    // (pull) dials the grant it received on `data_plane_host`. Byte
  2752	    // direction is the same either way (DESTINATION receives). The
  2753	    // NeedListSink gives the socket receive the same need-list strictness
  2754	    // the in-stream control loop applies inline; AbortOnDrop (inside the
  2755	    // responder run) bounds the accept task to this future. `resize_live`
  2756	    // tracks the stream count this end has grown to (epoch-0 plus each
  2757	    // accepted resize ADD) and `resize_ceiling` the receiver's advertised
  2758	    // max_streams — both directions resize (push arms+accepts, otp-4b-2;
  2759	    // pull dials, otp-5b-2), so both seed these from their epoch-0 streams.
  2760	    let recv_sink: Arc<dyn TransferSink> = Arc::new(data_plane::NeedListSink::new(
  2761	        Arc::clone(&sink),
  2762	        Arc::clone(&outstanding),
  2763	        // otp-7b: only a resume session accepts block records on the
  2764	        // data plane; the sink validates + claims them against the same
  2765	        // shared grant state the in-stream arms use.
  2766	        resume_enabled.then(|| data_plane::ResumeRecv {
  2767	            headers: Arc::clone(&resume_headers),
  2768	            resumed: Arc::clone(&files_resumed),
  2769	        }),
  2770	    ));
  2771	    let (mut data_plane_recv, mut resize_live, resize_ceiling) =
  2772	        match negotiated.responder_data_plane {
  2773	            // DESTINATION responder (push, otp-4b): accept + receive.
  2774	            Some(rdp) => {
  2775	                let initial = rdp.initial_streams() as usize;
  2776	                let run = rdp.spawn(recv_sink, progress.clone());
  2777	                let ceiling = run.ceiling;
  2778	                (
  2779	                    Some(data_plane::DestRecvPlane::Responder(run)),
  2780	                    initial,
  2781	                    ceiling,
  2782	                )
  2783	            }
  2784	            // DESTINATION initiator (pull, otp-5b): dial + receive when the
  2785	            // SOURCE responder granted a data plane and we have a host to dial.
  2786	            None => match (&negotiated.accept.data_plane, data_plane_host) {
  2787	                (Some(grant), Some(host)) => {
  2788	                    let initial = grant.initial_streams.max(1) as usize;
  2789	                    let run = data_plane::dial_destination_data_plane(
  2790	                        host,
  2791	                        grant,
  2792	                        recv_sink,
  2793	                        progress.clone(),
  2794	                        instruments.trace_data_plane,
  2795	                    )
  2796	                    .await?;
  2797	                    // otp-5b-2: the pull data plane resizes too. Seed
  2798	                    // `resize_live` from the epoch-0 streams dialed and bound
  2799	                    // growth by the capacity THIS end advertised in its open
  2800	                    // (it is the byte receiver) — the exact ceiling the SOURCE
  2801	                    // responder's dial already clamps to, so both ends agree
  2802	                    // even when the caller advertised a max_streams below this
  2803	                    // host's fresh local reading (codex otp-5b-2 F1). On a
  2804	                    // Resize frame the initiator dials the epoch-N socket (vs
  2805	                    // the responder path's arm).
  2806	                    let ceiling = crate::dial::receiver_stream_ceiling(
  2807	                        negotiated.open.receiver_capacity.as_ref(),
  2808	                    );
  2809	                    (
  2810	                        Some(data_plane::DestRecvPlane::Initiator(run)),
  2811	                        initial,
  2812	                        ceiling,
  2813	                    )
  2814	                }
  2815	                // A grant with no host to dial is an inconsistent initiator
  2816	                // config: fail fast, mirroring the SOURCE initiator
  2817	                // (`source_send_half`). The SOURCE responder has already bound
  2818	                // and blocks accepting the socket this end would dial, so
  2819	                // silently taking the in-stream branch cannot fall back — it
  2820	                // would deadlock until the responder's accept times out. A
   620	// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
   621	// Initiator (SOURCE) — dial, authenticate, send, resize
   622	// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
   623
   624	/// A resize the SOURCE has proposed and minted a credential for but not
   625	/// yet completed: the driver has sent (or will send) the matching
   626	/// `DataPlaneResize{ADD}` on the control lane and, on the peer's
   627	/// `DataPlaneResizeAck`, dials the epoch-N socket. At most one is in
   628	/// flight (the dial's `pending_epoch` enforces it; this is the
   629	/// driver-side record the ack is matched against).
   630	pub(super) struct PendingResize {
   631	    pub(super) epoch: u32,
   632	    pub(super) target_streams: u32,
   633	    pub(super) sub_token: Vec<u8>,
   634	}
   635
   636	/// How the SOURCE acquires each epoch-N data socket for a shape resize —
   637	/// the two connection roles of otp-5b. Byte direction is identical (the
   638	/// SOURCE sends), and `propose_resize` is the same either way; only socket
   639	/// acquisition flips.
   640	enum SourceSockets {
   641	    /// SOURCE **initiator** (push, otp-4b-2): dials each epoch-N socket to
   642	    /// the granted host:port.
   643	    Dial { host: String, tcp_port: u32 },
   644	    /// SOURCE **responder** (pull, otp-5b-2): accepts each epoch-N socket
   645	    /// off the listener it already bound for epoch-0, credential
   646	    /// `session_token ‖ sub_token`.
   647	    Accept { listener: TcpListener },
   648	}
   649
   650	/// A running source-side data plane: the dialed/accepted socket(s) wrapped
   651	/// as an ELASTIC sink pipeline that `SinkControl::Add` grows mid-run (the
   652	/// sf-2 shape correction). Planned payloads are fed via [`Self::queue`];
   653	/// closing via [`Self::finish`] drains the pipeline, emits each socket's
   654	/// END record, and returns the bytes this end sent.
   655	pub(super) struct SourceDataPlane {
   656	    payload_tx: Option<mpsc::Sender<TransferPayload>>,
   657	    control_tx: mpsc::UnboundedSender<SinkControl>,
   658	    // `AbortOnDrop<T>` wraps a `JoinHandle<T>`; the task's output is
   659	    // `Result<SinkOutcome>`, so `T` is that (not the JoinHandle).
   660	    pipeline: Option<AbortOnDrop<Result<SinkOutcome>>>,
   661	    // The byte SENDER owns the live dial, bounded by the byte RECEIVER's
   662	    // advertised capacity (contract §Invariants 5). The resize drives only
   663	    // its shape-correction stream count; the cheap-dial tuner is future
   664	    // work, so `chunk_bytes()`/`prefetch_count()` stay at the floor.
   665	    dial: Arc<TransferDial>,
   666	    source: Arc<dyn TransferSource>,
   667	    session_token: Vec<u8>,
   668	    pool: Arc<BufferPool>,
   669	    /// `[data-plane-client]` connect traces (`--trace-data-plane`,
   670	    /// otp-10a). Applied to the epoch-0 sockets at construction and to
   671	    /// each epoch-N resize socket in [`Self::add_stream`].
   672	    trace: bool,
   673	    /// How each epoch-N resize socket is acquired (dial for the SOURCE
   674	    /// initiator, accept for the SOURCE responder). The data plane grows
   675	    /// mid-transfer in both cases; the control-lane resize choreography is
   676	    /// identical — only this transport action flips (otp-5b-2).
   677	    sockets: SourceSockets,
   678	}
   679
   680	/// Dial the granted data plane and start the elastic send pipeline.
   681	/// `host` is the responder's host (the initiator connected the control
   682	/// plane to it; the data plane rides the same host on the granted port —
   683	/// contract §Transport: the initiator always dials). `receiver_capacity`
   684	/// is the DESTINATION's advertised profile from `SessionAccept`; it
   685	/// bounds the sender's dial ceiling (0/absent fields ⇒ conservative,
   686	/// never unlimited).
   687	pub(super) async fn dial_source_data_plane(
   688	    host: &str,
   689	    grant: &DataPlaneGrant,
   690	    receiver_capacity: Option<&CapacityProfile>,
   691	    source: Arc<dyn TransferSource>,
   692	    instruments: &SourceInstruments,
   693	) -> Result<SourceDataPlane> {
   694	    let initial = grant.initial_streams.max(1) as usize;
   695	    // The byte sender's dial, bounded by the receiver's advertised
   696	    // capacity. Seed the settled live count to the granted epoch-0
   697	    // streams — every shape-resize proposal steps from here.
   698	    let dial = TransferDial::conservative_within(receiver_capacity).shared();
   699	    dial.set_negotiated_streams(initial);
   700
   701	    // Epoch-0 handshake: session_token ‖ epoch0_sub_token.
   702	    let mut handshake = grant.session_token.clone();
   703	    handshake.extend_from_slice(&grant.epoch0_sub_token);
   704
   705	    // Provision the pool for the dial ceiling so resize-added sockets
   706	    // draw buffers from the same pool without re-pooling (as old push
   707	    // does — a shared pool sized for the maximum stream count).
   708	    let pool = Arc::new(BufferPool::for_data_plane(
   709	        dial.chunk_bytes(),
   710	        dial.ceiling_max_streams().max(1),
   711	    ));
   712	    let trace = instruments.trace_data_plane;
   713	    let mut sinks: Vec<Arc<dyn TransferSink>> = Vec::with_capacity(initial);
   714	    for _ in 0..initial {
   715	        let session = DataPlaneSession::connect(
   716	            host,
   717	            grant.tcp_port,
   718	            &handshake,
   719	            dial.chunk_bytes(),
   720	            dial.prefetch_count(),
   721	            trace,
   722	            dial.tcp_buffer_bytes(),
   723	            Arc::clone(&pool),
   724	        )
   725	        .await
   726	        .map_err(|err| dp_fault_io(&err, format!("dialing session data plane: {err:#}")))?;
   727	        // The source-side sink never reads its dst_root (it only sends);
   728	        // `root()` is consulted by the relay/receive case, not here.
   729	        sinks.push(Arc::new(DataPlaneSink::new(
   730	            session,
   731	            Arc::clone(&source),
   732	            PathBuf::new(),
   733	        )));
   734	    }
   735
   736	    let prefetch = dial.prefetch_count().max(1);
   737	    let (payload_tx, payload_rx) = mpsc::channel::<TransferPayload>(prefetch);
   738	    let (control_tx, control_rx) = mpsc::unbounded_channel::<SinkControl>();
   739	    let pipe_source = Arc::clone(&source);
   740	    let pipe_progress = instruments.progress.clone();
   741	    // Bounded by AbortOnDrop: a fault on the control lane that drops the
   742	    // SourceDataPlane aborts the pipeline task instead of leaking it.
   743	    let pipeline = AbortOnDrop::new(tokio::spawn(async move {
   744	        execute_sink_pipeline_elastic(
   745	            pipe_source,
   746	            sinks,
   747	            payload_rx,
   748	            prefetch,
   749	            pipe_progress.as_ref(),
   750	            Some(control_rx),
   751	        )
   752	        .await
   753	    }));
   754	    Ok(SourceDataPlane {
   755	        payload_tx: Some(payload_tx),
   756	        control_tx,
   757	        pipeline: Some(pipeline),
   758	        dial,
   759	        source,
   760	        session_token: grant.session_token.clone(),
   761	        pool,
   762	        trace,
   763	        // SOURCE initiator: each epoch-N resize socket is dialed to the
   764	        // granted host:port.
   765	        sockets: SourceSockets::Dial {
   766	            host: host.to_string(),
   767	            tcp_port: grant.tcp_port,
   768	        },
   769	    })
   770	}
   771
   772	/// Accept the granted epoch-0 socket(s) off a bound responder listener and
   773	/// start the elastic SEND pipeline over them — the SOURCE **responder**
   774	/// half of the pull data plane (otp-5b-1). Symmetric with
   775	/// [`dial_source_data_plane`] (the SOURCE **initiator** half): both return
   776	/// a [`SourceDataPlane`] the send half drives via `queue`/`finish`; only
   777	/// socket acquisition differs (accept here, dial there).
   778	/// `DataPlaneSession::from_stream` builds a send session from an already-
   779	/// accepted socket — the same primitive the old `pull_sync` daemon-send
   780	/// path uses. `receiver_capacity` is the DESTINATION initiator's advertised
   781	/// profile from its `SessionOpen` (the byte RECEIVER advertises capacity,
   782	/// wherever it initiates). The bound listener is retained so each epoch-N
   783	/// resize socket is accepted off it (otp-5b-2): the DESTINATION initiator
   784	/// dials, this end accepts, the control-lane frames identical to push.
   785	pub(super) async fn accept_source_data_plane(
   786	    bound: ResponderDataPlane,
   787	    receiver_capacity: Option<&CapacityProfile>,
   788	    source: Arc<dyn TransferSource>,
   789	    instruments: &SourceInstruments,
   790	) -> Result<SourceDataPlane> {
   791	    let initial = bound.initial_streams.max(1) as usize;
   792	    // The byte sender's dial, bounded by the receiver's advertised
   793	    // capacity; seed the live count to the granted epoch-0 streams. Growth
   794	    // is via resize (otp-5b-2): the accept-based epoch-N socket steps from
   795	    // here, one stream per epoch, same as the SOURCE initiator.
   796	    let dial = TransferDial::conservative_within(receiver_capacity).shared();
   797	    dial.set_negotiated_streams(initial);
   798
   799	    // Epoch-0 credential the dialing DESTINATION presents:
   800	    // session_token ‖ epoch0_sub_token (contract §Transport).
   801	    let mut epoch0 = bound.session_token.clone();
   802	    epoch0.extend_from_slice(&bound.epoch0_sub_token);
   803
   804	    let pool = Arc::new(BufferPool::for_data_plane(
   805	        dial.chunk_bytes(),
   806	        dial.ceiling_max_streams().max(1),
   807	    ));
   808	    let trace = instruments.trace_data_plane;
   809	    let mut sinks: Vec<Arc<dyn TransferSink>> = Vec::with_capacity(initial);
   810	    for _ in 0..initial {
   811	        let socket = accept_authenticated(&bound.listener, &epoch0).await?;
   812	        let session = DataPlaneSession::from_stream(
   813	            socket,
   814	            trace,
   815	            dial.chunk_bytes(),
   816	            dial.prefetch_count(),
   817	            Arc::clone(&pool),
   818	        )
   819	        .await;
   820	        sinks.push(Arc::new(DataPlaneSink::new(
   821	            session,
   822	            Arc::clone(&source),
   823	            PathBuf::new(),
   824	        )));
   825	    }
   826
   827	    let prefetch = dial.prefetch_count().max(1);
   828	    let (payload_tx, payload_rx) = mpsc::channel::<TransferPayload>(prefetch);
   829	    let (control_tx, control_rx) = mpsc::unbounded_channel::<SinkControl>();
   830	    let pipe_source = Arc::clone(&source);

  1104	        );

  1107	            "no byte past the faulted block may land (initiator {initiator_role:?})"
  1108	        );
  1109	    }

  1112	#[tokio::test]
  1113	async fn block_hashes_without_a_held_resume_need_fault_the_source() {
  1114	    // Choreography strictness: a BlockHashList must correlate with a
  1115	    // resume-flagged need the destination previously granted; an
  1116	    // uncorrelated list is a protocol violation, not a silent ignore.
  1117	    let tmp = tempfile::tempdir().unwrap();
  1118	    let src_root = tmp.path().join("src");
  1119	    std::fs::create_dir_all(&src_root).unwrap();
  1120	    write_tree(&src_root, &[("real.txt", b"real".to_vec(), 1_600_001_200)]);
  1121
  1122	    let source_cfg = SourceSessionConfig {
  1123	        instruments: Default::default(),
  1124	        hello: HelloConfig::default(),
  1125	        endpoint: SessionEndpoint::initiator(resume_open(TransferRole::Source, RESUME_BS)),
  1126	        plan_options: PlanOptions::default(),
  1127	        data_plane_host: None,
  1128	    };
  1129	    let (source_transport, mut peer) = in_process_pair();
  1130	    let source = Arc::new(FsTransferSource::new(src_root));
  1131	    let source_task = tokio::spawn(run_source(source_cfg, source_transport, source));
  1132
  1133	    assert!(matches!(recv_or_panic(&mut peer).await, Frame::Hello(_)));
  1134	    peer.send(hello_frame()).await.unwrap();
  1135	    assert!(matches!(recv_or_panic(&mut peer).await, Frame::Open(_)));
  1136	    peer.send(wire(Frame::Accept(Default::default())))
  1137	        .await
  1138	        .unwrap();
  1139	    loop {
  1140	        match recv_or_panic(&mut peer).await {
  1141	            Frame::ManifestEntry(_) => continue,
  1142	            Frame::ManifestComplete(_) => break,
  1143	            other => panic!("expected manifest stream, got {other:?}"),
  1144	        }
  1145	    }
  1146	    peer.send(wire(Frame::BlockHashes(BlockHashList {
  1147	        relative_path: "real.txt".into(),
  1148	        block_size: RESUME_BS,
  1149	        hashes: Vec::new(),
  1150	    })))
  1151	    .await
  1152	    .unwrap();
  1153
  1154	    let source_err = source_task.await.unwrap().unwrap_err();
  1155	    let fault = fault_of(&source_err);
  1156	    assert_eq!(fault.code, session_error::Code::ProtocolViolation);
  1157	    assert!(
  1158	        fault.message.contains("without a held resume need"),
  1159	        "got: {}",
  1160	        fault.message
  1161	    );
  1162	}

  1164	#[tokio::test(flavor = "multi_thread", worker_threads = 4)]
  1165	async fn many_tiny_files_reach_shape_target_when_source_initiates() {
  1166	    // sf-2 pin ported onto the unified session (otp-4b-2). The responder
  1167	    // grants the zero-knowledge single stream (no manifest seen at
  1168	    // SessionAccept); a 10k-tiny-file transfer over the TCP data plane
  1169	    // must re-run the shape table over the accumulated need list and grow
  1170	    // the stream count past 1 via `DataPlaneResize{ADD}`. Mirrors the old
  1171	    // push sf-2 pin (`shape_resize_e2e.rs`), now on the session: the
  1172	    // settled count is read from the destination's `data_plane_streams`.
  1173	    let tmp = tempfile::tempdir().unwrap();
  1174	    let src_root = tmp.path().join("src");
  1175	    let dst_root = tmp.path().join("dst");
  1176	    std::fs::create_dir_all(&src_root).unwrap();
  1177	    std::fs::create_dir_all(&dst_root).unwrap();
  1178	    const FILE_COUNT: usize = 10_000;
  1179	    for i in 0..FILE_COUNT {
  1180	        std::fs::write(src_root.join(format!("f{i:05}.bin")), b"x").unwrap();
  1181	    }
  1182
  1183	    // SOURCE initiator over the TCP data plane: the control lane rides the
  1184	    // in-process transport; the data-plane sockets ride loopback TCP (the
  1185	    // responder binds 0.0.0.0:0 and the source dials 127.0.0.1).
  1186	    let open = SessionOpen {
  1187	        initiator_role: TransferRole::Source as i32,
  1188	        compare_mode: ComparisonMode::SizeMtime as i32,
  1189	        in_stream_bytes: false,
  1190	        ..Default::default()
  1191	    };
  1192	    let source_cfg = SourceSessionConfig {
  1193	        instruments: Default::default(),
  1194	        hello: HelloConfig::default(),
  1195	        endpoint: SessionEndpoint::initiator(open),
  1196	        plan_options: PlanOptions::default(),
  1197	        data_plane_host: Some("127.0.0.1".into()),
  1198	    };
  1199	    let dest_cfg = DestinationSessionConfig {
  1200	        hello: HelloConfig::default(),
  1201	        endpoint: SessionEndpoint::Responder,
  1202	        data_plane_host: None,
  1203	        instruments: Default::default(),
  1204	        local_apply: None,
  1205	    };
  1206	    let (a, b) = in_process_pair();
  1207	    let source = Arc::new(FsTransferSource::new(src_root.clone()));
  1208	    let (source_result, dest_result) = tokio::time::timeout(SUITE_TIMEOUT, async {
  1209	        tokio::join!(
  1210	            run_source(source_cfg, a, source),
  1450	    let source = Arc::new(FsTransferSource::new(src_root.clone()));
  1451	    let (source_result, dest_result) = tokio::time::timeout(SUITE_TIMEOUT, async {
  1452	        tokio::join!(
  1453	            run_source(source_cfg, a, source),
  1454	            run_destination(dest_cfg, b, DestinationTarget::Fixed(dst_root.clone())),
  1455	        )
  1456	    })
  1457	    .await
  1458	    .expect("session run timed out");
  1459
  1460	    let summary = source_result.expect("source responder succeeds");
  1461	    let outcome = dest_result.expect("destination initiator succeeds");
  1462	    assert!(
  1463	        !summary.in_stream_carrier_used,
  1464	        "the pull data plane must ride TCP, not the in-stream carrier"
  1465	    );
  1466	    assert_eq!(
  1467	        summary, outcome.summary,
  1468	        "both ends must hold the same summary"
  1469	    );
  1470	    assert_eq!(outcome.summary.files_transferred, 4);
  1471	    assert_eq!(
  1472	        outcome.data_plane_streams,
  1473	        Some(1),
  1474	        "a 4-file need list stays single-stream (below the shape threshold)"
  1475	    );
  1476	    assert_trees_identical(&src_root, &dst_root);
  1477	}
  1478
  1479	#[tokio::test(flavor = "multi_thread", worker_threads = 4)]
  1480	async fn many_tiny_files_reach_shape_target_when_destination_initiates() {
  1481	    // otp-5b-2: the sf-2 shape correction in the PULL direction — the
  1482	    // mirror of `many_tiny_files_reach_shape_target_when_source_initiates`
  1483	    // (push). Here the DESTINATION is the *initiator* (dials the epoch-N
  1484	    // sockets it grows to) and the SOURCE is the *responder* (accepts them
  1485	    // off its bound listener). The control-lane `DataPlaneResize{ADD}` /
  1486	    // `DataPlaneResizeAck` frames are identical to push; only the transport
  1487	    // action flips. A 10k-tiny-file transfer must re-run the shape table
  1488	    // over the accumulated need list and grow the stream count past 1.
  1489	    let tmp = tempfile::tempdir().unwrap();
  1490	    let src_root = tmp.path().join("src");
  1491	    let dst_root = tmp.path().join("dst");
  1492	    std::fs::create_dir_all(&src_root).unwrap();
  1493	    std::fs::create_dir_all(&dst_root).unwrap();
  1494	    const FILE_COUNT: usize = 10_000;
  1495	    for i in 0..FILE_COUNT {
  1496	        std::fs::write(src_root.join(format!("f{i:05}.bin")), b"x").unwrap();
  1497	    }
  1498
  1499	    // DESTINATION initiator; SOURCE responder — roles flipped from the push
  1500	    // shape test, the data plane following connection role.
  1501	    let open = SessionOpen {
  1502	        initiator_role: TransferRole::Destination as i32,
  1503	        compare_mode: ComparisonMode::SizeMtime as i32,
  1504	        in_stream_bytes: false,
  1505	        // Wire contract: zero means unknown, not a one-stream cap. Pin it
  1506	        // on the destination-initiator orientation, where this end both
  1507	        // advertises and enforces the receiver ceiling.
  1508	        receiver_capacity: Some(CapacityProfile {
  1509	            max_streams: 0,
  1510	            ..Default::default()
  1511	        }),
  1512	        ..Default::default()
  1513	    };
  1514	    let source_cfg = SourceSessionConfig {
  1515	        instruments: Default::default(),
  1516	        hello: HelloConfig::default(),
  1517	        endpoint: SessionEndpoint::Responder, // binds + accepts + sends
  1518	        plan_options: PlanOptions::default(),
  1519	        data_plane_host: None, // a responder never dials
  1520	    };
  1521	    let dest_cfg = DestinationSessionConfig {
  1522	        hello: HelloConfig::default(),
  1523	        endpoint: SessionEndpoint::initiator(open), // dials + receives
  1524	        data_plane_host: Some("127.0.0.1".into()),
  1525	        instruments: Default::default(),
  1526	        local_apply: None,
  1527	    };
  1528	    let (a, b) = in_process_pair();
  1529	    let source = Arc::new(FsTransferSource::new(src_root.clone()));
  1530	    let (source_result, dest_result) = tokio::time::timeout(SUITE_TIMEOUT, async {
  1531	        tokio::join!(
  1532	            run_source(source_cfg, a, source),
  1533	            run_destination(dest_cfg, b, DestinationTarget::Fixed(dst_root.clone())),
  1534	        )
  1535	    })
  1536	    .await
  1537	    .expect("session run timed out");
  1538
  1539	    let summary = source_result.expect("source responder succeeds");
  1540	    let outcome = dest_result.expect("destination initiator succeeds");
  1541	    assert!(
  1542	        !summary.in_stream_carrier_used,
  1543	        "the pull sf-2 pin must ride the TCP data plane"
  1544	    );
  1545	    assert_eq!(summary.files_transferred, FILE_COUNT as u64);
  1546	    let streams = outcome
  1547	        .data_plane_streams
  1548	        .expect("data plane ran, stream count recorded");
  1549	    assert_eq!(
  1550	        streams, 8,
  1551	        "a {FILE_COUNT}-file transfer must reach the shape policy's eight-stream \
  1552	         target regardless of which endpoint initiated the session"
  1553	    );
  1554	    assert_trees_identical(&src_root, &dst_root);
  1555	}
  1556
  1557	#[tokio::test]
  1558	async fn preserves_mtime_on_streamed_files() {
  1559	    // Not part of the role matrix — pins that the file-record write
  1560	    // path applies the manifest mtime (parity with today's receive
  1561	    // paths, which the byte-identical asserts alone wouldn't catch).
  1562	    let tmp = tempfile::tempdir().unwrap();
  1563	    let src_root = tmp.path().join("src");
  1564	    let dst_root = tmp.path().join("dst");
  1565	    std::fs::create_dir_all(&src_root).unwrap();
  1566	    std::fs::create_dir_all(&dst_root).unwrap();
  1567	    write_tree(
  1568	        &src_root,
  1569	        &[("stamped.txt", b"stamp me".to_vec(), 1_555_555_555)],
  1570	    );
  1571
  1572	    let (source_result, dest_result) = run_session(
  1573	        TransferRole::Source,
  1574	        &src_root,
  1575	        &dst_root,
  1576	        PlanOptions::default(),
  1577	    )
  1578	    .await;
  1579	    source_result.unwrap();
  1580	    dest_result.unwrap();
  1581
  1582	    let meta = std::fs::metadata(dst_root.join("stamped.txt")).unwrap();
  1583	    let mtime = filetime::FileTime::from_last_modification_time(&meta);
  1584	    assert_eq!(mtime.unix_seconds(), 1_555_555_555);
  1585	}

  1587	// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
  1588	// Handshake refusals
  1589	// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
  1590
  1591	#[tokio::test]
  1592	async fn build_mismatch_refused_under_both_initiators() {
  1593	    for initiator_role in [TransferRole::Source, TransferRole::Destination] {
  1594	        let tmp = tempfile::tempdir().unwrap();
  1595	        let src_root = tmp.path().join("src");
  1596	        let dst_root = tmp.path().join("dst");
  1597	        std::fs::create_dir_all(&src_root).unwrap();
  1598	        std::fs::create_dir_all(&dst_root).unwrap();
  1599
  1600	        let open = basic_open(initiator_role);
  1601	        let (source_endpoint, dest_endpoint) = match initiator_role {
  1602	            TransferRole::Source => (SessionEndpoint::initiator(open), SessionEndpoint::Responder),
  1603	            _ => (SessionEndpoint::Responder, SessionEndpoint::initiator(open)),
  1604	        };
  1605	        let source_cfg = SourceSessionConfig {
  1606	            instruments: Default::default(),
  1607	            hello: HelloConfig {
  1608	                build_id: "0.1.0+aaaaaaaaaaaa".into(),
  1609	                contract_version: CONTRACT_VERSION,
  1610	            },
  1611	            endpoint: source_endpoint,
  1612	            plan_options: PlanOptions::default(),
  1613	            data_plane_host: None,
  1614	        };
  1615	        let dest_cfg = DestinationSessionConfig {
  1616	            hello: HelloConfig {
  1617	                build_id: "0.1.0+bbbbbbbbbbbb".into(),
  1618	                contract_version: CONTRACT_VERSION,
  1619	            },
  1620	            endpoint: dest_endpoint,
  1621	            data_plane_host: None,
  1622	            instruments: Default::default(),
  1623	            local_apply: None,
  1624	        };
  1625	        let (a, b) = in_process_pair();
  1626	        let source = Arc::new(FsTransferSource::new(src_root.clone()));
  1627	        let (source_result, dest_result) = tokio::time::timeout(SUITE_TIMEOUT, async {
  1628	            tokio::join!(
  1629	                run_source(source_cfg, a, source),
  1630	                run_destination(dest_cfg, b, DestinationTarget::Fixed(dst_root.clone())),
  1631	            )
  1632	        })
  1633	        .await
  1634	        .unwrap();
  1635
  1636	        for (end, err) in [
  1637	            ("source", source_result.unwrap_err()),
  1638	            ("destination", dest_result.err().unwrap()),
  1639	        ] {
  1640	            let fault = fault_of(&err);
  1641	            assert_eq!(
  1642	                fault.code,
  1643	                session_error::Code::BuildMismatch,
  1644	                "{end} must refuse with BUILD_MISMATCH (initiator {initiator_role:?})"
  1645	            );
  1646	            assert!(
  1647	                fault.message.contains("aaaaaaaaaaaa") && fault.message.contains("bbbbbbbbbbbb"),
  1648	                "{end} must name both build ids, got: {}",
  1649	                fault.message
  1650	            );
  1651	        }
  1652	        assert!(
  1653	            collect_tree(&dst_root).is_empty(),
  1654	            "no bytes may move on a refused handshake"
  1655	        );
  1656	    }
  1657	}

  1660	async fn contract_version_mismatch_is_refused() {
  1880	        &dst_root,
  1881	        MirrorMode::All,
  1882	        Some("*.txt"),
  1883	    )
  1884	    .await;
  1885	    let summary = sr.expect("source session");
  1886	    let _ = dr.expect("destination session");
  1887
  1888	    assert_eq!(

  1897	#[tokio::test]
  1898	async fn mirror_refused_when_source_scan_incomplete() {
  1899	    // otp-6b: mirroring on an incomplete source scan could delete files the
  1900	    // source still has (they were merely unreadable mid-scan). The
  1901	    // destination must refuse at ManifestComplete{scan_complete=false} and
  1902	    // delete nothing. Scripted source peer so we control the flag.
  1903	    let tmp = tempfile::tempdir().unwrap();
  1904	    let dst_root = tmp.path().join("dst");
  1905	    std::fs::create_dir_all(&dst_root).unwrap();
  1906	    write_tree(
  1907	        &dst_root,
  1908	        &[("victim.txt", b"keep".to_vec(), 1_500_000_000)],
  1909	    );
  1910
  1911	    let dest_cfg = DestinationSessionConfig {
  1912	        hello: HelloConfig::default(),
  1913	        endpoint: SessionEndpoint::Responder,
  1914	        data_plane_host: None,
  1915	        instruments: Default::default(),
  1916	        local_apply: None,
  1917	    };
  1918	    let (mut peer, dest_transport) = in_process_pair();
  1919	    let dest = tokio::spawn(run_destination(
  1920	        dest_cfg,
  1921	        dest_transport,
  1922	        DestinationTarget::Fixed(dst_root.clone()),
  1923	    ));
  1924
  1925	    let mut open = basic_open(TransferRole::Source);
  1926	    open.mirror_enabled = true;
  1927	    open.mirror_kind = MirrorMode::All as i32;
  1928	    peer.send(hello_frame()).await.unwrap();
  1929	    assert!(matches!(recv_or_panic(&mut peer).await, Frame::Hello(_)));
  1930	    peer.send(wire(Frame::Open(open))).await.unwrap();
  1931	    assert!(matches!(recv_or_panic(&mut peer).await, Frame::Accept(_)));
  1932
  1933	    // A manifest entry, then declare the scan INCOMPLETE.
  1934	    peer.send(wire(Frame::ManifestEntry(FileHeader {
  1935	        relative_path: "present.txt".into(),
  1936	        size: 1,
  1937	        mtime_seconds: 1_600_000_000,
  1938	        permissions: 0o644,
  1939	        checksum: vec![],
  1940	    })))
  1941	    .await
  1942	    .unwrap();
  1943	    peer.send(wire(Frame::ManifestComplete(ManifestComplete {
  1944	        scan_complete: false,
  1945	    })))
  1946	    .await
  1947	    .unwrap();
  1948
  1949	    let refusal = loop {
  1950	        match recv_or_panic(&mut peer).await {
  1951	            Frame::Error(e) => break e,
  1952	            Frame::NeedBatch(_) | Frame::NeedComplete(_) => continue,
  1953	            other => panic!("expected SessionError, got {other:?}"),
  1954	        }
  1955	    };
  1956	    assert_eq!(refusal.code, session_error::Code::Internal as i32);
  1957	    assert!(
  1958	        refusal.message.contains("scan"),
  1959	        "refusal must cite the incomplete scan, got: {}",
  1960	        refusal.message
  1961	    );
  1962	    let dest_err = dest.await.unwrap().unwrap_err();
  1963	    assert_eq!(fault_of(&dest_err).code, session_error::Code::Internal);
  1964	    assert!(
  1965	        dst_root.join("victim.txt").exists(),
  1966	        "nothing may be deleted on a refused mirror"
  1967	    );
  1968	}
