  1970	#[tokio::test]
  1971	async fn cancel_frame_during_mirror_purge_aborts_the_deletions() {
  1972	    // codex otp-10b-2 F1: a peer fault (CancelJob on the serving
  1973	    // source) arriving while the DESTINATION runs its mirror delete
  1974	    // pass must abort the pass and surface the fault — not sit unread
  1975	    // on the control lane while deletions run to completion behind a
  1976	    // cancelled session. Scripted source peer: an EMPTY manifest makes
  1977	    // every destination file extraneous, and the CANCELLED frame is
  1978	    // queued right behind SourceDone — the purge race reads it (biased
  1979	    // frame-first) and flips the abort flag before the pass's next
  1980	    // filesystem op.
  1981	    let tmp = tempfile::tempdir().unwrap();
  1982	    let dst_root = tmp.path().join("dst");
  1983	    std::fs::create_dir_all(&dst_root).unwrap();
  1984	    for i in 0..2000 {
  1985	        let path = dst_root.join(format!("victim_{i:04}.txt"));
  1986	        std::fs::write(&path, b"extraneous").unwrap();
  1987	    }
  1988
  1989	    let dest_cfg = DestinationSessionConfig {
  1990	        hello: HelloConfig::default(),
  1991	        endpoint: SessionEndpoint::Responder,
  1992	        data_plane_host: None,
  1993	        instruments: Default::default(),
  1994	        local_apply: None,
  1995	    };
  1996	    let (mut peer, dest_transport) = in_process_pair();
  1997	    let dest = tokio::spawn(run_destination(
  1998	        dest_cfg,
  1999	        dest_transport,
  2000	        DestinationTarget::Fixed(dst_root.clone()),
  2001	    ));
  2002
  2003	    let mut open = basic_open(TransferRole::Source);
  2004	    open.mirror_enabled = true;
  2005	    open.mirror_kind = MirrorMode::All as i32;
  2006	    peer.send(hello_frame()).await.unwrap();
  2007	    assert!(matches!(recv_or_panic(&mut peer).await, Frame::Hello(_)));
  2008	    peer.send(wire(Frame::Open(open))).await.unwrap();
  2009	    assert!(matches!(recv_or_panic(&mut peer).await, Frame::Accept(_)));
  2010
  2011	    // Empty complete scan → empty need list → the purge would delete
  2012	    // all 2000 files. Queue the cancel right behind SourceDone.
  2013	    peer.send(wire(Frame::ManifestComplete(ManifestComplete {
  2014	        scan_complete: true,
  2015	    })))
  2016	    .await
  2017	    .unwrap();
  2018	    assert!(matches!(
  2019	        recv_or_panic(&mut peer).await,
  2020	        Frame::NeedComplete(_)
  2021	    ));
  2022	    peer.send(wire(Frame::SourceDone(SourceDone {})))
  2023	        .await
  2024	        .unwrap();
  2025	    peer.send(wire(Frame::Error(SessionError {
  2026	        code: session_error::Code::Cancelled as i32,
  2027	        message: "job cancelled by operator".into(),
  2028	        ..Default::default()
  2029	    })))
  2030	    .await
  2031	    .unwrap();
  2032
  2033	    let dest_err = tokio::time::timeout(SUITE_TIMEOUT, dest)
  2034	        .await
  2035	        .expect("destination must not hang mid-purge")
  2036	        .unwrap()
  2037	        .expect_err("a cancelled session must not report success");
  2038	    assert_eq!(
  2039	        fault_of(&dest_err).code,
  2040	        session_error::Code::Cancelled,
  2041	        "the peer's CANCELLED must own the outcome, got: {dest_err:#}"
  2042	    );
  2043	    let survivors = std::fs::read_dir(&dst_root).unwrap().count();
  2044	    assert!(
  2045	        survivors > 0,
  2046	        "the purge must abort instead of deleting all 2000 entries \
  2047	         behind a cancelled session"
  2048	    );
  2049	}
  2050
  2051	#[tokio::test]
  2052	async fn cancel_mid_file_record_surfaces_the_peers_fault() {
  2053	    // otp-5b-3 (re-scoped, direction-agnostic): cancel while file DATA
  2054	    // is in flight. The scripted source opens a 3 MiB record, delivers
  2055	    // one partial FileData frame, then sends CANCELLED mid-record —
  2056	    // bytes still owed. The destination must surface the peer's fault
  2057	    // (code CANCELLED, message preserved), NOT a ProtocolViolation
  2058	    // about frame position, and must not finalize the partial file.
  2059	    // Direction-invariance (D-2026-07-05-1) makes this one scripted
  2060	    // shape stand for both user-facing directions: the in-stream
  2061	    // record receive is the same code under either initiator.
  2062	    let tmp = tempfile::tempdir().unwrap();
  2063	    let dst_root = tmp.path().join("dst");
  2064	    std::fs::create_dir_all(&dst_root).unwrap();
  2065
  2066	    let dest_cfg = DestinationSessionConfig {
  2067	        hello: HelloConfig::default(),
  2068	        endpoint: SessionEndpoint::Responder,
  2069	        data_plane_host: None,
  2070	        instruments: Default::default(),
  2071	        local_apply: None,
  2072	    };
  2073	    let (mut peer, dest_transport) = in_process_pair();
  2074	    let dest = tokio::spawn(run_destination(
  2075	        dest_cfg,
  2076	        dest_transport,
  2077	        DestinationTarget::Fixed(dst_root.clone()),
  2078	    ));
  2079
  2080	    peer.send(hello_frame()).await.unwrap();
  2081	    assert!(matches!(recv_or_panic(&mut peer).await, Frame::Hello(_)));
  2082	    peer.send(wire(Frame::Open(basic_open(TransferRole::Source))))
  2083	        .await
  2084	        .unwrap();
  2085	    assert!(matches!(recv_or_panic(&mut peer).await, Frame::Accept(_)));
  2086
  2087	    let size = 3 * 1024 * 1024 + 17;
  2088	    let header = FileHeader {
  2089	        relative_path: "big.bin".into(),
  2090	        size: size as u64,
  2091	        mtime_seconds: 1_600_000_000,
  2092	        permissions: 0o644,
  2093	        checksum: vec![],
  2094	    };
  2095	    peer.send(wire(Frame::ManifestEntry(header.clone())))
  2096	        .await
  2097	        .unwrap();
  2098	    peer.send(wire(Frame::ManifestComplete(ManifestComplete {
  2099	        scan_complete: true,
  2100	    })))
  2101	    .await
  2102	    .unwrap();
  2103
  2104	    // Missing at the destination → must be granted.
  2105	    let mut granted = false;
  2106	    loop {
  2107	        match recv_or_panic(&mut peer).await {
  2108	            Frame::NeedBatch(batch) => {
  2109	                granted |= batch.entries.iter().any(|e| e.relative_path == "big.bin");
  2110	            }

   197
   198	/// The one mutable tuning object for a transfer.
   199	#[derive(Debug)]
   200	pub struct TransferDial {
   201	    chunk_bytes: AtomicUsize,
   202	    prefetch_count: AtomicUsize,
   203	    /// 0 = unset (kernel default), matching the old `Option<usize>`.
   204	    tcp_buffer_bytes: AtomicUsize,
   205	    initial_streams: AtomicUsize,
   206	    max_streams: AtomicUsize,
   207	    // ── ue-r2-2 resize state (all epochs are the wire's monotonic
   208	    // resize ids; 0 is reserved for the initial stream set) ──────────
   209	    /// Settled live stream count. Epoch-0 write is
   210	    /// `set_negotiated_streams`; later writes come from
   211	    /// `resize_settled` on an accepted epoch.
   212	    live_streams: AtomicUsize,
   213	    /// Last settled epoch, in-flight proposal, and terminal-refusal bit.
   214	    /// These fields form one arbitration state: observing/claiming them
   215	    /// separately permits an ABA race across a concurrent settlement.
   216	    resize_epochs: Mutex<ResizeEpochState>,
   217	    #[cfg(test)]
   218	    resize_tick_test_hook: Mutex<Option<Arc<ResizeTickTestHook>>>,
   219	    #[cfg(test)]
   220	    resize_settle_test_hook: Mutex<Option<Arc<ResizeSettleTestHook>>>,
   221	    #[cfg(test)]
   222	    resize_lock_sequence: AtomicUsize,
   223	    /// Resize-eligible ticks since the last settle (cooldown clock).
   224	    ticks_since_settle: AtomicU32,
   225	    /// Consecutive same-direction tick counter: positive = "pipe clean
   226	    /// AND cheap dials maxed" streak, negative = "blocked AND cheap
   227	    /// dials floored" streak. Any other tick resets it.
   228	    resize_sustain: AtomicI32,
   229	    // Profile-clamped bounds, fixed at construction.
   230	    ceiling_chunk_bytes: usize,
   231	    ceiling_prefetch: usize,
   232	    ceiling_max_streams: usize,
   233	    ceiling_tcp_buffer_bytes: usize,
   234	}
   235
   236	/// One engine resize decision (`ue-r2-2`). The adapter that owns the
   237	/// control stream turns this into a wire `DataPlaneResize` (the engine
   238	/// stays wire-type-free here on purpose) and MUST eventually call
   239	/// [`TransferDial::resize_settled`] for the epoch — with what actually
   240	/// happened — or no further proposals are produced.
   241	#[derive(Debug, Clone, Copy, PartialEq, Eq)]
   242	pub struct ResizeProposal {
   243	    /// The wire epoch for this change (`resize_epoch() + 1`).
   244	    pub epoch: u32,
   245	    /// Absolute desired live count (idempotent, per the proto).
   246	    pub target_streams: usize,
   247	    /// Convenience: `target_streams > live` at proposal time.
   248	    pub add: bool,
   249	}
   250
   251	impl TransferDial {
   252	    fn lock_resize_epochs(&self) -> ResizeEpochGuard<'_> {
   253	        ResizeEpochGuard {
   254	            inner: self
   255	                .resize_epochs
   256	                .lock()
   257	                .expect("resize epoch state poisoned"),
   258	            #[cfg(test)]
   259	            acquisition: self
   260	                .resize_lock_sequence
   261	                .fetch_add(1, Ordering::SeqCst)
   262	                .saturating_add(1),
   263	        }
   264	    }
   265
   266	    /// Conservative start with default ceilings (no receiver profile).
   267	    pub fn conservative() -> Self {
   268	        Self::conservative_within(None)
   269	    }
   270
   271	    /// Conservative start bounded by the receiver's advertised
   272	    /// capacity profile. Per the `ue-r2-1b` contract, `0`/absent
   273	    /// fields mean UNKNOWN and keep the (already conservative)
   274	    /// default ceiling — never "unlimited". A profile can only lower
   275	    /// ceilings, never raise them above the defaults this slice.
   276	    pub fn conservative_within(profile: Option<&CapacityProfile>) -> Self {
   277	        let mut ceiling_chunk = DIAL_CEILING_CHUNK_BYTES;
   278	        let mut ceiling_prefetch = DIAL_CEILING_PREFETCH;
   279	        let ceiling_streams = receiver_stream_ceiling(profile);
   280	        let ceiling_tcp = DIAL_CEILING_TCP_BUFFER_BYTES;
   281	        if let Some(profile) = profile {
   282	            if profile.max_chunk_bytes > 0 {
   283	                ceiling_chunk = ceiling_chunk.min(profile.max_chunk_bytes as usize);
   284	            }
   285	            if profile.max_inflight_bytes > 0 {
   286	                // The in-flight budget bounds the CHUNK ceiling first
   287	                // (codex ue-r2-1e F1: with max_chunk unknown, a budget
   288	                // smaller than one chunk must still be honored — floor
   289	                // 64 KiB, matching the session's minimum buffer), then
   290	                // prefetch so prefetch × chunk stays within budget
   291	                // (floor of 1 so work still moves).
   292	                let inflight = profile.max_inflight_bytes as usize;
   293	                ceiling_chunk =
   294	                    ceiling_chunk.min(inflight.max(crate::buffer::DATA_PLANE_BUFFER_FLOOR));
   295	                let by_inflight = (inflight / ceiling_chunk.max(1)).max(1);
   296	                ceiling_prefetch = ceiling_prefetch.min(by_inflight);
   297	            }
   298	        }
   299	        Self {
   300	            chunk_bytes: AtomicUsize::new(DIAL_FLOOR_CHUNK_BYTES.min(ceiling_chunk)),
   301	            prefetch_count: AtomicUsize::new(DIAL_FLOOR_PREFETCH.min(ceiling_prefetch)),
   302	            tcp_buffer_bytes: AtomicUsize::new(0),
   303	            initial_streams: AtomicUsize::new(DIAL_FLOOR_INITIAL_STREAMS.min(ceiling_streams)),
   304	            max_streams: AtomicUsize::new(DIAL_FLOOR_MAX_STREAMS.clamp(1, ceiling_streams.max(1))),
   305	            live_streams: AtomicUsize::new(DIAL_FLOOR_INITIAL_STREAMS.min(ceiling_streams)),
   306	            resize_epochs: Mutex::new(ResizeEpochState::default()),
   307	            #[cfg(test)]
   308	            resize_tick_test_hook: Mutex::new(None),
   309	            #[cfg(test)]
   310	            resize_settle_test_hook: Mutex::new(None),
   311	            #[cfg(test)]
   312	            resize_lock_sequence: AtomicUsize::new(0),
   313	            ticks_since_settle: AtomicU32::new(0),
   314	            resize_sustain: AtomicI32::new(0),
   315	            ceiling_chunk_bytes: ceiling_chunk,
   316	            ceiling_prefetch,
   317	            ceiling_max_streams: ceiling_streams,
   318	            ceiling_tcp_buffer_bytes: ceiling_tcp,
   319	        }
   320	    }
   321
   322	    pub fn shared(self) -> Arc<Self> {
   323	        Arc::new(self)
   324	    }
   325
   326	    // ── live reads ───────────────────────────────────────────────────
   327	    pub fn chunk_bytes(&self) -> usize {
   328	        self.chunk_bytes.load(Ordering::Relaxed)
   329	    }
   330	    pub fn prefetch_count(&self) -> usize {
   331	        self.prefetch_count.load(Ordering::Relaxed)
   332	    }
   333	    /// `None` = leave the kernel default (old `tcp_buffer_size`
   334	    /// semantics). Connect-time dial.
   335	    pub fn tcp_buffer_bytes(&self) -> Option<usize> {
   336	        match self.tcp_buffer_bytes.load(Ordering::Relaxed) {
   337	            0 => None,
   338	            n => Some(n),
   339	        }
   340	    }
   430	        if let Some(hook) = test_hook.as_ref() {
   431	            hook.entered_acquisition
   432	                .store(state.acquisition(), Ordering::SeqCst);
   433	            hook.entered.wait();
   434	            hook.release.wait();
   435	        }
   436	        let ticks = self
   437	            .ticks_since_settle
   438	            .fetch_add(1, Ordering::Relaxed)
   439	            .saturating_add(1);
   440	        if delta_bytes == 0 {
   441	            self.resize_sustain.store(0, Ordering::Relaxed);
   442	            return None;
   443	        }
   444	        let sustain = if blocked_ratio < DIAL_STEP_UP_BLOCKED_RATIO && self.cheap_dials_maxed() {
   445	            let prev = self.resize_sustain.load(Ordering::Relaxed).max(0);
   446	            let next = prev.saturating_add(1);
   447	            self.resize_sustain.store(next, Ordering::Relaxed);
   448	            next
   449	        } else if blocked_ratio > DIAL_STEP_DOWN_BLOCKED_RATIO && self.cheap_dials_floored() {
   450	            let prev = self.resize_sustain.load(Ordering::Relaxed).min(0);
   451	            let next = prev.saturating_sub(1);
   452	            self.resize_sustain.store(next, Ordering::Relaxed);
   453	            next
   454	        } else {
   455	            self.resize_sustain.store(0, Ordering::Relaxed);
   456	            0
   457	        };
   458	        if ticks < RESIZE_COOLDOWN_TICKS {
   459	            return None;
   460	        }
   461	        let add = if sustain >= RESIZE_SUSTAIN_TICKS {
   462	            true
   463	        } else if sustain <= -RESIZE_SUSTAIN_TICKS {
   464	            false
   465	        } else {
   466	            return None;
   467	        };
   468	        let live = self.live_streams.load(Ordering::Relaxed).max(1);
   469	        let target = if add {
   470	            (live + 1).min(self.ceiling_max_streams.max(1))
   471	        } else {
   472	            live.saturating_sub(1).max(1)
   473	        };
   474	        if target == live {
   475	            // Already at the bound in the wanted direction.
   476	            self.resize_sustain.store(0, Ordering::Relaxed);
   477	            return None;
   478	        }
   479	        let epoch = state.settled_epoch.checked_add(1)?;
   480	        state.pending_epoch = Some(epoch);
   481	        #[cfg(test)]
   482	        if let Some(hook) = test_hook.as_ref() {
   483	            hook.claimed_acquisition
   484	                .store(state.acquisition(), Ordering::SeqCst);
   485	        }
   486	        self.resize_sustain.store(0, Ordering::Relaxed);
   487	        Some(ResizeProposal {
   488	            epoch,
   489	            target_streams: target,
   490	            add: target > live,
   491	        })
   492	    }
   493
   494	    /// sf-2: shape-correction proposal. On push the daemon proposes the
   495	    /// epoch-0 stream count from whatever manifest prefix it has seen at
   496	    /// the early flush (`FILE_LIST_EARLY_FLUSH_ENTRIES`), so a
   497	    /// many-tiny-file push can negotiate far fewer streams than
   498	    /// [`initial_stream_proposal`] assigns the full workload. As the
   499	    /// need list accumulates client-side, the client re-runs the shape
   500	    /// table and corrects upward through the normal resize wire.
   501	    ///
   502	    /// Unlike [`Self::resize_tick`] this is a definite signal — the
   503	    /// shape is known, not inferred from throughput — so there is no
   504	    /// sustain/cooldown discipline. It still honors one-in-flight and
   505	    /// the receiver-profile ceiling, still moves ONE stream per epoch
   506	    /// (the wire carries one `sub_token` per ADD), and never proposes
   507	    /// REMOVE: shrinking below a live count is throughput evidence and
   508	    /// stays the tuner's call.
   509	    pub fn propose_shape_resize(&self, desired_streams: usize) -> Option<ResizeProposal> {
   510	        let desired = desired_streams.clamp(1, self.ceiling_max_streams.max(1));
   511	        let mut state = self
   512	            .resize_epochs
   513	            .lock()
   514	            .expect("resize epoch state poisoned");
   515	        if state.refused || state.pending_epoch.is_some() {
   516	            return None;
   517	        }
   518	        let live = self.live_streams.load(Ordering::Relaxed).max(1);
   519	        if desired <= live {
   520	            return None;
   521	        }
   522	        let epoch = state.settled_epoch.checked_add(1)?;
   523	        state.pending_epoch = Some(epoch);
   524	        Some(ResizeProposal {
   525	            epoch,
   526	            target_streams: live + 1,
   527	            add: true,
   528	        })
   529	    }
   530
   531	    /// Settle the in-flight proposal with what ACTUALLY happened:
   532	    /// `effective_streams` is the live count now in effect (from the
   533	    /// peer's ack, or the local count if a post-ack dial failed and
   534	    /// nothing changed). `accepted = false` leaves the live count
   535	    /// untouched, consumes the refused epoch, and permanently disables
   536	    /// further proposals on this transfer. Stale epochs (not the pending
   537	    /// one) are ignored. Either way the cooldown clock restarts.
   538	    pub fn resize_settled(&self, epoch: u32, effective_streams: usize, accepted: bool) {
   539	        #[cfg(test)]
   540	        let mut state = {
   541	            let hook = self
   542	                .resize_settle_test_hook
   543	                .lock()
   544	                .expect("resize settle test hook poisoned")
   545	                .clone();
   546	            if let Some(hook) = hook {
   547	                match self.resize_epochs.try_lock() {
   548	                    Ok(state) => {
   549	                        hook.contended.store(false, Ordering::SeqCst);
   550	                        hook.observed.wait();
   551	                        state
   552	                    }
   553	                    Err(std::sync::TryLockError::WouldBlock) => {
   554	                        hook.contended.store(true, Ordering::SeqCst);
   555	                        hook.observed.wait();
   556	                        self.resize_epochs
   557	                            .lock()
   558	                            .expect("resize epoch state poisoned")
   559	                    }
   560	                    Err(std::sync::TryLockError::Poisoned(_)) => {
   561	                        panic!("resize epoch state poisoned")
   562	                    }
   563	                }
   564	            } else {
   565	                self.resize_epochs
   566	                    .lock()
   567	                    .expect("resize epoch state poisoned")
   568	            }
   569	        };
   570	        #[cfg(not(test))]
   571	        let mut state = self
   572	            .resize_epochs
   573	            .lock()
   574	            .expect("resize epoch state poisoned");
   575	        self.resize_settled_locked(&mut state, epoch, effective_streams, accepted);
   576	    }
   577
   578	    fn resize_settled_locked(
   579	        &self,
   580	        state: &mut ResizeEpochState,
   581	        epoch: u32,
   582	        effective_streams: usize,
   583	        accepted: bool,
   584	    ) -> bool {
   585	        if state.pending_epoch != Some(epoch) || epoch == 0 {
   586	            return false;
   587	        }
   588	        self.ticks_since_settle.store(0, Ordering::Relaxed);
   589	        self.resize_sustain.store(0, Ordering::Relaxed);
   590	        if accepted {
   591	            let clamped = effective_streams.clamp(1, self.ceiling_max_streams.max(1));
   592	            self.live_streams.store(clamped, Ordering::Relaxed);
   593	        }
   594	        // A refused request was still an observed wire epoch. Consuming it
   595	        // keeps future/duplicate traffic monotonic even though live count
   596	        // remains unchanged.
   597	        let settled = state.settle(epoch, accepted);
   598	        debug_assert!(settled);
   599	        settled
   600	    }
   601
   602	    /// Raise max_streams toward the ceiling (used when a peer's
   603	    /// negotiation allows more than the floor; still profile-bounded).
   604	    pub fn allow_streams_up_to(&self, streams: usize) {
   605	        let clamped = streams.clamp(1, self.ceiling_max_streams.max(1));
   606	        self.max_streams.store(clamped, Ordering::Relaxed);
   607	    }
   608
   609	    // ── tuner steps ──────────────────────────────────────────────────
   610	    /// One upward step of the cheap dials: chunk ×2 toward the
   611	    /// ceiling, prefetch +50% (at least +1) toward the ceiling, and
   612	    /// the tcp buffer to its ceiling (affects future sockets).
   613	    /// Returns true if anything moved.
   614	    pub fn step_up_cheap_dials(&self) -> bool {
   615	        let mut moved = false;
   616	        let chunk = self.chunk_bytes.load(Ordering::Relaxed);
   617	        let next = (chunk.saturating_mul(2)).min(self.ceiling_chunk_bytes);
   618	        if next > chunk {
   619	            self.chunk_bytes.store(next, Ordering::Relaxed);
   620	            moved = true;
   621	        }
   622	        let prefetch = self.prefetch_count.load(Ordering::Relaxed);
   623	        let next = (prefetch + (prefetch / 2).max(1)).min(self.ceiling_prefetch);
   624	        if next > prefetch {
   625	            self.prefetch_count.store(next, Ordering::Relaxed);
   626	            moved = true;
   627	        }
   628	        let tcp = self.tcp_buffer_bytes.load(Ordering::Relaxed);
   629	        if tcp < self.ceiling_tcp_buffer_bytes {
   630	            self.tcp_buffer_bytes
   631	                .store(self.ceiling_tcp_buffer_bytes, Ordering::Relaxed);
   632	            moved = true;
   633	        }
   634	        moved
   635	    }
   636
   637	    /// One downward step toward the floors. Returns true if anything
   638	    /// moved.
   639	    pub fn step_down_cheap_dials(&self) -> bool {
   640	        let mut moved = false;
   641	        let chunk = self.chunk_bytes.load(Ordering::Relaxed);
   642	        let next = (chunk / 2).max(DIAL_FLOOR_CHUNK_BYTES.min(self.ceiling_chunk_bytes));
   643	        if next < chunk {
   644	            self.chunk_bytes.store(next, Ordering::Relaxed);
   645	            moved = true;
   646	        }
   647	        let prefetch = self.prefetch_count.load(Ordering::Relaxed);
   648	        let next = (prefetch / 2)
   649	            .max(DIAL_FLOOR_PREFETCH.min(self.ceiling_prefetch))
   650	            .max(1);
   651	        if next < prefetch {
   652	            self.prefetch_count.store(next, Ordering::Relaxed);
   653	            moved = true;
   654	        }
   655	        moved
   656	    }
   657
   658	    /// One tuner tick: adjust from the observed blocked-time ratio
   659	    /// (write-blocked nanos across streams ÷ wall nanos × streams for
   660	    /// the tick window). Between the thresholds nothing moves
   661	    /// (hysteresis band).
   662	    pub fn apply_tick(&self, blocked_ratio: f64) -> bool {
   663	        if blocked_ratio < DIAL_STEP_UP_BLOCKED_RATIO {
   664	            self.step_up_cheap_dials()
   665	        } else if blocked_ratio > DIAL_STEP_DOWN_BLOCKED_RATIO {
   666	            self.step_down_cheap_dials()
   667	        } else {
   668	            false
   669	        }
   670	    }
   671	}
   672
   673	/// Workload-shape-aware initial stream proposal (`ue-r2-1f`): the
   674	/// end that KNOWS the workload shape proposes a starting stream
   675	/// count — file count matters as much as bytes (many small files
   676	/// parallelize on per-file overhead even at low byte totals). On push
   677	/// that is the receiving daemon (it has the manifest) clamped to its
   678	/// own advertised ceiling; on pull_sync it is the sending daemon (it
   679	/// enumerated the source) clamped to the CLIENT's advertised
   680	/// `receiver_capacity.max_streams` (`ue-r2-1g`) — either way the byte
   681	/// receiver's profile is the bound. Table carried over verbatim from
   682	/// the daemon push `desired_streams` ladder it retires (the ladder
   683	/// the old `tuning.rs` doc said "wins"), now engine-owned. The
   684	/// sender's dial clamps again on its side (`set_negotiated_streams`).
   685	/// Live mid-transfer stream changes arrive with `ue-r2-2` resize.
   686	pub fn initial_stream_proposal(total_bytes: u64, file_count: usize, ceiling: usize) -> u32 {
   687	    if file_count == 0 {
   688	        return 1;
   689	    }
   690	    let proposal: u32 = if total_bytes >= 32 * 1024 * 1024 * 1024 || file_count >= 200_000 {
   691	        16
   692	    } else if total_bytes >= 8 * 1024 * 1024 * 1024 || file_count >= 80_000 {
   693	        12
   694	    } else if total_bytes >= 2 * 1024 * 1024 * 1024 || file_count >= 50_000 {
   695	        10
   696	    } else if total_bytes >= 512 * 1024 * 1024 || file_count >= 10_000 {
   697	        8
   698	    } else if total_bytes >= 128 * 1024 * 1024 || file_count >= 2_000 {
   699	        4
   700	    } else if total_bytes >= 32 * 1024 * 1024 || file_count >= 256 {
   701	        2
   702	    } else {
   703	        1
   704	    };
   705	    proposal.min(ceiling.max(1) as u32)
   706	}
   707
   708	/// Blocked-time ratio for one tuner tick: the share of the tick's
   709	/// wall-clock (× stream count) the senders spent inside socket writes.
   710	/// 0 streams or a zero-length tick reads as "no signal" (0.0 — the
   711	/// hysteresis band holds the dial still rather than guessing).
   712	pub(crate) fn blocked_ratio(
   713	    delta_blocked_nanos: u64,
   714	    elapsed: std::time::Duration,
   715	    streams: usize,
   716	) -> f64 {
   717	    let denom = elapsed.as_nanos().saturating_mul(streams as u128);
   718	    if denom == 0 {
   719	        return 0.0;
   720	    }
   721	    (delta_blocked_nanos as f64 / denom as f64).clamp(0.0, 1.0)
   722	}
   723
   724	/// Growable per-transfer probe registry (`ue-r2-2`): resize adds a
   725	/// probe when a stream joins and removes it when one retires, and the
   726	/// tuner samples whatever is live each tick. Plain std mutex — locked
   727	/// only for a snapshot fold every 500ms and on resize events.
   728	pub type SharedStreamProbes =
   729	    Arc<std::sync::Mutex<Vec<crate::remote::transfer::progress::StreamProbe>>>;
   730
   731	/// Spawn the live tuner for one transfer (ue-r2-1e): every
   732	/// [`DIAL_TUNER_TICK`] it sums the PR1 per-stream `write_blocked`
   733	/// telemetry and steps the dial's cheap dials. Holds only a `Weak` to
   734	/// the dial, so it self-terminates within one tick of the transfer
   735	/// dropping its dial; callers may also abort the handle for prompt
   736	/// shutdown (`MultiStreamSender::finish` does).
   737	pub fn spawn_dial_tuner(
   738	    dial: &Arc<TransferDial>,
   739	    probes: Vec<crate::remote::transfer::progress::StreamProbe>,
   740	) -> tokio::task::JoinHandle<()> {
   741	    spawn_dial_tuner_with_resize(dial, Arc::new(std::sync::Mutex::new(probes)), None)
   742	}
   743
   744	/// `ue-r2-2` tuner: same cheap-dial stepping, but over a growable
   745	/// probe registry, plus the stream-resize policy when `resize_tx` is
   746	/// provided — each [`TransferDial::resize_tick`] proposal is forwarded
   747	/// to the adapter that owns the control stream (unbounded so a
   748	/// momentarily busy adapter cannot lose a proposal while the dial
   749	/// holds it pending). Callers without resize pass `None` and get
   750	/// exactly the ue-r2-1e behavior.
   751	pub fn spawn_dial_tuner_with_resize(
   752	    dial: &Arc<TransferDial>,
   753	    probes: SharedStreamProbes,
   754	    resize_tx: Option<tokio::sync::mpsc::UnboundedSender<ResizeProposal>>,
   755	) -> tokio::task::JoinHandle<()> {
   756	    let weak = Arc::downgrade(dial);
   757	    tokio::spawn(async move {
   758	        let mut last_blocked: u64 = 0;
   759	        let mut last_bytes: u64 = 0;
   760	        let mut last_tick = tokio::time::Instant::now();
   761	        loop {
   762	            tokio::time::sleep(DIAL_TUNER_TICK).await;
   763	            let Some(dial) = weak.upgrade() else { return };
   764	            let (blocked, bytes, streams) = {
   765	                let probes = probes.lock().expect("probe registry poisoned");
   766	                let (b, n) = probes.iter().fold((0u64, 0u64), |(b, n), p| {
   767	                    let snap = p.snapshot();
   768	                    (b + snap.write_blocked_nanos, n + snap.bytes_sent)
   769	                });
   770	                (b, n, probes.len())
   771	            };
   772	            let elapsed = last_tick.elapsed();
   773	            last_tick = tokio::time::Instant::now();
   774	            // A retired stream leaves the registry, so the monotonic
   775	            // sums can shrink across a REMOVE. Re-baseline and treat
   776	            // the tick as no-signal rather than reading a bogus delta.
   777	            if blocked < last_blocked || bytes < last_bytes {
   778	                last_blocked = blocked;
   779	                last_bytes = bytes;
   780	                if let Some(tx) = &resize_tx {
   900	        const MIB64: u64 = 1024 * 1024;
   901	        const GIB: u64 = 1024 * MIB64;
   902	        // Empty need-list → 1 (the old ladder's empty-guard).
   903	        assert_eq!(initial_stream_proposal(0, 0, 32), 1);
   904	        // Byte-keyed tiers: exact lower boundaries AND just-below each
   905	        // (codex ue-r2-1f: representative values would miss a doubled
   906	        // threshold).
   907	        assert_eq!(initial_stream_proposal(32 * MIB64 - 1, 10, 32), 1);
   908	        assert_eq!(initial_stream_proposal(32 * MIB64, 10, 32), 2);
   909	        assert_eq!(initial_stream_proposal(128 * MIB64 - 1, 10, 32), 2);
   910	        assert_eq!(initial_stream_proposal(128 * MIB64, 10, 32), 4);
   911	        assert_eq!(initial_stream_proposal(512 * MIB64 - 1, 10, 32), 4);
   912	        assert_eq!(initial_stream_proposal(512 * MIB64, 10, 32), 8);
   913	        assert_eq!(initial_stream_proposal(2 * GIB - 1, 10, 32), 8);
   914	        assert_eq!(initial_stream_proposal(2 * GIB, 10, 32), 10);
   915	        assert_eq!(initial_stream_proposal(8 * GIB - 1, 10, 32), 10);
   916	        assert_eq!(initial_stream_proposal(8 * GIB, 10, 32), 12);
   917	        assert_eq!(initial_stream_proposal(32 * GIB - 1, 10, 32), 12);
   918	        assert_eq!(initial_stream_proposal(32 * GIB, 10, 32), 16);
   919	        // File-count keys fire independently of bytes.
   920	        assert_eq!(initial_stream_proposal(1, 256, 32), 2);
   921	        assert_eq!(initial_stream_proposal(1, 2_000, 32), 4);
   922	        assert_eq!(initial_stream_proposal(1, 10_000, 32), 8);
   923	        assert_eq!(initial_stream_proposal(1, 50_000, 32), 10);
   924	        assert_eq!(initial_stream_proposal(1, 80_000, 32), 12);
   925	        assert_eq!(initial_stream_proposal(1, 200_000, 32), 16);
   926	        // Ceiling clamps the proposal (receiver profile authority).
   927	        assert_eq!(initial_stream_proposal(32 * GIB, 10, 6), 6);
   928	        assert_eq!(initial_stream_proposal(32 * GIB, 10, 0), 1, "floor 1");
   929	    }

   931	    #[test]
   932	    fn blocked_ratio_handles_edges() {
   933	        use std::time::Duration;
   934	        assert_eq!(blocked_ratio(0, Duration::from_millis(500), 4), 0.0);
   935	        assert_eq!(blocked_ratio(1_000, Duration::ZERO, 4), 0.0, "no signal");
   936	        assert_eq!(blocked_ratio(1_000, Duration::from_millis(500), 0), 0.0);
   937	        let half = blocked_ratio(500_000_000, Duration::from_millis(500), 2);
   938	        assert!((half - 0.5).abs() < 1e-9, "got {half}");
   939	        assert_eq!(
   940	            blocked_ratio(u64::MAX, Duration::from_nanos(1), 1),
   941	            1.0,
   942	            "clamped"
   943	        );
   944	    }
   945
   946	    #[tokio::test(start_paused = true)]
   947	    async fn tuner_steps_up_on_clean_telemetry_and_exits_when_dial_drops() {
   948	        use crate::remote::transfer::progress::{StreamId, StreamProbe};
   949	        let dial = TransferDial::conservative().shared();
   950	        let probes = [StreamProbe::new(StreamId(0)), StreamProbe::new(StreamId(1))];
   951	        let tuner_view: Vec<StreamProbe> = probes
   952	            .iter()
   953	            .map(|p| StreamProbe::from_telemetry(p.id(), p.telemetry()))
   954	            .collect();
   955	        let handle = spawn_dial_tuner(&dial, tuner_view);
   956	        // Let the spawned task run to its first sleep so the timer is
   957	        // registered before the clock moves.
   958	        tokio::task::yield_now().await;
   959
   960	        // codex F2: an idle tick (no bytes moved) must NOT step.
   961	        tokio::time::advance(DIAL_TUNER_TICK + std::time::Duration::from_millis(10)).await;
   962	        for _ in 0..16 {
   963	            tokio::task::yield_now().await;
   964	        }
   965	        assert_eq!(dial.chunk_bytes(), 16 * MIB, "idle tick is no signal");
   966
   967	        // One tick WITH byte progress and zero blocked time: step up.
   968	        probes[0].record_bytes(1024);
   969	        tokio::time::advance(DIAL_TUNER_TICK + std::time::Duration::from_millis(10)).await;
   970	        for _ in 0..16 {
   971	            if dial.chunk_bytes() > 16 * MIB {
   972	                break;
   973	            }
   974	            tokio::task::yield_now().await;
   975	        }
   976	        assert_eq!(dial.chunk_bytes(), 32 * MIB, "stepped up once");
   977
   978	        // Drop the transfer's dial: the tuner must self-terminate.
   979	        drop(dial);
   980	        tokio::time::advance(DIAL_TUNER_TICK + std::time::Duration::from_millis(10)).await;
   981	        tokio::time::timeout(std::time::Duration::from_secs(5), handle)
   982	            .await
   983	            .expect("tuner exits after the dial drops")
   984	            .expect("tuner does not panic");
   985	    }

   987	    #[test]
   988	    fn negotiated_streams_clamp_to_the_profile_ceiling() {
   989	        let dial = TransferDial::conservative_within(Some(&profile(6, 0, 0)));
   990	        dial.allow_streams_up_to(32);
   991	        assert_eq!(dial.max_streams(), 6, "peer cannot exceed the profile");
   992	        assert_eq!(dial.set_negotiated_streams(16), 6);
   993	        assert_eq!(dial.set_negotiated_streams(3), 3);
   994	        assert_eq!(dial.live_streams(), 3, "negotiation seeds the live count");
   995	    }
   996
   997	    // ── ue-r2-2 resize policy ────────────────────────────────────────
   998
   999	    /// Burn the cooldown with busy, in-band ticks that move no dials.
  1000	    fn burn_cooldown(dial: &TransferDial) {
  1001	        for _ in 0..RESIZE_COOLDOWN_TICKS {
  1002	            assert_eq!(dial.resize_tick(1024, 0.15), None, "in-band tick holds");
  1003	        }
  1004	    }
  1005
  1006	    #[test]
  1007	    fn resize_add_requires_maxed_cheap_dials_sustain_and_cooldown() {
  1008	        let dial = TransferDial::conservative();
  1009	        dial.set_negotiated_streams(4);
  1010
  1011	        // Clean pipe but cheap dials NOT at ceiling: never proposes,
  1012	        // no matter how long it holds (cheap dials escalate first).
  1013	        for _ in 0..16 {
  1014	            assert_eq!(dial.resize_tick(1024, 0.0), None);
  1015	        }
  1016
  1017	        // Pin the cheap dials at the ceiling, then a clean signal must
  1018	        // still survive the sustain requirement before proposing.
  1019	        while dial.step_up_cheap_dials() {}
  1020	        assert_eq!(dial.resize_tick(1024, 0.0), None, "sustain tick 1");
  1021	        let proposal = dial
  1022	            .resize_tick(1024, 0.0)
  1023	            .expect("sustained clean signal at maxed dials proposes");
  1024	        assert_eq!(
  1025	            proposal,
  1026	            ResizeProposal {
  1027	                epoch: 1,
  1028	                target_streams: 5,
  1029	                add: true
  1030	            }
  1031	        );
  1032	        assert!(dial.resize_pending());
  1033
  1034	        // In flight: no further proposals regardless of signal.
  1035	        for _ in 0..8 {
  1036	            assert_eq!(dial.resize_tick(1024, 0.0), None, "pending blocks");
  1037	        }
  1038
  1039	        // Accepted settle: live moves, epoch advances, cooldown blocks
  1040	        // the immediate next proposal even under a perfect signal.
  1041	        dial.resize_settled(1, 5, true);
  1042	        assert_eq!(dial.live_streams(), 5);
  1043	        assert_eq!(dial.resize_epoch(), 1);
  1044	        assert!(!dial.resize_pending());
  1045	        for _ in 0..(RESIZE_COOLDOWN_TICKS - 1) {
  1046	            assert_eq!(dial.resize_tick(1024, 0.0), None, "cooldown holds");
  1047	        }
  1048	        // Cooldown expired and the clean streak has been building the
  1049	        // whole time — the next clean tick proposes epoch 2.
  1050	        let next = dial.resize_tick(1024, 0.0).expect("epoch 2 proposes");
  1051	        assert_eq!(next.epoch, 2);
  1052	        assert_eq!(next.target_streams, 6);
  1053	    }
  1054
  1055	    #[test]
  1056	    fn resize_remove_requires_floored_cheap_dials_and_floors_at_one() {
  1057	        let dial = TransferDial::conservative();
  1058	        dial.set_negotiated_streams(2);
  1059	        burn_cooldown(&dial);
  1060
  1061	        // Blocked pipe with cheap dials at the floor (conservative
  1062	        // start IS the floor): two sustained ticks propose a drop.
  1063	        assert_eq!(dial.resize_tick(1024, 0.9), None, "sustain tick 1");
  1064	        let proposal = dial.resize_tick(1024, 0.9).expect("sustained block drops");
  1065	        assert_eq!(
  1066	            proposal,
  1067	            ResizeProposal {
  1068	                epoch: 1,
  1069	                target_streams: 1,
  1070	                add: false
  1071	            }
  1072	        );
  1073	        dial.resize_settled(1, 1, true);
  1074	        assert_eq!(dial.live_streams(), 1);
  1075
  1076	        // At one stream, a blocked pipe can never drop to zero.
  1077	        burn_cooldown(&dial);
  1078	        for _ in 0..8 {
  1079	            assert_eq!(dial.resize_tick(1024, 0.9), None, "floor at 1");
  1080	        }
  1081	    }
  1082
  1083	    #[test]
  1084	    fn resize_signal_interruptions_and_idle_reset_sustain() {
  1085	        let dial = TransferDial::conservative();
  1086	        dial.set_negotiated_streams(4);
  1087	        while dial.step_up_cheap_dials() {}
  1088	        burn_cooldown(&dial);
  1089
  1090	        // clean → idle → clean: the idle tick resets the streak, so
  1091	        // the second clean tick is streak 1, not 2.
  1092	        assert_eq!(dial.resize_tick(1024, 0.0), None);
  1093	        assert_eq!(dial.resize_tick(0, 0.0), None, "idle resets");
  1094	        assert_eq!(dial.resize_tick(1024, 0.0), None, "streak restarted");
  1095	        // clean → in-band → clean: same reset.
  1096	        assert_eq!(dial.resize_tick(1024, 0.15), None, "in-band resets");
  1097	        assert_eq!(dial.resize_tick(1024, 0.0), None, "streak restarted");
  1098	        assert!(dial.resize_tick(1024, 0.0).is_some(), "streak completes");
  1099	    }

  1101	    #[test]
  1102	    fn resize_refusal_is_terminal_consumes_epoch_and_ignores_stale_settles() {
  1103	        let dial = TransferDial::conservative();
  1104	        dial.set_negotiated_streams(4);
  1105	        while dial.step_up_cheap_dials() {}
  1106	        burn_cooldown(&dial);
  1107	        assert_eq!(dial.resize_tick(1024, 0.0), None);
  1108	        let proposal = dial.resize_tick(1024, 0.0).expect("proposes");
  1109
  1110	        // A stale/foreign epoch must not clear the pending slot.
  1111	        dial.resize_settled(proposal.epoch + 7, 9, true);
  1112	        assert!(dial.resize_pending(), "stale settle ignored");
  1113
  1114	        // Refusal: pending clears and live count stays put, but the wire
  1115	        // epoch is consumed and every resize producer becomes terminal.
  1116	        dial.resize_settled(proposal.epoch, dial.live_streams(), false);
  1117	        assert!(!dial.resize_pending());
  1118	        assert_eq!(dial.live_streams(), 4);
  1119	        assert_eq!(dial.resize_epoch(), proposal.epoch);
  1120	        assert_eq!(
  1121	            dial.resize_tick(1024, 0.0),
  1122	            None,
  1123	            "the tuner must not retry a refused resize"
  1124	        );
  1125	        assert_eq!(
  1126	            dial.propose_shape_resize(8),
  1127	            None,
  1128	            "shape correction must not retry a refused resize"
  1129	        );
  1130
  1131	        // Duplicate/stale settlements cannot reopen the terminal policy or
  1132	        // rewrite the consumed epoch/live count.
  1133	        dial.resize_settled(proposal.epoch, 5, true);
  1134	        assert_eq!(dial.resize_epoch(), proposal.epoch);
  1135	        assert_eq!(dial.live_streams(), 4);
  1136	        assert_eq!(dial.propose_shape_resize(8), None);
  1137	    }

  1139	    #[test]
  1140	    fn resize_epoch_lock_serializes_producers_across_settlement() {
  1141	        // The tuner lock spans eligibility, direction, live count, and claim:
  1142	        // pause after it acquires the lock and prove the arbitration mutex is
  1143	        // unavailable until the tick continues. This deterministically guards
  1144	        // the stale-decision window the re-review found.
  1145	        let accepted = Arc::new(TransferDial::conservative());
  1146	        while accepted.step_up_cheap_dials() {}
  1147	        burn_cooldown(&accepted);
  1148	        assert_eq!(accepted.resize_tick(1024, 0.0), None, "sustain tick 1");
  1149	        let hook = Arc::new(ResizeTickTestHook::new());
  1150	        *accepted
  1151	            .resize_tick_test_hook
  1152	            .lock()
  1153	            .expect("resize tick test hook poisoned") = Some(Arc::clone(&hook));
  1154	        let tuner = {
  1155	            let accepted = Arc::clone(&accepted);
  1156	            std::thread::spawn(move || accepted.resize_tick(1024, 0.0))
  1157	        };
  1158	        hook.entered.wait();
  1159	        let lock_spans_tick = matches!(
  1160	            accepted.resize_epochs.try_lock(),
  1161	            Err(std::sync::TryLockError::WouldBlock)
  1162	        );
  1163	        // Start the matching accepted settlement while the tuner is paused.
  1164	        // The settlement hook reports only after `try_lock` has actually
  1165	        // observed epoch arbitration, so this does not rely on a pre-call
  1166	        // channel signal or scheduler timing.
  1167	        let settle_hook = Arc::new(ResizeSettleTestHook::new());
  1168	        *accepted
  1169	            .resize_settle_test_hook
  1170	            .lock()
  1171	            .expect("resize settle test hook poisoned") = Some(Arc::clone(&settle_hook));
  1172	        let settler = {
  1173	            let accepted = Arc::clone(&accepted);
  1174	            std::thread::spawn(move || accepted.resize_settled(1, 2, true))
  1175	        };
  1176	        settle_hook.observed.wait();
  1177	        let settlement_contended = settle_hook.contended.load(Ordering::SeqCst);
  1178	        hook.release.wait();
  1179	        *accepted
  1180	            .resize_tick_test_hook
  1181	            .lock()
  1182	            .expect("resize tick test hook poisoned") = None;
  1183	        assert!(lock_spans_tick, "tuner released arbitration before claim");
  1184	        let first = tuner.join().unwrap().expect("tuner owns epoch 1");
  1185	        let entered_acquisition = hook.entered_acquisition.load(Ordering::SeqCst);
  1186	        let claimed_acquisition = hook.claimed_acquisition.load(Ordering::SeqCst);
  1187	        assert_ne!(entered_acquisition, 0, "tuner acquisition was recorded");
  1188	        assert_eq!(
  1189	            claimed_acquisition, entered_acquisition,
  1190	            "tuner released and reacquired arbitration before claim"
  1191	        );
  1192	        assert_eq!(first.epoch, 1);
  1193	        settler.join().unwrap();
  1194	        *accepted
  1195	            .resize_settle_test_hook
  1196	            .lock()
  1197	            .expect("resize settle test hook poisoned") = None;
  1198	        assert!(
  1199	            settlement_contended,
  1200	            "accepted settlement reached arbitration without contention"
  1201	        );
  1202	        assert_eq!(accepted.live_streams(), 2, "accepted settlement applied");
  1203	        assert!(!accepted.resize_pending(), "accepted epoch settled");
  1204	        assert_eq!(
  1205	            accepted.resize_tick(1024, 0.0),
  1206	            None,
  1207	            "accepted settlement resets cooldown"
  1208	        );
  1209
  1210	        // Refusal crossing: a shape producer begins while the settlement
  1211	        // lock is held. Once the same production helper records refusal and
  1212	        // releases the lock, the waiter must observe terminal state and may
  1213	        // not claim epoch 2.
  1214	        let refused = Arc::new(TransferDial::conservative());
  1215	        let first = refused.propose_shape_resize(8).expect("shape owns epoch 1");
  1216	        let mut refused_state = refused
  1217	            .resize_epochs
  1218	            .lock()
  1219	            .expect("resize epoch state poisoned");
  1220	        let (started_tx, started_rx) = std::sync::mpsc::channel();
  1221	        let shape = {
  1222	            let refused = Arc::clone(&refused);
  1223	            std::thread::spawn(move || {
  1224	                started_tx.send(()).unwrap();
  1225	                refused.propose_shape_resize(8)
  1226	            })
  1227	        };
  1228	        started_rx.recv().unwrap();
  1229	        assert!(refused.resize_settled_locked(&mut refused_state, first.epoch, 1, false));
  1230	        drop(refused_state);
  1231	        assert_eq!(shape.join().unwrap(), None, "refusal is terminal");
  1232	        assert_eq!(refused.resize_epoch(), first.epoch);
  1233	        assert!(!refused.resize_pending());
  1234	    }

  1236	    #[test]

  1239	        dial.set_negotiated_streams(4); // already at the profile ceiling

  1165	async fn many_tiny_files_reach_shape_target_when_source_initiates() {

  1196	        plan_options: PlanOptions::default(),
  1197	        data_plane_host: Some("127.0.0.1".into()),
  1198	    };

  1235	/// Hold one resize ACK in the SOURCE receive half. The data-plane payload
  1236	/// lane is independent, so a correct nonblocking ramp can keep moving work
  1237	/// while this control frame waits; a pre-dispatch settle cannot.
  1238	struct GateNthResizeAckRx {
  1239	    inner: Box<dyn FrameRx>,
  1240	    gate_at: usize,
  1241	    seen: Arc<AtomicUsize>,
  1242	    delivered: Arc<AtomicUsize>,
  1243	    waiting: Arc<AtomicBool>,
  1244	    reached: Arc<tokio::sync::Notify>,
  1245	    release: Arc<tokio::sync::Notify>,
  1246	}
  1247
  1248	#[async_trait::async_trait]
  1249	impl FrameRx for GateNthResizeAckRx {
  1250	    async fn recv(&mut self) -> eyre::Result<Option<TransferFrame>> {
  1251	        let frame = self.inner.recv().await?;
  1252	        let is_resize_ack = frame
  1253	            .as_ref()
  1254	            .is_some_and(|frame| matches!(frame.frame.as_ref(), Some(Frame::ResizeAck(_))));
  1255	        if is_resize_ack {
  1256	            let ordinal = self.seen.fetch_add(1, Ordering::SeqCst) + 1;
  1257	            if ordinal == self.gate_at {
  1258	                self.waiting.store(true, Ordering::SeqCst);
  1259	                self.reached.notify_one();
  1260	                self.release.notified().await;
  1261	            }
  1262	            self.delivered.fetch_add(1, Ordering::SeqCst);
  1263	        }
  1264	        Ok(frame)
  1265	    }
  1266	}
