.review/results/macmac-r12.codex-design.md:81:   T = min(10% of the source arm, 230ms), four cell states from an exact order-statistic CI,
.review/results/macmac-r12.codex-design.md:99:question", that is the most valuable thing you can tell me, and it will not offend me. If it
.review/results/macmac-r12.codex-design.md:137:The schedule is more careful than the causal question warrants, but I’ve found a structural issue already: the end-of-session “fabric re-check” verifies only MSS and source IP, not negotiated link speed. A 10GbE link that renegotiates to 1GbE can keep the same MTU, MSS, and IP and still be graded. I’m checking for the same live/dead protection pattern elsewhere and validating the verdict tests.
.review/results/macmac-r12.codex-design.md:183:| `REPRODUCES` | P1-shaped behavior can occur on these Macs without Windows; use them as a local debugging rig. | H1 confirmed or killed; platform-general behavior; Windows exonerated as a contributor; release acceptance. |
.review/results/macmac-r12.codex-design.md:185:| `MIXED` | Strong host×role, ordering, or endpoint interaction requiring decomposition. | Either reproduction or null. |
.review/results/macmac-r12.codex-design.md:192:Only `REPRODUCES` changes anything operationally: it changes where debugging can happen. Every substantive path still leads to direct instrumentation and a causal counterfactual, so the full experiment is close to theatre even before its remaining defects.
.review/results/macmac-r12.codex-design.md:200:- **HIGH — n=8 is enough for an exact median CI, not enough to promise a useful answer.** Under independent, identically distributed paired differences, `[min,max]` has 99.22% coverage for the population median. But `EFFECT` requires every pair to clear the margin and `NONE` requires every pair inside it. One soft pair produces `UNCLEAR`; modest control noise produces `CONTROLS-NOT-CLEAN`. No empirical power calculation justifies expecting decisiveness. ABBA controls first-order drift but does not establish independence or stationarity. See [PREREGISTRATION.md:201](/Users/michael/Dev/blit_v2/docs/bench/otp12-macmac-2026-07-14/PREREGISTRATION.md:201) and [PREREGISTRATION.md:380](/Users/michael/Dev/blit_v2/docs/bench/otp12-macmac-2026-07-14/PREREGISTRATION.md:380).
.review/results/macmac-r12.codex-design.md:210:- **BLOCKER — `min()` implements the opposite of the stated policy.** The document says an effect must matter by both the 10% bar and the 230 ms reference at [PREREGISTRATION.md:212](/Users/michael/Dev/blit_v2/docs/bench/otp12-macmac-2026-07-14/PREREGISTRATION.md:212). `min(src/10, 230)` lets either smaller standard win. The test deliberately labels `230 ms @ 2500 ms` as `REPRODUCES` at [otp12pf_mac_verdict_test.py:111](/Users/michael/Dev/blit_v2/scripts/otp12pf_mac_verdict_test.py:111), even though its ratio is 1.092 and the engine’s own acceptance bar says `PASS`. Thus the engine can confidently report “P1 reproduces” when P1’s defining invariance criterion passes.
.review/results/macmac-r12.codex-design.md:212:- **BLOCKER — the exact 10% boundary is simultaneously PASS and REPRODUCES.** The bar treats `≤1.10` as PASS at [otp12pf_mac_verdict.py:282](/Users/michael/Dev/blit_v2/scripts/otp12pf_mac_verdict.py:282), while `classify()` uses `CI_lo >= T` at [otp12pf_mac_verdict.py:251](/Users/michael/Dev/blit_v2/scripts/otp12pf_mac_verdict.py:251). The guard explicitly requires an exact 10% effect to reproduce at [otp12pf_mac_verdict_test.py:217](/Users/michael/Dev/blit_v2/scripts/otp12pf_mac_verdict_test.py:217). That is a direct policy contradiction, not statistical conservatism.
.review/results/macmac-r12.codex-design.md:224:- **BLOCKER — the end-fabric protection does not recheck the full fabric it promises.** Startup checks MTU, negotiated `10Gbase-T`, and link status at [bench_otp12pf_mac.sh:499](/Users/michael/Dev/blit_v2/scripts/bench_otp12pf_mac.sh:499). The end gate checks only MSS and local IP at [bench_otp12pf_mac.sh:557](/Users/michael/Dev/blit_v2/scripts/bench_otp12pf_mac.sh:557). A link can renegotiate from 10GbE to 1GbE while retaining MTU 9000, MSS 8948, and the same IP; the session will grade. This is exactly the duplicate-site defect class.
.review/results/macmac-r12.codex-design.md:228:- **MEDIUM — manifest fabric/hash probes discard producer status.** Local verdict/test hashes are not validated at [bench_otp12pf_mac.sh:739](/Users/michael/Dev/blit_v2/scripts/bench_otp12pf_mac.sh:739), and `mss_read` is embedded inside `echo` at [bench_otp12pf_mac.sh:750](/Users/michael/Dev/blit_v2/scripts/bench_otp12pf_mac.sh:750). A failed reader produces an empty “measured fabric” entry while `echo` succeeds and the run continues.
.review/results/macmac-r12.codex-design.md:234:**DO NOT RUN IT — first validate P1 with the corrected measurement on the known-failing Mac↔Windows pair and instrument the suspected path; the current rule can call a 1.092/PASS cell “REPRODUCES,” and the end-fabric gate is incomplete.**
.review/results/macmac-r12.codex-design.md:245:| `REPRODUCES` | P1-shaped behavior can occur on these Macs without Windows; use them as a local debugging rig. | H1 confirmed or killed; platform-general behavior; Windows exonerated as a contributor; release acceptance. |
.review/results/macmac-r12.codex-design.md:247:| `MIXED` | Strong host×role, ordering, or endpoint interaction requiring decomposition. | Either reproduction or null. |
.review/results/macmac-r12.codex-design.md:254:Only `REPRODUCES` changes anything operationally: it changes where debugging can happen. Every substantive path still leads to direct instrumentation and a causal counterfactual, so the full experiment is close to theatre even before its remaining defects.
.review/results/macmac-r12.codex-design.md:262:- **HIGH — n=8 is enough for an exact median CI, not enough to promise a useful answer.** Under independent, identically distributed paired differences, `[min,max]` has 99.22% coverage for the population median. But `EFFECT` requires every pair to clear the margin and `NONE` requires every pair inside it. One soft pair produces `UNCLEAR`; modest control noise produces `CONTROLS-NOT-CLEAN`. No empirical power calculation justifies expecting decisiveness. ABBA controls first-order drift but does not establish independence or stationarity. See [PREREGISTRATION.md:201](/Users/michael/Dev/blit_v2/docs/bench/otp12-macmac-2026-07-14/PREREGISTRATION.md:201) and [PREREGISTRATION.md:380](/Users/michael/Dev/blit_v2/docs/bench/otp12-macmac-2026-07-14/PREREGISTRATION.md:380).

The test deliberately labels `230 ms @ 2500 ms` as `REPRODUCES` at [otp12pf_mac_verdict_test.py:111](/Users/michael/Dev/blit_v2/scripts/otp12pf_mac_verdict_test.py:111), even though its ratio is 1.092 and the engine’s own acceptance bar says `PASS`. The guard explicitly requires an exact 10% effect to reproduce at [otp12pf_mac_verdict_test.py:217](/Users/michael/Dev/blit_v2/scripts/otp12pf_mac_verdict_test.py:217). That is a direct policy contradiction, not statistical conservatism.
.review/results/macmac-r12.codex-design.md:286:- **BLOCKER — the end-fabric protection does not recheck the full fabric it promises.** Startup checks MTU, negotiated `10Gbase-T`, and link status at [bench_otp12pf_mac.sh:499](/Users/michael/Dev/blit_v2/scripts/bench_otp12pf_mac.sh:499). A failed reader produces an empty “measured fabric” entry while `echo` succeeds and the run continues.
.review/results/macmac-r12.codex-design.md:296:**DO NOT RUN IT — first validate P1 with the corrected measurement on the known-failing Mac↔Windows pair and instrument the suspected path; the current rule can call a 1.092/PASS cell “REPRODUCES,” and the end-fabric gate is incomplete.**
   300	#
   301	# If the controls are genuinely clean, B is a few ms and this barely moves. If they are
   302	# marginal, it bites -- which is the point.
   303	dirty = []
   304	B_frac = 0.0          # RELATIVE, not raw milliseconds
   305	for c in CONTROLS:
   306	    x = cell.get(c, {})
   307	    if x.get("state") == "INCOMPLETE":
   308	        continue
   309	    c_pos, c_neg = thresholds(x["src"], 0.5)
   310	    x["ctrl_state"] = classify(x["ci"][0], x["ci"][1], x["rng"][0], x["rng"][1], c_pos, c_neg)
   311	    x["ctrl_T"] = c_pos
   312	    if x["ctrl_state"] != "NONE":
   313	        dirty.append(c)
   314	    elif x["src"]:
   315	        # B IS A FRACTION OF THE ARM, NOT A MILLISECOND COUNT (round-9 codex, BLOCKER).
   316	        # The controls run on DIFFERENT fixtures and therefore different arm speeds: the
   317	        # same 4.9% arm bias is 122 ms on a 2500 ms large-file control and 24 ms on a fast
   318	        # one. Carrying raw ms across them OVER-penalises a measurand slower than the
   319	        # control and UNDER-penalises one that is faster -- and the second direction is the
   320	        # dangerous one: a 4.9% bias measured on a fast control would license a measurand
   321	        # effect that is mostly rig. Take the bias as a FRACTION and scale it to whatever
   322	        # arm it is being applied to.
   323	        B_frac = max(B_frac, abs(x["rng"][0]) / x["src"], abs(x["rng"][1]) / x["src"])
   324
   325	# ---- pass 3: grade the measurands, against thresholds widened by the control bias -----
   326	# B >= T/2 IS NOT A CLEAN RIG (D-2026-07-14-4; round-11 codex HIGH, grok found the same
   327	# dead-zone independently). T is CAPPED at DELTA_REF, but the bias a clean control is
   328	# permitted to carry is a FRACTION of its arm -- so on a SLOW measurand the two diverge:
   329	# clean controls at 4.9% of a 1000 ms arm give B = 490 ms on a 10000 ms measurand, while
   330	# T stays 230. Then T - B < 0 -- A NULL IS ARITHMETICALLY IMPOSSIBLE -- while T + B still
   331	# licenses an EFFECT of which up to 68% is PERMITTED RIG BIAS, at a ratio of 1.072, i.e.
   332	# inside the project's own invariance bar. A confidently wrong reproduction, off a rig
   333	# certified clean.
   334	#
   335	# The owner's rule (verbatim: "Refuse to grade"): if the rig's own permitted noise reaches
   336	# HALF the effect being hunted, nothing read off it can be attributed. Same principle as a
   337	# dirty control -- a noisy rig is fixed by a QUIETER RIG, not by grading it with an asterisk.
   338	bias_over = []
   339	for c in MEASURANDS:
   340	    x = cell.get(c, {})
   341	    if x.get("state") == "INCOMPLETE":
   342	        continue
   343	    t_pos, t_neg = thresholds(x["src"])
   344	    B = B_frac * x["src"]                    # the control bias, on THIS cell's arm
   345	    x["T"] = t_pos
   346	    x["B"] = B
   347	    # `t_pos > 0` is NOT a fail-open: src_med is a POSITIVE integer by ms_of(), so t_pos is
   348	    # only zero in a mutant that accepts zero timings -- and the guard keeps THAT mutant's
   349	    # dangerous path (zeros -> EFFECT -> REPRODUCES) reachable, instead of masking it behind
   350	    # this refusal and killing the mutation for the wrong reason.
   351	    if t_pos > 0 and B >= t_pos / 2.0:
   352	        bias_over.append(c)
   353	    # ONE call, and B moves every bound AGAINST the reader: an EFFECT must clear T + B, a
   354	    # NULL must fit inside T - B. (It used to widen the NONE window on the way in and get
   355	    # tightened again on the way out -- see classify().)
   356	    x["state"] = classify(x["ci"][0], x["ci"][1], x["rng"][0], x["rng"][1], t_pos, t_neg, B)
   357	    # THE UNHARDENED STATE (B = 0), kept ONLY for the MIXED test below. B hardens each CELL,
   358	    # but through MIXED's precedence it could make the SESSION verdict EASIER -- see there.
   359	    x["state0"] = classify(x["ci"][0], x["ci"][1], x["rng"][0], x["rng"][1], t_pos, t_neg, 0.0)
   360
   361	# Controls also carry a state for the report; measurands carry a ctrl_state for symmetry.
   362	for c in cell:
   363	    x = cell[c]
   364	    if x.get("state") == "INCOMPLETE":
   365	        continue
   366	    if "state" not in x:                                  # a control: report its own state
   367	        t_pos, t_neg = thresholds(x["src"])
   368	        x["T"] = t_pos
   369	        x["B"] = 0.0
   370	        x["state"] = classify(x["ci"][0], x["ci"][1], x["rng"][0], x["rng"][1], t_pos, t_neg)
   371	    x.setdefault("ctrl_state", "-")
   372
   373	# ---- outputs -----------------------------------------------------------------------
   374	with open(sum_p, "w") as f:
   375	    f.write("cell,arm,median_ms,avg_ms,best_ms,worst_ms,voided,runs\n")
   376	    for (c, a) in sorted(by):
   377	        v = by[(c, a)]
   378	        f.write("%s,%s,%d,%d,%d,%d,%d,%s\n" % (c, a, round(med_arm(v)), sum(v) // len(v),
   379	                                               min(v), max(v), voided.get((c, a), 0),
   380	                                               " ".join(map(str, v))))
   381
   382	with open(pair_p, "w") as f:
   383	    f.write("cell,n,srcinit_med,destinit_med,ratio,bar,D_ms,CI_lo,CI_hi,range_lo,range_hi,"
   384	            "coverage,T_ms,B_ms,sign_p,k_pos,state,control_state\n")
   385	    for c in sorted(cell):
   386	        x = cell[c]
   387	        if x["state"] == "INCOMPLETE":
   388	            f.write("%s,%d,,,,,,,,,,,,,,,INCOMPLETE,\n" % (c, x["n"]))
   389	            continue
   390	        f.write("%s,%d,%d,%d,%.3f,%s,%d,%d,%d,%d,%d,%.4f,%d,%d,%.4f,%d/%d,%s,%s\n" % (
   391	            c, x["n"], round(x["src"]), round(x["dst"]), x["ratio"], x["bar"], x["D"],
   392	            x["ci"][0], x["ci"][1], x["rng"][0], x["rng"][1], x["cov"],
   393	            round(x["T"]), round(x.get("B", 0)), x["p"], x["k"], x["n"],
   394	            x["state"], x["ctrl_state"]))
   395
   396	with open(ver_p, "w") as f:
   397	    f.write("comparison,kind,lhs_ms,rhs_ms,ratio,bar\n")
   398	    for c in sorted(cell):
   399	        x = cell[c]
   400	        if x["state"] == "INCOMPLETE":
   401	            f.write("%s,invariance,,,,INCOMPLETE\n" % c)
   402	        else:
   403	            f.write("%s,invariance,%d,%d,%.3f,%s\n"
   404	                    % (c, round(x["src"]), round(x["dst"]), x["ratio"], x["bar"]))
   405
   406	# ---- THE SESSION VERDICT -----------------------------------------------------------
   407	incomplete = [c for c in REGISTERED if cell.get(c, {}).get("state") == "INCOMPLETE"]
   408	m = {c: cell[c]["state"] for c in MEASURANDS if not incomplete}
   409	# The UNHARDENED states (B = 0). MIXED is decided on these -- see the MIXED branch.
   410	m0 = {c: cell[c]["state0"] for c in MEASURANDS if not incomplete}
   411
   412	if incomplete:
   413	    verdict = "INCOMPLETE"
   414	    why = ("cells short of their %d pairs, or with a CI below the registered %.0f%% "
   415	           "coverage: %s. No verdict is read." % (PAIRS, 100 * MIN_COVERAGE,
   416	                                                  ", ".join(incomplete)))
   417	elif SESSION_VOID:
   418	    verdict = "RIG-VOID"
   419	    why = "the harness voided this session: %s. No verdict is read." % SESSION_VOID
   420	elif dirty or bias_over:
   421	    verdict = "CONTROLS-NOT-CLEAN"
   422	    why = ""
   423	    if dirty:
   424	        why += ("control cell(s) are not free of an arm asymmetry at T/2: %s. P1 is claimed "
   425	                "TCP-only and mixed-only; if the gRPC/large controls may be carrying the same "
   426	                "asymmetry, then NEITHER a reproduction NOR a null is readable off this rig. "
   427	                % ", ".join("%s(%s, D=%+dms, CI=[%+d,%+d], T/2=%d)"
   428	                            % (c, cell[c]["ctrl_state"], cell[c]["D"], cell[c]["ci"][0],
   429	                               cell[c]["ci"][1], round(cell[c]["T"] / 2))
   430	                            for c in dirty))
   431	    if bias_over:
   432	        # The controls PASSED at T/2 and the rig is STILL not clean enough to read: their
   433	        # permitted residual bias, scaled onto this measurand's (slower) arm, is at least
   434	        # HALF the effect we came to measure. D-2026-07-14-4.
   435	        why += ("the controls are clean at T/2, but the residual bias they could NOT exclude "
   436	                "reaches HALF the effect threshold on: %s. The permitted rig bias is a "
   437	                "FRACTION of the arm while T is CAPPED at %d ms, so on an arm this slow a "
   438	                "null is impossible (T-B <= 0 or nearly so) and an 'effect' could be mostly "
   439	                "rig. NOTHING is readable off this rig -- not a reproduction, not a null. "
   440	                % (", ".join("%s(T=%dms, B=%dms, B/T=%.2f)"
   441	                             % (c, round(cell[c]["T"]), round(cell[c]["B"]),
   442	                                cell[c]["B"] / cell[c]["T"] if cell[c]["T"] else 0.0)
   443	                             for c in bias_over), DELTA_REF))
   444	    why += ("There is no escalation: a noisy rig is fixed by a QUIETER RIG, not more pairs.")
   445	elif "EFFECT" in m0.values() and "INVERTED" in m0.values():
   446	    # DECIDED ON THE UNHARDENED STATES (round-11 codex, HIGH). B hardens each CELL, but
   447	    # through this branch's PRECEDENCE it could make the SESSION verdict EASIER: with
   448	    # measurands at +110 and -94 (src=1000), controls at zero give MIXED -- but clean
   449	    # controls at +5 push the -94 cell out of INVERTED (it needs <= -95.9), the MIXED branch
   450	    # stops firing, and the session reports REPRODUCES. A NOISIER RIG PRODUCED A STRONGER
   451	    # CLAIM. B must only ever HARDEN, so the two-directions-disagree test is taken on the
   452	    # states BEFORE B is applied: a cell whose CI clears -T on its own carries evidence of
   453	    # inversion no amount of control noise can un-say, and a session where one direction
   454	    # shows the effect and the other inverts it is inconclusive -- whatever B is.
   455	    #
   456	    # (EFFECT-at-B is a SUBSET of EFFECT-at-0, so this can only ever fire MORE often, never
   457	    # less. That is the direction it must move in.)
   458	    verdict = "MIXED"
   459	    why = ("one direction shows the effect and the other INVERTS it -- a host x role "
   460	           "interaction this rig cannot decompose. Inconclusive for the question. (Judged "
   461	           "on the UNHARDENED cell states: the control bias B may only ever HARDEN a "
   462	           "verdict, and it must not be able to dissolve a disagreement into a claim.)")
   463	elif "EFFECT" in m.values():
   464	    verdict = "REPRODUCES"
   465	    why = ("P1 reproduces WITHOUT a Windows peer, in: %s. Scoped to THIS pair: it shows "
   466	           "P1 CAN occur macOS<->macOS, so it is not waivable as 'Windows residue'. It "
   467	           "does NOT establish a platform-general cost, does NOT name the mechanism, "
   468	           "does NOT kill H1 (the code H1 accuses runs here too), and leaves macOS/APFS "
   469	           "and host x role explanations OPEN."
   470	           % ", ".join(c for c, s in m.items() if s == "EFFECT"))
   471	elif "INVERTED" in m.values():
   472	    verdict = "INVERTED"
   473	    why = ("source-initiated is the SLOW arm in: %s. A NEW finding; never bank it as "
   474	           "'P1 absent'." % ", ".join(c for c, s in m.items() if s == "INVERTED"))
   475	elif all(s == "NONE" for s in m.values()):
   476	    verdict = "DOES-NOT-REPRODUCE"
   477	    why = ("both TCP-mixed cells EXCLUDE an effect of size T, and every control is clean "
   478	           "at T/2 -- a genuine equivalence result. Scoped to THIS pair: P1 did not "
   479	           "reproduce macOS<->macOS. That is CONSISTENT with 'the Windows peer is "
   480	           "required' but does NOT prove it -- it could equally be a property of these "
   481	           "two machines, their disks, or this macOS version.")
   482	else:
   483	    verdict = "UNCLEAR"
   484	    why = ("the CI spans the threshold in: %s. The rig could not resolve an effect of "
   485	           "size T either way -- this is NOT 'P1 vanishes'. Fix the rig, do not add pairs."
   486	           % ", ".join(c for c, s in m.items() if s == "UNCLEAR"))
   487
   488	out = ["SESSION VERDICT: %s" % verdict, "", why, "",
   489	       "Per cell. T = min(srcinit_median/10, %d ms). Controls must be NONE at T/2, and B is"
   490	       % DELTA_REF,
    90	    env.update(env_extra or {})
    91	    out = subprocess.run([sys.executable, engine(), runs, meta,
    92	                          os.path.join(tmp, "s.csv"), os.path.join(tmp, "p.csv"),
    93	                          os.path.join(tmp, "v.csv"), os.path.join(tmp, "sv.txt")],
    94	                         env=env, capture_output=True, text=True)
    95	    # A DELIBERATE refusal is the engine WORKING, not failing: exit 2 (a corrupt or
    96	    # impossible row) or an explicit REFUSING (a pinned constant or cell role tampered with).
    97	    if out.returncode == 2 or (out.returncode != 0 and "REFUSING" in (out.stderr or "")):
    98	        return "ENGINE-REFUSED"
    99	    if out.returncode != 0:
   100	        return "ENGINE-CRASH: " + (out.stderr.strip().splitlines() or ["?"])[-1]
   101	    return out.stdout.splitlines()[0].split(":", 1)[1].strip()
   102
   103
   104	# (name, kwargs, must_be, must_not_be)
   105	CASES = [
   106	    # --- a real effect must never read as nothing --------------------------------
   107	    ("codex r1: a 190ms effect on 7/8 pairs is not a null",
   108	     dict(measurand_d=[0, 180, 180, 190, 190, 200, 200, 200], src=2000),
   109	     "UNCLEAR", "DOES-NOT-REPRODUCE"),
   110
   111	    ("codex r2: a rig-W-sized effect (230ms) in EVERY pair, on a slow 2500ms arm",
   112	     dict(measurand_d=[230] * 8, src=2500, control_d=[0] * 8),
   113	     "REPRODUCES", "DOES-NOT-REPRODUCE"),
   114
   115	    ("codex r2: an effect the 10% bar alone would forgive (240ms @ 2500)",
   116	     dict(measurand_d=[-100, -50, 0, 50, 100, 200, 220, 240], src=2500, control_d=[0] * 8),
   117	     "UNCLEAR", "DOES-NOT-REPRODUCE"),
   118
   119	    ("codex r2: the inverting threshold is -src/11, not -src/10 (CI [-190,0] @ 2000)",
   120	     dict(measurand_d=[-190, -190, 0, 0, 0, 0, 0, 0], src=2000, control_d=[0] * 8),
   121	     "UNCLEAR", "DOES-NOT-REPRODUCE"),
   122
   123	    # --- an artifact must never read as an effect --------------------------------
   124	    ("codex r2: 7 positive + 1 negative is not a reproduction",
   125	     dict(measurand_d=[-20, 300, 310, 320, 330, 340, 350, 360], src=1000),
   126	     "UNCLEAR", "REPRODUCES"),
   127
   128	    ("codex r5: a 1ms paired effect is not a reproduction, whatever the medians do",
   129	     dict(measurand_d=[1] * 13 + [-4500] * 3,
   130	          src=[1000] * 7 + [1200] * 6 + [5000] * 3,
   131	          control_d=[5] * 16, control_src=1000, pairs=16),
   132	     None, "REPRODUCES"),
   133
   134	    ("codex r6: nor when the marginal bar fails in the MATCHING direction",
   135	     dict(measurand_d=[400] * 3 + [1] * 13, src=[1000] * 8 + [1200] * 8,
   136	          control_d=[5] * 16, control_src=1000, pairs=16),
   137	     None, "REPRODUCES"),
   138
   139	    ("one huge outlier must not manufacture a reproduction (the CI's LOWER bound decides)",
   140	     dict(measurand_d=[10, 10, 10, 10, 10, 10, 10, 800], src=1000),
   280	     "REPRODUCES", "UNCLEAR"),
   281
   282	    # codex r11, MEDIUM: the ARM median controls T, B and the bar -- and the LOW median (which
   283	    # is registered only for the paired D) is anti-conservative on a BIMODAL arm. Here the arm
   284	    # is 4x1000 + 4x5000: the low median calls it 1000ms, so +100 is "a 10% effect" -> EFFECT.
   285	    # The conventional median calls it 3000ms, where the same +100 is 3.3% -- below both bars.
   286	    # Rig W's fast arm is ALREADY bimodal (~730/~840), so this is not a synthetic worry.
   287	    ("codex r11: a BIMODAL arm must not shrink T (the arm median is conventional, not low)",
   288	     dict(measurand_d=[100] * 8, src=[1000] * 4 + [5000] * 4, control_d=[0] * 8),
   289	     "DOES-NOT-REPRODUCE", "REPRODUCES"),
   290
   291	    ("codex r8: a bimodal arm cannot hide from the RANGE (a null is judged on every pair)",
   292	     dict(measurand_d=[-110, 0, -110, 110, 110, 0, -110, 0], src=730,
   293	          control_d=[0] * 8),
   294	     "UNCLEAR", "DOES-NOT-REPRODUCE"),
   295
   296	    ("a null the rig could not have SEEN is UNCLEAR, not a null",
   297	     dict(measurand_d=[-400, -300, -100, 0, 0, 100, 300, 400], src=2000),
   298	     "UNCLEAR", "DOES-NOT-REPRODUCE"),
   299
   300	    # --- integrity ---------------------------------------------------------------
   301	    ("a missing registered cell is INCOMPLETE, never filtered away",
   302	     dict(measurand_d=[-4, -2, -1, 0, 0, 1, 2, 3], src=2000,
   303	          drop_cells=("qn_tcp_mixed",)),
   304	     "INCOMPLETE", "DOES-NOT-REPRODUCE"),
   305
   306	    ("grok r3: n=1 with complete=yes must not grade at 0% CI coverage",
   307	     dict(measurand_d=[0], src=2000, control_d=[5], control_src=1000),
   308	     "INCOMPLETE", "DOES-NOT-REPRODUCE"),
   309
   310	    ("grok r3: a harness-detected session void (end-load) refuses a verdict",
   311	     dict(measurand_d=[-4, -2, -1, 0, 0, 1, 2, 3], src=2000,
   312	          void_reason="end-load on q is 9.1 (> 3.0)"),
   313	     "RIG-VOID", "DOES-NOT-REPRODUCE"),
   314
   315	    ("codex r10: a session of ZERO timings must not report an EFFECT",
   316	     dict(measurand_d=[0] * 8, src=0, control_d=[0] * 8, control_src=0),
   317	     "ENGINE-REFUSED", "REPRODUCES"),
   318
   319	    ("codex r10: the CELL ROLES are pinned -- a dirty control cannot be dropped from the set",
   320	     dict(measurand_d=[-4, -2, -1, 0, 0, 1, 2, 3], src=2000,
   321	          control_d=[230] * 8, control_src=2500,
   322	          env_extra={"CONTROL_CELLS": "nq_grpc_mixed"}),
   323	     "ENGINE-REFUSED", "DOES-NOT-REPRODUCE"),
   324
   325	    ("codex r5: DELTA_REF_MS is PINNED -- the rule is not tunable from the environment",
   326	     dict(measurand_d=[-4, -2, -1, 0, 0, 1, 2, 3], src=2000,
   327	          env_extra={"DELTA_REF_MS": "240"}),
   328	     "ENGINE-REFUSED", "DOES-NOT-REPRODUCE"),
   329	]
   330
   331	MUTATIONS = [
   332	    ("the control threshold is the SAME as the measurand's, not half (grok r6)",
   333	     ['    c_pos, c_neg = thresholds(x["src"], 0.5)',
   334	      '    c_pos, c_neg = thresholds(x["src"], 1.0)'],
   335	     "clean at T but DIRTY at T/2"),
   440	    src = open(DEFAULT_VERDICT).read()
   441	    start = src.index("def classify(")
   442	    end = src.index("\n\n", src.index("return \"UNCLEAR\"", start))
   443	    ns = {}
   444	    exec(src[start:end], ns)
   445	    classify = ns["classify"]
   446	    bad = 0
   447	    checks = [
   448	        # ci narrow (outliers trimmed), range wide: a bimodal arm. MUST NOT be NONE.
   449	        ("bimodal: CI=[1,1] but range=[-110,110], T=73", (1, 1, -110, 110, 73, -66), "UNCLEAR"),
   450	        ("clean: CI and range both inside T",            (2, 3, -4, 3, 73, -66),      "NONE"),
   451	        ("a real effect clears T",                       (80, 90, 75, 95, 73, -66),   "EFFECT"),
   452	        ("an inverted effect clears -T",                 (-90, -80, -95, -75, 73, -66), "INVERTED"),
   453	    ]
   454	    for name, args, want in checks:
   455	        got = classify(*args)
   456	        ok = got == want
   457	        print("  %-46s -> %-8s %s" % (name, got, "ok" if ok else "*** FAIL (want %s) ***" % want))
   458	        if not ok:
   459	            bad += 1
   460
   461	    # THE IDENTITY THE WHOLE RULE LEANS ON: at the registered n=8, the >=95% order-statistic
   462	    # interval IS [min, max]. That is why nothing can be trimmed -- not a null, not B. If
   463	    # this ever stops holding, the CI/RANGE distinctions above become live and the engine
   464	    # must refuse that n (it does).
   465	    ns2 = {}
   466	    src2 = open(DEFAULT_VERDICT).read()
   467	    st = src2.index("def median_ci(")
   468	    exec(src2[st:src2.index("\n\n", src2.index("return best", st))],
   469	         {"comb": __import__("math").comb, "MIN_COVERAGE": 0.95}, ns2)
   470	    import random as _r
   471	    rr = _r.Random(9)
   472	    for _ in range(200):
   473	        d = [rr.randint(-500, 500) for _ in range(8)]
   474	        lo, hi, cov = ns2["median_ci"](d)
   475	        if (lo, hi) != (min(d), max(d)) or cov < 0.95:
   476	            print("  *** FAIL: at n=8 the CI is NOT the full range: %s vs %s" % ((lo, hi), (min(d), max(d))))
   477	            bad += 1
   478	            break
   479	    else:
   480	        print("  %-46s -> %-8s ok" % ("n=8: the >=95% CI IS [min,max] (200 draws)", "identity"))
   481	    return bad
   482
   483
   484	def run_cases():
   485	    bad = []
   486	    for name, kw, must_be, must_not in CASES:
   487	        got = session(**kw)
   488	        ok = not (must_be and got != must_be) and not (must_not and got == must_not)
   489	        print("%-66s -> %-20s %s" % (name[:66], got, "ok" if ok else "*** FAIL ***"))
   490	        if not ok:
   491	            bad.append(name)
   492	            print("      expected %s / must not be %s" % (must_be, must_not))
   493	    return bad
   494
   495
   496	def fuzz(n=300):
   497	    """No input may land outside the registered outcomes. The CONTROLS are fuzzed too --
   498	    pinning them clean once left every dirty-control path unexercised, and that is
   499	    exactly where a BLOCKER was hiding."""
   500	    rng = random.Random(4242)
   501	    bad = 0
   502	    for _ in range(n):
   503	        got = session(measurand_d=[rng.randint(-600, 600) for _ in range(8)],
   504	                      src=rng.choice([600, 1000, 2000, 2500, 5000]),
   505	                      control_d=[rng.randint(-300, 300) for _ in range(8)],
   506	                      control_src=rng.choice([600, 1000, 2500, 5000]))
   507	        if got not in OUTCOMES:
   508	            print("*** UNREGISTERED OUTCOME %r" % got)
   509	            bad += 1
   510	    print("fuzz: %d/%d inputs produced a registered outcome (measurand AND controls)"
   511	          % (n - bad, n))
   512	    return bad
   513
   514
   515	def mutate():
   516	    src = open(DEFAULT_VERDICT).read()
   517	    bad = 0
   518	    for name, subs, key in MUTATIONS:
   519	        body = src
   520	        for i in range(0, len(subs), 2):
   521	            old, new = subs[i], subs[i + 1]
   522	            if old not in body:     # the engine drifted: the proof is STALE, not passing
   523	                print("*** STALE MUTATION (target not found): %s" % name)
   524	                bad += 1
   525	                body = None
   180	# one-directional P1 out of nothing. The pre-registration has claimed an equal settle
   181	# on both arms through revisions 3, 4 and 5. It was never applied.
   182	#
   183	# Found only by EXECUTING it (round-5 codex flagged the ignored exit status; running
   184	# it showed the status was ALWAYS failure). `bash -n` sees nothing here.
   185	SETTLE_SEC="$(awk -v m="$SETTLE_MS" 'BEGIN{printf "%.3f", m/1000}')"
   186	[[ "$SETTLE_SEC" =~ ^[0-9]+\.[0-9]+$ ]] || { echo "FATAL: settle seconds did not compute ('$SETTLE_SEC')" >&2; exit 1; }
   187	LOAD_MAX=3.0               # start AND end load1 bar on both Macs
   188	DRAIN_ITERS=60
   189	DRAIN_QUIET=3
   190	DRAIN_MBPS=2               # destination disk must be below this to start a window
   191	DELTA_REF_MS=230           # rig W's measured Delta_P1 — THE reference effect
   192	TIMER_TOLERANCE_MS=120     # the timer self-test's allowed error on a 1000 ms sleep
   193
   194	# THE REGISTERED TOPOLOGY. These are assigned HERE, BELOW the override check, for the same
   195	# reason SETTLE_MS is: assigned above it, the pinning line itself would set the variable and
   196	# the check would then see it as "present in the environment" and refuse EVERY run. (It did.
   197	# Caught by RUNNING it -- the first draft of this fix put them at the top of the file and the
   198	# harness refused to start. A protection that cannot PASS is as dead as one that cannot FAIL.)
   199	N_IP=10.1.10.92                       # nagatha, 10GbE en11
   200	N_NIC=en11
   201	N_MAC=00:e0:4d:01:4c:a3               # nagatha's OWN en11 MAC (measured)
   202	Q_SSH=michael@q
   203	Q_IP=10.1.10.54                       # q, 10GbE en8
   204	Q_NIC=en8
   205	Q_MAC=00:01:d2:19:04:a3               # q's OWN en8 MAC (measured)
   206
   207	# RUNS and PORT are registered too (round-11 grok, LOW): RUNS was `${RUNS:-8}` and absent from
   208	# the refusal list above, so it was pinned only by a preflight check -- a weaker guarantee than
   209	# every other registered constant, for the one number the whole rule depends on (n is EXACTLY
   210	# 8; at any larger n the >=95% interval starts TRIMMING and a bimodal arm yields a false null).
   211	# The preflight check STAYS: it also guards a source edit, which this line cannot.
   212	RUNS=8
   213	PORT=9031
   214
   215	# THE FABRIC (round-11 codex, BLOCKER). Measured on both hosts, 2026-07-14, before any data:
   216	#   nagatha en11: mtu 9000, media 10Gbase-T <full-duplex,flow-control>, status active
   217	#   q       en8 : mtu 9000, media 10Gbase-T <full-duplex>,              status active
   218	#   path    MSS : 8948 in BOTH directions (9000 - 20 IP - 20 TCP - 12 timestamps)
   219	REGISTERED_MTU=9000
   220	REGISTERED_MEDIA="10Gbase-T"   # the NEGOTIATED media. A 1GbE NIC reports 1000baseT.
   221	# The MSS is the ONLY one of these that proves the PATH rather than the local NIC: both ends
   222	# can sit at MTU 9000 while a 1500 hop between them clamps the segments. It is the gate pf-0
   223	# used (start AND end of every session) and it is the number that killed MTU as a cause of P1.
   224	REGISTERED_MSS=8948
   225
   430	}
   431
   432	load1() { hrun "$1" "sysctl -n vm.loadavg" | nocr | awk '{print $2}'; }
   433	load_gate() {
   434	  local h="$1" l ok
   435	  l="$(load1 "$h")" || l=""
   436	  [[ "$l" =~ ^[0-9]+\.?[0-9]*$ ]] || die_blind "$(hname "$h"): cannot read load1 (got '$l') — refusing"
   437	  ok="$(awk -v l="$l" -v m="$LOAD_MAX" 'BEGIN{print (l+0 <= m+0) ? 1 : 0}')"
   438	  [[ "$ok" == 1 ]] || die "$(hname "$h"): load1 is $l (> $LOAD_MAX) — a bench END must be quiet. Find what is running first."
   439	}
   440
   441	link_gate() {   # both directions; the peer's ARP must be the PEER's MAC, never our own
   442	  local h="$1" o peer_ip want got raw route_nic nic
   443	  o="$(other "$h")"; peer_ip="$(hip "$o")"; want="$(hmac "$o" | norm_mac)"; nic="$(hnic "$h")"
   444	  [[ -n "$want" ]] || die_blind "$(hname "$o"): its configured MAC does not parse — refusing"
   445	  hrun "$h" "ping -c1 -W1 '$peer_ip' >/dev/null 2>&1" \
   446	    || die "$(hname "$h") cannot ping $peer_ip — the link is down"
   447	  # The ARP entry ON THE NIC THE TRAFFIC WILL EGRESS. `arp -n <ip>` prints one line
   448	  # PER INTERFACE that has an entry — q holds entries for nagatha on en0, en1 AND
   449	  # en8 — so an unfiltered $4 yields a MULTI-LINE string that can never equal a
   450	  # single MAC. (Measured: this refused a perfectly good link. It is also the more
   451	  # correct check: a stale entry on the 1GbE NIC is irrelevant to the 10GbE path.)
   452	  # A PROBE THAT COULD NOT RUN IS NOT AN ABSENT ARP ENTRY (round-11 codex, MEDIUM). Both of
   453	  # these read a command substitution whose status was discarded, so a broken ssh, a missing
   454	  # `arp`, or a failed `route` produced an EMPTY value and died with the message for a
   455	  # genuinely bad link — which the self-test then scores [FIRED] ("the gate works!") instead
   456	  # of [BROKEN] ("the gate is blind"). The refusal was right by luck; the classification was
   457	  # wrong, and a blind gate that looks like a working one is exactly what fails open at 3am.
   458	  # The sentinel distinguishes them: no `A:...:A` back at all means the probe never ran.
   459	  # The sentinel is `=`-delimited, NOT `:`-delimited: a MAC address is ALL colons, so a
   460	  # `A:<value>:A` frame can never be parsed back out and the gate reads BLIND on a perfectly
   461	  # good link. (Caught by running the self-test: [BROKEN] on both hosts. It failed CLOSED, so
   462	  # no wrong data — but a blind gate is the very thing this frame was added to prevent.)
   463	  raw="$(hrun "$h" "v=\$(arp -n '$peer_ip' 2>/dev/null | awk -v nic='$nic' '\$5 == \"on\" && \$6 == nic {print \$4}' | head -1)
   464	echo \"A=\${v:-NONE}=A\"" | nocr | sed -n 's/.*A=\([^=]*\)=A.*/\1/p' | head -1)" || raw=""
   465	  [[ -n "$raw" ]] \
   466	    || die_blind "$(hname "$h"): the ARP probe for $peer_ip could not run at all — refusing (a probe that cannot answer must not answer 'fine')"
   467	  [[ "$raw" != NONE ]] \
   468	    || die "$(hname "$h"): no ARP entry for $peer_ip ON $nic — the 10GbE path has not resolved the peer"
   469	  got="$(printf '%s\n' "$raw" | norm_mac)"
   470	  [[ -n "$got" ]] \
   471	    || die_blind "$(hname "$h"): the ARP entry for $peer_ip did not parse as a MAC (got '$raw') — refusing"
   472	  [[ "$got" == "$want" ]] \
   473	    || die "$(hname "$h"): ARP for $peer_ip is $got but the peer's real MAC is $want. If it equals OUR OWN NIC's MAC this is the documented BLACK HOLE (a host route on a directly-connected subnet) — 100% packet loss while \`route -n get\` still reports the right interface."
   474	  route_nic="$(hrun "$h" "v=\$(route -n get '$peer_ip' 2>/dev/null | awk '/interface:/{print \$2}')
   475	echo \"R:\${v:-NONE}:R\"" | nocr | sed -n 's/.*R:\([^:]*\):R.*/\1/p' | head -1)" || route_nic=""
   476	  [[ -n "$route_nic" ]] \
   477	    || die_blind "$(hname "$h"): the route probe for $peer_ip could not run at all — refusing"
   478	  [[ "$route_nic" == "$(hnic "$h")" ]] \
   479	    || die "$(hname "$h"): route to $peer_ip egresses '$route_nic', not the 10GbE NIC '$(hnic "$h")' — the multi-NIC trap (macOS routes by network SERVICE order, so a 1GbE NIC can win and every run would go over gigabit)."
   480	}
   481
   482	# THE REGISTERED FABRIC, PROVED ON THE WIRE (round-11 codex, BLOCKER: it never was).
   483	#
   484	# link_gate above proves the peer resolves on the right NIC and the route egresses it. That
   485	# says NOTHING about the NIC's SPEED or the path's MTU: a 1GbE interface, or a 9000-MTU NIC
   486	# behind a 1500 hop, passes every one of those checks. pf-0 spent 256 runs proving MTU moves
   487	# wall time on this fabric, so an unverified MTU is not a footnote -- it is a confound sitting
   488	# directly on the measurand.
   489	#
   490	# TWO gates, because they prove different things:
   491	#   topology_gate  the LOCAL NIC: its MTU, its NEGOTIATED media (a 1GbE link says 1000baseT),
   492	#                  and that the link is actually up.
   493	#   mss_gate       the PATH, end to end: a real TCP connection to the peer's registered IP,
   494	#                  and the MSS the two kernels agreed on. Both ends can sit at MTU 9000 while
   495	#                  a 1500 hop between them clamps segments to 1448 -- only the MSS sees that.
   496	#                  It also reports the LOCAL ADDRESS the kernel chose, which proves the
   497	#                  traffic EGRESSED the registered 10GbE IP and not one of the eight other
   498	#                  interfaces this Mac has.
   499	topology_gate() {
   500	  local h="$1" nic raw mtu media st
   501	  nic="$(hnic "$h")"
   502	  # The rc is carried in a sentinel: `$(ifconfig ...)` in a pipeline discards it, and an
   503	  # ifconfig that ERRORED must never read as a NIC that merely lacks the fields.
   504	  raw="$(hrun "$h" "ifconfig '$nic' 2>/dev/null; echo \"RC:\$?:RC\"" | nocr)" || raw=""
   505	  [[ "$raw" == *"RC:0:RC"* ]] \
   506	    || die_blind "$(hname "$h"): cannot read ifconfig $nic — refusing (a gate that cannot answer must not pass)"
   507	  mtu="$(printf '%s\n' "$raw"   | sed -n 's/.*[[:space:]]mtu[[:space:]]\([0-9][0-9]*\).*/\1/p' | head -1)"
   508	  media="$(printf '%s\n' "$raw" | sed -n 's/^[[:space:]]*media:[[:space:]]*\(.*\)$/\1/p'       | head -1)"
   509	  st="$(printf '%s\n' "$raw"    | sed -n 's/^[[:space:]]*status:[[:space:]]*\(.*\)$/\1/p'      | head -1)"
   510	  [[ "$mtu" =~ ^[0-9]+$ ]] \
   511	    || die_blind "$(hname "$h"): cannot parse the MTU of $nic (got '$mtu') — refusing"
   512	  [[ "$mtu" == "$REGISTERED_MTU" ]] \
   513	    || die "$(hname "$h"): $nic is at MTU $mtu, but the REGISTERED fabric is MTU $REGISTERED_MTU. pf-0 spent 256 runs establishing that MTU moves wall time on this rig — a run at the wrong MTU is not the registered experiment."
   514	  [[ -n "$media" ]] \
   515	    || die_blind "$(hname "$h"): $nic reports no negotiated media — refusing (an unknown link speed is not a 10GbE link)"
   516	  [[ "$media" == *"$REGISTERED_MEDIA"* ]] \
   517	    || die "$(hname "$h"): $nic negotiated '$media', not $REGISTERED_MEDIA. THE RUN WOULD BE OVER GIGABIT — this Mac has other interfaces and macOS routes by network SERVICE order."
   518	  [[ "$st" == active ]] \
   519	    || die "$(hname "$h"): $nic status is '$st', not active"
   520	  log "  fabric on $(hname "$h"): $nic mtu $mtu, media $media, status $st"
   521	}
   522
   523	# ONE reader, TWO policies. THE recurring defect in this harness is the SAME PROBE WRITTEN
   524	# TWICE and fixed once (a fail-open pgrep lived on in its duplicate; the drain was fixed by
   525	# value and left failing by status). The path is checked at preflight (REFUSE, no data yet)
   526	# AND at the end of the session (VOID, the data exists) — pf-0's start-and-end rule — so the
   527	# probe itself exists exactly once and the two callers differ only in what they do about it.
   528	mss_read() {   # $1 = host -> "<mss> <local_ip>", or "" if the probe could not answer
   529	  local h="$1" peer
   530	  peer="$(hip "$(other "$h")")"
   531	  # ONE process: connect to the peer's REGISTERED ip and ask the kernel what MSS it agreed.
   532	  # Port 22 is sshd, up on both ends by definition (the harness itself got here over it).
   533	  hrun "$h" "$(hpy "$h") - '$peer' <<'PYEOF'
   534	import socket, sys
   535	s = socket.create_connection((sys.argv[1], 22), timeout=5)
   536	print('M:%d:%s:M' % (s.getsockopt(socket.IPPROTO_TCP, socket.TCP_MAXSEG), s.getsockname()[0]))
   537	s.close()
   538	PYEOF" | nocr | sed -n 's/.*M:\([0-9][0-9]*\):\([0-9.][0-9.]*\):M.*/\1 \2/p' | head -1
   539	}
   540
   541	mss_gate() {   # PREFLIGHT policy: no data has been taken, so REFUSE.
   542	  local h="$1" out mss local_ip peer
   543	  peer="$(hip "$(other "$h")")"
   544	  out="$(mss_read "$h")" || out=""
   545	  [[ -n "$out" ]] \
   546	    || die_blind "$(hname "$h"): cannot measure the negotiated MSS to $peer — refusing (an unproven path is not a proven one)"
   547	  read -r mss local_ip <<<"$out"
   548	  [[ "$mss" =~ ^[0-9]+$ ]] \
   549	    || die_blind "$(hname "$h"): the MSS probe returned '$mss' — refusing"
   550	  [[ "$mss" == "$REGISTERED_MSS" ]] \
   551	    || die "$(hname "$h"): the PATH to $peer negotiated MSS $mss, not the registered $REGISTERED_MSS. Both NICs can sit at MTU $REGISTERED_MTU while a 1500 hop between them clamps the segments (MSS 1448) — and THAT is the run pf-0 already measured as a DIFFERENT CONDITION."
   552	  [[ "$local_ip" == "$(hip "$h")" ]] \
   553	    || die "$(hname "$h"): the connection to $peer EGRESSED $local_ip, not the registered $(hip "$h") — the traffic is leaving on a DIFFERENT INTERFACE (this Mac has eight others, all at MTU 1500)."
   554	  log "  path from $(hname "$h") -> $(hname "$(other "$h")") ($peer): MSS $mss via $local_ip — the fabric is jumbo end-to-end"
   555	}
   700	      hrun "$h" "test -x '$p'" || die "$(hname "$h"): missing/not executable: $p"
   701	      embeds_clean "$h" "$p" || die "$(hname "$h"): $p is not a CLEAN +$EXPECT_SHA (same-build rule D-2026-07-05-2; a read error also fails here, by design)"
   702	    done
   703	    hrun "$h" "sudo -n /usr/sbin/purge" || die "$(hname "$h") cannot purge without a password — every run would read WARM"
   704	    # THE SAME pgrep FAIL-OPEN AS THE QUIESCENCE GATE, IN A DUPLICATE SITE I DID NOT
   705	    # TOUCH (round-5 codex, HIGH). `if hrun ... pgrep; then die; fi` reads rc>=2 (a
   706	    # BROKEN probe, or a failed ssh) as "no daemon is running" and sails on. Every
   707	    # process probe now goes through this one rc-aware helper -- there is no second
   708	    # site left to forget.
   709	    case "$(pgrep_state "$h" blit-daemon)" in
   710	      RUNNING) die "$(hname "$h"): a blit-daemon is already running — stop it first" ;;
   711	      NONE)    : ;;
   712	      *)       die "$(hname "$h"): cannot probe for a stale blit-daemon — refusing (a gate that cannot answer must not answer 'fine')" ;;
   713	    esac
   714	    for w in large mixed small; do
   715	      want="$(fix_count "$w")"; wantb="$(fix_bytes "$w")"
   716	      got="$(hrun "$h" "find '$(hmod "$h")/src_$w' -type f 2>/dev/null | wc -l" | tr -cd '0-9')"
   717	      gotb="$(hrun "$h" "find '$(hmod "$h")/src_$w' -type f -exec stat -f %z {} + 2>/dev/null | awk '{s+=\$1} END{printf \"%d\", s+0}'" | tr -cd '0-9')"
   718	      [[ "${got:-0}" == "$want" && "${gotb:-0}" == "$wantb" ]] \
   719	        || die "$(hname "$h"): src_$w is ${got:-0} files/${gotb:-0} bytes, want $want/$wantb — the arms must read identical trees"
   720	    done
   721	    link_gate "$h"
   722	    topology_gate "$h"          # the NIC: MTU + negotiated media (never checked before r11)
   723	    mss_gate "$h"               # the PATH: the MSS the two kernels actually agreed
   724	    resolve_disk "$h" || die "$(hname "$h"): cannot establish the drain device — refusing"
   725	  done
   726	  measure_ssh_rtt
   727	  log "preflight OK  build=$EXPECT_SHA  harness=$HARNESS_HEAD  runs/arm=$RUNS  settle=${SETTLE_MS}ms"
   728	  log "  load1: nagatha=$(load1 n)  q=$(load1 q)"
   729	}
   730
   731	write_manifest() {
   732	  local f="$OUT_DIR/staging-manifest.txt" h nb nd qb qd vh th
   733	  # Hashes computed FIRST, in the caller's shell: `die` inside $(...) exits only the
   734	  # subshell, so the old code wrote an EMPTY hash and called it provenance.
   735	  nb="$(sha256_of n "$N_BLIT")"   || die "nagatha: cannot hash $N_BLIT"
   736	  nd="$(sha256_of n "$N_DAEMON")" || die "nagatha: cannot hash $N_DAEMON"
   737	  qb="$(sha256_of q "$Q_BLIT")"   || die "q: cannot hash $Q_BLIT"
   738	  qd="$(sha256_of q "$Q_DAEMON")" || die "q: cannot hash $Q_DAEMON"
   739	  vh="$(shasum -a 256 "$VERDICT_PY" | cut -d' ' -f1)"
   740	  th="$(shasum -a 256 "$VERDICT_TEST" | cut -d' ' -f1)"
  1280	    else log "  [BROKEN] drain device  — could not resolve the physical disk"; SELFTEST_BROKEN=$((SELFTEST_BROKEN+1)); fi
  1281	    # The paths the old self-test claimed and did not run (round-5 codex, HIGH):
  1282	    gate_probe "purge         (sudo -n, or every run reads WARM)" hrun "$h" "sudo -n /usr/sbin/purge"
  1283	    case "$(pgrep_state "$h" blit-daemon)" in
  1284	      NONE)    log "  [OK]     stale daemon  (rc-aware probe: none running)" ;;
  1285	      RUNNING) log "  [FIRED]  stale daemon  (one IS running — the gate would refuse)"; SELFTEST_FIRED=$((SELFTEST_FIRED+1)) ;;
  1286	      *)       log "  [BROKEN] stale daemon  — the probe could not answer"; SELFTEST_BROKEN=$((SELFTEST_BROKEN+1)) ;;
  1287	    esac
  1288	    # DRAIN-TIMEOUT is a genuinely busy disk (the gate WORKING); DRAIN-ERROR is a blind
  1289	    # probe. Scoring them the same made the classification untrustworthy (grok r6, F7).
  1290	    local dr; dr="$(drain_host "$h")"
  1291	    case "$dr" in
  1292	      drained*)      log "  [OK]     drain loop    ($dr)" ;;
  1293	      DRAIN-TIMEOUT) log "  [FIRED]  drain loop    — the disk is genuinely busy; the gate would void the pair"; SELFTEST_FIRED=$((SELFTEST_FIRED+1)) ;;
  1294	      *)             log "  [BROKEN] drain loop    — the probe could not answer ('$dr')"; SELFTEST_BROKEN=$((SELFTEST_BROKEN+1)) ;;
  1295	    esac
  1296	    selftest_fsync "$h"
  1297	    log "  [--]     mac parse (no gawk strtonum): $(hmac "$h") -> $(hmac "$h" | norm_mac)"
  1298	  done
  1299	  SESSION_VOID_REASON=""; end_load_gate
  1300	  if [[ -z "$SESSION_VOID_REASON" ]]; then
  1301	    log "  [OK]     end-load gate (both Macs under $LOAD_MAX; it CAN void a session)"
  1302	  elif [[ "$SESSION_VOID_REASON" == *"could not be read"* ]]; then
  1303	    # An UNREADABLE end-load is a blind probe, not a busy machine (grok r6, F7).
  1304	    log "  [BROKEN] end-load gate — $SESSION_VOID_REASON"; SELFTEST_BROKEN=$((SELFTEST_BROKEN+1))
  1305	  else
  1306	    log "  [FIRED]  end-load gate — $SESSION_VOID_REASON"; SELFTEST_FIRED=$((SELFTEST_FIRED+1))
  1307	  fi
  1308	  # The END-of-session fabric re-check, exercised for real. Same three-way scoring: a probe
  1309	  # that cannot answer is BLIND, a fabric that genuinely changed is the gate WORKING.
  1310	  SESSION_VOID_REASON=""; end_mss_gate
  1311	  if [[ -z "$SESSION_VOID_REASON" ]]; then
  1312	    log "  [OK]     end-fabric gate (MSS $REGISTERED_MSS on both ends; it CAN void a session)"
  1313	  elif [[ "$SESSION_VOID_REASON" == *"could not be read"* ]]; then
  1314	    log "  [BROKEN] end-fabric gate — $SESSION_VOID_REASON"; SELFTEST_BROKEN=$((SELFTEST_BROKEN+1))
  1315	  else
  1316	    log "  [FIRED]  end-fabric gate — $SESSION_VOID_REASON"; SELFTEST_FIRED=$((SELFTEST_FIRED+1))
  1317	  fi
  1318	  SESSION_VOID_REASON=""
  1319	  measure_ssh_rtt
  1320	  log ""
  1321	  log "SELFTEST: $SELFTEST_FIRED gate(s) refused a genuinely unmet condition; $SELFTEST_BROKEN blind."
  1322	  log "NOT exercised here (they need a real transfer): daemon start/lsof/teardown, the"
  1323	  log "smoke transfer, the ABBA pair loop, pair-voiding, and the manifest. PREFLIGHT_ONLY=1"
  1324	  log "covers the manifest and the build-provenance gates. This self-test does NOT claim"
  1325	  log "to run every gate — the previous one did, and it was not true."
  1326	  log "THIS IS NOT CLEARANCE TO TAKE DATA. The review is."
  1327	  if (( SELFTEST_BROKEN > 0 )); then
  1328	    log "SELFTEST FAILED: $SELFTEST_BROKEN gate(s) are BLIND."
  1329	    exit 1
  1330	  fi
  1331	  log "SELFTEST PASSED: every gate exercised here can answer."
  1332	}
  1333
  1334	main() {
  1335	  if [[ "$SELFTEST" == 1 ]]; then
  1336	    EXPECT_SHA="${EXPECT_SHA:-$REGISTERED_BUILD}"
  1337	    HARNESS_HEAD="$(git -C "$REPO_ROOT" rev-parse --short HEAD)"
  1338	    HARNESS_SHA256="$(shasum -a 256 "$SELF" | cut -d' ' -f1)"
  1339	    selftest
  1340	    exit 0
  1341	  fi
  1342	  preflight
  1343	  write_manifest
  1344	  if [[ "$PREFLIGHT_ONLY" == 1 ]]; then
  1345	    log "PREFLIGHT_ONLY: checks passed; no daemon started, nothing timed"
  1346	    exit 0
  1347	  fi
  1348	  log "session $SESSION_TAG  build=$EXPECT_SHA  nagatha=$N_IP  q=$Q_IP"
  1349	  echo "cell,arm,build,initiator,run,ms,flush_ms,settled_ms,rtt_ms,files,bytes,exit,drain,cold,valid" > "$CSV"
  1350	  echo "cell,pairs_attempted,complete" > "$META"
  1351	  daemon_start n; daemon_start q
  1352	  smoke n; smoke q
  1353
  1354	  run_all_cells
  1355
  1356	  # End-load AND end-fabric, BEFORE the verdict is computed. Both can VOID the session.
  1357	  # The fabric is re-proved at the END for the same reason pf-0 did it: a link that flapped
  1358	  # or re-routed mid-session (onto the 1GbE NIC, or a 1500 hop) would have carried some of
  1359	  # the timed windows, and a start-only check cannot see that. A gate measured once at the
  1360	  # start is not a gate on a run taken an hour later.
  1361	  log "  load1 (end): nagatha=$(load1 n)  q=$(load1 q)"
  1362	  end_load_gate
  1363	  [[ -n "$SESSION_VOID_REASON" ]] || end_mss_gate
  1364	  if [[ -n "$SESSION_VOID_REASON" ]]; then
  1365	    log "ERROR: SESSION VOID — $SESSION_VOID_REASON"
  1366	    touch "$OUT_DIR/SESSION-VOID"
  1367	  fi
  1368	  compute_verdicts
  1369	  log "=== SUMMARY (cold, drained, durable; ABBA) ==="
  1370	  column -t -s, "$OUT_DIR/summary.csv" | tee -a "$OUT_DIR/bench.log"
  1371	  log "=== PAIRED STATS (the rule is graded on these) ==="
  1372	  column -t -s, "$OUT_DIR/paired.csv" | tee -a "$OUT_DIR/bench.log"
  1373	  log "=== SESSION VERDICT (computed by the harness from the PRE-REGISTERED rule) ==="
  1374	  cat "$OUT_DIR/session_verdict.txt" | tee -a "$OUT_DIR/bench.log"
  1375	  log "runs: $CSV"
  1376	}
  1377
  1378	# EXPECT_SHA is required for anything that touches the rig's binaries; SELFTEST
  1379	# supplies the registered default so the gates can be exercised without ceremony.
  1380	if [[ "$SELFTEST" != 1 ]]; then
  1381	  EXPECT_SHA="${EXPECT_SHA:?set EXPECT_SHA to the pinned build (e.g. f35702a)}"
  1382	  HARNESS_HEAD="$(git -C "$REPO_ROOT" rev-parse --short HEAD)"
  1383	  HARNESS_SHA256="$(shasum -a 256 "$SELF" | cut -d' ' -f1)"
  1384	fi
  1385	main "$@"
     1	# otp-12 Mac↔Mac rig — PRE-REGISTRATION (written before any timed run)
     2
     3	**Status**: Pre-registered, **revision 11**. **NO DATA EXISTS YET.**
     4
     5	> ## THE RULE IN ONE PARAGRAPH (rev 11 — D-2026-07-14-3 "simplify", amended by D-2026-07-14-4)
     6	>
     7	> Per cell, take the **paired** ABBA differences (`n` is **exactly 8**), their median, one
     8	> **exact ≥95% order-statistic CI**, and the **full RANGE**. Compare against **one threshold**
     9	> `T = min(10% of the source arm, 230 ms)`, hardened by `B` — the arm bias the clean controls
    10	> could **not exclude**. Four states, exhaustive by construction: **EFFECT** (`CI_lo ≥ T+B`),
    11	> **INVERTED** (`CI_hi ≤ −T−B`), **NONE** (the **full range** inside `±(T−B)` — an effect of
    12	> size T is *excluded*), **UNCLEAR** (anything else). **Every control must be NONE at T/2, and
    13	> `B` must stay under `T/2` on every measurand, or no verdict about the measurand is read at
    14	> all** — not a reproduction, and not a null. The 1.10 bar is reported and takes **no part** in
    15	> this; the sign test is reported, not decided on.
    16	>
    17	> **A NULL is judged on the full RANGE, an EFFECT on the CI.** At the registered `n=8` these
    18	> are the same two numbers — the ≥95% interval **is** `[min, max]`, so nothing can be trimmed.
    19	> That identity is what the whole rule leans on, and the engine **refuses any other `n`**.
    20	>
    21	> **`B` may only ever HARDEN a verdict.** Every rule below that touches `B` exists because a
    22	> review round found a way for it to make some verdict *easier* instead.
    23	>
    24	> That is the whole rule. Eleven review rounds found ~110 defects and **most of the worst were
    25	> in the decision rule, not the measurement** — the complexity *was* the defect. What
    26	> pre-registration is actually for is kept: the question, the statistic and the thresholds are
    27	> fixed **before any data exists**, and the harness **computes** the verdict.
    28	>
    29	> *(Rev 11 rewrote this paragraph. Through rev 10 it still described the rev-8 rule — a NONE
    30	> judged on the CI, no `B`, no `−src/11` — while the body and the code had moved on. Two
    31	> reviewers flagged it independently: **the summary a reader trusts must not describe a rule
    32	> the code does not implement.**)*
    33
    34	> ## ⛔ CORRECTION THAT THIS DOCUMENT OWES ITS READER
    35	>
    36	> **Revisions 3, 4 and 5 of this document asserted that a fixed, equal `SETTLE_MS`
    37	> window precedes the fsync on both arms. THAT WAS NEVER TRUE.** The settle was
    38	> computed by an `awk` inside a command substitution whose quoting was wrong, so the
    39	> awk errored, `sleep` received an empty argument and failed, and the code discarded
    40	> its exit status. **The settle has never executed — not once, in any revision.**
    41	>
    42	> It was introduced in `24660ae` — **the commit that added it to fix the
    43	> free-writeback asymmetry that reverses sign with direction**, i.e. the artifact
    44	> judged capable of *manufacturing a one-directional P1 out of nothing*. **The fix for
    45	> that BLOCKER never ran.**
    46	>
    47	> Nothing is retracted, because **no data was ever taken**. It is fixed, it is
    48	> validated at preflight, and `SELFTEST=1` now proves it on a real tree. But this
    49	> document was wrong for three revisions, and it says so here rather than quietly
    50	> correcting the text below.
    51
    52	Every revision of this document and its instrument has been reviewed before it
    53	measured anything, and **every review has found defects capable of a false claim**:
    54
    55	- Round 1 (design, `f0343f4`): NOT READY — 1 BLOCKER + 7 HIGH + 1 LOW → **9/9
    56	  accepted** (`.review/results/macmac-prereg.gpt-verdict.md`).
    57	- Round 2 (instrument, `e1e351d`): NOT READY — 3 BLOCKER + 6 HIGH + 1 MEDIUM + 1
    58	  LOW → **11/11 accepted** (`.review/results/macmac-harness.gpt-verdict.md`).
    59	- Round 3 (reworked instrument, `24660ae`): **NOT READY** — codex: 5 BLOCKER + 6
    60	  HIGH + 1 MEDIUM → **12/12 accepted**; **grok** (second reviewer, D-2026-07-14-2)
    61	  independently **confirmed both blockers with its own measurements** and found **3
    62	  more** → **15/15 accepted**.
    63	  (`.review/results/macmac-harness-r2.{gpt,grok}-verdict.md`)
    64	- Round 4 (the round-3 rework, `cae2e0f`): **NOT SAFE TO RUN** — **grok**, which
    65	  **drove the engine to a clean `VANISHES` while every control carried the full
    66	  rig-W effect** → **9 findings, 9 accepted** (1 BLOCKER, 3 HIGH, 4 MEDIUM, 1 LOW).
    67	  (`.review/results/macmac-harness-r3.grok-verdict.md`)
    68	- Round 5 (the round-4 rework, `a9460ce`): **NOT READY / NOT SAFE TO RUN** — **codex**
    69	  (3 BLOCKER, 6 HIGH, 2 MEDIUM) **and grok**, which converged on the **same BLOCKER
    70	  independently**: the materiality bug, **for the third round running**, in a branch
    71	  neither had been shown. → **12 findings, 12 accepted.** Plus **the dead settle**
    72	  (above), which the review's finding exposed but did not itself find.
    73	  (`.review/results/macmac-harness-r5.verdict.md`)
    74
    75	- Round 6 (the round-5 rework, `aebd50b`): **NOT READY** — **codex** (3 BLOCKER) **and
    76	  grok** (2 BLOCKER), converging *again* on both hunted classes: the **marginal bar still
    77	  substituted for paired magnitude** (a **1 ms** paired effect reported `REPRODUCES` at
    78	  n=16), a control at **D=+229** — *one millisecond* under the reference effect —
    79	  **certified as clean**, uncertified controls **blocked only the null and not a
    80	  reproduction**, and the settle repair was **still not provable** (a no-op `sleep` would
    81	  have passed while the log narrated "settle included"). → **13 findings, 13 accepted.**
    82	  (`.review/results/macmac-harness-r6.{codex,grok}.md`)
    83	- Round 7 (`1e03063`): **NOT READY** from both again — the drain fails open (a
    84	  `drained_*` value followed by a non-zero exit), rev 7's text contradicted itself, and
    85	  the settle could still be shadowed. → **the owner chose to SIMPLIFY the rule rather than
    86	  harden it again (D-2026-07-14-3).** This document is the result.
    87	  (`.review/results/macmac-harness-r7.{codex,grok}.md`)
    88
    89	**Seven rounds. 80+ findings, all accepted, none rejected. Still no datum taken** — which is
    90	the only reason none of it became a retraction.
   260	### The controls are a PRECONDITION, at HALF the threshold
   261
   262	**Every control must be `NONE` at `T/2`.** Half, because certifying a control with the
   263	very number that *defines* the effect is incoherent: it would let the gRPC control carry
   264	all but 1 ms of P1 while we call the rig clean (round 6 drove exactly that).
   265
   266	**If any control fails, NO verdict about the measurand is read — not a reproduction, and
   267	not a null.** Uncertainty about a rig-wide confound is not evidence that the confound is
   268	absent, and P1's whole claim is that the effect is *specific* to TCP × mixed.
   269
   270	**And "clean" is not "zero" (round-8, codex, BLOCKER).** A control sitting at `+49` with
   271	`T/2 = 50` certifies — but *that 49 ms of arm bias may be riding in the measurand too*, so a
   272	measurand effect of exactly `T` could be half real and half rig. The bias the controls **fail
   273	to exclude** is therefore carried into the measurand's thresholds:
   274
   275	    B = the arm bias the clean controls could NOT rule out, taken from each control's
   276	        full RANGE (not its CI — the CI trims, and a bound must never be computed by
   277	        trimming), as a FRACTION of the arm, then scaled to the cell it is applied to.
   278
   279	    an EFFECT must clear   T + B     (the bias could be INFLATING it)
   280	    a NULL must fit inside T − B     (the bias could be MASKING an effect)
   281
   282	**B is RELATIVE, not raw milliseconds (round-9, codex, BLOCKER).** The controls run
   283	different fixtures at different arm speeds: the *same* 4.9% bias is 122 ms on a 2500 ms
   284	large-file control and 24 ms on a fast one. Carrying raw ms across them **under-penalises a
   285	measurand faster than the control** — and that is the dangerous direction, because it would
   286	license a measurand effect that is mostly rig.
   287
   288	If the controls are genuinely clean, `B` is a few ms and this barely moves. If they are
   289	marginal, it bites — which is the point.
   290
   291	### `B ≥ T/2` on ANY measurand ⇒ `CONTROLS-NOT-CLEAN` (rev 11 — D-2026-07-14-4, owner: "Refuse to grade")
   292
   293	**Passing at `T/2` is necessary but NOT sufficient.** `T` is **capped** at Δ_ref = 230 ms, but
   294	the bias a *clean* control is permitted to carry is a **fraction of its arm** (< 5%), scaled onto
   295	whatever arm it is applied to. On a **slow** measurand the two diverge without limit:
   296
   297	    clean controls at +49 ms on a 1000 ms arm  →  B_frac = 4.9%
   298	    a measurand whose arm is 10 000 ms         →  T = 230 ms,  B = 490 ms
   299
   300	Then `T − B < 0`: **a null is arithmetically impossible.** And `T + B` still licenses an
   301	`EFFECT` of which **up to 68% is permitted rig bias** — at a ratio of 1.072, i.e. *inside* the
   302	project's own invariance bar. A confidently wrong reproduction, read off a rig its own controls
   303	certified clean (round-11, codex HIGH; grok found the same dead-zone independently).
   304
   305	**So: if `B` reaches HALF the effect threshold on any measurand, the session is
   306	`CONTROLS-NOT-CLEAN` and no verdict is read.** The rig's own permitted noise is at least half
   307	the effect being hunted; nothing read off it can be attributed. This is the same principle
   308	already applied to a dirty control — *a noisy rig is fixed by a **quieter rig**, not by grading
   309	it with an asterisk*. **Cost accepted (owner): a marginal rig yields NO answer** and must be
   310	quietened and re-run.
   311
   312	*(The gate is written `t_pos > 0 and B >= t_pos / 2`. The `t_pos > 0` term is not a fail-open:
   313	`src_med` is a **positive integer** by construction — the engine refuses a non-positive timing —
   314	so `t_pos` can only be zero in a **mutant** that accepts zeros, and the term keeps that mutant's
   315	dangerous path (zeros → EFFECT → REPRODUCES) reachable by the guard test instead of masking it
   316	behind this refusal.)*
   317
   318	### The controls are CONTEMPORANEOUS with the measurands
   319
   320	The schedule is **slot-major**: within slot *i*, **every** cell takes one ABBA pair before
   321	any cell takes slot *i+1*. All six cells therefore span the same wall-clock window.
   322
   323	**And the order ROTATES by slot (round-9, codex, HIGH).** A *fixed* order put both measurand
   324	cells ahead of every control in every slot — so a **periodic** transient could land on the
   325	measurands and never on the controls that exist to catch it. Over 8 slots each cell occupies
   326	each position.
   327
   328	*(Round-8, codex, HIGH: both measurand cells used to run first and the controls afterwards
   329	— so **the controls certified a window they were never in**. A transient could hit the
   330	measurand and be gone before the controls ran, and they would pronounce the rig clean.)*
   331
   332	### The session verdict
   333
   334	1. **INCOMPLETE** — any registered cell short of its `RUNS` pairs, or with a CI below 95%
   335	   coverage. (Checked against the **data**; `meta.complete` is not believed. **Exactly 8 paired
   336	   slots AND exactly 8 valid rows in EACH arm**: an unpaired or duplicate valid row would sit in
   337	   the arm's list and skew its **median** — and therefore `T`, `B` and the bar — while the pair
   338	   count still looked right.)
   339	2. **RIG-VOID** — the harness voided the session (end-load, or the **fabric changed** under it;
   340	   see Gates).
   341	3. **CONTROLS-NOT-CLEAN** — any control is not `NONE` at `T/2`, **or `B ≥ T/2` on any
   342	   measurand** (see above).
   343	4. **MIXED** — one direction `EFFECT`, the other `INVERTED`: a host×role interaction this
   344	   rig cannot decompose. **Decided on the UNHARDENED cell states (`B = 0`)** — rev 11,
   345	   round-11 codex HIGH. `B` hardens each *cell*, but through this branch's **precedence** it
   346	   could make the *session* verdict **easier**: with measurands at `+110` and `−94` on a 1000 ms
   347	   arm, controls at **zero** give `MIXED`, while clean controls at `+5` push the `−94` cell out
   348	   of `INVERTED`, the `MIXED` branch stops firing, and the session reports **`REPRODUCES`**. *A
   349	   noisier rig produced a stronger claim.* A cell whose CI clears `−T` on its own carries
   350	   evidence of inversion that control noise cannot un-say. (`EFFECT`-at-`B` is a subset of
   351	   `EFFECT`-at-0, so this branch can only ever fire **more** often — the direction it must move
   352	   in.)
   353	5. **REPRODUCES** — `EFFECT` in **either** direction. *(P1's rig-W signature is
   354	   one-directional, so demanding both would rewrite the finding. A messy sibling is
   355	   reported, never substituted.)*
   356	6. **INVERTED** — a new finding; never banked as "P1 absent".
   357	7. **DOES-NOT-REPRODUCE** — **both** measurand cells `NONE`, with clean controls. A
   358	   genuine equivalence result.
   359	8. **UNCLEAR** — otherwise. **This is not a null.** There is no remedy in more pairs: fix the rig.
   360
   361	### What is deliberately ABSENT, and why that is safe
   362
   363	- **The 1.10 bar takes NO part in inference.** It is computed on the *marginal medians*,
   364	  reported in every row as the project's **acceptance** criterion, and never consulted.
   365	  The marginal and paired statistics can disagree in **direction and magnitude**, and
   366	  every attempt to let one stand in for the other produced a false verdict.
   367	- **The sign test is reported, not decided on.** At n=8 the CI already implies it
   368	  (`CI_lo >= T > 0` means *every* pair clears T), so making it a second gate only added
   369	  an interaction to get wrong. It is printed per cell.
   370	- **No `UNSTABLE` / `PARTIAL` / `BAR-FAIL-INCONSISTENT` / `UNDERPOWERED` branches, and no
   371	  precedence stack.** A bimodal arm **widens the CI**, and a wide CI lands in `UNCLEAR` —
   372	  which is exactly what those branches were hand-coding. Every run of every arm is still
   373	  printed in `summary.csv`, so bimodality remains visible to the reader.
   374	- **A real but SUB-THRESHOLD effect is reported, not buried.** A cell can be `NONE` and
   375	  still carry a consistent effect below T (e.g. 99 ms on a 1000 ms arm, on 7 of 8 pairs).
   376	  The verdict prints a NOTE naming it. It does not change the outcome — the threshold was
   377	  registered in advance — but it is **not nothing**, and it does not hide inside the word
   378	  "none".
   379
   380	### There is NO escalation. `n` is EXACTLY 8.
   381
   382	The old `RUNS=16` escalation is **removed** (owner, 2026-07-14). A null is judged on the
   383	**full range**, which only **widens** with n — so more pairs could never rescue an `UNCLEAR`
   384	rig, nor certify a marginal control; and if you already have an `EFFECT`, you do not need
   385	them.
   386
   387	**And `n` must be EXACTLY 8, not "at least 8" (round-9, grok, BLOCKER).** At the registered
   388	n=8 the ≥95% interval **is** `[min, max]` — it *cannot* trim. At any larger n it starts
   389	trimming outliers, and a bimodal arm then yields a narrow median CI and a false verdict:
   390	grok drove a 16-pair CSV (three pairs at −500 trimmed away, thirteen at +200 left) straight
   470
   471	   **Now, and only now: equalized, and BOUNDED — not "removed" (round-3 HIGH).** The
   472	   settle window is **equal on both arms** (250 ms, computed once at top level,
   473	   validated at startup, and its failure **VOIDS the pair**). The residual is the ssh
   474	   dispatch difference, **measured at ~15 ms** (median of 5, warm mux, recorded in the
   475	   manifest every session; a failed ssh now refuses rather than contributing a
   476	   flattering number). A pre-fsync delay of 10/20/200 ms produced **no measurable
   477	   change** in fsync time here (72–94 ms, no trend) — APFS fsync on this rig is
   478	   per-file-metadata bound, not writeback bound — so a 15 ms residual cannot plausibly
   479	   move it. **That is a bound from measurement, not a removal by construction, and this
   480	   document no longer claims otherwise.** `SELFTEST=1` walks a real tree and proves the
   481	   settle actually executed.
   482
   483	## Gates — every one fails CLOSED, and every one is EXECUTED
   484
   485	Round 2 found the round-1 "fixes" **had never been run** (`bash -n` is not an
   486	execution): the preflight **could not succeed at all** — `grep -c` exits 1 on no
   487	match, so a **clean** binary tripped the dirty-marker probe and died, and `norm_mac`
   488	used gawk's `strtonum()`, absent from stock macOS awk.
   489
   490	`SELFTEST=1` **exercises the gates for real and takes no data.** It reports three
   491	states — `[OK]`, `[FIRED]` (a genuinely unmet condition: the gate *works*), and
   492	`[BROKEN]` (**the probe cannot answer at all**) — and **exits non-zero on any BROKEN**,
   493	because *a blind gate is precisely what fails open on the night*. It also **prints what
   494	it does NOT cover**.
   495
   496	*(Round-5 codex, HIGH: the previous self-test labelled **every** nonzero result
   497	`[FIRED]` — including a probe that could not answer — exited zero, and claimed "every
   498	gate executes" while never touching drain, purge, daemon, fsync/settle, stale-daemon or
   499	end-load. **A self-test that overstates itself is the very fail-open it exists to
   500	hunt.**)*
   501
   502	It has now earned itself three times: it caught `link_gate` **refusing a perfectly good
   503	link** (`arp -n <ip>` prints **one line per interface** — `q` holds entries for nagatha
   504	on en0, en1 *and* en8 — so the unfiltered MAC was a three-line string that could never
   505	equal one MAC; the gate now checks the entry **on the egress NIC**, the more correct
   506	question anyway); it caught **the dead settle**; and it caught **itself** breaking its
   507	own next gate (it ran `resolve_disk` in a subshell, which discarded the global that
   508	function exists to set, so the drain then had no device and blamed the harness).
   509
   510	- **QUIESCENCE, BOTH MACS** — refuse if `codex`/`cargo`/`rustc` runs on **either**
   511	  Mac. `pgrep` rc≥2 is an **error**, not "quiet" (rev 3 could not tell them apart).
   512	- **TIME MACHINE, BOTH MACS** — refuse if a backup is running **or if autobackup is
   513	  merely ENABLED** (macOS repeats hourly; pf-0's fired 1 minute before its run). A
   514	  read error refuses.
   515	- **SPOTLIGHT, BOTH MACS** — `mds_stores` CPU, taken as the **MAX across samples**
   516	  (rev 3 took the last, so a late idle sample could overwrite an earlier busy one);
   517	  a failed `top` is an **error**, not 0%.
   518	- **LOAD** — `load1` on both Macs at start **and end**. A start `load1` above 3.0
   519	  refuses; an **end** `load1` above 3.0 **VOIDS THE SESSION** (`RIG-VOID`), because a
   520	  mid-session load spike is exactly the contamination the start gate exists to stop.
   521	  *(Round-4, grok: rev 4 moved the end-load logging before the verdict and its
   522	  comment claimed a session "can void on it" — but the code only **logged** it and
   523	  graded anyway. A doc claim the code did not honour: the very defect class this
   524	  review exists to kill.)*
   525	- **COLD CACHES** both ends every run (`sudo -n /usr/sbin/purge`); a failed purge
   526	  **VOIDS the pair**.
   527	- **DRAIN** — destination disk quiet before each window (`< 2 MB/s`, 3 consecutive
   528	  2 s samples). The device is **RESOLVED from the module path** through its APFS
   529	  physical store (grok: rev 3 hardcoded `disk0` and could certify a disk the data
   530	  never touched — and on APFS a *synthesized* disk can read idle while the physical
   531	  store saturates). A **non-numeric** `iostat` sample is an **error**, never "quiet"
   532	  (rev 3 read it as zero and **certified drainage**).
   533	- **DURABILITY** — the per-file `fsync` walk runs **on the destination host for both
   534	  arms**, is timed, and returns `NA` on a missing tree → the pair **VOIDS**.
   535	- **FIXTURES** verified by **count AND byte sum** on both ends before any timed run.
   536	- **PROVENANCE** — clean `+f35702a` on all four binaries (never `.dirty`); the
   537	  harness, the **verdict engine** and its **guard test** are all hashed into the
   538	  manifest; the instrument must be **committed and clean** in git (a modified
   539	  harness must not be able to claim the reviewed commit); `EXPECT_SHA` must equal
   540	  the **registered** build. `die` inside `$(...)` exits only the subshell, so the
   541	  hash functions now **return non-zero** and the caller dies (rev 3 wrote an **empty
   542	  hash** and called it provenance).
   543	- **DAEMON LIFECYCLE** — the pid comes from `$!` (not `pgrep | head -1`, which picks
   544	  the first of whatever is running); it must be **alive AND LISTENING** on the port;
   545	  teardown is **verified** (a failed probe is a failure, not "GONE") and a survivor
   546	  is recorded, not discarded.
   547	- **LINK** — peer ARP **on the egress NIC** resolves to the **peer's** MAC (a host
   548	  route on a directly-connected subnet installs a black hole that still reports the
   549	  right interface), and the route egresses the 10GbE NIC (macOS routes by service
   550	  order, so a 1GbE NIC can win and every run would go over gigabit).
   551	- **THE REGISTERED FABRIC — the NIC *and* the PATH (rev 11; round-11 codex, BLOCKER).**
   552	  Until rev 11 the topology was **not enforced at all**: NIC/IP/MAC were env-overridable and
   553	  **the MTU and link speed were never checked**, so the whole run could have gone over the
   554	  **1GbE** NIC or at **MTU 1500** — after pf-0 spent **256 runs** establishing that MTU moves
   555	  wall time on this fabric. nagatha alone has **eight** other 1500-MTU interfaces. Now:
   556	  * the topology (`N_IP`/`N_NIC`/`N_MAC`/`Q_IP`/`Q_NIC`/`Q_MAC`/`Q_SSH`, plus `RUNS` and
   557	    `PORT`) is **pinned in code** and the harness **refuses to start** if any of those names is
   558	    merely *present* in the environment — the same rule the thresholds already had;
   559	  * **the NIC**: MTU **9000**, negotiated media **10Gbase-T** (a 1GbE link reports
   560	    `1000baseT`), status `active` — on **both** Macs;
   561	  * **the PATH**: a real TCP connection to the peer's **registered IP**, and the **MSS the two
   562	    kernels agreed** — **8948** (= 9000 − 20 IP − 20 TCP − 12 timestamps). *Both NICs can sit
   563	    at MTU 9000 while a 1500 hop between them clamps segments to 1448 — **only the MSS sees
   564	    that**.* This is the gate pf-0 used. The probe also reports the **local address the kernel
   565	    chose**, which proves the traffic **egressed the registered 10GbE IP** and not one of the
   566	    other interfaces.
   567	  * **start AND end.** The fabric is re-proved when the session finishes; a link that flapped
   568	    or re-routed mid-session **VOIDS the session** (`RIG-VOID`). A gate measured once at the
   569	    start is not a gate on a run taken an hour later.
   570	  * the **measured** fabric is written into the manifest, not just the registered one.

exec
/bin/zsh -lc "nl -ba scripts/otp12pf_mac_verdict.py | sed -n '200,370p;430,480p'
nl -ba scripts/otp12pf_mac_verdict_test.py | sed -n '100,125p;205,225p'

   201	    best = None

   209	def sign_p(d):
   210	    """Reported, never decided on."""
   211	    nz = [x for x in d if x]
   212	    n = len(nz)
   213	    if not n:
   214	        return 1.0, 0, 0
   215	    k = sum(1 for x in nz if x > 0)
   216	    tail = sum(comb(n, i) for i in range(min(k, n - k) + 1))
   217	    return min(1.0, 2.0 * tail / 2 ** n), k, n

   220	def thresholds(s_med, scale=1.0):
   221	    """T_pos and T_neg -- NOT symmetric in ms, because the 1.10 bar is symmetric in
   222	    RATIO: +src/10 reaches ratio 1.10, but only -src/11 reaches the INVERSE 1.10.
   223	    Both capped at DELTA_REF, so an effect must matter by the project's bar AND be the
   224	    size of the one rig W measured. `scale` = 0.5 for controls."""
   225	    return (min(s_med / 10.0, float(DELTA_REF)) * scale,
   226	            -min(s_med / 11.0, float(DELTA_REF)) * scale)
   227

   229	def classify(ci_lo, ci_hi, rng_lo, rng_hi, t_pos, t_neg, B=0.0):
   230	    """THE RULE. Four states, mutually exclusive and exhaustive BY CONSTRUCTION.
   231
   232	    EFFECT/INVERTED use the >=95% CI on the median; NONE uses the FULL RANGE. At the
   233	    registered n=8 these coincide (the CI IS the range), so nothing can be trimmed either
   234	    way -- the distinction is the SEMANTICS that keeps the rule sound if a larger n is ever
   235	    registered, and the engine REFUSES any n but 8.
   236
   237	    NONE uses the FULL RANGE -- EVERY pair must lie inside +-T. Round 8 (codex, BLOCKER):
   238	    a >=95% CI at n>8 TRIMS outliers, so a BIMODAL arm produces a NARROW median CI and a
   239	    FALSE NULL (driven: CI = [1,1] from modes at +-110). An equivalence claim must never be
   240	    reachable by trimming away the very pairs that contradict it. This is also why
   241	    bimodality needs no special branch: it cannot hide from the range.
   242
   243	    B IS APPLIED HERE, ONCE, AND IT ONLY EVER HARDENS (round-11 grok, MEDIUM). It used to be
   244	    folded into the thresholds by the CALLER -- which passed `t_pos + B` and so WIDENED the
   245	    NONE window -- and a second check downstream then tightened the null back to `T - B`. The
   246	    net effect was right and the intermediate call was wrong, which is a bug waiting for the
   247	    next person who "simplifies" it. Now every state reads the same B in the same direction:
   248	    an EFFECT must CLEAR T + B (the bias could be inflating it), and a NULL must FIT INSIDE
   249	    T - B (the bias could be masking one). Both bounds move AGAINST the reader.
   250	    """
   251	    if ci_lo >= t_pos + B:
   252	        return "EFFECT"
   253	    if ci_hi <= t_neg - B:
   254	        return "INVERTED"
   255	    if t_neg + B < rng_lo and rng_hi < t_pos - B:
   256	        return "NONE"
   257	    return "UNCLEAR"
   258

   261	cell = {}
